EDCS-00380907.text_edition: Aurelius  Annianus p Iulianus lius Sabinus Valerius Iulianus Iunius Antonius Iulius Valerius  Valerius Bassinus
EDCS-00380907.translation: Aurelius Annianus, P. Iulianus, L. Sabinus, Valerius Iulianus, Iunius Antonius, Iulius Valerius, Valerius Bassinus.
EDCS-00380907.summary: This inscription lists a series of Roman personal names, possibly indicating individuals associated with a particular monument or record, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-00380911.summary: This inscription presents two Roman names, Minicius Marcus, likely identifying an individual.

EDCS-00380914.text_edition: Legio XIII gemina Iulius Iulianus  CXIIII
EDCS-00380914.summary: This inscription records the name of Legio XIII Gemina and an individual named Iulius Iulianus, followed by the number 114, which could represent a cohort, century, or a unit designation, dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-00380918.summary: This inscription, likely a builder's mark or dedication, records that someone whose name began with 'Diocl' made the object or monument, probably from the late Imperial period.

EDCS-03000781.summary: This inscription gives the abbreviated names of a Roman man, Gaius Iulius, followed by an initial M, probably the beginning of his cognomen.

EDCS-03000782.summary: This inscription provides the abbreviated names of Lucius Ophoni, followed by an initial A, likely the beginning of his cognomen.

EDCS-03000783.summary: This inscription presents the abbreviated names of Lucius Ophoni, followed by an initial L, likely the beginning of his cognomen.

EDCS-03000784.summary: This inscription records the abbreviated names of an individual, Quintus, followed by two Q initials, likely representing a patronymic or clan name and a cognomen.

EDCS-03300768.text_edition: Aeterno Optimo Maximo Publius Aelius Alexander ex viso
EDCS-03300768.translation: To the Eternal, Best, Greatest, Publius Aelius Alexander, by vision.
EDCS-03300768.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to a deity (the Eternal, Best, Greatest), made by Publius Aelius Alexander following a vision, likely dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-03300770.text_edition: Apollini Domesticus Publi Aeli Proculi beneficiarii praefecti legionis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-03300770.translation: To Apollo, Domesticus, a beneficiarius of the prefect of the legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-03300770.summary: This is a votive inscription to Apollo, offered by Domesticus, who held the rank of beneficiarius under a legionary prefect, fulfilling a vow willingly and deservedly, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-03300771.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Diva Augusta Faustina, likely the deified empress Faustina the Elder, indicating a public or private act of veneration.

EDCS-03300772.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina, Aelius Bassus
EDCS-03300772.summary: This inscription records Aelius Bassus as a member of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, likely part of a military record, funerary monument, or dedication.

EDCS-03300773.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina, Aelius Iulius
EDCS-03300773.summary: This inscription records Aelius Iulius as a member of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, likely part of a military record, funerary monument, or dedication.

EDCS-03300774.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina, Aelius Philippus
EDCS-03300774.summary: This inscription records Aelius Philippus as a member of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, likely part of a military record, funerary monument, or dedication.

EDCS-03300775.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina, Antonius Eventus
EDCS-03300775.summary: This inscription records Antonius Eventus as a member of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, likely part of a military record, funerary monument, or dedication.

EDCS-03300776.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina, Antonius Firmus
EDCS-03300776.summary: This inscription records Antonius Firmus as a member of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, likely part of a military record, funerary monument, or dedication.

EDCS-03300777.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina, Aurelius Callistratus
EDCS-03300777.summary: This inscription records Aurelius Callistratus as a member of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, likely part of a military record, funerary monument, or dedication.

EDCS-03300778.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina, Aurelius Godes
EDCS-03300778.summary: This inscription records Aurelius Godes as a member of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, likely part of a military record, funerary monument, or dedication.

EDCS-03300779.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina, Aurelius Mommo
EDCS-03300779.summary: This inscription records Aurelius Mommo as a member of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, likely part of a military record, funerary monument, or dedication.

EDCS-03300780.text_edition: Legio XIII gemina Luci Auri Secundi
EDCS-03300780.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina, of Lucius Auris Secundus
EDCS-03300780.summary: This inscription records Lucius Auris Secundus as a member of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, likely part of a military record, funerary monument, or dedication.

EDCS-03300781.summary: This fragment likely records a member named Euphron- belonging to the Thirteenth Twin Legion, probably from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-03300782.summary: This inscription records Flavius Martinus as a member of the Thirteenth Twin Legion, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-03300783.summary: This inscription records Flavius Vitalis as a member of the Thirteenth Twin Legion, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-03300784.summary: This inscription records Iulius Deiotarus as a member of the Thirteenth Twin Legion, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-03300785.summary: This inscription records Iulius Iulianus as a member of the Thirteenth Twin Legion, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-03300786.summary: This inscription records Iulius Logetus as a member of the Thirteenth Twin Legion, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-03300787.summary: This inscription records Iulius Maximus as a member of the Thirteenth Twin Legion, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-03300788.summary: This inscription records Iulius Serenus as a member of the Thirteenth Twin Legion, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-03300789.summary: This inscription records Iustus as a member of the Thirteenth Twin Legion, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-03300790.text_edition: Legio XIII gemina Locretius sic Acvila sic
EDCS-03300790.translation: Thirteenth Twin Legion, Locretius, thus Aquila, thus
EDCS-03300790.summary: This inscription records Locretius as a member of the Thirteenth Twin Legion, with an unusual additional mention of Aquila, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-03300791.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina (of) Ofellius Annianus.
EDCS-03300791.summary: This is a military inscription, likely a property mark, indicating ownership or association of Ofellius Annianus with the Legio XIII Gemina, probably dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-03300792.summary: This military inscription, probably a property mark, records the association of Rufus with the Legio XIII Gemina, likely from the early Imperial era.

EDCS-03300793.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina (of) Ulpius Fronto.
EDCS-03300793.summary: This military inscription, likely a property mark, identifies Ulpius Fronto with the Legio XIII Gemina, probably from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-03300794.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina (of) Valerius C.
EDCS-03300794.summary: This military inscription, possibly a property mark, indicates the association of Valerius C. with the Legio XIII Gemina, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-03300795.summary: This military inscription, probably a property mark, records the association of Victor with the Legio XIII Gemina, likely from the early Imperial era.

EDCS-03300796.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Valerius vixit annis XL Aurelia Banea coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-03300796.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelius Valerius lived for 40 years. Aurelia Banea placed this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-03300796.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Aurelius Valerius at age 40, set up by his wife Aurelia Banea, likely dating to the High Imperial period.

EDCS-03300798.text_edition: Dis Manibus Dules Maximi vixit annis XLVII IVAL AXIS annis  ANIA Nice coniu lio   posuit
EDCS-03300798.translation: To the Divine Manes. Dules Maximi lived for 47 years. [---] Nice placed this for [her] well-deserving husband.
EDCS-03300798.summary: This fragmented funerary inscription commemorates Dules Maximi, who lived 47 years, and was set up by his wife Nice, probably dating to the High Imperial period.

EDCS-03700693.summary: This brief inscription records the name Staglius Aphrodisius, possibly indicating ownership or identification, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-03700695.summary: This is a fragmented milestone inscription, recording a distance of seven miles and mentioning an emperor, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-03700697.summary: This military inscription, likely a property mark, indicates the association of Julianus with the Legio XIII Gemina, probably dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-03700698.text_edition: Dis Manibus Antoniae Paulae Quartus miles legionis XIII g(eminae) et Valerius Crescentinus
EDCS-03700698.translation: To the Divine Manes of Antonia Paula. Quartus, a soldier of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, and Valerius Crescentinus (set this up).
EDCS-03700698.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Antonia Paula by a soldier of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina and Valerius Crescentinus, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-03700699.text_edition: Ulpius Do pro s(alute) commeantium votum libens solvit
EDCS-03700699.translation: Ulpius Do gladly fulfilled his vow for the safety of those traveling.
EDCS-03700699.summary: This inscription records Ulpius Do's fulfillment of a vow made for the safety of travelers, suggesting a dedication to a deity protecting journeys, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-03700700.text_edition: Aesculapio et Hygiae Marcus Gallius Epictetus Augustalis coloniae Apulensis et Gallia Severina eius et Gallius Surus et Gallius Severinus et Gallius Severianus filii pro salute sua suorumque somno monitus porticum fecit per pedes XXXVI
EDCS-03700700.translation: To Aesculapius and Hygia, Marcus Gallius Epictetus, Augustalis of the colony of Apulum, and Gallia Severina his wife, and Gallius Surus, Gallius Severinus, and Gallius Severianus, their sons, made a portico 36 feet long, prompted by a dream, for their own health and that of their kin.
EDCS-03700700.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a 36-foot portico to Aesculapius and Hygia by Marcus Gallius Epictetus, an Augustalis of Apulum, his wife, and their three sons, motivated by a dream for the health of their family, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-03700701.text_edition: Herculi defensori Titus Claudius Durianus in reditu ex Urbe sospes votum solvit libens merito de sua pecunia
EDCS-03700701.translation: To Hercules the Defender, Titus Claudius Durianus, safe upon his return from the City, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow with his own money.
EDCS-03700701.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Hercules the Defender by Titus Claudius Durianus, who fulfilled his vow upon his safe return from Rome, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-03700702.text_edition: Silvano sancto  Solon ex voto fecit dedicavitque  Kalendas  Pisone et Iul(iano) conssulibus
EDCS-03700702.translation: To Holy Silvanus, Solon made and dedicated this in fulfillment of a vow on the Kalends, when Piso and Julianus were consuls.
EDCS-03700702.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Silvanus by Solon, explicitly dated to the consulship of Piso and Julianus (AD 205), making it a precisely dated monument.

EDCS-03700704.text_edition: Nymphis sanctissimis Marcus Aurelius Mosianus optio I legionis XIII geminae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-03700704.translation: To the Most Holy Nymphs, Marcus Aurelius Mosianus, optio I of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-03700704.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to the Nymphs by Marcus Aurelius Mosianus, an optio of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-03700705.text_edition: Genio Imperatoris Antonini Augusti Pii patris patriae Marcus Statius Priscus legatus Augusti pro praetore votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-03700705.translation: To the Genius of the Emperor Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of his country, Marcus Statius Priscus, legate of the Emperor with propraetorian power, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-03700705.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to the Genius of Emperor Antoninus Pius by Marcus Statius Priscus, an imperial legate, dating to the reign of Antoninus Pius (AD 138-161).

EDCS-04900900.text_edition: Mercurio Augusto sacrum Decimus Iunius Teles votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-04900900.translation: Sacred to Mercurius Augustus, Decimus Iunius Teles gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-04900900.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Mercurius Augustus by Decimus Iunius Teles, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-04900901.text_edition: Herculi cives Romani per Satriaanum et Decimum magistros
EDCS-04900901.translation: To Hercules, the Roman citizens through Satrianus and Decimus, magistrates.
EDCS-04900901.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Hercules by Roman citizens, facilitated by their magistrates Satrianus and Decimus, suggesting a communal religious act from the Imperial period.

EDCS-04900902.text_edition: Terrae Matri cives Romani per Satrianum et Decumum magistros
EDCS-04900902.translation: To Terra Mater, the Roman citizens through Satrianus and Decimus, magistrates.
EDCS-04900902.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Terra Mater by Roman citizens, facilitated by their magistrates Satrianus and Decimus, suggesting a communal religious act from the Imperial period.

EDCS-04900903.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Dis hospitibus huiusce loci Marcus Pollius Marci filius Oufentina Hispanus Mediolano |(centurio) legionis V Macedonicae piae constantis et Caedicia Marcia eius cum suis
EDCS-04900903.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and to the guest gods of this place, Marcus Pollius Hispanus, son of Marcus, of the Oufentina tribe, from Mediolanum, centurion of the Fifth Legion Macedonica Pia Constans, and his wife Caedicia Marcia, with their dependents [dedicated this].
EDCS-04900903.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter and local gods by a Roman centurion and his wife, likely marking a religious offering or a votive gift.

EDCS-04900904.text_edition: Fortunae Domesticae Moschus Augusti libertus tabularius et Aurelia Asclepiodote ex voto
EDCS-04900904.translation: To Fortuna Domestica, Moschus, freedman of Augustus and archivist, and Aurelia Asclepiodote, from a vow.
EDCS-04900904.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Fortuna Domestica by a freedman of Augustus, who was an archivist, and Aurelia Asclepiodote, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-04900905.text_edition: Aurelius Valens sisquiplicarius sic alae Inlyricorum sic vixit nns XLV Tzetzi coniux coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-04900905.translation: Aurelius Valens, duplicarius of the Illyrian cavalry ala, lived 45 years. His wife Tzetzi placed this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-04900905.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Tzetzi for her husband Aurelius Valens, a cavalry officer, who died at the age of 45.

EDCS-04900906.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelia Kara vixit annos XL Ulpius Dassius veteranus ex decurione coniugi pientissimae bene merenti fecit
EDCS-04900906.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Aelia Kara lived 40 years. Ulpius Dassius, a veteran ex-decurion, made this for his most devoted and well-deserving wife.
EDCS-04900906.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Ulpius Dassius, a veteran and former decurion, to his wife Aelia Kara, who lived for 40 years.

EDCS-04900909.summary: This is a short dedicatory inscription to the goddess Diana, specifically associated with the province of Dacia Ripensis.

EDCS-04900911.summary: This is a simple dedicatory inscription to the goddess Diana by Cornelia Marcellina.

EDCS-04900912.summary: This is a simple dedicatory inscription to the goddess Hygia by Cornelia Marcellina.

EDCS-04900913.summary: This is a simple dedicatory inscription to the Nymphs by Baebius Ingenuus.

EDCS-04900914.summary: This is a simple dedicatory inscription to the Nymphs by Licinia Cale.

EDCS-04900915.translation: To the Nymphs, Decebalus, son of Lucius, placed this.
EDCS-04900915.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the Nymphs by Decebalus, son of Lucius.

EDCS-04900916.text_edition: Nymphis sanctis Augustis simul et tibi sancta Deana fontique vestro rettulit sua vota libens saluti ter refirmatus aquis Germisarae Marcus Aurelius Theodotus votum solvit libens merito Imperatore Commodo Felice consule VI VIII Kalendas Commodas
EDCS-04900916.translation: To the holy Augustan Nymphs and likewise to you, holy Diana, and to your spring, Marcus Aurelius Theodotus willingly paid his vows for his well-being, three times restored by the waters of Germisara. He willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, with Imperator Commodus Felix as consul for the sixth time, on the 8th day before the Kalends of Commodus (August 25th).
EDCS-04900916.summary: This inscription records Marcus Aurelius Theodotus's fulfillment of a vow to the Nymphs and Diana for his health, specifically mentioning the healing waters of Germisara, dated to August 25, 190 CE, during the sixth consulship of Emperor Commodus.

EDCS-04900918.text_edition: Nymphis sacrum Lucius Livius Marcellus IIviralis coloniae pro filiis ex visu libens merito
EDCS-04900918.translation: Sacred to the Nymphs. Lucius Livius Marcellus, duovir of the colony, on behalf of his sons, based on a vision, willingly and deservedly [made this].
EDCS-04900918.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Nymphs by Lucius Livius Marcellus, a duovir of a colony, made on behalf of his sons following a vision.

EDCS-04900919.text_edition: Nymphis Titus Fabius Aquileiensis tribunus numeri singularium Britannicianorum
EDCS-04900919.translation: To the Nymphs, Titus Fabius Aquileiensis, tribune of the numeri singularium Britannicianorum (auxiliary unit of Britannician picked men).
EDCS-04900919.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Nymphs by Titus Fabius Aquileiensis, a military tribune of a specialized unit from Britain.

EDCS-05000698.summary: This is a very brief and incomplete inscription, likely part of a name or dedication, with Tiberius and two abbreviated elements.

EDCS-05000699.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Arranius Epaphroditus et Arranius Niger filius posuerunt
EDCS-05000699.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Marcus Arranius Epaphroditus and Arranius Niger, his son, erected this.
EDCS-05000699.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus erected by Marcus Arranius Epaphroditus and his son, Arranius Niger.

EDCS-05000700.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Vibius Nicostratus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-05000700.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Gaius Vibius Nicostratus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05000700.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Gaius Vibius Nicostratus.

EDCS-05000701.text_edition: Chrestus Zoili ex voto pro filio Demosthene testamento libens merito solvit
EDCS-05000701.translation: Chrestus, son of Zoilos, from a vow, on behalf of his son Demosthenes, paid this from his will, willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-05000701.summary: This inscription details Chrestus, son of Zoilos, fulfilling a vow on behalf of his son Demosthenes, making the payment from his will.

EDCS-05000703.translation: Flavinus, son of Castor, by the command of Hercules.
EDCS-05000703.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Flavinus, son of Castor, made in accordance with a command from Hercules.

EDCS-05000704.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Aurelius Diocles beneficiarius procuratoris
EDCS-05000704.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Titus Aurelius Diocles, beneficiarius of the procurator.
EDCS-05000704.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Titus Aurelius Diocles, who held the military rank of beneficiarius under a procurator.

EDCS-05000706.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Statiae Festae Caius Restitutius Iustus et Caius Iulius Priscinus votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-05000706.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the well-being of Statia Festa. Gaius Restitutius Iustus and Gaius Iulius Priscinus willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-05000706.summary: This inscription records Gaius Restitutius Iustus and Gaius Iulius Priscinus fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the well-being of Statia Festa.

EDCS-05000708.text_edition: Aurelius Occon Quietia  votum libentes m(erito)? p(osuerunt)
EDCS-05000708.translation: Aurelius Occon Quietia gladly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-05000708.summary: This is a votive inscription by Aurelius Occon Quietia, indicating the fulfillment of a vow, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-05000710.text_edition: Deae virgini Dianae stabili venatrici examinatrici Aurelius Marcellus praefectus eqquitum
EDCS-05000710.translation: To the maiden goddess Diana, constant hunter, examiner, Aurelius Marcellus, prefect of cavalry [dedicates this].
EDCS-05000710.summary: This is a votive dedication to the goddess Diana, specifically honoring her as a huntress and examiner, made by Aurelius Marcellus, a prefect of cavalry, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-05000711.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Conservatori Marcus Iunius Iunianus praefectus equitum votum solvit
EDCS-05000711.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the Preserver, Marcus Junius Junianus, prefect of cavalry, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05000711.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Conservator, dedicated by Marcus Junius Junianus, a prefect of cavalry, in fulfillment of a vow, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05200580.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Quintus Marius Proculus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-05200580.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Quintus Marius Proculus, a consular beneficiarius, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05200580.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, dedicated by Quintus Marius Proculus, a beneficiarius consularis, in fulfillment of a vow, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-05200581.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Calpurnius Priscinus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-05200581.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Gaius Calpurnius Priscinus, a consular beneficiarius, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05200581.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, dedicated by Gaius Calpurnius Priscinus, a beneficiarius consularis, in fulfillment of a vow, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-05200582.text_edition: Mercurio Augusto sacrum Nasidius Primus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-05200582.translation: Sacred to Mercury Augustus, Nasidius Primus gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05200582.summary: This is a votive dedication to Mercury Augustus by Nasidius Primus, marking the fulfillment of a vow, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05200583.text_edition: Neptuno Augusto votum solvit libens merito Nassidius sic Primus
EDCS-05200583.translation: To Neptune Augustus, Nassidius Primus thus gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05200583.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Neptune Augustus by Nassidius Primus, indicating the fulfillment of a vow, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05200584.text_edition: Maelantonio Dasas et Dazurius pro salutae sic Maniatium votum solverunt libentes
EDCS-05200584.translation: Maelantonius Dasas and Dazurius fulfilled their vows for the health of Maniatium.
EDCS-05200584.summary: This is a votive inscription by Maelantonius Dasas and Dazurius, made for the well-being of Maniatium, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-05200590.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Iucundius Verus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-05200590.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Gaius Jucundius Verus, a consular beneficiarius, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05200590.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, dedicated by Gaius Jucundius Verus, a beneficiarius consularis, in fulfillment of a vow, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-05200592.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication to a deity named Nao, made by Julia Beu[c--], probably dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-05200593.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Calpurnius Priscinus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit merito libens
EDCS-05200593.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Gaius Calpurnius Priscinus, a consular beneficiarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05200593.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Gaius Calpurnius Priscinus, a beneficiarius consularis, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely a votive altar from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-05200594.text_edition: Aerecurae Aelius Scenus Bala filius votum solvit libens
EDCS-05200594.translation: To Aerecura, Aelius Scenus Bala, her son, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05200594.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to the goddess Aerecura, made by Aelius Scenus Bala, her son, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-05200596.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Tritio Gar votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-05200596.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Tritius Gar willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05200596.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, made by Tritius Gar, likely a private individual, from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-05200597.text_edition: Terrae Matri Batonianus posuit votum libens merito
EDCS-05200597.translation: To Mother Earth, Batonianus willingly and deservedly placed this vow.
EDCS-05200597.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Terra Mater (Mother Earth) by Batonianus, likely a private individual, from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-05200598.text_edition: Neptuni sic sacrum Surio Sumeletis votum solvit libens
EDCS-05200598.translation: Sacred to Neptune: Surius, son of Sumeletis, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05200598.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Neptune, made by Surius, son of Sumeletis, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-05200599.text_edition: Nimphis sic sacrum Implaius Sumeletis votum solvit libens
EDCS-05200599.translation: Sacred to the Nymphs: Implaius, son of Sumeletis, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05200599.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to the Nymphs, made by Implaius, son of Sumeletis, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-05200600.translation: B. Zosimus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05200600.summary: This inscription records a simple votive dedication by B. Zosimus, likely a private individual, from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-05200602.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Sidera (the Stars or a local deity), made by Aelius Quintus, likely a private individual, from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-05200605.text_edition: Aelius Equester veteranus ex |(centurione) Aureliae Iuiuni coniugi virginiae bene merenti con sic quen sic vixit annis LX posuit titulum et sibi
EDCS-05200605.translation: Aelius Equester, a veteran, former centurion, erected this titulus for Aurelia Iuiuni, his virginal and well-deserving wife, who lived for 60 years, and for himself.
EDCS-05200605.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument erected by Aelius Equester, a veteran and former centurion, for his wife Aurelia Iuiuni, who died at age 60, and also for himself, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-06100498.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Caio Vibio Afinio Gallo Veldumiano Volusiano Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate II patri patriae consuli II proconsuli ordo coloniae Aureliae Apulensis Chrysopolis numini eius dedicavit
EDCS-06100498.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Vibius Afinius Gallus Veldumianus Volusianus, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the second time, Father of his Country, Consul for the second time, Proconsul: the order of the colony Aurelia Apulensis Chrysopolis dedicated this to his divinity.
EDCS-06100498.summary: This inscription is a public dedication by the colonial council of Aurelia Apulensis Chrysopolis to Emperor Volusianus, likely from 251-253 AD.

EDCS-07000829.summary: This is a dedication made by Aelius Frontianus to the rural god Silvanus Domesticus, likely on a small altar or votive object.

EDCS-07000832.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Fortunatus Caesaris nostri verna Valerianus votum solvit libens
EDCS-07000832.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Fortunatus Valerianus, a slave born in our emperor's household, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07000832.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Fortunatus Valerianus, a 'verna' (household slave) of the emperor, indicating his gratitude or fulfillment of a promise.

EDCS-07000833.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Marci Valeri Martini decurionis coloniae votum libens posuit
EDCS-07000833.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the well-being of Marcus Valerius Martinus, decurion of the colony, a vow was willingly placed.
EDCS-07000833.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, made for the health of Marcus Valerius Martinus, a decurion of a Roman colony, suggesting a communal or public act of piety.

EDCS-07000834.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Aelius Baebius votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07000834.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Publius Aelius Baebius willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07000834.summary: This is a votive inscription by Publius Aelius Baebius to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, commemorating the fulfillment of a vow, likely on a small altar or tablet.

EDCS-07000837.text_edition: Dis Manibus Flavius Maximus Surus sacerdos vixit nnos XLV Castor et Foebus sic pientissimi bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-07000837.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Flavius Maximus Surus, a priest, lived 45 years. Castor and Phoebus, most dutiful, placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-07000837.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Flavius Maximus Surus, a priest, erected by Castor and Phoebus, likely his relatives or associates, commemorating his life and priesthood.

EDCS-07000842.text_edition: Iuppiter Maximus Σabidas Hutri votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07000842.translation: Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Sabidas Hutri willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07000842.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Sabidas Hutri to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, indicating the fulfillment of a vow with the appropriate sentiment 'libens merito'.

EDCS-07000843.text_edition: Pro salute et victoria Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Antonini Commodi Pii Felicis Augusti nostri restitutoris commerciorum et Genio publici portorii Illyrici Claudius Xenophon procurator Augusti nostri per Marcionem et Pollionem vilicos
EDCS-07000843.translation: For the well-being and victory of our Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Commodus Pius Felix Augustus, restorer of trade, and to the Genius of the Public Tax of Illyricum, Claudius Xenophon, our emperor's procurator, through Marcionius and Pollio, his estate managers.
EDCS-07000843.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Commodus and the Genius of the public tax of Illyricum, made by Claudius Xenophon, the imperial procurator, via his 'vilici', likely commemorating an administrative act or imperial beneficence related to trade in the late 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07000844.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Imperatoris Marci Aureli Antonini Augusti et Commodi [Caesaris] et Genio publici portorii vectigalis Illyrici procurante Pompeio Longo procuratore Augusti Felix eius vilicus
EDCS-07000844.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the well-being of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus and Commodus Caesar, and to the Genius of the public tax of Illyricum, under the governorship of Pompeius Longus, the emperor's procurator, his estate manager Felix [placed this].
EDCS-07000844.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of the public tax of Illyricum, made for the health of Emperors Marcus Aurelius and Commodus, likely placed by Felix, an estate manager under the procurator Pompeius Longus, around the late 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07400623.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelio Dubitato missicio alae Batavorum ex ala numeri Illyricorum qui vixit annos XXX heredes et coniunx fecerunt hic situs est
EDCS-07400623.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Aelius Dubitatus, a veteran of the Batavian cavalry ala, from the cavalry unit of the Illyrian cohort, who lived 30 years. His heirs and wife made this. He is laid here.
EDCS-07400623.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aelius Dubitatus, a veteran of the cavalry, placed by his heirs and wife, detailing his military service and age at death.

EDCS-07400629.text_edition: Dis Manibus Fll(aviae) Ursulae vixit annos LXXXIII Flavia Caemina liberta pro pietate patronae suae posuit bene merenti
EDCS-07400629.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Flavia Ursula, who lived 83 years. Flavia Caemina, her freedwoman, placed this out of piety for her well-deserving patron.
EDCS-07400629.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Flavia Ursula, placed by her freedwoman Flavia Caemina out of loyalty and respect, commemorating Ursula's long life and their patron-client relationship.

EDCS-07400630.text_edition: VA  miles legionis XIII geminae exceptor legati Augusti vixit nnos XX Marcus Valerius Maximinus  actarius cohortis
EDCS-07400630.translation: VA, a soldier of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, an excertor (clerk) for the imperial legate, lived 20 years. Marcus Valerius Maximinus, records clerk for the cohort.
EDCS-07400630.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a soldier and clerk named VA, and also mentions Marcus Valerius Maximinus, another clerk, indicating a military context.

EDCS-07400631.text_edition: Deo sancto aeterno Aurelius l ex voto posuit
EDCS-07400631.translation: To the holy and eternal god, Aurelius L. placed this as a vow.
EDCS-07400631.summary: This is a votive inscription by Aurelius L. dedicating an offering to a holy and eternal god, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-07400633.summary: This is a simple votive inscription recording that Aurelius Saturninus fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-07600401.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Naevius Felicio cum suis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07600401.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Marcus Naevius Felicio, with his household, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07600401.summary: This is a votive inscription by Marcus Naevius Felicio and his family to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, indicating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-07600402.text_edition: Deo Silvano Caius Iulius Metrobianus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07600402.translation: To the god Silvanus, Gaius Julius Metrobianus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07600402.summary: This is a votive inscription by Gaius Julius Metrobianus to the god Silvanus, indicating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-07600409.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ulpius Adiutor vixit annos LXXX Ulpia Iaia coniux vixit annos L filii et filia suis parentibus posierunt sic bene merentibus
EDCS-07600409.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Ulpius Adiutor lived 80 years. Ulpia Iaia, his wife, lived 50 years. Their sons and daughter placed this for their well-deserving parents.
EDCS-07600409.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ulpius Adiutor and his wife Ulpia Iaia, erected by their children.

EDCS-07600410.text_edition: Dis Manibus Gaius Valerius Dius vixit annos LX
EDCS-07600410.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Gaius Valerius Dius lived 60 years.
EDCS-07600410.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Valerius Dius, who lived to be 60 years old.

EDCS-08400449.text_edition: Numini Saturno regi patri deorum  et Latonae Publius Raecius Primus beneficiarius legati legionis V Macedonicae piae fidelis posuit ex voto domo Zigali  Afer
EDCS-08400449.translation: To the divine power of Saturn, king and father of the gods, and to Latona, Publius Raecius Primus, a beneficiarius of the legate of the Fifth Legion Macedonica, loyal and faithful, placed this from his vow, from his home in Zigalis, an African.
EDCS-08400449.summary: This is a votive inscription by Publius Raecius Primus, a beneficiarius of the Legio V Macedonica, to Saturn and Latona, fulfilling a vow and noting his origin from Zigalis in Africa.

EDCS-08500532.text_edition: Viator  Firmus Aelius Priscus Aelius Domitianus Aelius Florus Aelius Valens Aelius Quintus  Atressus  Firmus  Alexander  Germanus  Valens Secundus Macer Vialis Maximus Rufinus Valentinus Capito Crescens Longinus    Iulius Messius Iulius Siro Pomponius Valens Attius Maximus Aelius Valens Aelius Quadratus Aelius Iulianus Aelius Talanus Licinius Priscus Attius Longinus Aelius Iustus Aelius Valens Aelius Balbinus Flavius Niger Iulius Verus Iulius Bassianus Aelius Bassus Aelius Festus Flavius Flavos sic Claudius Candidianus Caelius Saturn(inus) Aelius     Aelius  Ulpius Verus Aelius Appius Valerius Verus Cuspius Valens Aelius Mestrius Antonius Valens ex equite Egnatius Libo Marcius Firminus Flavius Venustus Valerius Succesus Aelius Agrippa Aelius Quintus Aelius Firmus Aelius Crescens Aelius Capito Aelius Tertius Aelius Valens Aelius Propincus
EDCS-08500532.translation: Viator Firmus, Aelius Priscus, Aelius Domitianus, Aelius Florus, Aelius Valens, Aelius Quintus, Atressus Firmus, Alexander, Germanus, Valens Secundus, Macer, Vialis, Maximus, Rufinus, Valentinus, Capito, Crescens, Longinus, Julius Messius, Julius Siro, Pomponius Valens, Attius Maximus, Aelius Valens, Aelius Quadratus, Aelius Julianus, Aelius Talanus, Licinius Priscus, Attius Longinus, Aelius Justus, Aelius Valens, Aelius Balbinus, Flavius Niger, Julius Verus, Julius Bassianus, Aelius Bassus, Aelius Festus, Flavius Flavos, Claudius Candidianus, Caelius Saturninus, Aelius [...], Aelius [...], Ulpius Verus, Aelius Appius, Valerius Verus, Cuspius Valens, Aelius Mestrius, Antonius Valens, formerly a cavalryman, Egnatius Libo, Marcius Firminus, Flavius Venustus, Valerius Succesus, Aelius Agrippa, Aelius Quintus, Aelius Firmus, Aelius Crescens, Aelius Capito, Aelius Tertius, Aelius Valens, Aelius Propincus.
EDCS-08500532.summary: This inscription appears to be a list of numerous names, possibly a roster of individuals, perhaps soldiers or members of a collegium, given the mention of a former cavalryman.

EDCS-08500533.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Gai C Hastae consularis trium Daciarum stratores eius legionis XIII geminae agentes Proculo legato
EDCS-08500533.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, for the well-being of Gaius C. Hasta, consular governor of the three Dacias, his stratores (groomsmen/orderlies) of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, acting on behalf of Proculus, the legate.
EDCS-08500533.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the health of the consular governor Gaius C. Hasta, set up by his stratores from Legio XIII Gemina, acting for their legate Proculus, indicating a military dedication in Dacia.

EDCS-08500537.text_edition: ---aere collato I IVOVIVSI Iulius Ru     SC SED ellus s  Surillus Marcus miles  Sigillus Aquileiensis Maximus Nicomedis Iulius Statorius Gaianus Agrippae Aurelius Amamus  Atius  Surus   Caius Maioris Aurelius Charito Cassius Valens Aurelius Sossius miles Aurelius Marcus miles Aurelius Valerius Romanius Arist Aurelius Silvanus Hermes Domnionis Aurelius Proclus Gaianus A Crescens  Aurelius    Aurelius A Aper I Caius Antonius Iulianus  Act Nus Aurelius  CIC Valerius V
EDCS-08500537.translation: --- with money collected, Julius Ru[---], Marcus the soldier, Sigillus Aquileiensis, Maximus Nicomedis, Julius Statorius Gaianus Agrippae, Aurelius Amamus, Atius Surus, Gaius Maioris, Aurelius Charito, Cassius Valens, Aurelius Sossius the soldier, Aurelius Marcus the soldier, Aurelius Valerius Romanius, Arist, Aurelius Silvanus Hermes Domnionis, Aurelius Proclus Gaianus, A. Crescens, Aurelius [---], Aurelius A. Aper, Gaius Antonius Iulianus, Act. Nus, Aurelius [---], Valerius V[---].
EDCS-08500537.summary: This inscription lists numerous individuals, likely dedicants or members of an organization, who contributed money, but the monument's purpose and recipient are lost.

EDCS-08500538.translation: Carus [---] on account of the honor [---]
EDCS-08500538.summary: This fragment mentions an individual named Carus and suggests a dedication made 'on account of the honor,' but the full context is lost.

EDCS-08500539.text_edition: sacrum Publius Aelius Rusticus decurio municipii Ampelensium votum vix satis reddidit
EDCS-08500539.translation: Sacred. Publius Aelius Rusticus, decurion of the municipality of Ampelum, barely fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-08500539.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Publius Aelius Rusticus, a town councilor of Ampelum, indicating a vow that he struggled to fulfill.

EDCS-08500540.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Val(erius) Telegonus pro se et suos sic votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-08500540.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Gaius Valerius Telegonus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for himself and his family.
EDCS-08500540.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, made by Gaius Valerius Telegonus on behalf of himself and his family.

EDCS-08500541.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo ex voto E Mucianus pro se et pro SOSOSE
EDCS-08500541.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, from a vow. E. Mucianus for himself and for [---].
EDCS-08500541.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, made by E. Mucianus in fulfillment of a vow for himself and others.

EDCS-08500542.text_edition: Dis Manibus Curneliae sic Quintae vixit annos XX et
EDCS-08500542.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Cornelia Quinta lived for 20 years and [---].
EDCS-08500542.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Cornelia Quinta, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-08500548.text_edition: Dis Manibus Rufus Diomedis vixit annos IV
EDCS-08500548.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Rufus Diomedis lived for 4 years.
EDCS-08500548.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Rufus Diomedis, who passed away at the age of four.

EDCS-08500549.text_edition: Dis Manibus Pontiae Syusae vixit annos V et Pontiae Asiae matri eius vixit annos
EDCS-08500549.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Pontia Syusa lived for 5 years, and Pontia Asia, her mother, lived for [---] years.
EDCS-08500549.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Pontia Syusa, who lived for five years, and her mother, Pontia Asia, whose age is partially lost.

EDCS-08500550.text_edition: vixit annos LX Lucius coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-08500550.translation: He/She lived for 60 years. Lucius placed this for his well-deserving spouse.
EDCS-08500550.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Lucius for his deceased spouse, who lived to be 60 years old.

EDCS-08500553.text_edition: Silvano Silvestri sacrum Varro Titi posuit
EDCS-08500553.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Silvester. Varro, son of Titus, placed this.
EDCS-08500553.summary: This inscription is a sacred dedication to the god Silvanus Silvester, set up by Varro, the son of Titus.

EDCS-08500556.text_edition: Herculi Augusto Caius Sempronius Urbanus procurator Augusti
EDCS-08500556.translation: To Hercules Augustus, Gaius Sempronius Urbanus, imperial procurator.
EDCS-08500556.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Hercules Augustus by Gaius Sempronius Urbanus, an imperial procurator, likely on a votive altar or monument.

EDCS-08500557.text_edition: Volcano miti Caius Sempronius Urbanus procurator Augusti
EDCS-08500557.translation: To gentle Vulcan, Gaius Sempronius Urbanus, imperial procurator.
EDCS-08500557.summary: This inscription records a dedication to gentle Vulcan by Gaius Sempronius Urbanus, an imperial procurator, suggesting a personal or official act of piety.

EDCS-08500558.text_edition: Apollini Granno et Sironae Caius Sempronius Urbanus procurator Augusti
EDCS-08500558.translation: To Apollo Grannus and Sirona, Gaius Sempronius Urbanus, imperial procurator.
EDCS-08500558.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Celtic deities Apollo Grannus and Sirona by Gaius Sempronius Urbanus, an imperial procurator, reflecting a blend of Roman and local religious practices.

EDCS-08500559.text_edition: Marti Singili et Minervae Caius Sempronius Urbanus procurator Augusti
EDCS-08500559.translation: To Mars Singilis and Minerva, Gaius Sempronius Urbanus, imperial procurator.
EDCS-08500559.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Mars Singilis and Minerva by Gaius Sempronius Urbanus, an imperial procurator, indicating a devotional act to these martial and wisdom deities.

EDCS-08500561.text_edition: Fortunae Augustae Aelius Apollinaris procurator Augusti
EDCS-08500561.translation: To Fortuna Augusta, Aelius Apollinaris, imperial procurator.
EDCS-08500561.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Fortuna Augusta by Aelius Apollinaris, an imperial procurator, likely commemorating good fortune or a wish for continued imperial prosperity.

EDCS-08500564.text_edition: Apollini sancto Marcus Lucceius Felix procurator Augusti nostri
EDCS-08500564.translation: To holy Apollo, Marcus Lucceius Felix, procurator of our Augustus.
EDCS-08500564.summary: This inscription records a dedication to holy Apollo by Marcus Lucceius Felix, an imperial procurator, indicating a religious offering from a high-ranking official.

EDCS-08500565.text_edition: Dianae sanctae Marcus Lucceius Felix procurator Augusti nostri
EDCS-08500565.translation: To holy Diana, Marcus Lucceius Felix, procurator of our Augustus.
EDCS-08500565.summary: This inscription records a dedication to holy Diana by Marcus Lucceius Felix, an imperial procurator, demonstrating a veneration for the goddess of the hunt and wilderness.

EDCS-08500567.text_edition: Marcus Lucceius Felix procurator Augusti nostri consecravit
EDCS-08500567.translation: Marcus Lucceius Felix, procurator of our Augustus, consecrated this.
EDCS-08500567.summary: This inscription records the consecration of an unspecified monument or object by Marcus Lucceius Felix, an imperial procurator, highlighting an act of official or personal devotion.

EDCS-08500569.text_edition: Deae praesentissimae Core Marcus Lucceius Felix procurator Augusti nostri et Hostilia Faustina eius
EDCS-08500569.translation: To the most present goddess Core, Marcus Lucceius Felix, procurator of our Augustus, and Hostilia Faustina, his wife.
EDCS-08500569.summary: This inscription records a joint dedication to the goddess Core by Marcus Lucceius Felix, an imperial procurator, and his wife Hostilia Faustina, suggesting a family's religious piety to a local or prominent deity.

EDCS-08500570.text_edition: Numinibus praesentissimis Apollini et Bono Puero Marcus Aurelius Marcus vir egregius procurator Augusti nostri agens vice praesidis Heraclida alumnus pro salute patroni sui posuit
EDCS-08500570.translation: To the most present numina, Apollo and Bonus Puer, Marcus Aurelius Marcus, an excellent man, procurator of our Augustus, acting in the place of the governor, with his foster-son Heraclida, set this up for the health of his patron.
EDCS-08500570.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Marcus Aurelius Marcus, an imperial procurator and acting governor, along with his foster-son Heraclida, to Apollo and Bonus Puer for the well-being of his patron, indicating a complex act of gratitude and devotion from an elite official.

EDCS-08500574.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Caio Messio Quinto Traiano Decio Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate consuli II proconsuli ala I Hispanorum Campagonum Deciana civium Romanorum devota numini maiestatique eius ex quaestura sua
EDCS-08500574.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Messius Quintus Traianus Decius Pius Felix Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power, consul for the second time, proconsul, the First Wing of Campagonian Spaniards, called Deciana, of Roman citizens, devoted to his divinity and majesty, from their own quaestorship.
EDCS-08500574.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the Ala I Hispanorum Campagonum Deciana C.R. to the Emperor Decius, likely commemorating a renewal of their loyalty or a specific achievement during his reign, possibly around 250 AD.

EDCS-08500577.text_edition: Cohors I Flavia Hispanorum Mmiliaria |(centuria) Valeri Clumeni sic Rassini sic Ursionis
EDCS-08500577.translation: The First Flavian Cohort of Spaniards, milliary. The century of Valerius Clumenus, likewise Rassinus, likewise Ursio.
EDCS-08500577.summary: This inscription records the names of centuries within the Cohors I Flavia Hispanorum milliaria, likely serving as a property mark or a record of centuries stationed at a particular location.

EDCS-08500578.text_edition: centuria Severini Quintus cohors I Hispanorum quingenaria
EDCS-08500578.translation: The century of Severinus. Quintus. The First Cohort of Spaniards, quingenary.
EDCS-08500578.summary: This inscription identifies a specific century, that of Severinus, within the First Quingenary Cohort of Spaniards, possibly indicating ownership or a record of a unit's presence.

EDCS-08500579.text_edition: Cohors I Ulpia Hispanorum centuria Cai Pomponi Valer
EDCS-08500579.translation: The First Ulpian Cohort of Spaniards. The century of Gaius Pomponius Valerius.
EDCS-08500579.summary: This inscription designates the century of Gaius Pomponius Valerius belonging to the First Ulpian Cohort of Spaniards, likely a military unit marker or property inscription.

EDCS-08500580.text_edition: Cohors I Flavia Ulpia Hispanorum centuria Luci Turrani Antedori  TI
EDCS-08500580.translation: The First Flavian Ulpian Cohort of Spaniards. The century of Lucius Turranius Antedorus. TI.
EDCS-08500580.summary: This inscription records the century of Lucius Turranius Antedorus within the First Flavian Ulpian Cohort of Spaniards, probably indicating military property or unit assignment.

EDCS-08500581.translation: The century of Secius Ocius Sedatus.
EDCS-08500581.summary: This inscription likely indicates a century of a specific military unit, led by Secius Ocius Sedatus, possibly a property mark or record of an assigned group.

EDCS-08500582.translation: The First Ituraean Cohort. The century of Turnus.
EDCS-08500582.summary: This inscription identifies a century, led by Turnus, within the First Ituraean Cohort, serving as a military marker for a unit or a specific portion of a structure.

EDCS-08500588.text_edition: Marci Aureli Cassianus et Veteranus aediles  semis
EDCS-08500588.translation: Marcus Aurelius Cassianus and Veteranus, aediles. A half-as.
EDCS-08500588.summary: This inscription records Marcus Aurelius Cassianus and Veteranus as aediles, possibly indicating their contribution or oversight of a project, with a mention of a monetary unit.

EDCS-08500590.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aurelius Respectus miles cohortis I Britannicae
EDCS-08500590.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aurelius Respectus, soldier of the First British Cohort.
EDCS-08500590.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to Aurelius Respectus, a soldier serving in the First British Cohort, indicating his burial and military affiliation.

EDCS-08600625.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcus Iulius M(arci)? f(ilius) Ulpia Victorinus Sarmizegetusa librarius consularis stipendiorum V vixit annis XXIII
EDCS-08600625.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Marcus Iulius Victorinus, son of Marcus, from Ulpia Sarmizegetusa, a consular clerk, served for five stipends, lived for twenty-three years.
EDCS-08600625.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Marcus Iulius Victorinus, a consular clerk from Sarmizegetusa, detailing his origin, profession, years of service, and age at death.

EDCS-08600626.text_edition: Genio coloniae Sarmizegetusae Quintus Axius Aelianus vir egregius procurator Augusti nostri
EDCS-08600626.translation: To the Genius of the colony of Sarmizegetusa, Quintus Axius Aelianus, distinguished man, procurator of our Augustus, [set this up].
EDCS-08600626.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Quintus Axius Aelianus, a procurator, to the Genius (protective spirit) of the colony of Sarmizegetusa.

EDCS-08600629.text_edition: Publi Corneli Coloni chelidonium opobalsamatum ad caligines  Publi Corneli Coloni dioxus ad aspritudines et genas callosas  Publi Corneli Coloni diapsoricum opobalsamatum ad claritatem
EDCS-08600629.translation: Of Publius Cornelius Colonus: chelidonium opobalsamatum for dimness of sight. Of Publius Cornelius Colonus: dioxus for roughnesses and calloused cheeks. Of Publius Cornelius Colonus: diapsoricum opobalsamatum for clarity [of vision].
EDCS-08600629.summary: This inscription lists medical remedies and their uses, attributed to Publius Cornelius Colonus, likely from a professional's sign or tombstone.

EDCS-08900553.text_edition: Apollini Dianae et Leto sic ceterisque dis deabusque huiusque loci salutaribus ex imperio numinis Caius Iulius Frontonianus veteranus legionis V Macedonicae piae ex beneficiario consularis decurio coloniae Apulensis pro se et suis pecunia sua pontem exstruxit
EDCS-08900553.translation: To Apollo, Diana, and Leto, and likewise to the other salutary gods and goddesses of this place, by command of the deity, Gaius Iulius Frontonianus, a veteran of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, Pious, formerly a beneficiary of the consular governor, a decurion of the colony of Apulum, built a bridge at his own expense for himself and his family.
EDCS-08900553.summary: This inscription records that Gaius Iulius Frontonianus, a veteran and decurion, built a bridge at his own expense for himself and his family, in fulfillment of a divine command, dedicating it to Apollo, Diana, Leto, and other local deities.

EDCS-08900554.text_edition: Matri Deum Magnae Titus Fabius Barbatus pro salute
EDCS-08900554.translation: To the Great Mother of the Gods, Titus Fabius Barbatus, for his well-being.
EDCS-08900554.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Titus Fabius Barbatus to the Magna Mater (Great Mother of the Gods) for his own well-being.

EDCS-08900555.text_edition: Matribus Aus Publius Aelius Secundinus eques legionis XIII geminae Antoninianae ex voto posuit libenter
EDCS-08900555.translation: To the Mothers, Publius Aelius Secundinus, an equestrian of the Thirteenth Gemina Antoniniana Legion, placed this willingly, in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-08900555.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication by Publius Aelius Secundinus, an equestrian of the Thirteenth Legion, to the Matres (Mothers).

EDCS-08900557.text_edition: Filomelus armorum custos legionis XIII geminae vixit annos XXXII  Corolla et Aurelius Chrestus et Aelia Victorina heredes eius bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-08900557.translation: Filomelus, keeper of arms of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, lived 32 years. Corolla and Aurelius Chrestus and Aelia Victorina, his heirs, placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-08900557.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Filomelus, an armorum custos of the Thirteenth Legion, erected by his heirs Corolla, Aurelius Chrestus, and Aelia Victorina.

EDCS-08900559.text_edition: M PAE Flavia ula domoa vixit annos L  hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur a maceria? in fronte pedesX in agro pedes  ad angulos r pedes CXX
EDCS-08900559.translation: M. Pae[---] Flavia ula, a woman of the house, lived 50 years. This monument will not pass to an heir. From the wall, 10 feet in front, [---] feet in the field, 120 feet at the corners.
EDCS-08900559.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Flavia ula, indicating her age and specifying restrictions on the monument's inheritance and its dimensions.

EDCS-08900562.text_edition: Sergius Maximus fratri pientissimo faciendum curavit
EDCS-08900562.translation: Sergius Maximus arranged for this to be made for his most dutiful brother.
EDCS-08900562.summary: This is a funerary inscription, recording that Sergius Maximus had the monument made for his very devoted brother.

EDCS-08900563.text_edition: stupendiorum sic V vixit annis XXV posuit Valerius Senecio beneficiarius legati bene merenti fecit
EDCS-08900563.translation: [He served] 5 years of stipends, lived 25 years. Valerius Senecio, a beneficiary of the legate, placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-08900563.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an individual who served five stipends and lived 25 years, set up by Valerius Senecio, a legate's beneficiary.

EDCS-08900566.text_edition: stipendiorum XV vixit annos XXXV Aelius Marcus et Aelius Agrippa heredes faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-08900566.translation: [He served] 15 years of stipends, lived 35 years. Aelius Marcus and Aelius Agrippa, his heirs, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-08900566.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an individual who served 15 years and lived 35 years, erected by his heirs Aelius Marcus and Aelius Agrippa.

EDCS-08900575.text_edition: Lucio Aelio Aurelio Commodo filio Imperatoris Titi Aeli Hadriani Antonini Augusti Pii patris patriae res publica municipii Hadriani Drobetae decreto decurionum
EDCS-08900575.translation: To Lucius Aelius Aurelius Commodus, son of Imperator Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of his country. The republic of the Hadrianic municipality of Drobeta, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08900575.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the municipality of Drobeta, by decree of its decurions, to Lucius Aelius Aurelius Commodus, son of Emperor Antoninus Pius, likely dating to Commodus's youth (mid-2nd century AD).

EDCS-08901108.text_edition: Silvano Domestico Flavius Mar votum solvit libens
EDCS-08901108.translation: To Silvanus Domesticus, Flavius Mar willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-08901108.summary: This is a votive inscription by Flavius Mar to the god Silvanus Domesticus, indicating a private act of devotion.

EDCS-08901109.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription containing parts of names, likely a funerary or dedicatory text, but too incomplete to determine its full purpose.

EDCS-08901112.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus Felix Parthicus maximus pontifex maximus Britannicus maximus tribuniciae potestatis XVI imperator II consul IIII pater patriae proconsul fecit
EDCS-08901112.translation: Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus Felix, greatest Parthian victor, greatest Briton, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the sixteenth time, acclaimed imperator for the second time, consul for the fourth time, father of his country, proconsul, made this.
EDCS-08901112.summary: This inscription details the extensive titles and accomplishments of Emperor Caracalla (Marcus Aurelius Antoninus), recording his act of construction during his sixteenth year of tribunician power (213 AD).

EDCS-08901115.text_edition: Aelius Thema veteranus ex |(centurione) decurio coloniae LAMAT patri
EDCS-08901115.translation: Aelius Thema, a veteran, former centurion, decurion of the colony of LAMAT, to his father.
EDCS-08901115.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aelius Thema, a veteran and decurion, to his father.

EDCS-08901119.text_edition: Aelia siN vixit annos XL Marcus Cocceius Alexander veteranus ex |(centurione) augur municipii Septimii Porolissensis coniugi
EDCS-08901119.translation: Aelia sinned, lived 40 years. Marcus Cocceius Alexander, a veteran, former centurion, augur of the municipium Septimium Porolissensis, to his wife.
EDCS-08901119.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Marcus Cocceius Alexander, a veteran and augur of Porolissum, to his wife Aelia, who lived for 40 years.

EDCS-08901134.translation: To the Paternal Gods, Aurelius Surianus...
EDCS-08901134.summary: This is a brief, fragmentary religious inscription dedicated to the 'Paternal Gods' by Aurelius Surianus.

EDCS-08901135.summary: This is a highly fragmentary inscription, likely containing a dedication or reference to a patron, but too incomplete to ascertain its full meaning.

EDCS-08901137.text_edition: Caius Iulius Sabinus bis IIviralis et patronus municipii Hadriani Drobetae ob honorem flamoni Cai Iuli Naesi Sabini nepotis sui cryptam vetustate dilapsam pecunia sua reformavit et exaltiavit
EDCS-08901137.translation: Gaius Iulius Sabinus, twice duumvir and patron of the Hadrianic municipality of Drobeta, for the honor of the flamenship of Gaius Iulius Naesus Sabinus, his grandson, restored and raised a crypt that had fallen into disrepair due to age, at his own expense.
EDCS-08901137.summary: This inscription records the restoration and elevation of an old crypt by Gaius Iulius Sabinus, a prominent citizen of Drobeta, in honor of his grandson's flamenship.

EDCS-09001563.text_edition: Aurelius Cavianus ex votu sic posuit
EDCS-09001563.translation: Aurelius Cavianus placed this in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-09001563.summary: This is a votive inscription where Aurelius Cavianus fulfilled a vow by setting up this monument.

EDCS-09200448.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  Gaius Sentius Bassus tubicen legionis XIII geminae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-09200448.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Gaius Sentius Bassus, trumpeter of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09200448.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Jupiter by a trumpeter of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-09200470.text_edition: Genio |(centuriae) Aurelius Asclepiades votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-09200470.translation: To the Genius of the centuria. Aurelius Asclepiades willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09200470.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to the protective spirit (Genius) of a military century, made by Aurelius Asclepiades, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-09300004.text_edition: Pro salute Imperatoris Marci Aureli Antonini Augusti Pii Felicis deo patrio Belo numerus Palmyrenorum sagittariorum templum vi ignis consumptum pecunia sua restituerunt dedicanteF#dedicante#DEDICANTF Caio Iulio Septimio Castino consulari III Daciar(um) M(arco) Ulpio Victore procuratore Augusti provinciae Porolissensis curam agente Tito Flavio Saturnino |(centurione) legionis V Macedonicae piae constantis
EDCS-09300004.translation: For the well-being of the Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Pius Felix, the troop of Palmyrene archers restored at their own expense the temple consumed by fire to their ancestral god Bel, with Gaius Iulius Septimius Castinus, consular of the Three Dacias, dedicating it, and Marcus Ulpius Victor, procurator Augusti of the province Porolissensis, overseeing the work, and Titus Flavius Saturninus, centurion of the Fifth Legion Macedonica Pia Constans, taking charge.
EDCS-09300004.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a temple to the god Bel by a unit of Palmyrene archers for the welfare of Emperor Caracalla (Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Pius Felix), dedicated by a consular governor and overseen by a procurator and a centurion, dating to the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-09300505.text_edition: Legioni V Macedonicae piae constanti  Lucius U<lp=II>(ius)#Ulp(ius)#UII Clem(ens) Caius Valerius Lupus Caius Tiberius Crescens commanuculis sic posuerunt
EDCS-09300505.translation: To the Fifth Legion Macedonica Pia Constans, Lucius Ulpius Clemens, Gaius Valerius Lupus, Gaius Tiberius Crescens, fellow soldiers, thus set this up.
EDCS-09300505.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Fifth Legion Macedonica Pia Constans by three of its soldiers, possibly commemorating a joint action or service, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-09300506.text_edition: Silvano Domestico Valerius Valerianus votum susceptum libens solvit
EDCS-09300506.translation: To Silvanus Domesticus, Valerius Valerianus willingly fulfilled his undertaken vow.
EDCS-09300506.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Silvanus Domesticus, a god associated with forests and homes, made by Valerius Valerianus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-09300507.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Silvano ceterisque deis deabusque conservatoribus Publius Arrius anus S v(otum)? s(olvit)? l(ibens) merito
EDCS-09300507.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and Silvanus and the other preserving gods and goddesses, Publius Arrius Anus S. willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09300507.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Jupiter, Silvanus, and other protective deities by Publius Arrius Anus S., likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-09300508.text_edition: Dis Manibus Claudius Valentinus vixit annis XX Valeria Valentina filio piissimo opto sit tibi terra levis
EDCS-09300508.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Claudius Valentinus lived for 20 years. Valeria Valentina [to her] most dutiful son. May the earth be light for you.
EDCS-09300508.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument dedicated to Claudius Valentinus, who died at age 20, set up by his mother Valeria Valentina, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-09300511.text_edition: Aelia Ingenua Einis Iris hic iacet sub terra misera  alita es in annis plus Fata vetarunt iam nulla est  parentes mater
EDCS-09300511.translation: Aelia Ingenua, Einis, Iris, lies here under the earth, miserable. You were nurtured for more years, [but] the Fates forbade it; now there is nothing left. Her parents, mother.
EDCS-09300511.summary: This inscription is a poignant funerary epitaph for Aelia Ingenua, also known as Einis or Iris, set up by her mother, lamenting her untimely death, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-09301198.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  Silius Cai filius Vellina Crispinus Aquileia |(centurio) legionis XIII geminae strator Luci Iuli Proculi legati Augusti pro praetore votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-09301198.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Silius Crispinus, son of Caius, of the Vellina tribe, from Aquileia, centurion of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, groom of Lucius Iulius Proculus, imperial legate with propraetorian power, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09301198.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Jupiter by Silius Crispinus, a centurion of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina and a groom (strator) for an imperial legate, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-09301201.summary: This inscription is a very brief votive dedication to Silvanus Silvester, a god associated with untamed forests, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-09301203.text_edition: Deo Herhiboli Aelius Nisa sacerdos posuit
EDCS-09301203.translation: To the god Herhibolus, Aelius Nisa, a priest, set this up.
EDCS-09301203.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Herhibolus, set up by Aelius Nisa, a priest.

EDCS-09301206.text_edition: Nymphis Auggustis pro salute dominnorum n[nostrorum] dedicante Flavio Postumo consulari
EDCS-09301206.translation: To the August Nymphs, for the health of our lords, dedicated by Flavius Postumus, consular.
EDCS-09301206.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the August Nymphs for the emperor's health, made by Flavius Postumus, a consular official.

EDCS-09301208.text_edition: Deae Nemesi Reginae Caius Valerius Maximus pecumarius sic ex visu posuit
EDCS-09301208.translation: To the Queen goddess Nemesis, Gaius Valerius Maximus, a livestock dealer, thus set this up as a result of a vision.
EDCS-09301208.summary: This is a votive inscription to the goddess Nemesis Regina, set up by Gaius Valerius Maximus, a livestock dealer, following a vision.

EDCS-09301233.text_edition: Deo Invicto Herculi Magusano pro salute sua et suorum Publius Aelius Maximus a a militiis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-09301233.translation: To the Unconquered god Hercules Magusanus, for his and his family's health, Publius Aelius Maximus, from military service, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09301233.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to Hercules Magusanus, made by Publius Aelius Maximus, a veteran, for the safety of himself and his family.

EDCS-09400616.text_edition: Deo Sancto Aurelius  votum libens solvit
EDCS-09400616.translation: To the Holy God, Aurelius willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09400616.summary: This is a votive inscription to a Holy God, made by Aurelius.

EDCS-09400620.translation: The Thirteenth Twin Legion, Aelius Bassus.
EDCS-09400620.summary: This inscription records the name of Aelius Bassus, associated with Legio XIII Gemina, likely on a military object or marker.

EDCS-09400621.translation: The Thirteenth Twin Legion, Aelius Iulius.
EDCS-09400621.summary: This inscription records the name of Aelius Iulius, associated with Legio XIII Gemina, likely on a military object or marker.

EDCS-09400622.translation: The Thirteenth Twin Legion, Aelius Philippus.
EDCS-09400622.summary: This inscription records the name of Aelius Philippus, associated with Legio XIII Gemina, likely on a military object or marker.

EDCS-09400623.translation: The Thirteenth Twin Legion, Aelius Valens.
EDCS-09400623.summary: This inscription records the name of Aelius Valens, associated with Legio XIII Gemina, likely on a military object or marker.

EDCS-09400624.translation: The Thirteenth Twin Legion, of Annaeus Saturninus.
EDCS-09400624.summary: This inscription records the name of Annaeus Saturninus, associated with Legio XIII Gemina, likely on a military object or marker indicating ownership or dedication.

EDCS-09400625.text_edition: Legio XIII gemina Antoni Aeli Ateni
EDCS-09400625.translation: The Thirteenth Twin Legion, Antonius Aelius Atenius.
EDCS-09400625.summary: This inscription records the name of a soldier, Antonius Aelius Atenius, belonging to the Thirteenth Twin Legion, likely part of a building stamp or an ownership mark.

EDCS-09400628.translation: The Thirteenth Twin Legion, Aurelius Callistratus.
EDCS-09400628.summary: This inscription records the name of a soldier, Aurelius Callistratus, associated with the Thirteenth Twin Legion, probably indicating ownership or a contribution.

EDCS-09400630.translation: The Thirteenth Twin Legion, Flavius Heliodorus.
EDCS-09400630.summary: This inscription records the name of a soldier, Flavius Heliodorus, from the Thirteenth Twin Legion, likely a mark of their presence or work.

EDCS-09400631.translation: The Thirteenth Twin Legion, Flavius Vitalis.
EDCS-09400631.summary: This inscription records the name of a soldier, Flavius Vitalis, serving in the Thirteenth Twin Legion, possibly a label of property or responsibility.

EDCS-09400632.translation: The Thirteenth Twin Legion, Iulius Deiotarus.
EDCS-09400632.summary: This inscription records the name of a soldier, Iulius Deiotarus, belonging to the Thirteenth Twin Legion, possibly as an identifier on an object or structure.

EDCS-09400633.translation: The Thirteenth Twin Legion, Iulius Marcianus.
EDCS-09400633.summary: This inscription records the name of a soldier, Iulius Marcianus, from the Thirteenth Twin Legion, likely indicating his association with a military installation or item.

EDCS-09400634.translation: Of Iulius Serenus, the Thirteenth Twin Legion.
EDCS-09400634.summary: This inscription records that the Thirteenth Twin Legion belongs to or is associated with Iulius Serenus, possibly indicating a property mark or a unit under his command.

EDCS-09400635.translation: The Thirteenth Twin Legion, Lucretius Aquila.
EDCS-09400635.summary: This inscription records the name of a soldier, Lucretius Aquila, of the Thirteenth Twin Legion, likely for identification purposes on a building component or military equipment.

EDCS-09400636.translation: Memmius Fronto, the Thirteenth Twin Legion.
EDCS-09400636.summary: This inscription records the name of Memmius Fronto, associated with the Thirteenth Twin Legion, probably as a soldier or an officer marking his presence or contribution.

EDCS-09400638.translation: The Thirteenth Twin Legion, Statius Sentianus.
EDCS-09400638.summary: This inscription records the name of a soldier, Statius Sentianus, from the Thirteenth Twin Legion, likely serving as a signature or ownership mark.

EDCS-09400639.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina, Ulpius Fronto.
EDCS-09400639.summary: This is an abbreviated inscription, likely indicating a dedication or ownership by Ulpius Fronto from the Legio XIII Gemina.

EDCS-09401171.text_edition: Saluti Marcus Aurelius Apollinaris procurator Augusti cum suis
EDCS-09401171.translation: To Salus, Marcus Aurelius Apollinaris, procurator of the Emperor, with his household.
EDCS-09401171.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the goddess Salus by Marcus Aurelius Apollinaris, an imperial procurator, along with his family.

EDCS-09401176.text_edition: Dis Manibus Eustinae Afri vixit XXX Aurelius Iustinus |(centurio bene merenti posuit
EDCS-09401176.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Eustina, daughter of Afer, who lived 30 years. Aurelius Iustinus, centurion, placed this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-09401176.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication by Aurelius Iustinus, a centurion, to his deceased wife, Eustina.

EDCS-09401177.text_edition: Silvano Domesstico Aurelius Viator scriba municipii Septimi Potaissensium
EDCS-09401177.translation: To Silvanus Domesticus, Aurelius Viator, scribe of the municipality of Septimius Potaissa.
EDCS-09401177.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the deity Silvanus Domesticus by Aurelius Viator, a municipal scribe from Potaissa.

EDCS-09401186.translation: [A place granted] to Domitius [---] by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-09401186.summary: This inscription records the official granting of a plot of land to an individual named Domitius by a decree of the municipal council (decurions).

EDCS-09401554.translation: of the municipality of Potaissa. Farewell.
EDCS-09401554.summary: This fragment appears to be part of a larger inscription, possibly a funerary text or public monument, referencing the municipality of Potaissa and concluding with a farewell.

EDCS-09700296.text_edition: Deo Forti Phoebo Apollini Parthico Caius Cassius Vitalis |(centurio) legionis V Macedonicae piae constantis libens posuit
EDCS-09700296.translation: To the Strong God Phoebus Apollo Parthicus, Caius Cassius Vitalis, centurion of the Fifth Legion Macedonica, dutiful and steadfast, willingly placed this.
EDCS-09700296.summary: This inscription records a willing dedication to the syncretic deity Phoebus Apollo Parthicus by Caius Cassius Vitalis, a centurion of Legio V Macedonica.

EDCS-09700298.text_edition: Dis Manibus Spediae Luci filiae Severae coniugi Luci Valeri Montani Quinti fili primi pili legionis XIII geminae Valeria Montanilla filia matri pientissimae et Tycheni libertae posuit
EDCS-09700298.translation: To the spirits of the departed, to Spedia Severa, daughter of Lucius, wife of Lucius Valerius Montanus, son of Quintus, first spear centurion of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina. Valeria Montanilla, daughter, placed this for her most devoted mother and for Tyche, her freedwoman.
EDCS-09700298.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by Valeria Montanilla to her exceptionally devoted mother, Spedia Severa, wife of a primus pilus of Legio XIII Gemina, and also to her freedwoman, Tyche.

EDCS-09700299.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina, Aurelius Menander.
EDCS-09700299.summary: This abbreviated inscription likely signifies a connection to or dedication by Aurelius Menander from the Legio XIII Gemina.

EDCS-09700318.text_edition: I legionis XXII Primigeniae to filio
EDCS-09700318.translation: of the Twenty-Second Legion Primigenia... to his son.
EDCS-09700318.summary: This fragmented inscription, likely a funerary monument, records a dedication by an unpreserved individual from Legio XXII Primigenia to his son.

EDCS-09700703.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Cassius Severinus |(centurio) legionis V Macedonicae
EDCS-09700703.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Cassius Severinus, centurion of the Fifth Macedonian Legion.
EDCS-09700703.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a centurion of the Legio V Macedonica, likely a religious dedication.

EDCS-09700705.text_edition: Iovi? Optimo? Maximo? Aeterno pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris   subsub Primii Senecionis |(centurionis) legionis XIII geminae tabularium a solo restituit
EDCS-09700705.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Aeternus, for the well-being of the Emperor Caesar, under Primius Senecio, centurion of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, restored the archive from its foundation.
EDCS-09700705.summary: This inscription records the restoration of an archive building by a centurion of the Legio XIII Gemina, dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Aeternus for the Emperor's welfare, suggesting a public works project.

EDCS-09700706.text_edition: N  E I Aelius  Iulius Priscus Cassius Senecio Frontius Aquila Aurelius Iulianus Aelius Potens Valerius Vindex Aelius Sabinus Domitius Septimus Aelius Quimiber Iulius Ingenuus Antonius Aquilinus cohortis VII Sabinius Bithus Aelius Crispus Aurelius Terentianus Iulius Iustus   S  Aelius Dines Sabinius Victor Ulpius Secundus Pomp Ianuarius Maximius Cornelianus nnius Amu Iulius Severus Valerius Celsus Aurelius Batavus
EDCS-09700706.translation: N.E.I. Aelius Iulius Priscus, Cassius Senecio, Frontius Aquila, Aurelius Iulianus, Aelius Potens, Valerius Vindex, Aelius Sabinus, Domitius Septimus, Aelius Quimiber, Iulius Ingenuus, Antonius Aquilinus of the Seventh Cohort, Sabinius Bithus, Aelius Crispus, Aurelius Terentianus, Iulius Iustus, S. Aelius Dines, Sabinius Victor, Ulpius Secundus, Pomp. Ianuarius, Maximius Cornelianus, Nnius Amu, Iulius Severus, Valerius Celsus, Aurelius Batavus.
EDCS-09700706.summary: This inscription is a list of names, possibly military personnel given the mention of a cohort, likely recording individuals present for an event or part of a roster.

EDCS-09700709.text_edition: IarI  Lucano   veterano legionis X[III geminae &
EDCS-09700709.translation: To Iarius Lucanus, veteran of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion...
EDCS-09700709.summary: This is a funerary or honorific inscription for Iarius Lucanus, identifying him as a veteran of the Legio XIII Gemina.
