EDCS-09700711.translation: The Thirteenth Gemina Legion. Aurelius Demetrius.
EDCS-09700711.summary: This inscription records Aurelius Demetrius, likely a soldier or associated individual, with his affiliation to the Legio XIII Gemina, possibly a simple roster entry or property mark.

EDCS-09700712.translation: The Thirteenth Gemina Legion. Aurelius Godes.
EDCS-09700712.summary: This inscription records Aurelius Godes, likely a soldier or associated individual, with his affiliation to the Legio XIII Gemina, possibly a simple roster entry or property mark.

EDCS-09700713.summary: This inscription records Flavius Martinus, likely a soldier or associated individual, with his affiliation to the Legio XIII, possibly a simple roster entry or property mark.

EDCS-09700714.translation: The Thirteenth Gemina Legion. Aurelius Menander.
EDCS-09700714.summary: This inscription records Aurelius Menander, likely a soldier or associated individual, with his affiliation to the Legio XIII Gemina, possibly a simple roster entry or property mark.

EDCS-09700715.translation: The Thirteenth Gemina Legion. Lucretius Aquila.
EDCS-09700715.summary: This inscription records Lucretius Aquila, likely a soldier or associated individual, with his affiliation to the Legio XIII Gemina, possibly a simple roster entry or property mark.

EDCS-09700716.translation: The Thirteenth Gemina Legion, (of) Annaeus Saturninus.
EDCS-09700716.summary: This inscription records the Legio XIII Gemina in connection with Annaeus Saturninus, possibly indicating ownership, dedication, or a property mark of the legion associated with this individual.

EDCS-09700717.translation: Aurelius Cono, of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina.
EDCS-09700717.summary: This is a simple textual record of an individual, Aurelius Cono, and his military affiliation with the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, likely from a tombstone or military diploma, probably dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-09700718.summary: This inscription is a very brief record of an individual's name, Publius Aelius Terentius, likely part of a larger monument or dedication, possibly from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-09700722.text_edition: Silvano Domestico sacrum Valerius Them veteranus decurio municipii votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-09700722.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Domesticus. Valerius Them, a veteran, a decurion of the municipality, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09700722.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the deity Silvanus Domesticus by Valerius Them, a veteran and municipal decurion, indicating a fulfillment of a vow, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-09700723.text_edition: Dis Manibus us veteranus ex  vixit annos LXXXV militavit Passer signifer vixit annos XXXIII Aelia  vixit annos XVII Aurelius  miles numeri Palmyrenorum Porolissensium vixit Aurelia Sabina et Aureliusus actarius
EDCS-09700723.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. [---] veteran, lived 85 years, served [---]. Passer, standard-bearer, lived 33 years. Aelia [---], lived 17 years. Aurelius [---], soldier of the numeri Palmyrenorum Porolissensium, lived [---]. Aurelia Sabina and Aurelius [---], accountant.
EDCS-09700723.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating several individuals, including a veteran, a standard-bearer, a woman, a soldier from a Palmyrene unit stationed in Porolissum, and an accountant, providing their names, ages, and military/civilian roles, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-09700725.text_edition: Bebeia Tyche VIS vixit annos  Aelius Maenemachus |(centurio) coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-09700725.translation: Bebeia Tyche, lived [---] years. Aelius Maenemachus, centurion, erected this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-09700725.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by the centurion Aelius Maenemachus for his wife, Bebeia Tyche, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-09700726.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno pro salute domini nostri Marci Antoni Gordiani Pii Felicis Augusti et Sabiniae Trantquillinae Augustae Solinus Tertius? se ac suis v(otum) s(olvit) l(ibens) m(erito)
EDCS-09700726.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, for the health of our lord Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus and Sabinia Tranquillina Augusta, Solinus Tertius willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for himself and his household.
EDCS-09700726.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus by Solinus Tertius, offered for the health of Emperor Gordian III and Empress Tranquillina, likely dating to 241-244 AD.

EDCS-09700727.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute domini nostri [[]] [] Augusti
EDCS-09700727.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the health of our lord [---] Augustus.
EDCS-09700727.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, offered for the health of an unnamed Roman emperor, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-09700728.text_edition: GI Aurel Ver sororis dolentis SA
EDCS-09700728.translation: [---] Aurel[ius] Ver[us], from his grieving sister [---].
EDCS-09700728.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, likely a tombstone, dedicated by a grieving sister for Aurelius Verus, probably dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-09800285.text_edition: Μηνὶ Ἀνειικήτῳ Aurelius Marcianus signifer legionis voto posuit
EDCS-09800285.translation: To Men Aniketos. Aurelius Marcianus, standard-bearer of the legion, erected this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-09800285.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Men Aniketos by Aurelius Marcianus, a legionary standard-bearer, commemorating the fulfillment of a vow, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-09800287.text_edition: Aurelius Victor librarius  legionis V Macedonicae  votum solvit
EDCS-09800287.translation: Aurelius Victor, librarius of the Fifth Legion Macedonica, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09800287.summary: This is a votive inscription by Aurelius Victor, a librarius (clerk) of the Fifth Legion Macedonica, recording the fulfillment of a vow, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-09800288.text_edition: D(is) M(anibus) Aelia Ianuaria vixit annos
EDCS-09800288.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aelia Ianuaria lived for [an unspecified number of] years.
EDCS-09800288.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name and age (partially missing) of the deceased, Aelia Ianuaria.

EDCS-10200172.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publio Aelio Malcho veterano vixit nnos XXXXII vivo se fecit sibi et coniugi carissimae et filio pientissimo
EDCS-10200172.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Publius Aelius Malchus, a veteran, who lived 42 years. He made this for himself while alive, and for his dearest wife and his most devoted son.
EDCS-10200172.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Publius Aelius Malchus, a veteran, during his lifetime for himself, his wife, and his son.

EDCS-10200183.translation: The Thirteenth Twin Legion. Belonging to Flavus Martinus.
EDCS-10200183.summary: This inscription records the name of Flavus Martinus and his association with the Legio XIII Gemina, likely indicating ownership or dedication by him.

EDCS-10200186.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Antonio Capitoni veterano legionis IIII Flaviae felicis Antonius Paternus filius et Antonia Primitiva liberta posuerunt
EDCS-10200186.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Gaius Antonius Capito, a veteran of the Fourth Flavian Felix Legion. Antonius Paternus, his son, and Antonia Primitiva, his freedwoman, set this up.
EDCS-10200186.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by Antonius Paternus (son) and Antonia Primitiva (freedwoman) for Gaius Antonius Capito, a veteran of Legio IIII Flavia Felix.

EDCS-10701843.text_edition: Libero Patri deo Publius Aelius Marcianus decurio coloniae Napocensium pro sua et suorum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10701843.translation: To Father Liber, a god. Publius Aelius Marcianus, decurion of the colony of Napoca, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for himself and his family.
EDCS-10701843.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the god Liber Pater, made by Publius Aelius Marcianus, a decurion of Napoca, in fulfillment of a vow for himself and his family.

EDCS-10701850.summary: This fragmented inscription preserves a partial name, possibly Valerius, followed by illegible characters.

EDCS-10701865.summary: This short inscription identifies the workshop or officina belonging to Primus, likely indicating the maker of an object.

EDCS-10701869.summary: This fragmented inscription mentions Emperor Marcus, likely the beginning of a longer imperial title or dedication.

EDCS-11200045.text_edition: Ex permissu splendidissimi ordinis coloniae Sarmizegetusae metropolis er Augustalis coloniae eiusdem aedem collegarum vetustate dilapsam sumptibus suis restituit
EDCS-11200045.translation: By permission of the most splendid order of the colony of Sarmizegetusa, the metropolis and Augustalis colony of the same, he restored at his own expense the temple of the collegia, which had collapsed due to old age.
EDCS-11200045.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a temple belonging to collegia, which had fallen into ruin, by an unnamed individual, with permission from the colonial authorities of Sarmizegetusa.

EDCS-11200046.text_edition: Pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Commodi Pii Augusti Marcus Pomponius Severus decurio coloniae quaestor patronus collegii fabrum et Marcus Urbius Valerianus patronus collegii eiusdem aedem pecunia sua fecerunt
EDCS-11200046.translation: For the well-being of Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Commodus Pius Augustus, Marcus Pomponius Severus, decurion of the colony, quaestor, patron of the guild of craftsmen, and Marcus Urbius Valerianus, patron of the same guild, built this temple with their own money.
EDCS-11200046.summary: This inscription records the construction of a temple by Marcus Pomponius Severus and Marcus Urbius Valerianus, both patrons of the guild of craftsmen, for the well-being of Emperor Commodus.

EDCS-11200048.text_edition: Titus Ancharius Octavius decurio coloniae Sarmizegetusae metropolis patronus decuriae XV porticum per pedes XXXXV ob honorem patronatus ex suo fecit
EDCS-11200048.translation: Titus Ancharius Octavius, decurion of the colony of Sarmizegetusa, metropolis, patron of the 15th decuria, built a portico forty-five feet long at his own expense in honor of his patronage.
EDCS-11200048.summary: This inscription records Titus Ancharius Octavius, a decurion and patron, funding the construction of a portico in Sarmizegetusa during the High Roman Empire.

EDCS-11200049.text_edition: Libero Patri Augusto sacrum Lucius Apuleius Marcus decurio coloniae quaestor porticus cum cubiculis a vii sic hostium exustos pecunia sua ob scribatum restituit permittente ordine
EDCS-11200049.translation: Sacred to Liber Pater Augustus. Lucius Apuleius Marcus, decurion of the colony, quaestor, restored at his own expense the portico with rooms, burnt by enemies, from 'a VII' (a specific point or marker), for the sake of his office, with the order's permission.
EDCS-11200049.summary: This inscription details Lucius Apuleius Marcus, a decurion and quaestor, financing the restoration of a portico and rooms in honor of Liber Pater Augustus, which had been destroyed by enemies.

EDCS-11200051.text_edition: Caelesti Virgini Augustae sacrum Nemesianus Caesaris nostri servos sic librarius templum a solo pRecunia#pecunia#RECUNIA sua ex voto fecit
EDCS-11200051.translation: Sacred to the Celestial Virgin Augusta. Nemesianus, slave and scribe of our Caesar, built this temple from the ground up at his own expense, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-11200051.summary: This inscription describes Nemesianus, a slave and scribe, constructing a temple to the Celestial Virgin Augusta from his own funds, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-11200053.text_edition: In honorem domus divinae Lucius Ophonius Papiria Domitius Priscus IIvir coloniae Dacicae pecunia sua fecit locus datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-11200053.translation: In honor of the divine house, Lucius Ophonius Papiria Domitius Priscus, duumvir of the Dacian colony, made this at his own expense; the site was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-11200053.summary: This inscription records Lucius Ophonius Papiria Domitius Priscus, a duumvir, funding the construction of a monument for the imperial house, with the land provided by the decurions.

EDCS-11200087.text_edition: Publius Aelius Caius Publicia Eu Publius Aelius Iulianus Publius Aelius Statorius Aelia Mo Publius Aelius Caianus Claudia Et
EDCS-11200087.translation: Publius Aelius Caius Publicia Eu, Publius Aelius Iulianus, Publius Aelius Statorius Aelia Mo, Publius Aelius Caianus Claudia Et.
EDCS-11200087.summary: This inscription is a fragmentary list of names, likely recording members of a family or a group dedicated to some purpose.

EDCS-11200093.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Iulio Philippo Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate V consuli III patri patriae proconsuli concilium III provinciarum Daciarum devotum numini maiestatique eius
EDCS-11200093.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus Pius Felix Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the 5th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the country, proconsul: the Third Council of the Dacian provinces, devoted to his divinity and majesty.
EDCS-11200093.summary: This inscription is a dedication from the Third Council of the Dacian provinces to Emperor Philip the Arab, honoring his titles and imperial majesty, likely dating to 247 AD.

EDCS-11200094.text_edition: Publio Aelio Hammonio viro egregio I
EDCS-11200094.translation: To Publius Aelius Hammonius, eminent man, I.
EDCS-11200094.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription dedicated to Publius Aelius Hammonius, identified as an 'egregius vir', indicating a member of the equestrian order.

EDCS-11200096.text_edition: Caio Arrio Antonino filio Cai Arri Antonini legato Augusti pro praetore colonia Ulpia Traiana Augusta Dacica Sarmizegetusa
EDCS-11200096.translation: To Gaius Arrius Antoninus, son of Gaius Arrius Antoninus, legate of Augustus with propraetorian power, the colony Ulpia Traiana Augusta Dacica Sarmizegetusa [dedicated this].
EDCS-11200096.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the colony of Ulpia Traiana Augusta Dacica Sarmizegetusa to Gaius Arrius Antoninus, the son of a provincial governor.

EDCS-11200097.text_edition: Caio Arrio Quadrato filio Cai Arri Antonini legato Augusti pro praetore colonia Ulpia Traiana Augusta Dacica Sarmizegetusa
EDCS-11200097.translation: To Gaius Arrius Quadratus, son of Gaius Arrius Antoninus, legate of Augustus with propraetorian power, the colony Ulpia Traiana Augusta Dacica Sarmizegetusa [dedicated this].
EDCS-11200097.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the colony of Ulpia Traiana Augusta Dacica Sarmizegetusa to Gaius Arrius Quadratus, also a son of a provincial governor.

EDCS-11200100.text_edition: Marco Sedatio Cai filio Quirina Severiano Iulio Acri Metilio Nepoti Rufino Tiberio Rutiliano Censori tribuno plebis quaestori provinciae Siciliae praetori legato Augusti legionis V Macedonicae curatori viae Flaminiae legato Augusti pro praetore provinciae Daciae consuli colonia Ulpia Traiana Augusta Dacica Sarmizegetusa patrono
EDCS-11200100.translation: To Marcus Sedatius, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, Severianus Iulius Acrus Metilius Nepos Rufinus Tiberius Rutilianus Censor, tribune of the plebs, quaestor of the province of Sicily, praetor, legate of Augustus of the V Macedonica Legion, curator of the Via Flaminia, legate of Augustus with propraetorian power of the province of Dacia, consul, the colony Ulpia Traiana Augusta Dacica Sarmizegetusa, its patron.
EDCS-11200100.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the colony of Ulpia Traiana Augusta Dacica Sarmizegetusa to its patron, Marcus Sedatius, listing his extensive senatorial career and titles, including consul and legate of Dacia.

EDCS-11200101.text_edition: Marco Sedatio Cai filio Quirina Severiano tribuno plebis quaestori provinciae Siciliae praetori legato Augusti legionis V Macedonicae curatori viae Flaminiae legato Augusti pro praetore provinciae Daciae consuli colonia Ulpia Traiana Augusta Dacica Sarmizegetusa patrono
EDCS-11200101.translation: To Marcus Sedatius Severianus, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, tribune of the plebs, quaestor of the province of Sicily, praetor, legate of Augustus for the Fifth Macedonian Legion, curator of the Flaminian Way, legate of Augustus pro praetore for the province of Dacia, consul. The colony of Ulpia Traiana Augusta Dacica Sarmizegetusa (dedicated this) to its patron.
EDCS-11200101.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the colony of Ulpia Traiana Augusta Dacica Sarmizegetusa to Marcus Sedatius Severianus, commemorating his distinguished senatorial career and his role as their patron, likely dating to the mid-2nd century AD.

EDCS-11200103.text_edition: Aeliae Publi filiae Lucillae Aelii Theodorus et Firminus alumni loco dato decreto decurionum
EDCS-11200103.translation: To Aelia Lucilla, daughter of Publius. Aelii Theodorus and Firminus, her foster-children, (erected this). The plot was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-11200103.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Aelia Lucilla's foster-children, Aelii Theodorus and Firminus, with the burial plot formally granted by the municipal council.

EDCS-11200105.text_edition: Firmo veterano legionis IIII Flaviae felicis decurioni coloniae Daciae Ulpiae Traianae Augustae Sarmizegetusae
EDCS-11200105.translation: To Firmus, veteran of the Fourth Flavian Felix Legion, decurion of the colony of Dacia Ulpia Traiana Augusta Sarmizegetusa.
EDCS-11200105.summary: This inscription honors Firmus, a veteran who achieved the status of a decurion in the colony of Dacia Ulpia Traiana Augusta Sarmizegetusa, likely a posthumous or honorary dedication.

EDCS-11200106.text_edition: Caio Iulio Cai filio Papiria Valerio veterano legionis XIII geminae Severianae ex beneficiario consularis decurioni et IIvirali coloniae Sarmizegetusae metropolis CCai Iulii Valerianus beneficiarius consularis Carus frumentarius et decurio coloniae supra scriptae Fronto miles cohortis I praetoriae scriniarius praeffectorum praetorio et decurio coloniae eiusdem Valeria et Carissima filii memoriae patris locus datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-11200106.translation: To Gaius Julius Valerius, son of Gaius, of the Papiria tribe, veteran of the Thirteenth Gemina Severiana Legion, former beneficiarius consularis, decurion and duumviral of the colony of Sarmizegetusa, metropolis. Gaius Julius Valerianus, beneficiarius consularis, Carus, frumentarius and decurion of the above-written colony, Fronto, soldier of the First Praetorian Cohort, scriniarius of the praetorian prefects, and decurion of the same colony, Valeria and Carissima, his daughters, (erected this) in memory of their father. The plot was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-11200106.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Gaius Julius Valerius, a veteran, decurion, and former duumviral of Sarmizegetusa, erected by his daughters and other colleagues, with the burial plot granted by the decurions, likely dating to the early-to-mid 3rd century AD.

EDCS-11200109.text_edition: Marco Opellio Marci filio Papiria Adiutori decurioni coloniae IIviro iuris dicundi praefecto collegii fabrum plebs aere conlato locus datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-11200109.translation: To Marcus Opellius Adiutor, son of Marcus, of the Papiria tribe, decurion of the colony, duumvir with judicial power, prefect of the college of craftsmen. The plebs, by collected money, (erected this). The plot was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-11200109.summary: This inscription is a public dedication by the plebs, who collected money, to Marcus Opellius Adiutor, honoring his civic offices as decurion, duumvir, and prefect of the college of craftsmen, with the burial plot officially sanctioned by the decurions.

EDCS-11200111.text_edition: Marco Procilio Iuliano decurioni coloniae equo publico
EDCS-11200111.translation: To Marcus Procilius Julianus, decurion of the colony, with a public horse.
EDCS-11200111.summary: This inscription honors Marcus Procilius Julianus as a decurion of the colony, notable for being granted a public horse, signifying a high civic honor or status.

EDCS-11200113.text_edition: Ulpio Domitio Ulpi filio Papiria Rufino decurioni coloniae Sarmizegetusae Ulpius Domitius Hermes libertus locus datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-11200113.translation: To Ulpius Domitius Rufinus, son of Ulpius, of the Papiria tribe, decurion of the colony of Sarmizegetusa. Ulpius Domitius Hermes, his freedman, (erected this). The plot was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-11200113.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Ulpius Domitius Rufinus, a decurion of Sarmizegetusa, by his freedman Ulpius Domitius Hermes, with the burial plot provided by decree of the decurions.

EDCS-11200115.text_edition: Caio Valerio Cai filio Papiria Suro decurioni coloniae Sarmizegetusae metropolis aedilicio praefecto collegii fabrum et IIvirali coloniae eiusdem Titus Aurelius Emeritus |(centurio) legionis VI victricis Severianae socero locus datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-11200115.translation: To Gaius Valerius Surus, son of Gaius, of the Papiria tribe, decurion of the colony of Sarmizegetusa, metropolis, former aedile, prefect of the college of craftsmen, and duumviral of the same colony. Titus Aurelius Emeritus, centurion of the Sixth Victrix Severiana Legion, (erected this) for his father-in-law. The plot was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-11200115.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Titus Aurelius Emeritus, a centurion, to his father-in-law, Gaius Valerius Surus, an influential citizen who held several municipal offices in Sarmizegetusa, with the burial plot approved by the decurions, likely dating to the early-to-mid 3rd century AD.

EDCS-11200119.text_edition: Ordini Augustalium Publius Antonius Super decurio coloniae Sarmizegetusae metropolis dono dedit
EDCS-11200119.translation: Publius Antonius Super, decurion of the colony of Sarmizegetusa, metropolis, gave (this) as a gift to the Order of Augustales.
EDCS-11200119.summary: This inscription records a donation made by Publius Antonius Super, a decurion of Sarmizegetusa, to the local Order of Augustales, indicating a gesture of civic generosity or patronage.

EDCS-11200129.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning 'the elect' and a partial name, possibly a personal or official dedication with a Sallustianus, but lacking sufficient context for a complete summary.

EDCS-11200138.text_edition: Aesculapio et Hygiae pro salute Aeliae Florae et Mettiorum Proteni Cassiani Florae filiae Caius Mettius Cassianus IIvir coloniae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-11200138.translation: To Aesculapius and Hygia, for the well-being of Aelia Flora and the Mettii Protenus, Cassianus, Flora the daughter, Gaius Mettius Cassianus, duumvir of the colony, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11200138.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Gaius Mettius Cassianus, a duumvir, to Aesculapius and Hygia for the health of his family members.

EDCS-11200140.text_edition: Numini Aesculapio et Hygiae Ulpius Avillius Florentinus ex voto
EDCS-11200140.translation: To the Divinity Aesculapius and Hygia, Ulpius Avillius Florentinus, in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-11200140.summary: This inscription is a votive offering made by Ulpius Avillius Florentinus to Aesculapius and Hygia.

EDCS-11200141.text_edition: Numini Augusto Aesculapi sacrum Lucius Bononius Saturninus ex viso libens
EDCS-11200141.translation: Sacred to the August Divinity Aesculapius, Lucius Bononius Saturninus, willingly, as a result of a vision.
EDCS-11200141.summary: This inscription is a sacred dedication to Aesculapius, made by Lucius Bononius Saturninus after experiencing a vision.

EDCS-11200142.translation: For the well-being of A. Claudius Gaianus.
EDCS-11200142.summary: This fragment of an inscription indicates a dedication made for the health of A. Claudius Gaianus.

EDCS-11200143.text_edition: Euprepes Luci Valeri Eutychi Asclepio et Hygiae dono dedit
EDCS-11200143.translation: Euprepes, freedman of Lucius Valerius Eutychus, gave this as a gift to Asclepius and Hygia.
EDCS-11200143.summary: This inscription records a gift made by Euprepes, a freedman, to Asclepius and Hygia.

EDCS-11200144.text_edition: Aesculapio et Hygiae Publius Flaccinius Felix  ex voto
EDCS-11200144.translation: To Aesculapius and Hygia, Publius Flaccinius Felix, in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-11200144.summary: This inscription is a votive offering made by Publius Flaccinius Felix to Aesculapius and Hygia.

EDCS-11200145.text_edition: Aesculapio et Hygiae pro salute Lucillae filiae Publius Flaccinius Felix decurio collegii fabrum ex voto
EDCS-11200145.translation: To Aesculapius and Hygia, for the well-being of his daughter Lucilla, Publius Flaccinius Felix, decurion of the college of craftsmen, in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-11200145.summary: This inscription records a votive dedication made by Publius Flaccinius Felix, a decurion, to Aesculapius and Hygia for the health of his daughter Lucilla.

EDCS-11200146.text_edition: Aesculapio et Hygiae Augustis Caius Titius Agathopus Augustalis coloniae Sisciae et Sarmizegetusae ex voto
EDCS-11200146.translation: To the August Aesculapius and Hygia, Gaius Titius Agathopus, Augustalis of the colonies of Siscia and Sarmizegetusa, in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-11200146.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Gaius Titius Agathopus, an Augustalis, to Aesculapius and Hygia.

EDCS-11200159.text_edition: ISI mis fonte V nis est CE ens Marcus Ulpius
EDCS-11200159.translation: Isis from the source... Marcus Ulpius...
EDCS-11200159.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, possibly a dedication involving Isis and a water source, made by Marcus Ulpius.

EDCS-11200160.text_edition: Deo Aeterno et Iunoni et Angelis Marcus Procilius Aphrodisius Augustalis coloniae metropolis et Seximia Hermione et Procilia filia colitoribus dono dederunt posuerunt
EDCS-11200160.translation: To the Eternal God and Juno and the Angels, Marcus Procilius Aphrodisius, Augustalis of the colony metropolis, and Seximia Hermione and their daughter Procilia, gave and placed this as a gift, as worshippers.
EDCS-11200160.summary: This inscription records a dedication and gift made by Marcus Procilius Aphrodisius, an Augustalis, along with Seximia Hermione and their daughter Procilia, to the Eternal God, Juno, and the Angels.

EDCS-11200161.text_edition: Deae Caelesti sacrum Liberalis Augusti nostri verna adiutor tabularii votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-11200161.translation: Sacred to the Goddess Caelestis. Liberalis, a home-born slave of our Augustus, an assistant in the accounts office, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11200161.summary: This inscription is a dedication by a low-ranking imperial slave to the goddess Caelestis, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-11200162.text_edition: Cautopati sacrum Synethus adiutor tabularii votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-11200162.translation: Sacred to Cautopates. Synethus, an assistant in the accounts office, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11200162.summary: This inscription is a dedication by an accounts office assistant to Cautopates, a Mithraic deity, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-11200167.text_edition: Dito Patri et Proserpinae sacrum  Caius Valerius Zeno decurio et IIvir coloniae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-11200167.translation: Sacred to Father Dis and Proserpina. Gaius Valerius Zeno, decurion and duumvir of the colony, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11200167.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the underworld deities Dis Pater and Proserpina by a local magistrate, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-11200170.text_edition: Fortunae Daciarum Caius Sempronius Urbanus procurator Augusti
EDCS-11200170.translation: To the Fortune of the Dacias. Gaius Sempronius Urbanus, imperial procurator.
EDCS-11200170.summary: This inscription is a dedication by an imperial procurator to the personified Fortune of the Dacias province.

EDCS-11200172.text_edition: Priscus ex iusso posuit libens merito
EDCS-11200172.translation: Priscus, by order, willingly and deservedly set this up.
EDCS-11200172.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Priscus, stating he set it up by divine or human command.

EDCS-11200173.text_edition: Daciae Felicis et Genio domus divinae Diogenes Eutychetis Augg[g](ustorum) nn[n](ostrorum) dispensatoris vikarius sic votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-11200173.translation: To Blessed Dacia and the Genius of the Divine House. Diogenes, a deputy of Eutyches, dispensator of our Augusti, thus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11200173.summary: This inscription is a dedication by an imperial slave, a deputy dispensator, to personified Blessed Dacia and the Genius of the imperial household, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-11200175.text_edition: Deae Haechatae sic pro salutae sic Aeliae Saturninae coniugis suae Haerennius sic Gemellinus tribunus statum coniugis redemit ex visu et votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-11200175.translation: To the Goddess Hecate. For the health of his wife Aelia Saturnina, Herennius Gemellinus, tribune, thus redeemed the status of his wife by a vision, and willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11200175.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the goddess Hecate by a tribune, Herennius Gemellinus, who, following a vision, 'redeemed' his wife's status and fulfilled a vow for her health.

EDCS-11200177.text_edition: Dis immortalibus et numini sanctissimorum Augustorum Lucius Octavius Felix procurator promotus ad ducenariam provinciae Delmatiae sic
EDCS-11200177.translation: To the immortal gods and the divine power of the most holy Augusti. Lucius Octavius Felix, procurator promoted to a ducenarian (salary-grade) of the province of Dalmatia.
EDCS-11200177.summary: This inscription is a dedication by an imperial procurator, Lucius Octavius Felix, to the immortal gods and the emperors, likely celebrating his promotion.

EDCS-11200179.text_edition: Iunoni Regini sic Marcia Cara  votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-11200179.translation: To Juno Regina. Marcia Cara willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-11200179.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Marcia Cara to Juno Regina, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-11200180.text_edition: Iunoni Reginae Caius Sempronius Urbanus procurator Augusti
EDCS-11200180.translation: To Juno Regina. Gaius Sempronius Urbanus, imperial procurator.
EDCS-11200180.summary: This inscription is a dedication by an imperial procurator, Gaius Sempronius Urbanus, to Juno Regina.

EDCS-11200182.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Terentius Primus pro salute sua et suorum
EDCS-11200182.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Terentius Primus [set this up] for his own well-being and that of his family.
EDCS-11200182.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Terentius Primus for the safety of himself and his household, likely a private dedication.

EDCS-11200184.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Ulpius Maximianus beneficiarius consularis votum libens posuit
EDCS-11200184.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Ulpius Maximianus, a beneficiarius consularis, willingly and deservedly placed this vow.
EDCS-11200184.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, dedicated by Ulpius Maximianus, a beneficiarius consularis (an aide to a provincial governor), indicating a fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-11200186.text_edition: Dedicatum epulo Iovis X Kalendas Iunias Aviola et Severo consulibus
EDCS-11200186.translation: Dedicated with a feast of Jupiter on the tenth day before the Kalends of June, when Aviola and Severus were consuls.
EDCS-11200186.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a feast of Jupiter on May 23rd, and provides a precise date by naming the consuls Aviola and Severus, likely in the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-11200188.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marti Augusto pro salute Iuli Flaccini legati Augusti pro praetore translati in legionem XIII geminam posuerunt
EDCS-11200188.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, to Mars Augustus, for the well-being of Iulius Flaccinus, imperial legate with propraetorian power, transferred to the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, they placed this.
EDCS-11200188.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Mars Augustus, made for the safety of Iulius Flaccinus, an imperial legate who was transferred to the Legio XIII Gemina, probably by his subordinates or beneficiaries.

EDCS-11200189.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Minervae Diis Consentibus Saluti Fortunae Reduci Apollini Dianae Victrici Nemesi Mercurio Herculi Soli Invicto Aesculapio Hygiae diis deabusque immortalibus Publius Aelius Hammonius vir egregius procurator Auggustorum
EDCS-11200189.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Juno, Minerva, the Di Consentes, Salus, Fortuna Redux, Apollo, Diana Victrix, Nemesis, Mercury, Hercules, Sol Invictus, Aesculapius, Hygia, and to all immortal gods and goddesses, Publius Aelius Hammonius, an egregius vir, procurator of the Augusti, [dedicated this].
EDCS-11200189.summary: This is a comprehensive dedicatory inscription to a wide pantheon of Roman and other deities, set up by Publius Aelius Hammonius, an 'egregius vir' and procurator of the emperors, possibly a personal expression of piety or an official act.

EDCS-11200191.text_edition: Deo numini Libero Patri ex voto Claudius Anicetus Augustalis coloniae
EDCS-11200191.translation: To the divine spirit, Liber Pater, according to his vow, Claudius Anicetus, Augustalis of the colony [dedicated this].
EDCS-11200191.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Liber Pater, made by Claudius Anicetus, an Augustalis (a priest of the imperial cult) of a colony, indicating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-11200195.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription containing the name Gaius Spedius, likely part of a larger dedication or record with missing details.

EDCS-11200196.translation: [---] for the well-being of the Octavii.
EDCS-11200196.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription dedicated for the health or well-being of a family named Octavius, with the beginning of the text missing.

EDCS-11200201.text_edition: Deo Malalag(beli) Epipodius Augusti nostri verna vilicus votum solvit
EDCS-11200201.translation: To the god Malagabel, Epipodius, a slave born in our emperor's household, the vilicus, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11200201.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Malagabel, made by Epipodius, an imperial 'vilicus' (estate manager) and 'verna' (born a slave in the imperial household), in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-11200202.text_edition: Meni Cilvastiano Gaius Petronius Iustus cornicularius procuratoris ex voto
EDCS-11200202.translation: To Men Cilvastianus, Gaius Petronius Iustus, a cornicularius of the procurator, according to his vow [dedicated this].
EDCS-11200202.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the deity Men Cilvastianus by Gaius Petronius Iustus, a 'cornicularius' (a junior officer or clerk) to a procurator, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-11200203.text_edition: Minervae Augustae Marcus Aurelius Valens magister kampi actarius l pa
EDCS-11200203.translation: To Minerva Augusta, Marcus Aurelius Valens, master of the field, records clerk, (made this offering).
EDCS-11200203.summary: This is a votive inscription to Minerva Augusta, made by Marcus Aurelius Valens, who held the titles of 'master of the field' and 'records clerk'.

EDCS-11200205.translation: Primus placed this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-11200205.summary: This is a votive inscription recording that Primus fulfilled a vow by placing this monument.

EDCS-11200217.text_edition: Invicto deo Serapidi Caesidius Respectus procurator Augusti nostri et Apronilla eius
EDCS-11200217.translation: To the invincible god Serapis, Caesidius Respectus, procurator of our Augustus, and Apronilla, his (wife, made this offering).
EDCS-11200217.summary: This is a dedication to the god Serapis, made by Caesidius Respectus, an imperial procurator, and his wife Apronilla.

EDCS-11200218.text_edition: Marcus Aurelius Valens decurio coloniae ex voto posuit
EDCS-11200218.translation: Marcus Aurelius Valens, decurion of the colony, placed this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-11200218.summary: This is a votive inscription made by Marcus Aurelius Valens, a decurion of the colony, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-11200221.text_edition: $ Aur]elius Dexter donum de suo posuit loco dato decreto decurionum
EDCS-11200221.translation: Aurelius Dexter placed this gift from his own funds, on a plot granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-11200221.summary: This inscription records Aurelius Dexter's donation, made at his own expense and on land officially granted by the city council's decree.

EDCS-11200222.text_edition: Euhemerus pro salute Antonini fili sui
EDCS-11200222.translation: Euhemerus, for the health of his son Antoninus.
EDCS-11200222.summary: This is an inscription dedicated by Euhemerus for the well-being of his son, Antoninus.

EDCS-11200227.summary: This fragmented inscription likely records a dedication or monument set up by Julius Rufinus and another individual whose name begins with F.

EDCS-11200228.text_edition: - Sarmizegetusae Caius Aurelius Primus CIV  PM-
EDCS-11200228.summary: This fragmentary inscription mentions Gaius Aurelius Primus at Sarmizegetusa, likely indicating a monument or record related to him in that location.

EDCS-11200230.translation: Venconia Severina, as a result of a vision.
EDCS-11200230.summary: This is a votive inscription made by Venconia Severina, prompted by a divine vision.

EDCS-11200234.translation: Flavia Revocata placed this in fulfillment of a vow to the most present and serene goddess.
EDCS-11200234.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Flavia Revocata to a very present and peaceful goddess, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-11200235.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelio Macrino GETTI decurioni coloniae Aelius Macrinius Epidianus qui et Epidius
EDCS-11200235.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Aelius Macrinus, decurion of the colony, from GETTI. Aelius Macrinius Epidianus, also known as Epidius, made this.
EDCS-11200235.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aelius Macrinius Epidianus to Aelius Macrinus, a decurion of the colony, likely a relative or associate, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11200239.text_edition: Dis Manibus his tegitur terris Antonia quam generavit Pergamos excelso monte superposita pascuis Nisonis titulum Antonius
EDCS-11200239.translation: To the Divine Manes. Antonia, whom Pergamos bore, lies buried in these lands, positioned on a high mountain above the pastures of Nisonis. Antonius made this title.
EDCS-11200239.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Antonius for Antonia, who was born to Pergamos, likely a tombstone marking her burial place, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11200240.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Aquilio Domitiano vixit nnos
EDCS-11200240.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Gaius Aquilius Domitianus. He lived for [---] years.
EDCS-11200240.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Aquilius Domitianus, likely a tombstone, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11200241.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelia Hygia Iunior vixit annos VII menses VI Aelia Venusta neptiae fecit
EDCS-11200241.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelia Hygia Iunior lived for 7 years and 6 months. Aelia Venusta made this for her granddaughter.
EDCS-11200241.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aelia Venusta to her granddaughter, Aurelia Hygia Iunior, who died young, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11200242.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caecilia Cai filia Valentina vixit annos VXEMAIVS
EDCS-11200242.translation: To the Divine Manes. Caecilia Valentina, daughter of Gaius, lived for [---] years.
EDCS-11200242.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caecilia Valentina, daughter of Gaius, likely a tombstone, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11200243.text_edition: Dis Manibus Claudiae Turpillae vixit annos item Caro Caesaris nostri vernae vixit annnos  Herculanus Aug(usti) lib(ertus) adiutor tabularii uxori et filio bene merentibus fecit
EDCS-11200243.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Claudia Turpilla, who lived for [---] years. Also, to Carus, our Caesar's home-born slave, who lived for [---] years. Herculanus, freedman of Augustus, assistant in the record office, made this for his deserving wife and son.
EDCS-11200243.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Herculanus, an Augustan freedman and assistant tabularius, to his wife Claudia Turpilla and his son Carus, a home-born slave of the emperor, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11200244.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marci Domiti Primi vixit annos LX collegium fabrum
EDCS-11200244.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Marcus Domitius Primus, who lived for 60 years. The college of artisans made this.
EDCS-11200244.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Domitius Primus, erected by the collegium fabrum (college of artisans), likely commemorating one of their members, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11200246.text_edition: Dis Manibus Flavio Fortunato Augustali coloniae ex decuria VII collegii fabrum
EDCS-11200246.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Flavius Fortunatus, Augustalis of the colony, from the seventh decuria of the college of artisans.
EDCS-11200246.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Flavius Fortunatus, an Augustalis of the colony and a member of the college of artisans, likely placed by his colleagues or family, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11200249.text_edition: Dis Manibus Quintus Iulius Secundinus veteranus legionis XIII geminae vixit annos LX Quintus Iulius Secundus filius patri pientissimo
EDCS-11200249.translation: To the Divine Manes. Quintus Iulius Secundinus, a veteran of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, lived for 60 years. Quintus Iulius Secundus, his son, made this for his most devoted father.
EDCS-11200249.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Quintus Iulius Secundus to his father, Quintus Iulius Secundinus, a veteran of the Legio XIII Gemina, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11200251.text_edition: Dis Manibus Macrino civi Trevero Attius Valens et Carantius Germanus faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-11200251.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Macrinus, a Treveran citizen. Attius Valens and Carantius Germanus had this made.
EDCS-11200251.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Macrinus, a citizen from the Treveri tribe, commissioned by Attius Valens and Carantius Germanus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11200252.text_edition: Dis Manibus Saturninae Iacchius coniugi et Timotheus bene merenti fecerunt
EDCS-11200252.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Iacchius and Timotheus made this for Saturnina, their well-deserving wife/consort.
EDCS-11200252.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a dedication to Saturnina by Iacchius (her husband/consort) and Timotheus, likely indicating a joint family monument.

EDCS-11200254.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucius Valerius Epigonus vixit annos XXXX hic situs est
EDCS-11200254.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Valerius Epigonus lived 40 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-11200254.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Valerius Epigonus, stating his age at death and burial place, characteristic of a tombstone or urn dedication.

EDCS-11200255.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Valeri Philini vixit annos LXX collegium fabrum
EDCS-11200255.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Valerius Philinus, who lived 70 years. The college of artisans (or builders).
EDCS-11200255.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Valerius Philinus, indicating that it was dedicated by a collegium fabrum (guild of artisans/builders), suggesting a collective burial or memorial by his professional association.

EDCS-11200268.translation: Convertina? [---] to Plotia, her spouse. PALMA.
EDCS-11200268.summary: This appears to be a funerary dedication, possibly by Convertina, to her spouse Plotia, with the word 'PALMA' potentially indicating a victory or honorific element.

EDCS-11200271.translation: Decurion of the colony, Saturninus, decurion.
EDCS-11200271.summary: This is an inscription mentioning Saturninus, a decurion (councillor) of a Roman colony, likely part of an honorary monument or a public building dedication.

EDCS-11200323.summary: This is a brief inscription giving the name Publius Antonius Super, likely an ownership mark, a signet, or part of a longer, damaged text.

EDCS-11200324.summary: This is a very abbreviated inscription with initials Marcus C. O., likely part of a name, an ownership mark, or a short label on an object.

EDCS-11200325.summary: This is a very abbreviated inscription with initials Quintus C. S., likely part of a name, an ownership mark, or a short label on an object.

EDCS-11200331.summary: This is a short inscription bearing the name Aelius Iulianus, likely an ownership mark, a simple dedicatory text, or a component of a larger inscription.

EDCS-11200335.summary: This inscription appears to be a potter's stamp or a label from a workshop, indicating that it belongs to the pottery of Fortis and is dedicated to or for Victor, perhaps a craftsman or a recipient.

EDCS-11200350.text_edition: Dianae Augustae Quintus Vibius Donatus praefectus cohortis  donum dedit libens
EDCS-11200350.translation: To Diana Augusta, Quintus Vibius Donatus, prefect of the cohort, willingly gave this gift.
EDCS-11200350.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Quintus Vibius Donatus, a military prefect, to the goddess Diana Augusta, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-11200352.summary: This fragmentary inscription preserves part of a name, Dizo Posidonius or Posidona, but its context and purpose are lost.

EDCS-11200355.summary: This extremely fragmentary inscription contains parts of a name ending in '-nus' and the possible rank of centurion, but its meaning is largely illegible.

EDCS-11200356.text_edition: Dis Manibus Glavicida annorum XXX Valeria Vellavia coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-11200356.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Glavicida, 30 years old. Valeria Vellavia [made this] for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-11200356.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Valeria Vellavia for her husband Glavicida, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-11200365.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publio Aelio Aeliano vixit nnos III dies XXX Piperas Timostrati dispensatoris vikarius sic et Aelia Epictesis parentes filio bene merenti fecerunt
EDCS-11200365.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Publius Aelius Aelianus, who lived for 3 years and 30 days. Piperas, a vicarius of Timostratus the dispensator, and Aelia Epictesis, his parents, made this for their well-deserving son.
EDCS-11200365.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Piperas, a vicarius, and Aelia Epictesis to their young son, Publius Aelius Aelianus, who died at the age of three years and thirty days.

EDCS-11200366.text_edition: Agnam dono dedit Oppius Iulianus miles
EDCS-11200366.translation: Oppius Julianus, a soldier, gave a lamb as a gift.
EDCS-11200366.summary: This inscription records a gift of a lamb made by Oppius Julianus, a soldier, likely as a religious offering or donation.

EDCS-11200367.text_edition: Rogo et peto primicere sic tunc sic puellam Bariam teretrum perdis ego Sterius modo rudi cunscio sic me reddere Bessam exsium visu peto et rogo primicere sic reddas mihi teretrum tum venet Bessa volando Flaccus aliam ullam emet et tollet mihi cor pater Flaccus
EDCS-11200367.translation: I ask and I beg, chief, if then you destroy the little girl Baria with a drill, I, Sterius, now conscious in my ignorance, ask to return Bessa, exiled from sight. I ask and I beg, chief, that you return to me the drill. Then Bessa will come flying. Flaccus will buy another one and take my heart, father Flaccus.
EDCS-11200367.summary: This unusual inscription appears to be a magical or curse tablet, invoking a 'chief' (primicerius) for a strange request involving a drill, a girl named Baria, and the return of Bessa, with a mention of Flaccus.

EDCS-11200374.text_edition: Sub cura Hermogeni praepositi ripae legionis VII Claudiae partis citerioris
EDCS-11200374.translation: Under the care of Hermogenes, commander of the bank of the Seventh Claudian Legion, of the nearer part.
EDCS-11200374.summary: This inscription identifies a post or responsibility held by Hermogenes, who was in charge of a section of the bank belonging to Legio VII Claudia.

EDCS-11200378.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Bellinus Titi Iuli Saturnini conductoris publici portorii servus |contrascriptor votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-11200378.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Bellinus, a slave and clerk of Titus Iulius Saturninus, a public tax collector, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11200378.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Bellinus, a slave and clerk working for a public tax collector, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-11200380.text_edition: vixit annos XV et Iuliae Provincialis vixit annos XXV Provi[ncialis &
EDCS-11200380.translation: lived 15 years, and Iulia Provincialis lived 25 years. Provi[ncialis] & [---]
EDCS-11200380.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, providing the ages of two individuals, one of whom is Iulia Provincialis, and likely commemorating them.

EDCS-11200401.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelius Cornelius veteranus vixit nnos LXXX Aelia Primitiva coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-11200401.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aelius Cornelius, a veteran, lived 80 years. Aelia Primitiva placed this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-11200401.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aelia Primitiva to her husband, Aelius Cornelius, a veteran who lived to eighty years old.

EDCS-11200402.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Surus mil(es) cohortis III Delmatarum vixit annos XXXX Aurelius Proculinus signifer et heres eFt#et#FT Quintina coniux et Surilleo sic et Quintinus et Sura pii patri bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-11200402.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aurelius Surus, a soldier of the Third Cohort of Dalmatians, lived 40 years. Aurelius Proculinus, standard-bearer and heir, and Quintina, his wife, and Surilleo, and likewise Quintinus and Sura, dutiful children, placed this for their well-deserving father.
EDCS-11200402.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Surus, a soldier of the Third Cohort of Dalmatians, erected by his heir, his wife, and his children.

EDCS-11200403.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Aurelius.

EDCS-11200404.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ulpius Vitalio vixit annos XVII Fabius Hatius annorum LX Valentina tlitulum fecet sic --
EDCS-11200404.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Ulpius Vitalio lived 17 years. Fabius Hatius lived 60 years. Valentina made the titulus thus [---].
EDCS-11200404.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ulpius Vitalio and Fabius Hatius, erected by Valentina.

EDCS-11200413.text_edition: Dis Manibus Antestius Valentinus miles cohortis III Delmatarum
EDCS-11200413.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Antestius Valentinus, a soldier of the Third Cohort of Dalmatians.
EDCS-11200413.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Antestius Valentinus, a soldier of the Third Cohort of Dalmatians.

EDCS-11200422.text_edition: Cohortis I Vindelicorum Iuli Martialis centuria Clementis
EDCS-11200422.translation: Of the First Cohort of Vindelici, the centuria of Iulius Martialis, under Clemens.
EDCS-11200422.summary: This inscription records the unit and centuria of a military cohort, specifically the First Cohort of Vindelici, under the command of Clemens in the centuria of Iulius Martialis.

EDCS-11200443.text_edition: decurio ulus decurio anus decurio  decurio us |(centurio)  decurio    Lucius Verus  Litua  Marcianus Severus Bo    o Ianuarius Marcianus  genus decurio
EDCS-11200443.translation: Decurio, decurio, decurio, decurio, centurio, decurio. Lucius Verus, Litua, Marcianus Severus, Bo[---], Ianuarius Marcianus, decurio.
EDCS-11200443.summary: This inscription lists the names and ranks (decurio, centurio) of several individuals, including Lucius Verus, likely indicating a military or municipal context.

EDCS-11200444.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari divi Antonini filio divi Hadriani nepoti divi Traiani Parthici pronepoti divi Nervae abnepoti Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto Armeniaco Parthico maximo imperatori II tribunicia potestate XVIIII consuli III cohors I sagittariorum
EDCS-11200444.translation: To the Imperator Caesar, son of the divine Antoninus, grandson of the divine Hadrian, great-grandson of the divine Trajan Parthicus, great-great-grandson of the divine Nerva, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Armeniacus Parthicus Maximus, Imperator for the second time, holding tribunician power for the nineteenth time, consul for the third time: the First Cohort of Archers.
EDCS-11200444.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by the First Cohort of Archers to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (Caracalla), detailing his full imperial titulature and powers, likely from AD 216.

EDCS-11200446.text_edition: Dis Patriis Marcellus L DEL numeri Palmyrenorum Tibiscensium vI(otum)#v(otum)#I solvit libens merito
EDCS-11200446.translation: To the ancestral gods. Marcellus, [---] of the unit of Palmyrenes from Tibiscum, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11200446.summary: This inscription records Marcellus, a member of the Palmyrene unit stationed at Tibiscum, fulfilling a vow to the ancestral gods.

EDCS-11200447.text_edition: Genio numeri Palmyrenorum Tibiscensium etet horrei dis patriis et pro salute Auggustorum nnostrorum Publius Aelius Servius veteranus ex optione n(umeri)? s(upra) scripti ex voto posuit
EDCS-11200447.translation: To the Genius of the unit of Palmyrenes from Tibiscum and the granary, to the ancestral gods, and for the health of our Augusti, Publius Aelius Servius, a veteran by choice from the aforesaid unit, placed this from a vow.
EDCS-11200447.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Publius Aelius Servius, a veteran of the Palmyrene unit from Tibiscum, to the Genius of his unit, the granary, the ancestral gods, and for the health of the emperors, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-11200448.text_edition: Deo Soli Ierhaboli pro salutem sic dd(ominorum) nn(ostrorum) Augg(ustorum) Aurelius Laecanius Paulinus veteranus ex custode armorum cohortis I Vindelicorum et decurio coloniae Sarmizegetusae votum libens solvit
EDCS-11200448.translation: To the god Sol Ierhabol, for the welfare of our lords, the Augusti, Aurelius Laecanius Paulinus, a veteran, formerly keeper of arms for the First Cohort of Vindelici and a decurion of the colonia Sarmizegetusa, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11200448.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Sol Ierhabol by a veteran, Aurelius Laecanius Paulinus, for the welfare of the emperors, dating likely to the Roman Imperial period given the mention of Augusti and his military and civic roles.

EDCS-11200449.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Laecanius Paulinus veteranus votum solvit
EDCS-11200449.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Laecanius Paulinus, a veteran, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11200449.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by the veteran Laecanius Paulinus, likely a simpler or companion dedication to the preceding one, given the shared name.

EDCS-11200450.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Conservatori Publius is Antoninus quod effugerit periculum votum libens solvit
EDCS-11200450.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Conservator, Publius Antoninus willingly fulfilled his vow because he escaped danger.
EDCS-11200450.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Conservator by Publius Antoninus, made in gratitude for his escape from danger, indicating a personal act of thanksgiving.

EDCS-11200451.text_edition: Aelius Victorinus decurio quaestoricius coloniae Apulensis pro salute sua et suorum ex voto libens posuit merito
EDCS-11200451.translation: Aelius Victorinus, decurion quaestoricius of the colonia Apulensis, willingly and deservedly placed this for his own welfare and that of his family, in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-11200451.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication by Aelius Victorinus, a decurion quaestoricius of the colonia Apulensis, for the welfare of himself and his family, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-11200455.text_edition: Silvano Domestico sacrum pro salute M
EDCS-11200455.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Domesticus, for the welfare of M[---].
EDCS-11200455.summary: This inscription is a sacred dedication to Silvanus Domesticus, a deity associated with woodlands and household protection, made for the welfare of an individual whose name begins with M.

EDCS-11200460.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publius Aelius Claudianus miles numeri Palmyrenorum Tibiscensium  vixit annos SC A MP M
EDCS-11200460.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Publius Aelius Claudianus, a soldier of the numeri Palmyrenorum Tibiscensium, lived 100 years and [---] months.
EDCS-11200460.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Publius Aelius Claudianus, a soldier of the Palmyrene unit stationed at Tibiscum, recording his age at death and marking his burial.

EDCS-11200461.text_edition: Dis Manibus lius Male E miles ex numero Palmyrenorum vixit annos ZI
EDCS-11200461.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. [---]lius Male E[---], a soldier from the numeri Palmyrenorum, lived for 71 years.
EDCS-11200461.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for a soldier from the Palmyrene unit, noting his name and age at death, dedicated to the Manes.

EDCS-11200462.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelius Sebler ex numero Maurorum Tibiscensium vixit nns VL et Aeliae SaTVS vixit annis XXX et Ael(ius) Valens filius eorum AT I heres ponendum curavit
EDCS-11200462.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aelius Sebler, from the numeri Maurorum Tibiscensium, lived for 50 years; and Aelia SaTVS lived for 30 years; and Aelius Valens, their son, as heir, took care to have this placed.
EDCS-11200462.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument erected by Aelius Valens for his parents, Aelius Sebler, a soldier from the Mauritanian unit at Tibiscum, and Aelia SaTVS, recording their ages at death.

EDCS-11200463.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Publius Aelius [---].
EDCS-11200463.summary: This inscription is a fragmentary funerary dedication to the Manes for Publius Aelius, indicating a tombstone or memorial.

EDCS-11200464.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publia Aelia IngCenua#Ingenua#INCENUA vixit annos II et Publia Ingenua Florentina vixit annos II parentes posuerunt
EDCS-11200464.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Publia Aelia Ingenua lived for 2 years, and Publia Ingenua Florentina lived for 2 years. Their parents placed this.
EDCS-11200464.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument dedicated by parents to their two young daughters, Publia Aelia Ingenua and Publia Ingenua Florentina, who both died at the age of two.

EDCS-11200465.text_edition: Dis Manibus Antonio Marco et Valerio Iuliano militibus ex Palmyrenis vixerunt annos XXX posuit Aelius Priscus bene merentibus
EDCS-11200465.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aelius Priscus placed this for the well-deserving soldiers Antonius Marcus and Valerius Iulianus, from among the Palmyrenes, who lived for 30 years.
EDCS-11200465.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aelius Priscus to two Palmyrene soldiers, Antonius Marcus and Valerius Iulianus, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-11200466.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Barsemia vixit annos XV Aelia Artem<e=I>dora#Artemedora#ARTEMIDORA et Aurelius Barbarcas filio pusuerunt sic et fratri Menio
EDCS-11200466.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelius Barsemia lived for 15 years. Aelia Artemedora and Aurelius Barbarcas placed this for their son, and likewise for his brother Menius.
EDCS-11200466.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Aelia Artemedora and Aurelius Barbarcas for their son, Aurelius Barsemia, who lived for 15 years, and also for his brother Menius.

EDCS-11200467.text_edition: Bana Gaius MAEIN optio patri et filio bene merentibus AKHFS
EDCS-11200467.translation: Bana Gaius MAEIN, optio, to his well-deserving father and son. AKHFS.
EDCS-11200467.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Gaius MAEIN, an optio, for his father and son, possibly dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-11200468.text_edition: Dis Manibus Brisanus Aulusani miles co[hortis &
EDCS-11200468.translation: To the Divine Manes. Brisanus, son of Aulusanus, soldier of the cohort [---].
EDCS-11200468.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Brisanus, son of Aulusanus, a soldier of a cohort, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-11200469.text_edition: S M D Claudius M veteranus ex numero Palmyrenorum Tibiscensium vixit annos XXXV militavit annos? CRA vixit annos XXV Au et MaD B V B filio bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-11200469.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Claudius M, a veteran from the number of the Palmyrenes of Tibiscum, lived for 35 years. He served for [---] years. [---] lived for 25 years. Au[---] and MaD B V B placed this for their well-deserving son.
EDCS-11200469.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Claudius M, a Palmyrene veteran from Tibiscum who lived for 35 years, and also mentions another individual who lived 25 years, for whom parents placed the monument.

EDCS-11200470.text_edition: Dis Manibus Flavius Quintus PRI ex MARO vixit annos XXX Iulia Maxima coniugi piissimo faciendum curavit
EDCS-11200470.translation: To the Divine Manes. Flavius Quintus PRI ex MARO lived for 30 years. Iulia Maxima saw to the erection of this for her most devoted husband.
EDCS-11200470.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Iulia Maxima for her most devoted husband, Flavius Quintus, who lived for 30 years.

EDCS-11200471.text_edition: Dis Manibus Neses Ierhei ex numero Palmyrenorum vixit annos XXV Malchus et Ierheus fratri bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-11200471.translation: To the Divine Manes. Neses Ierheus, from among the Palmyrenes, lived for 25 years. Malchus and Ierheus placed this for their well-deserving brother.
EDCS-11200471.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Malchus and Ierheus to their well-deserving brother, Neses Ierheus, a Palmyrene who lived for 25 years.

EDCS-11200472.text_edition: Dis Manibus Procula Batava vixit annos XXVI A
EDCS-11200472.translation: To the Divine Manes. Procula Batava lived for 26 years. A[---].
EDCS-11200472.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Procula Batava, who lived for 26 years, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-11200473.text_edition: Dis Manibus Temai Dassi filio annorum XX
EDCS-11200473.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Temai, son of Dassus, 20 years old.
EDCS-11200473.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Temai, the son of Dassus, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-11200475.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valeriae Crescentillae vixit nnos XXXV Caius Caecilius Felix Augustalis coloniae coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-11200475.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Valeria Crescentilla, who lived for 35 years. Gaius Caecilius Felix Augustalis coloniae placed this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-11200475.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Gaius Caecilius Felix, an Augustalis of the colonia, to his well-deserving wife, Valeria Crescentilla, who lived for 35 years.

EDCS-11200476.text_edition: Dis Manibus DISLARIIA s librarius numeri    vixit annos XXXV ENNI BE vixit annos XX posuit  bene merenti  Dis Manibus TXEMA VLA SE V  ID EILIVS bene merenti
EDCS-11200476.translation: To the Divine Manes. DISLARIUS, an accountant of the number, lived 35 years. ENNI BE lived 20 years. He placed this for the well-deserving. To the Divine Manes. TXEMA VLA SE V ID EILIUS for the well-deserving.
EDCS-11200476.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the deaths of multiple individuals, erected by an unspecified dedicator for the well-deserving departed.

EDCS-11200479.translation: His father-in-law placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-11200479.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by a father-in-law for a well-deserving individual, likely recording a death.

EDCS-11200564.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a personal dedication or funerary epitaph, identifying an individual named Marcus A[---] Syrus.

EDCS-11200569.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely a personal name on a funerary marker or a simple dedication, identifying Aurelius Alexander.

EDCS-11200618.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi filio divi Marci Antonini Pii Germanici Sarmatici nepoti divi Hadriani abnepoti divi Traiani Parthici et divi Nervae adnepoti Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto res publica coloniae Septimiae Drobetensium
EDCS-11200618.translation: To Emperor Caesar, son of Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus, Adiabenicus, Parthicus maximus; grandson of the divine Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus; great-grandson of the divine Hadrian; great-great-grandson of the divine Trajan Parthicus and the divine Nerva; Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus. The Republic of the colony of Septimia Drobetensium.
EDCS-11200618.summary: This is a public dedication, likely a statue base, honoring Emperor Caracalla (Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus) with his full titulature and extensive imperial ancestry, erected by the colony of Septimia Drobetensium during the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-11200621.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari [Marco Iulio] [Philippo] Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate consuli patri patriae proconsuli cohors I sagittariorum [Philip][piana] |(miliaria) equitata devota numini maiestatique eius
EDCS-11200621.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Iulius Philippus Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power, consul, father of the country, proconsul. The First Cohort of archers, Philippiana, a thousand strong, mounted, devoted to his divinity and majesty.
EDCS-11200621.summary: This is a military dedication, likely a statue base or altar, erected by the First Philippian Cohort of mounted archers in honor of Emperor Philip the Arab (Marcus Julius Philippus Augustus), likely dating to the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-11200627.text_edition: Pro salute Augustorum Luci Septimi Severi et Marci Aureli Antonini et Publi Septimio sic Getae Caesaris sub cura Aureli Heracliti procuratoris Auggustorum Eutyches et Apulensis servi vilici tabularium a solo fecerunt
EDCS-11200627.translation: For the welfare of the Augusti Lucius Septimius Severus and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, and Publius Septimius Geta Caesar, under the care of Aurelius Heraclitus, procurator of the Augusti, Eutyches and Apulensis, slaves and estate managers, built the archive from the ground up.
EDCS-11200627.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription recording the construction of an archive building from its foundations by the slaves Eutyches and Apulensis, estate managers, under the supervision of Aurelius Heraclitus, in honor of the imperial family (Septimius Severus, Caracalla, and Geta) during the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-11200631.text_edition: Aram Iovi Cohortali sacrum PPOO Lupus tribunus
EDCS-11200631.translation: A sacred altar to Jupiter Cohortalis. Lupus, tribune.
EDCS-11200631.summary: This is a religious dedication, an altar, to Jupiter Cohortalis, set up by a tribune named Lupus.

EDCS-11200635.translation: lius, a decurion, placed this according to a vow.
EDCS-11200635.summary: This is a fragmentary votive inscription, placed by a decurion named [---]lius in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-11200639.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio? Titio Cai filio Victricensi Ianuario |(centurioni) legionis IIII Flaviae frumentario vixit nnos LVI C(aius) Titius Epipodius libertus et heres optimo patrono bene merenti faciendum curavit hic situs est
EDCS-11200639.translation: To the Divine Manes. Caius? Titius Ianuarius, son of Caius, from Victrix, centurion of the Fourth Flavian Legion, frumentarius, lived 56 years. Caius Titius Epipodius, his freedman and heir, took care to have this made for his excellent and well-deserving patron. He is laid here.
EDCS-11200639.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caius Titius Ianuarius, a centurion and frumentarius of Legio IIII Flavia, who died at age 56, erected by his freedman and heir, Caius Titius Epipodius, for his well-deserving patron.

EDCS-11200640.text_edition: Dis Manibus Flaviae Valentinae Sirmiensi vixit nnos XL dies XVII coniugi carissimae et pientissimae et dignissimae ob principium castitatis Maecius Domitius |(centurio) legionis V Macedonicae
EDCS-11200640.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Flavia Valentina of Sirmium, who lived 40 years and 17 days, a most dear, devoted, and worthy wife, on account of her principle of chastity, Maecius Domitius, a centurion of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, [set this up].
EDCS-11200640.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Maecius Domitius, a centurion, to his beloved wife Flavia Valentina, praising her chastity.

EDCS-11200641.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Publius Aelius Iada Publio Aelio Viatori filio vixit annos III bene merenti posuit
EDCS-11200641.translation: To the Divine Manes. Publius Aelius Iada erected this for his well-deserving son Publius Aelius Viator, who lived for 3 years.
EDCS-11200641.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Publius Aelius Iada for his young son, Publius Aelius Viator, who died at the age of three.

EDCS-11200643.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcus Arrius Saturninus decurio municipii Hadriani Drobetensium vixit nnos XVI Arria Saturnina soror et heres iuxta voluntatem testamenti faciEundum#faciundum#FACEUNDUM curavit
EDCS-11200643.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Arrius Saturninus, decurion of the municipium of Hadriani Drobetenses, lived 16 years. Arria Saturnina, his sister and heir, saw to its erection according to the wishes of his will.
EDCS-11200643.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Arrius Saturninus, a decurion who died at 16, erected by his sister and heir Arria Saturnina, following his will.

EDCS-11200644.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulia Kalligenia vixit annos XL Iulius Daciscus vixit annos  Valerius Vivibius vixit annos L Iulius Paet(inus) Vibenus Augustalis coloniae Drubetensium coniugi pientissimae et amicis bene merentibus posuit
EDCS-11200644.translation: To the Divine Manes. Julia Kalligenia lived 40 years. Julius Daciscus lived [unspecified] years. Valerius Vivibius lived 50 years. Julius Paetinus Vibenus, Augustalis of the colony of Drobeta, erected this for his most devoted wife and well-deserving friends.
EDCS-11200644.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Julius Paetinus Vibenus, an Augustalis, to his devoted wife Julia Kalligenia and two friends, Julius Daciscus and Valerius Vivibius.

EDCS-11200645.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Minicio Simphoro sic Augustali municipii Hadriani Dobretensium Minicia
EDCS-11200645.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Marcus Minicius Simphorus, Augustalis of the municipium of Hadriani Drobetenses. Minicia [set this up].
EDCS-11200645.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Minicius Simphorus, an Augustalis, likely erected by a woman named Minicia.

EDCS-11200646.translation: ] of Aelius P[...]. Lived for [...] years.
EDCS-11200646.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, preserving parts of a name and an age.

EDCS-11200648.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Laudice sic Syrae Collina Philadelphia vixit annis XXXC Hera coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-11200648.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Laudice, a Syrian from Collina Philadelphia, who lived 30 years. Hera erected this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-11200648.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Laudice, a Syrian woman, erected by her husband Hera, who praises her as well-deserving.

EDCS-11200649.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Antio Herculano beneficiario consularis
EDCS-11200649.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Marcus Antius Herculanus, beneficiary of the consular.
EDCS-11200649.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Marcus Antius Herculanus, who held the military position of beneficiarius consularis.

EDCS-11200650.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Grata Vitalis.
EDCS-11200650.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription dedicated to a person named Grata Vitalis.

EDCS-11200652.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Julia Fortunata.
EDCS-11200652.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription dedicated to Julia Fortunata.

EDCS-11200654.translation: Ica, a mother, to her most dutiful son.
EDCS-11200654.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a mother named Ica to her pious son.

EDCS-11200657.text_edition: IL Aurelius Atellanus veteranus candidatus legionis V Macedonicae
EDCS-11200657.translation: Lucius Aurelius Atellanus, a veteran candidate of the Fifth Macedonian Legion.
EDCS-11200657.summary: This inscription records the name and military status of Lucius Aurelius Atellanus, a veteran and candidate for office within the Legio V Macedonica.

EDCS-11200669.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning a person named Aelia and possibly a military cohort.

EDCS-11200672.text_edition: Quadrubiis sic Aurelius Marci filius Philiscus votum solvit libens
EDCS-11200672.translation: To the Quadrivia. Thus Aurelius Philiscus, son of Marcus, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11200672.summary: This inscription records a vow made by Aurelius Philiscus, son of Marcus, to the deities of crossroads (the Quadrivia).

EDCS-11200675.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning names such as Claudia and another ending in -nianus.

EDCS-11200706.text_edition: Aurelius Mercurius milis sic cohortis I sagittariorum in figlinis magister super milites LX scripsit Aurelius Iulianus milis sic cohortis primae
EDCS-11200706.translation: Aurelius Mercurius, a soldier of the First Cohort of Archers, wrote this as master over 60 soldiers in the tile factories. Aurelius Julianus, a soldier of the First Cohort.
EDCS-11200706.summary: This inscription records that Aurelius Mercurius, a soldier and master in the tile factories, wrote this text concerning 60 soldiers under him, with Aurelius Julianus also mentioned as a soldier of the same cohort.

EDCS-11200721.text_edition: Aurelius Gratinianus pro immunitate votum vovit
EDCS-11200721.translation: Aurelius Gratinianus made a vow for immunity.
EDCS-11200721.summary: This inscription records that Aurelius Gratinianus made a vow to ensure his immunity, likely from taxes or public duties.

EDCS-11200727.translation: Castor placed this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-11200727.summary: This inscription records that Castor dedicated this monument as an ex-voto offering.

EDCS-11200728.translation: To the Lady Placida. Valerius Mexy made a vow.
EDCS-11200728.summary: This inscription records a vow made by Valerius Mexy to the goddess Domina Placida.

EDCS-11200729.text_edition: Soli Invicto Mithrae pro salute sua suorumque Caius Antonius Iulianus votum libens merito solvit
EDCS-11200729.translation: To the Unconquered Sun Mithras, for his own well-being and that of his family, Gaius Antonius Julianus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11200729.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Sol Invictus Mithras by Gaius Antonius Julianus for his and his family's well-being.

EDCS-11200731.translation: To the Divine Manes of Marcus Cassius Herculanus.
EDCS-11200731.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Cassius Herculanus.

EDCS-11200741.text_edition: Dianae Reginae Dioscorus Ianuari et [] []I contubernalis eTx#ex#TX votum sic posuerunt
EDCS-11200741.translation: To Diana Regina, Dioscorus, son of Ianuarius, and [---] [---] his messmate, thus placed this vow.
EDCS-11200741.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Diana Regina by Dioscorus, son of Ianuarius, and his messmate.

EDCS-11200743.summary: This inscription is a maker's mark indicating that Ambiurus crafted the object.

EDCS-11200747.text_edition: Dis Manibus Sura vixit annos T
EDCS-11200747.translation: To the Divine Manes, Sura lived for [---] years.
EDCS-11200747.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sura, noting their age at death.

EDCS-11200770.text_edition: Septimius decurio coloniae Drubetae vixit annis LXV ClIodii#Clodii#CIODI Septimius et Peregrinus R
EDCS-11200770.translation: Septimius, decurion of the colony of Drubeta, lived for 65 years. Clodius Septimius and Peregrinus [---].
EDCS-11200770.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Septimius, a decurion of Drubeta, placed by Clodius Septimius and Peregrinus.

EDCS-11200781.text_edition: Reginae Aurelius Antoninus ex voto posuit
EDCS-11200781.translation: To Regina, Aurelius Antoninus placed [this] as a vow.
EDCS-11200781.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Regina by Aurelius Antoninus.

EDCS-11200784.text_edition: Deae placidae pro salute Marcianae et Quintiliani filiorum
EDCS-11200784.translation: To the peaceful goddess, for the health of their children Marciana and Quintilianus.
EDCS-11200784.summary: This inscription is a dedication to a peaceful goddess made for the health of Marciana and Quintilianus, who are identified as children of the dedicants.

EDCS-11200785.summary: This inscription is likely a maker's mark or a short dedication by Lucius Aurelius, possibly for an individual named Eronu or an object associated with them.

EDCS-11200787.text_edition: Herculi Invicto pro salute rinus et Iulianus posuerunt
EDCS-11200787.translation: To Hercules Invictus, for the health of [---]rinus and Iulianus placed [this].
EDCS-11200787.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Hercules Invictus for the health of [---]rinus and Iulianus.

EDCS-11200807.summary: This inscription is a maker's mark, indicating that Lollianus produced the mold for an object.

EDCS-11200808.summary: This short inscription records that Brutus made a 'form', likely referring to a mold or a physical shape for construction or art.

EDCS-11200883.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Iulius Vitalianus ex voto posuit
EDCS-11200883.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Publius Iulius Vitalianus placed this in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-11200883.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Publius Iulius Vitalianus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-11200898.text_edition: Salvis ddominis nnostris Philippis Auggustis felix legio VII Claudia pia fidelis vestra |(centuriae) VI hastatorum prioris et posterioris devotae numini maiestatique eorum  RNS-
