EDCS-12000869.translation: Sacred to the Unconquered God Mithras. Statorius Anus, a decurion and flamen of the municipium Septimum Apulense, made this temple of the Invincible for his own well-being and that of his family, with his own money.
EDCS-12000869.summary: This inscription records the dedication and construction of a temple to the Unconquered God Mithras by Statorius Anus, a local official, for the welfare of himself and his family, funded personally.

EDCS-12000870.text_edition: Silvano Valerius Martialis pro filio votum libens solvit
EDCS-12000870.translation: To Silvanus, Valerius Martialis willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for his son.
EDCS-12000870.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to the god Silvanus by Valerius Martialis, made willingly and deservedly on behalf of his son.

EDCS-12000871.text_edition: Mercurio Hilari Marcus Herennius Faustus legatus Augusti
EDCS-12000871.translation: To Mercurius Hilarus, Marcus Herennius Faustus, legate of Augustus.
EDCS-12000871.summary: This is a dedication made to Mercurius Hilarus by Marcus Herennius Faustus, who served as an Augustan legate.

EDCS-12000872.text_edition: us Archelaus |(centurio) legionis XIII geminae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-12000872.translation: Archelaus, centurion of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-12000872.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Archelaus, a centurion of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, who willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.

EDCS-12100795.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-12100795.text_edition: Imperator Caesar divi Traiani Parthici filius divi Nervae nepos Traianus Hadrianus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate IIII consul III Palmyrenis sagittariis ex Syria qui sunt in Dacia superiore sub Iulio Severo civitatem dedit iis quorum nomina subscripta sunt ante diem III Kalendas Iulias Caio Publicio Marcello Lucio Rutilio Propinquo consulibus Barici Male filio Palmyra descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aenea quae fixa est Romae in muro post templum divi Augusti ad Minervam      Luci Attei Atteiani Iuni Eutychi Publi Atini Flori Quinti Fabi Titi Luci Pulli Anthi Titi Claudi Erotis Quinti Atini Crescentis
EDCS-12100795.translation: Emperor Caesar Traianus Hadrianus Augustus, son of the divine Traianus Parthicus, grandson of the divine Nerva, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the fourth time, consul for the third time, granted citizenship to Palmyrene archers from Syria who are in Dacia Superior under Julius Severus, to those whose names are written below, on the third day before the Kalends of July, with Gaius Publicius Marcellus and Lucius Rutilius Propinquus as consuls, to Baricus, son of Male, from Palmyra. Copied and verified from the bronze tablet which is fixed in Rome on the wall behind the temple of the divine Augustus near Minerva. (Witnesses:) Lucius Atteius Atteianus, Iunius Eutychus, Publius Atinius Florus, Quintus Fabius Titus, Lucius Pullius Anthus, Titus Claudius Erotus, Quintus Atinius Crescens.
EDCS-12100795.summary: This inscription is a military diploma from Emperor Hadrian, dated to June 29, 120 AD, granting Roman citizenship and conubium to Palmyrene archers from Syria serving in Dacia Superior, listing the recipient and witnesses.

EDCS-12100813.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-12100813.text_edition: Imperator Caesar divi Traiani Parthici filius divi Nervae nepos Traianus Hadrianus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XVII consul III pater patriae equitibus et peditibus qui militaverunt in alis II et cohortibus VI quae appellantur Siliana civium Romanorum et I Tungrorum Frontoniana et I Ulpia Brittonum |(miliaria) et I Britannica |(miliaria) et I Hispanorum et I Batavorum |(miliaria) et I Aelia Gaesatorum |(miliaria) et II Brittonum |(miliaria) et sunt in Dacia Porolissensi sub Flavio Italico quinis et vicenis pluribusve stipendiis emeritis dimissis honesta missione quorum nomina subscripta sunt ipsis liberis posterisque eorum civitatem dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas iis data aut si qui caelibes essent cum iis quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singuli singulas ante diem VI Nonas Iulias Quinto Flavio Tertullo Quinto Iunio Rustico consulibus cohortis I Britannicae |(miliariae) cui praeest Tiberius Claudius Fortis Capua ex pedite Sepenesto Rivi filio Cornon descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aenea quae fixa est Romae in muro post templum divi Augusti ad Minervam  Imperator Caesar divi Traiani Parthici filius divi Nervae nepos Traianus Hadrianus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XVII consul III pater patriae equitibus et peditibus qui militaverunt in alis II et cohortibus VI quae appellantur Siliana civium Romanorum et I Tungrorum Frontoniana et I Ulpia Brittonum |(miliaria) et I Britannica |(miliaria) et I Hispanorum et II Batavorum |(miliaria) et I Aelia Gaesatorum |(miliaria) et II Brittonum |(miliaria) et sunt in Dacia Porolissensi sub Flavio Italico quinis et vicenis pluribusve stipendiis emeritis dimissis honesta missione quorum nomina subscripta sunt ipsis liberis posterisque eorum civitatem dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas iis data aut siqui caelibes essent cum iis quas postea [duxissent dumtaxat singuli &
EDCS-12100813.translation: Emperor Caesar Traianus Hadrianus Augustus, son of the divine Traianus Parthicus, grandson of the divine Nerva, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the seventeenth time, consul for the third time, father of his country, granted citizenship to the cavalrymen and infantrymen who served in the two alae and six cohorts, which are called Siliana civium Romanorum, and I Tungrorum Frontoniana, and I Ulpia Brittonum (milliaria), and I Britannica (milliaria), and I Hispanorum, and I Batavorum (milliaria), and I Aelia Gaesatorum (milliaria), and II Brittonum (milliaria), and are in Dacia Porolissensis under Flavius Italicus, after having completed twenty-five or more stipends, dismissed with an honorable discharge, whose names are written below, to them, their children and descendants, and conubium with the wives they had at the time citizenship was granted to them, or if any were celibate, with those they would later marry, provided each man married only one woman, on the sixth day before the Nones of July, with Quintus Flavius Tertullus and Quintus Iunius Rusticus as consuls, to Sepenestus, son of Rivus, from Cornon, a foot-soldier of the I Britannica (milliaria) cohort, commanded by Tiberius Claudius Fortis, from Capua. Copied and verified from the bronze tablet which is fixed in Rome on the wall behind the temple of the divine Augustus near Minerva. Emperor Caesar Traianus Hadrianus Augustus, son of the divine Traianus Parthicus, grandson of the divine Nerva, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the seventeenth time, consul for the third time, father of his country, granted citizenship to the cavalrymen and infantrymen who served in the two alae and six cohorts, which are called Siliana civium Romanorum, and I Tungrorum Frontoniana, and I Ulpia Brittonum (milliaria), and I Britannica (milliaria), and I Hispanorum, and II Batavorum (milliaria), and I Aelia Gaesatorum (milliaria), and II Brittonum (milliaria), and are in Dacia Porolissensis under Flavius Italicus, after having completed twenty-five or more stipends, dismissed with an honorable discharge, whose names are written below, to them, their children and descendants, and conubium with the wives they had at the time citizenship was granted to them, or if any were celibate, with those they would later marry, provided each man married only one woman.
EDCS-12100813.summary: This is a military diploma from Emperor Hadrian, dated to July 2, 133 AD, granting Roman citizenship and conubium to cavalrymen and infantrymen honorably discharged from various auxiliary units stationed in Dacia Porolissensis, specifically naming Sepenestus, a foot-soldier from the I Britannica cohort.

EDCS-12200247.text_edition: Invicto deo Serapidi Marcus Lucceius Felix procurator Augusti nostri
EDCS-12200247.translation: To the Invincible god Serapis, Marcus Lucceius Felix, procurator of our Augustus.
EDCS-12200247.summary: This is a dedication to the Invincible god Serapis by Marcus Lucceius Felix, an imperial procurator.

EDCS-12200248.translation: Lucceius Felix, procurator of our Augustus.
EDCS-12200248.summary: This inscription records the name and title of Lucceius Felix, a procurator, likely honoring or dedicating something to the emperor.

EDCS-12200251.text_edition: Invicto Mithrae Marti Camulo Mercurio Rosmertae Quintus Axius Aelianus vir egregius procurator Auggustorum Ioni
EDCS-12200251.translation: To the Unconquered Mithras, Mars Camulus, Mercury, Rosmerta, Quintus Axius Aelianus, distinguished man, procurator of the Augusti, to Ion.
EDCS-12200251.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Quintus Axius Aelianus, a procurator, to several deities, including Mithras, Mars Camulus, Mercury, and Rosmerta, and possibly to a person named Ion, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12200252.text_edition: Aesculapio Saluti Epionae Veneri ubique Neptuno Salaciae cupidinibus fontibus aquis Quintus Axius Aelianus vir egregius procurator Auggustorum Ioni
EDCS-12200252.translation: To Aesculapius, Salus, Epiona, Venus everywhere, Neptune, Salacia, the Cupids, the Springs, the Waters, Quintus Axius Aelianus, distinguished man, procurator of the Augusti, to Ion.
EDCS-12200252.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Quintus Axius Aelianus, a procurator, to a wide array of deities associated with health and water, including Aesculapius, Salus, Venus, and Neptune, and possibly to a person named Ion, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12200257.summary: This inscription is a fragmentary record, preserving only parts of a name, possibly Aurelius, and other illegible text.

EDCS-12200258.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publio Aelio Martiali vixit nnis XLV collegium fabrum
EDCS-12200258.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Publius Aelius Martialis. He lived 45 years. The college of craftsmen (dedicated this).
EDCS-12200258.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument dedicated to Publius Aelius Martialis, who lived 45 years, by the college of craftsmen (collegium fabrum).

EDCS-12200260.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Flavio Marci filio Papiria Epstelae veterano ex tribuni beneficiario vixit
EDCS-12200260.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Marcus Flavius, son of Marcus, of the Papiria tribe, Epstelae, a veteran, formerly a beneficiarius of the tribune. He lived...
EDCS-12200260.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument dedicated to Marcus Flavius, a veteran and former beneficiarius of a tribune, possibly erected by his family or comrades.

EDCS-12300237.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-12300237.translation: The Emperor Caesar Domitianus Augustus Germanicus, son of the deified Vespasian, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 5th time, acclaimed emperor for the 12th time, perpetual censor, consul for the 12th time, father of his country, granted citizenship to the cavalrymen and infantrymen who serve in the two alae called veteran Gaetulorum and 1st Thracum Mauretana, and in the four cohorts, 1st Augusta Lusitanorum, and 1st and 2nd Thracum, and 2nd Cantabrorum, and who are in Judaea under Gnaeus Pompeius Longinus, who had served 25 stipends, and to their children and descendants; and granted the right of marriage with the wives they then had when citizenship was given to them, or, if they were bachelors, with those whom they later married, one man to one woman only, on the 3rd day before the Ides of May, when Sextus Octavius Fronto and Tiberius Iulius Candidus Marius Celsus were consuls; to the cavalryman Seuthes, son of Traibithes, Coloteticus, of the 2nd Cohort of Thracians, commanded by Claudius Montanus. Copied and verified from the bronze tablet which is affixed in Rome on the Capitol, behind the trophies of Germanicus, on the tribunal which is at the temple of Fides Populi Romani. Witnesses: Decimus Consius Alcmus, Gaius Terentius Natalis, Publius Sallienus Philumenus, Tiberius Claudius Erastus, Gaius Hostilius Martialis, Publius Silius Hermes, Gaius Quintius Philetus.
EDCS-12300237.summary: This is a military diploma issued by Emperor Domitian in 86 CE, granting Roman citizenship and conubium (right of marriage) to auxiliary soldiers, specifically a Thracian cavalryman named Seuthes, who had completed 25 years of service in Judaea.

EDCS-12300312.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-12300312.translation: The Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, son of the deified Hadrian, grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-grandson of the deified Nerva, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 21st time, acclaimed emperor for the 2nd time, consul for the 4th time, father of his country, granted Roman citizenship to the cavalrymen and infantrymen who served in the three alae and three cohorts and vexillations who are with the native Mauretanians in Dacia Superior and are under Statius Priscus, legate, having completed 25 stipends and been honorably discharged, those who did not have Roman citizenship; and the right of marriage with the wives they then had when citizenship was given to them, or with those whom they later married, one man to one woman only, on the 8th day before the Ides of July, when Fabianus and Bassus were consuls; to Heptaporis, son of Isus, Bessus, a common soldier of the 1st Ala of Gauls and Bosporans, commanded by Licinius Nigrinus. Copied and verified from the bronze tablet which is affixed in Rome on the wall behind the temple of the deified Augustus, near Minerva. Witnesses: Marcus Servilius Geta, Lucius Pullius Chresimus, Marcus Sentilius Iasus, Tiberius Iulius Felix, Gaius Bellus Urb[...], Gaius Pomponius Statianus, Publius Ocilius Priscus.
EDCS-12300312.summary: This is a military diploma issued by Emperor Antoninus Pius in 158 CE, granting Roman citizenship and conubium (right of marriage) to auxiliary soldiers, specifically a common soldier named Heptaporis Bessus from the 1st Ala of Gauls and Bosporans, who had served in Dacia Superior.

EDCS-12300364.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-12300364.text_edition: Imperator Caesar divi Nervae filius Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XIIII imperator VI consul V pater patriae peditibus et equitibus qui militant in cohorte I Brittonum milliaria Ulpia torquata pia fideli civium Romanorum quae est in Dacia sub Decimo Terentio Scauriano quorum nomina subscripta sunt pie et fideliter expeditione Dacica functis ante emerita stipendia civitatem Romanam dedit ante diem III Idus Augustas Darnithithi Lucio Minicio Natale Quinto Silvano Graniano consulibus pediti Marco Ulpio Adcobrovati filio Novanticoni Ratis descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aenea quae fixa est Romae in muro post templum divi Augusti ad Minervam  Imperator Caesar divi Nervae filius Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus pontifex maximus tribuniciae potestatis XIIII imperator VI consul V pater patriae peditibus et equitibus qui militant in cohorte I Brittonum milliaria Ulpia torquata pia felici civium Romanorum quae est in Dacia sub Decimo Terentio Scauriano quorum nomina subscripta sunt pie et fideliter expeditione Dacica functis ante  emerita stipendia civitatem Romanam dedit ante diem III Idus Augustas Darnithithi Lucio Minicio Natale Quinto Silvano Graniano consulibus pediti Marco Ulpio AdcobProvati#Adcobrovati#ADCOPROVATI filio Novantico Ratis descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aenea quae fixa est Romae  Publi Corneli Alexandri Luci Pulli Verecundi Publi Atini Amerimni Cai Tuticani Saturnini Luci Pulli Trophimi Cai Iuli Parati Marci Iuni Eutychi
EDCS-12300364.translation: The Emperor Caesar Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, son of the deified Nerva, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 14th time, acclaimed emperor for the 6th time, consul for the 5th time, father of his country, granted Roman citizenship to the infantrymen and cavalrymen who serve in the 1st Cohort of Britons, milliaria, Ulpia torquata, pia fidelis, of Roman citizens, which is in Dacia under Decimus Terentius Scaurianus, whose names are subscribed, having performed loyally and faithfully in the Dacian expedition before completing their stipends, on the 3rd day before the Ides of August, when Lucius Minicius Natalis and Quintus Silvanus Granianus were consuls; to the infantryman Marcus Ulpius, son of Adcobrovatus, Novantico Ratis. Copied and verified from the bronze tablet which is affixed in Rome on the wall behind the temple of the deified Augustus, near Minerva. Witnesses: Publius Cornelius Alexander, Lucius Pullius Verecundus, Publius Atinius Amerimnus, Gaius Tuticanus Saturninus, Lucius Pullius Trophimus, Gaius Iulius Paratus, Marcus Iunius Eutychus.
EDCS-12300364.summary: This is a military diploma issued by Emperor Trajan in 110 CE, granting Roman citizenship to an infantryman named Marcus Ulpius Novantico Ratis, who served in the 1st Cohort of Britons in Dacia, for his loyal service in the Dacian expedition.

EDCS-12300367.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-12300367.text_edition: Imperator Caesar divi Nervae filius Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XIIII imperator VI consul V pater patriae iis qui militaverunt equites et pedites in alis quattuor et cohortibus decem et octo quae appellantur I Augusta Ituraeorum et I Claudia et I Britannica civium Romanorum et II nnum veterana et I Brittonum |(miliaria) Ulpia torquata civium Romanorum et I Vindelicorum |(miliaria) civium Romanorum pia fidelis et I Britannica |(miliaria) civium Romanorum et I Flavia Hispanorum |(miliaria) et I Montanorum et I Thracum civium Romanorum et I Cretum sagittariorum et I Hispanorum pia fidelis et I Pannoniorum veterana pia fidelis et II Hispanorum et II Britannorum |(miliaria) civium Romanorum pia fidelis et II Gallorum Pannonica et II Gallorum Macedonica et II Flavia Commagenorum sagittariorum et V Lingonum et V Gallorum et VI Thracum et VIII Raetorum civium Romanorum et pedites Britannici et sunt in Dacia sub Decimo Terentio Scauriano quinis et vicenis pluribusve stipendiis emeritis dimissis honesta missione quorum nomina subscripta sunt ipsis liberis posterisque eorum civitatem dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas iis data aut si qui caelibes essent cum iis quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singuli singulas ante diem VI Nonas Iulias Caio Eruciano Silone Lucio Catilio Severo consulibus cohortis I Brittonum |(miliariae) Ulpiae torquatae civium Romanorum cui praeest Marcus Aemilius Bassus ex pedite Marco Ulpio Sacci filio Longino Belgo et Vitali filio eius descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aenea quae fixa est Romae in muro post templum divi Augusti ad Minervam  Imperator Caesar divi Nervae filius Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XIIII imperator VI consul V pater patriae is qui militaverunt equites et pedites in alis quattuor et cohortibus decem et octo quae appellantur I Augusta Ituraeorum et I Claudia et I Britannica civium Romanorum et II nnum veterana et I Brittonum |(miliaria) Ulpia torquata civium Romanorum et I Vindelicorum |(miliaria) civium Romanorum pia fidelis et I Britannica |(miliaria) civium Romanorum et I Flavia H=IIispanorum#Hispanorum#IIISPANOR |(miliaria) et I Montanorum et I Thracum civium Romanorum et I Cretum sagittariorum et I Hispanorum pia fidelis et I PannoniorQ(um)#Pannonior(um)#PANNONIOQ veterana pia fidelis et II Hispanorum et II nnum |(miliaria) civium Romanorum pia fidelis et II Gallorum Pannonica et II Gallorum Macedonica et II Flavia Commagenorum sagittariorum et V Lingonum et V Gallorum et VI Thracum et VIII Raetorum civium Romanorum et pedites Britannici et sunt in Dacia sub Decimo Terentio Scauriano quinis et vicenis pluribusve stipendiis emeritis dimissis honesta missione quorum nomina subscripta sunt ipsis liberis posterisque eorum civitatem dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas iis data aut si qui caelibes essent cum iis quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singuli singu singulas ante diem VI Nonas Iulias Caio Eruciano Silone Lucio Catilio Severo consulibus cohortis I Brittonum Ulpiae |(miliariae) torquatae civium Romanorum cui praeest Marcus Aemilius Bassus ex pedite Marco Ulpio Sacci filio Longino Belgo et Vitali filio eius descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aenea quae fixa est Romae  Publi Corneli Alexandri Luci Pulli Verecundi Publi Atini Amerimni Luci Pulli Trophimi Cai Tuticani Saturnini Cai Iuli Parati Marci Iuni Eutychi
EDCS-12300367.translation: The Emperor Caesar Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, son of the deified Nerva, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 14th time, acclaimed emperor for the 6th time, consul for the 5th time, father of his country, granted citizenship to those cavalrymen and infantrymen who served in the four alae and eighteen cohorts which are called 1st Augusta Ituraeorum, and 1st Claudia, and 1st Britannica of Roman citizens, and 2nd Veteran, and 1st Brittonum milliaria Ulpia torquata of Roman citizens, and 1st Vindelicorum milliaria of Roman citizens, pia fidelis, and 1st Britannica milliaria of Roman citizens, and 1st Flavia Hispanorum milliaria, and 1st Montanorum, and 1st Thracum of Roman citizens, and 1st Cretum sagittariorum, and 1st Hispanorum pia fidelis, and 1st Pannoniorum veterana pia fidelis, and 2nd Hispanorum, and 2nd Britannorum milliaria of Roman citizens, pia fidelis, and 2nd Gallorum Pannonica, and 2nd Gallorum Macedonica, and 2nd Flavia Commagenorum sagittariorum, and 5th Lingonum, and 5th Gallorum, and 6th Thracum, and 8th Raetorum of Roman citizens, and the British infantrymen, and who are in Dacia under Decimus Terentius Scaurianus, having completed 25 or more stipends and been honorably discharged; and to their children and descendants, and the right of marriage with the wives they then had when citizenship was given to them, or, if they were bachelors, with those whom they later married, one man to one woman only, on the 6th day before the Nones of July, when Gaius Erucianus Silo and Lucius Catilius Severus were consuls; to the infantryman Marcus Ulpius Longinus, son of Saccus, a Belgian, and his son Vitalis, of the 1st Cohort of Britons milliaria Ulpia torquata of Roman citizens, commanded by Marcus Aemilius Bassus. Copied and verified from the bronze tablet which is affixed in Rome on the wall behind the temple of the deified Augustus, near Minerva. Witnesses: Publius Cornelius Alexander, Lucius Pullius Verecundus, Publius Atinius Amerimnus, Lucius Pullius Trophimus, Gaius Tuticanus Saturninus, Gaius Iulius Paratus, Marcus Iunius Eutychus.
EDCS-12300367.summary: This is a military diploma issued by Emperor Trajan in 110 CE, granting Roman citizenship and conubium (right of marriage) to auxiliary soldiers, specifically an infantryman named Marcus Ulpius Longinus and his son Vitalis, who served in the 1st Cohort of Britons in Dacia, after completing their service.

EDCS-12700100.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Conservatori Titus Scruius Vitalis tribunus cohortis III Campestris votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-12700100.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the Preserver, Titus Scruius Vitalis, tribune of the Third Campestrian Cohort, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-12700100.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Conservator by Titus Scruius Vitalis, a military tribune, fulfilling a vow, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-12700121.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulius Crescens veteranus ex vixit annos LV
EDCS-12700121.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulius Crescens, a veteran, lived 55 years.
EDCS-12700121.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulius Crescens, a veteran who lived 55 years, probably erected by his family or comrades in the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-12700185.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo veterani et cives Romani Micienses per Titum Aurelium Verum et Cornelium Fortunatum magistros votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-12700185.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the veterans and Roman citizens of Mici willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow through Titus Aurelius Verus and Cornelius Fortunatus, masters.
EDCS-12700185.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by the veterans and Roman citizens of Mici, acting through two elected masters, fulfilling a vow, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-12700188.text_edition: Dis Manibus Zenon Tarasi miles numeri Germanicianorum vixit annos XXXIII Iulia marito pientissimo
EDCS-12700188.translation: To the Divine Manes. Zeno, son of Taras, a soldier of the Numerus Germanicianorum, lived 33 years. Iulia to her most devoted husband.
EDCS-12700188.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Iulia to her most devoted husband Zeno, son of Taras, a soldier in the Numerus Germanicianorum, who lived 33 years, probably from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-12800327.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Cimisteno Primus et Primianus ex voto posuerunt
EDCS-12800327.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Cimistenus, Primus and Primianus set this up from a vow.
EDCS-12800327.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Cimistenus by Primus and Primianus as a result of a vow, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-12800328.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Cimisteno pro salute imperi posuit Aurelius Iulianus
EDCS-12800328.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Cimistenus, Aurelius Iulianus set this up for the welfare of the empire.
EDCS-12800328.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Cimistenus by Aurelius Iulianus for the welfare of the empire, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12800332.text_edition: Minervae Augustae exceptores consularis  tinus ssianus ssianus Florus  Severus  Valens
EDCS-12800332.translation: To Minerva Augusta, the clerks of the consular, [---]tinus, [---]ssianus, [---]ssianus, Florus, Severus, Valens.
EDCS-12800332.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Minerva Augusta by several clerks of a consular, whose names are partially preserved, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-13302477.text_edition: Silvano Domestico Mucianus optio Palmyrenorum votum posuit libens merito
EDCS-13302477.translation: To Silvanus Domesticus, Mucianus, optio of the Palmyrenes, willingly and deservedly set up this vow.
EDCS-13302477.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Silvanus Domesticus by Mucianus, an optio (junior officer) of the Palmyrene unit, fulfilling a vow, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-13302479.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo ala II Pannoniorum cui praeest Caius Caelius Salvianus praefectus
EDCS-13302479.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the Second Pannonian Cavalry Wing, commanded by Caius Caelius Salvianus, prefect.
EDCS-13302479.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by the Ala II Pannoniorum, an auxiliary cavalry unit, under the command of its prefect, Caius Caelius Salvianus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-13302480.text_edition: Silvano Dom(estico) Marcus Ulpius Provincialis vota libens merito solvit
EDCS-13302480.translation: To Silvanus Domesticus, Marcus Ulpius Provincialis willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vows.
EDCS-13302480.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Silvanus Domesticus by Marcus Ulpius Provincialis, who willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vows, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13302485.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Aelius Heupropes sic votum posuit libenter
EDCS-13302485.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Aelius Heupropes thus gladly placed his vow.
EDCS-13302485.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aelius Heupropes, likely on a small altar or monument, dating probably to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13302486.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Aurelius Aelianus miles legionis V Macedonicae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-13302486.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Aurelius Aelianus, a soldier of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-13302486.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, dedicated by a soldier of the Legio V Macedonica, likely in thanks for a successful outcome to a request or prayer, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13302487.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Ulpius Lucanus votum reddidit libens merito
EDCS-13302487.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Ulpius Lucanus willingly and deservedly rendered his vow.
EDCS-13302487.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Ulpius Lucanus, probably for an answered prayer, dating to the Imperial era.

EDCS-13302488.text_edition: miles legionis V Macedonicae piae constantis voto Iovi Optimo Maximo voto rettulit
EDCS-13302488.translation: A soldier of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, Pious and Constant, offered a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-13302488.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, made by a soldier of the Legio V Macedonica Pia Constans, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13302490.text_edition: Iovi optimo maximo Caius Cassius Cai filius Collina Proculeianus Epiphania |(centurio) legionis XIII geminae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-13302490.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Gaius Cassius Proculeianus, son of Gaius, of the Collina tribe, Epiphania, a centurion of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-13302490.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a centurion of Legio XIII Gemina, Gaius Cassius Proculeianus, probably commemorating a fulfilled promise or prayer, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13302491.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Claudius Nic beneficiarius votum solvit
EDCS-13302491.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Claudius Nic, a beneficiarius, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-13302491.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Claudius Nic, a beneficiarius (a military officer), likely for an answered prayer, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13302492.translation: To Jupiter Depulsor, Titus Valerius Plares.
EDCS-13302492.summary: This is a brief dedication to Jupiter Depulsor (Jupiter the Averter of Evil) by Titus Valerius Plares, likely an altar or small monument, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13302493.text_edition: ] Saturninus Silvano votum libens animo solvit
EDCS-13302493.translation: [...] Saturninus willingly and with a grateful spirit fulfilled his vow to Silvanus.
EDCS-13302493.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Silvanus by Saturninus, likely in gratitude for a wish granted, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13302494.text_edition: Soli Mithrae P(ublius) Aelius Gordianus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-13302494.translation: To Sol Mithras, Publius Aelius Gordianus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-13302494.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Sol Mithras by Publius Aelius Gordianus, likely associated with a Mithraic cult site, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13302495.text_edition: Ius Ianus domo Faventia veteranus legionis XIII geminae vixit annos LX Ulpia Priscilla coniugi et Veratia Statuta filio bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-13302495.translation: Ius Ianus, from Faventia, a veteran of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, lived 60 years. Ulpia Priscilla, to her deserving husband, and Veratia Statuta, to her deserving son, placed this.
EDCS-13302495.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Ius Ianus, a veteran of Legio XIII Gemina, placed by his wife Ulpia Priscilla and his son Veratia Statuta, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13302526.summary: This is a military inscription, likely a dedication, by the Legio VII Claudia with an individual named Cuppis also mentioned.

EDCS-13302527.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Parthicus maximus Britannicus maximus pontifex maximus cohors II Nervia Brittonum |(miliaria) Antoniniana pia fidelis statuam posuit
EDCS-13302527.translation: The Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, greatest Parthicus, greatest Britannicus, pontifex maximus. The Second Nervian Cohort of Britons, 1000 strong, Antoniniana, pious, loyal, set up this statue.
EDCS-13302527.summary: This military dedication records the erection of a statue by the Cohors II Nervia Brittonum in honor of Emperor Caracalla (Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus), likely dating to his reign (early 3rd century AD).

EDCS-13302532.text_edition: Invicto Mythrae sic Potinus ex voto posuit
EDCS-13302532.translation: To the Unconquered Mithras, so Potinus set this up in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-13302532.summary: This inscription is a religious dedication to Mithras by Potinus, made in fulfillment of a vow, characteristic of Mithraic cult practices.

EDCS-13302533.translation: Marcus Cocceius Caupianus, prefect of the civitas of the Boii. Cocceia Dagouassa.
EDCS-13302533.summary: This inscription records the names of Marcus Cocceius Caupianus, a prefect of the civitas Boiorum, and Cocceia Dagouassa, likely indicating a family or official connection.

EDCS-13302758.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Conservatori pro salute Publi Aeli Antipatri sacerdotis arae Augusti sua suorumque omnium Onesimus  actor votum solvit
EDCS-13302758.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Conservator, for the well-being of Publius Aelius Antipater, priest of the Altar of Augustus, and all his household, Onesimus, his agent, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-13302758.summary: This is a religious dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Conservator, made by Onesimus, an agent, on behalf of Publius Aelius Antipater, a priest of the Altar of Augustus, for their collective well-being.

EDCS-13400086.summary: This inscription is a fragmentary dedication or an ownership mark referring to Emperor Nerva, dating to his reign (96-98 AD).

EDCS-13400172.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Sabasio pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti et Iuliae Augusti matris Augustae a deo iussus fecit Lucius Aurelius Marcianus aedilicius
EDCS-13400172.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Sabazius, for the well-being of Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus and Julia Augusta, mother of Augustus, by divine command, Lucius Aurelius Marcianus, an aedilician, made this.
EDCS-13400172.summary: This is a religious dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Sabazius, made by Lucius Aurelius Marcianus by divine command, for the health of Emperor Caracalla and Julia Domna (his mother), dating to Caracalla's reign.

EDCS-13400220.summary: This is a fragmentary religious dedication to Jupiter Sabazius, made by an individual named Aurelius, whose full name is partially lost.

EDCS-13500276.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus Felix Parthicus maximus pontifex maximus Britannicus maximus tribunicia potestate XVI imperator II consul IIII pater patriae proconsul fecit
EDCS-13500276.translation: Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus Felix, greatest Parthicus, pontifex maximus, greatest Britannicus, holder of tribunician power for the 16th time, imperator for the 2nd time, consul for the 4th time, father of the country, proconsul, made this.
EDCS-13500276.summary: This inscription details the extensive titles and accomplishments of Emperor Caracalla, indicating he commissioned or built the monument it adorned, and can be precisely dated to his 16th year of tribunician power (213-214 AD).

EDCS-13600270.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Terrae Matri pro salute Titi Aeli Mari flaminis coloniae conductoris pascui et salinarum Atticus actor eius votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-13600270.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Mother Earth, for the well-being of Titus Aelius Marius, flamen of the colony, contractor of pastures and salt works, Atticus, his agent, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-13600270.summary: This is a religious dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Terra Mater, made by Atticus, an agent, on behalf of Titus Aelius Marius, a prominent flamen and contractor, for their well-being.

EDCS-13600285.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Aelius Marcellinus beneficiarius consularis legionis V Macedonicae Gordianae agens Samo cum regione Ansamensium sub segno sic pro salute sua et suorum votum libens posuit  Arriano et Papo consulibus
EDCS-13600285.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Publius Aelius Marcellinus, beneficiarius consularis of the Fifth Macedonian Gordian Legion, acting at Samo with the region of the Ansamenses under the sign, for his own safety and that of his family, willingly placed this vow. In the consulship of Arrianus and Papus.
EDCS-13600285.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus made by a beneficiarius consularis for his and his family's safety, dating to the consulship of Arrianus and Papus (AD 243).

EDCS-13600286.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Scantius Lucius beneficiarius consularis agens curam stationis ex voto posuit
EDCS-13600286.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, Scantius Lucius, beneficiarius consularis, in charge of the station, placed this vow as promised.
EDCS-13600286.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, made by a beneficiarius consularis fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-13600287.text_edition: Deae Nemesi Scantius Lucius beneficiarius consularis agens curam stationis multis insidiis numinibus liberatus votum posuit Claudio Iuliano II et Bruttio Crispino consulibus
EDCS-13600287.translation: To the goddess Nemesis, Scantius Lucius, beneficiarius consularis, in charge of the station, having been freed from many traps by the deities, placed this vow. In the consulship of Claudius Julianus II and Bruttius Crispinus.
EDCS-13600287.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to the goddess Nemesis by a beneficiarius consularis, made in gratitude for deliverance from danger, dating to the consulship of Claudius Julianus II and Bruttius Crispinus (AD 224).

EDCS-13600288.text_edition: Deae Nemesi Reginae Oclatius Florentinus beneficiarius consularis legionis V Macedonicae Severianae iterato agens statione Agricola et Clementino consulibus
EDCS-13600288.translation: To the goddess Nemesis Regina, Oclatius Florentinus, beneficiarius consularis of the Fifth Macedonian Severian Legion, acting again at the station. In the consulship of Agricola and Clementinus.
EDCS-13600288.summary: This inscription is a renewed dedication to the goddess Nemesis Regina by a beneficiarius consularis, Oclatius Florentinus, dating to the consulship of Agricola and Clementinus (AD 230).

EDCS-13600289.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aurelius Titus vixit annos LX Aurelia Gemellina vixit annos XX Aurelius Cotes eques alae Flaviae faciendum curavit maritae pientissimae et socro
EDCS-13600289.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Aurelius Titus lived 60 years. Aurelia Gemellina lived 20 years. Aurelius Cotes, a cavalryman of the Ala Flavia, ensured this was made for his most devoted wife and his father-in-law.
EDCS-13600289.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Aurelius Cotes, a cavalryman, for his deceased wife Aurelia Gemellina and his father-in-law Aurelius Titus.

EDCS-13600442.text_edition: Dis Penatibus Laribus Militaribus Lari Viali Neptuno Saluti Fortunae Reduci Aesculapio Dianae Apollini Herculi Spei Favori Publius Catius Sabinus tribunus militum legionis XIII geminae votum libens solvit
EDCS-13600442.translation: To the Penates, Military Lares, Lar Vialis, Neptune, Salus, Fortuna Redux, Aesculapius, Diana, Apollo, Hercules, Spes, Favor: Publius Catius Sabinus, military tribune of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-13600442.summary: This inscription is a votive offering dedicated to a wide array of deities, made by Publius Catius Sabinus, a military tribune of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion.

EDCS-13600459.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Carvilius Statius votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-13600459.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Lucius Carvilius Statius willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-13600459.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, made by Lucius Carvilius Statius in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-13600460.translation: To Silvanus Domesticus, Julianus fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-13600460.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Silvanus Domesticus, made by Julianus in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-14800191.text_edition: Claudius Maximus et Ingenuius Superstes stateram publicam cum servatore liberto publico posuerunt locus datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-14800191.translation: Claudius Maximus and Ingenuius Superstes erected a public steelyard with a public freedman as its guardian. The site was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-14800191.summary: This inscription records the erection of a public steelyard (scale) and its freedman guardian by Claudius Maximus and Ingenuius Superstes, with the location approved by the municipal council.

EDCS-14800195.summary: This inscription is a fragmentary dedication to the goddess Epona Regina.

EDCS-14800196.text_edition: Eponae sanctae pro salute Cai Iulii Septimii Castini legati Augusti pro praetore III Daciarum Libella superiumentarius eius votum solvit
EDCS-14800196.translation: To holy Epona, for the well-being of Gaius Iulius Septimius Castinus, imperial legate with propraetorian power of the Three Dacias, Libella, his chief groom, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14800196.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the goddess Epona by Libella, a chief groom, for the health of his superior, Gaius Iulius Septimius Castinus, who was the imperial legate of Dacia, likely dating to the 3rd century AD.

EDCS-14800197.text_edition: Apollini Salutari pro salute filiorum sua coiiugis suorumque Caius Iulius Varianus
EDCS-14800197.translation: To Apollo Salutaris, for the well-being of his children, his wife, and his own, Gaius Iulius Varianus [dedicated this].
EDCS-14800197.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Apollo Salutaris by Gaius Iulius Varianus, seeking the well-being of his children, wife, and himself, likely a votive offering.

EDCS-15000026.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  Caius Iulius Priscus IIviralis muncipii Septimi Potaissensium ex visu libens posuit
EDCS-15000026.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Gaius Iulius Priscus, a duumvir of the municipality of Septimium Potaissa, willingly placed this in accordance with a vision.
EDCS-15000026.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Gaius Iulius Priscus, a duumvir of Potaissa, made in consequence of a divine vision, indicating his civic prominence in the community.

EDCS-15000027.summary: This inscription is a very brief personal name, likely part of a tombstone or an ownership mark, providing only the name Manius Servius Donatus.

EDCS-15000028.text_edition: Herculi Invicto sacrum Trosius Crispus |(centurio) legionis V Macedonicae piae constantis votum libens solvit merito
EDCS-15000028.translation: Sacred to Hercules Invictus, Trosius Crispus, centurion of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, Pious and Constant, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15000028.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Hercules Invictus by Trosius Crispus, a centurion of Legio V Macedonica, indicating a personal fulfillment of a vow by a military officer.

EDCS-15000065.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelius Dines visit sic annos LXXXV et posuit Aurelius Ditugentus filius et heres patri carissimo bene merenti
EDCS-15000065.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aelius Dines lived 85 years, and Aurelius Ditugentus, his son and heir, placed this for his dearest, well-deserving father.
EDCS-15000065.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument erected by Aurelius Ditugentus for his beloved father, Aelius Dines, recording his age at death (85 years).

EDCS-15000149.text_edition: Aeterno Caio Bet Galato ex voto
EDCS-15000149.translation: To the Eternal Gaius Bet Galatus, from a vow.
EDCS-15000149.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to 'the Eternal Gaius Bet Galatus,' likely a deified or revered individual, made in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-15000150.text_edition: Aeterno sanctissimo pientissimoque Caius Iulius Diocletianus equo publico decurio et augur coloniae Sarmizegetusae decurio coloniae Apulensium patronus causarum ex voto
EDCS-15000150.translation: To the Eternal, Most Holy, and Most Pious, Gaius Iulius Diocletianus, decurion with a public horse and augur of the colony of Sarmizegetusa, decurion of the colony of Apulum, patron of legal causes, [dedicated this] from a vow.
EDCS-15000150.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to an unnamed eternal, holy, and pious entity by Gaius Iulius Diocletianus, a prominent civic official holding multiple decurial and priestly positions in Roman Dacia, made in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-15100006.text_edition: Aesculapio et Hygiae Lucius Iulius Bassinus decurio coloniae Apulensis IIvir coloniae Napocensis flamen coloniae Drobetensis flamen municipii Diernensis decurio municipiorum Apulensis et Porolissensis tribunus legionis IIII Flaviae pro salute Iuliae Beronices coniugis votum libens solvit
EDCS-15100006.translation: To Aesculapius and Hygia, Lucius Iulius Bassinus, decurion of the colony of Apulum, duumvir of the colony of Napoca, flamen of the colony of Drobeta, flamen of the municipality of Dierna, decurion of the municipalities of Apulum and Porolissum, tribune of the Fourth Flavian Legion, for the well-being of his wife Iulia Beronice, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15100006.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Aesculapius and Hygia by Lucius Iulius Bassinus, a highly decorated Roman official holding numerous civic and military roles, for the health of his wife Iulia Beronice.

EDCS-15100131.text_edition: Herculenti Gorgias Posidonius ex visu posuit
EDCS-15100131.translation: To Herculens, Gorgias Posidonius placed this from a vision.
EDCS-15100131.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to the god Herculens by Gorgias Posidonius, indicating it was erected in response to a divine vision or dream.

EDCS-15100132.text_edition: Herculi Iulius Victor veteranus et Iulius Herculanus votum solverunt libenter
EDCS-15100132.translation: To Hercules, Julius Victor, a veteran, and Julius Herculanus willingly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-15100132.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Hercules by two individuals, Julius Victor and Julius Herculanus, likely on a small altar or tablet, indicating a fulfilled promise to the deity.

EDCS-15100133.summary: This is a fragmentary votive inscription dedicated to Hercules Augustus by Valerius Pontianus, likely part of an altar or monument, with much of the original text lost.

EDCS-15100134.summary: This extremely short inscription simply records the name Julius Quintus, likely from a small personal marker, a funerary context, or a brief dedication.

EDCS-15100135.text_edition: Isidi myrionimae sacrum Aelia Iusta pro Ver FirmNo#Firmo#FIRNO filio votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15100135.translation: Sacred to Isis of the myriad names, Aelia Iusta willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow for her son, Verus Firmus.
EDCS-15100135.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Isis by Aelia Iusta, made on behalf of her son Verus Firmus, indicating a grateful fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-15100136.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Depulsor, Avonius Saeclaris [dedicated this].
EDCS-15100136.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Depulsor (the averter of evil) by Avonius Saeclaris, likely on an altar or monument.

EDCS-15100139.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Aelius Onesimus Augustalis coloniae Apulense ex viso
EDCS-15100139.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Titus Aelius Onesimus, Augustalis of the colony of Apulum, [dedicated this] in accordance with a vision.
EDCS-15100139.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Titus Aelius Onesimus, a priest of the imperial cult (Augustalis) from the colony of Apulum, fulfilling a vow made after a divine vision.

EDCS-15200069.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription dedicated to the goddess Baltis Caelestis, likely a deity associated with a specific locality or cult, with the rest of the text missing.

EDCS-15300203.text_edition: Dianae Augustae Marcus Ulpius Respectus veteranus ex stratore consularis et Aelia Valentina votum libentes solverunt
EDCS-15300203.translation: To Diana Augusta, Marcus Ulpius Respectus, a veteran who was formerly a consular orderly, and Aelia Valentina willingly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-15300203.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Diana Augusta by Marcus Ulpius Respectus, a veteran and former orderly to a provincial governor, and Aelia Valentina, indicating a fulfilled vow likely on an altar or statue base.

EDCS-15300209.text_edition: QOuadruvis#Quadruvis#OUADRUVIS aram Cornelia Daphnis votum solvit libens animo meritoque
EDCS-15300209.translation: To Quadruvia, Cornelia Daphnis willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow, dedicating an altar.
EDCS-15300209.summary: This is a votive inscription, likely on an altar, dedicated to the goddess Quadruvia by Cornelia Daphnis, recording the grateful fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-15300210.text_edition: Apollini ex viso cohors I Brittonum |(miliaria) equitata pia fidelis civium Romanorum cui praeest Cludius
EDCS-15300210.translation: To Apollo, in accordance with a vision, the First Cohort of Britons, one thousand strong, cavalry, dutiful and loyal, of Roman citizens, commanded by Cludius, [dedicated this].
EDCS-15300210.summary: This is a votive dedication to Apollo, made in response to a vision, by the First Cohort of Britons, a Roman auxiliary military unit, indicating their piety and loyalty under the command of Cludius.

EDCS-15400014.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo LEIIP Atius Atianus posuerunt
EDCS-15400014.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. LEIIP Atius Atianus set this up.
EDCS-15400014.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Atius Atianus.

EDCS-15400015.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  Aticius Lig et ticius  V
EDCS-15400015.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Aticius Ligatus and Ticius V. (This inscription is incomplete or damaged.)
EDCS-15400015.summary: This fragmentary inscription appears to be a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aticius Ligatus and Ticius V.

EDCS-15400017.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Aurelius Ianuarius b(ene)f(iciarius) consularis voto libens posuit
EDCS-15400017.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Aurelius Ianuarius, a beneficiarius consularis, willingly placed this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-15400017.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aurelius Ianuarius, a beneficiarius consularis, likely dating to the imperial period when this rank was common.

EDCS-15400018.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Florius Maximus |(centurio) votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15400018.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Florius Maximus, a centurion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15400018.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Florius Maximus, a centurion, in fulfillment of a vow, probably from the imperial era.

EDCS-15400019.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Gaius Iulius Epigenes votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15400019.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Gaius Iulius Epigenes willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15400019.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Gaius Iulius Epigenes, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-15400020.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Imperatoris Luci Septimi Severi Pertenacis Augusti et Aureli Antonini Caesaris Marcus Iulius Quirinus Augustalis coloniae Apulensis ob honorem patronatus collegii Pontobithynorum ianuas et valuas ad introitum templi fecit
EDCS-15400020.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. For the well-being of the Emperor Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus and Aurelius Antoninus Caesar, Marcus Iulius Quirinus, Augustalis of the colony of Apulum, on account of the honor of the patronage of the college of Pontobithynians, made the doors and panels at the entrance of the temple.
EDCS-15400020.summary: This inscription records the dedication of temple doors and panels by Marcus Iulius Quirinus, an Augustalis of Apulum, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the well-being of Emperor Septimius Severus and Caracalla, likely dating to the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-15400021.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Pomponius Fuscus IIvir coloniae Apulensis votum libens solvit
EDCS-15400021.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Gaius Pomponius Fuscus, duumvir of the colony of Apulum, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15400021.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Gaius Pomponius Fuscus, a duumvir of the colony of Apulum, indicative of a municipal official.

EDCS-15400022.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute MMarcorum Ulpiorum   Iovi
EDCS-15400022.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. For the well-being of the Marci Ulpii... To Jupiter.
EDCS-15400022.summary: This fragmentary inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the health of several Marci Ulpii.

EDCS-15400023.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Ulpius Respectus veteranus legionis XIII geminae Severianae ex stratore consularis votum libens solvit
EDCS-15400023.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Marcus Ulpius Respectus, a veteran of the Thirteenth Gemina Severiana Legion, formerly a consular strator, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15400023.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marcus Ulpius Respectus, a veteran of Legio XIII Gemina and a former consular strator, indicating a high-ranking military dedicant from the Severan period (early 3rd century AD).

EDCS-15400025.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Iunoni Reginae Caius Iulius Iustus veteranus legionis XIII geminae et Caius Iulius Octavianus filius eius ex voto
EDCS-15400025.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and to Juno the Queen. Gaius Iulius Iustus, a veteran of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, and Gaius Iulius Octavianus, his son, did this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-15400025.summary: This inscription records a joint votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno Regina by Gaius Iulius Iustus, a veteran of Legio XIII Gemina, and his son Gaius Iulius Octavianus.

EDCS-15400028.text_edition: Deo Libero Patri Aurelius Renatus miles legionis XIII geminae voto libens posuit
EDCS-15400028.translation: To the god Liber Pater, Aurelius Renatus, soldier of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, willingly placed this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-15400028.summary: This is a votive inscription by a soldier of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, Aurelius Renatus, dedicating an offering to the god Liber Pater, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-15400029.summary: This is a brief dedication to Liber Pater by Gaius Iulius Chrestus, likely a votive inscription or a titulus recording ownership.

EDCS-15400030.text_edition: Iulius beneficiarius consularis donum dedit dedicavitque
EDCS-15400030.translation: Iulius, beneficiarius consularis, gave and dedicated this gift.
EDCS-15400030.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a gift by a beneficiarius consularis named Iulius.

EDCS-15400031.text_edition: Pro salute Augusti Magnae Deum Matri sanctum Titus Flavius Longinus veteranus ex decurione alae II nnoniorum decurio coloniae Dacicae decurio municipii Napocensis decurio kanabarum legionis XIII geminae et Claudia Candida coniunx et Flavii Longinus Clementina Marcellina filii ex imperio pecunia sua fecerunt locus datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-15400031.translation: For the well-being of the Augustus, Titus Flavius Longinus, a veteran, formerly a decurion of the Ala II Nnoniorum, a decurion of the Dacian colony, a decurion of the municipium Napocense, a decurion of the canabae of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, and his wife Claudia Candida, and their children Flavii Longinus, Clementina, Marcellina, made this by command and at their own expense for the Great Mother of the Gods, a sacred place given by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-15400031.summary: This elaborate inscription records the dedication of a sacred place to the Great Mother of the Gods for the emperor's well-being by Titus Flavius Longinus, a distinguished veteran with multiple decurial positions, his wife, and children, likely dating from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-15400033.text_edition: Soli Invicto Mythrae sic Caius Iulius Marcianus signifer legionis XIII geminae libens posuit
EDCS-15400033.translation: To Sol Invictus Mithras, Gaius Iulius Marcianus, standard-bearer of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, willingly placed this.
EDCS-15400033.summary: This is a votive inscription by Gaius Iulius Marcianus, a standard-bearer of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, dedicated to Sol Invictus Mithras, indicating participation in the Mithraic cult.

EDCS-15400036.summary: This is a simple votive inscription by Aelius Mestrius, indicating an offering or dedication he made willingly.

EDCS-15400037.text_edition: Marcus Ulpius Linus imaginifer legionis XIII geminae
EDCS-15400037.translation: Marcus Ulpius Linus, imaginifer of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina.
EDCS-15400037.summary: This inscription identifies Marcus Ulpius Linus as an imaginifer, a standard-bearer of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, likely from a dedication or a tombstone.

EDCS-15400038.summary: This fragment records a beneficiarius consularis, whose name ends in '-imus', fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-15400039.text_edition: Quadruviis Marcus Gallius Caminas decurio coloniae Sarmizegetusae cum Sabina eius  votum solvit
EDCS-15400039.translation: To the Quadruviae, Marcus Gallius Caminas, decurion of the colony of Sarmizegetusa, with his Sabina, fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-15400039.summary: This votive inscription records Marcus Gallius Caminas, a decurion of Sarmizegetusa, along with his wife Sabina, fulfilling a vow to the Quadruviae, likely a local or minor deity.

EDCS-15400040.text_edition: reginae Bersius Ingenuus  votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15400040.translation: --- to the queen, Bersius Ingenuus fulfilled a vow willingly, deservedly.
EDCS-15400040.summary: This fragment of an inscription records Bersius Ingenuus fulfilling a vow to an unspecified 'queen', likely a goddess, willingly and deservedly.

EDCS-15400043.text_edition: Deanae sacrum Titus Flavius Italicus primus IIIIvir municipii cum Statilia Lucia coniuge et Flavio Statiliano filio ex voto
EDCS-15400043.translation: Sacred to Diana. Titus Flavius Italicus, the first of the quattuorviri of the municipality, with Statilia Lucia, his wife, and Flavius Statilianus, his son, made this from a vow.
EDCS-15400043.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the goddess Diana by Titus Flavius Italicus, a municipal official, along with his wife and son, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-15400044.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Flavius Italicus primus IIIIvir municipii Aureli Apulensis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15400044.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Titus Flavius Italicus, the first of the quattuorviri of the municipality of Aurelius Apulensis, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15400044.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Titus Flavius Italicus, a prominent municipal official from Apulum.

EDCS-15400045.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo dis deabusque paternis Publius Aelius Stefanus votum libens merito posuit
EDCS-15400045.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, and to the ancestral gods and goddesses, Publius Aelius Stefanus willingly and deservedly placed this vow.
EDCS-15400045.summary: This inscription records a vow made by Publius Aelius Stefanus to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and his ancestral deities.

EDCS-15400047.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Soli Bussurigio Publius December Aelius voto posuit
EDCS-15400047.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Sol Bussurigius, Publius December Aelius placed this from a vow.
EDCS-15400047.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Publius December Aelius to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Sol Bussurigius, a syncretic deity.

EDCS-15400048.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Custodi Iunoni Minervae ceterisque dis deabusque Publius Aelius Antipater IIviralis coloniae Apulensis et Antonia Iulia eius et Aelii Antipater Iulianus Genialis decccuriones coloniae equo publico et Iulia filii eorum pro salute sua suorumque
EDCS-15400048.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Custos, Juno, Minerva, and the other gods and goddesses, Publius Aelius Antipater, a former duovir of the colony of Apulum, and Antonia Iulia, his wife, and Aelii Antipater Iulianus Genialis, decurions of the colony with public horse, and Iulia, their children, made this for their own health and that of their family.
EDCS-15400048.summary: This inscription records a dedication to a pantheon of gods by Publius Aelius Antipater, a former duovir of Apulum, his wife, and their children, for their well-being.

EDCS-15400049.text_edition: Iovi Victori Iovi Depulsori  Marcus Domestius Restitutus |(centurio) legionis XIII geminae X hastatus posterior votum solvit libens merito Commodo et Laterano X Kalendas Septembres
EDCS-15400049.translation: To Jupiter Victor, Jupiter Depulsor, Marcus Domestius Restitutus, centurion of the Thirteenth Gemina legion, tenth junior hastatus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow on the tenth day before the Kalends of September, in the consulship of Commodus and Lateranus.
EDCS-15400049.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled to Jupiter Victor and Jupiter Depulsor by Marcus Domestius Restitutus, a centurion of Legio XIII Gemina, dated to August 23, 186 CE.

EDCS-15400050.text_edition: LiLbero#Libero#LLBERO Patri Claudius Heraclides decurio votum libens merito
EDCS-15400050.translation: To Father Liber, Claudius Heraclides, a decurion, willingly and deservedly made this vow.
EDCS-15400050.summary: This inscription records a vow to Liber Pater by Claudius Heraclides, a decurion.

EDCS-15400051.text_edition: Deo Invicto Mithrae Titus Aurelius Fabia Marcus veteranus legionis XIII geminae dono dedit dedicavit
EDCS-15400051.translation: To the Unconquered God Mithras, Titus Aurelius Fabia Marcus, a veteran of the Thirteenth Gemina legion, gave and dedicated this as a gift.
EDCS-15400051.summary: This inscription records a dedication and gift to the Unconquered God Mithras by Titus Aurelius Fabia Marcus, a veteran of Legio XIII Gemina, for a Mithraic monument.

EDCS-15400052.text_edition: Deo Invicto Mithrae Titus Aurelius Fabia Marcus veteranus legionis XIII geminae
EDCS-15400052.translation: To the Unconquered God Mithras, Titus Aurelius Fabia Marcus, a veteran of the Thirteenth Gemina legion.
EDCS-15400052.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Unconquered God Mithras by Titus Aurelius Fabia Marcus, a veteran of Legio XIII Gemina.

EDCS-15400053.text_edition: Nemesi Reginae Caius Iulius Valens haruspex coloniae Apulensis et antistes huiusce loci somnio monitus libens posuit
EDCS-15400053.translation: To Nemesis Regina, Caius Iulius Valens, a haruspex of the colony of Apulum and priest of this place, willingly placed this, having been warned in a dream.
EDCS-15400053.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Nemesis Regina by Caius Iulius Valens, a haruspex and local priest from Apulum, who acted upon a dream.

EDCS-15400054.text_edition: Saturno Securo Marcus Herennius Faustus legatus Augusti
EDCS-15400054.translation: To Saturn Securus, Marcus Herennius Faustus, legate of Augustus.
EDCS-15400054.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Saturn Securus, offered by Marcus Herennius Faustus, a legate of Augustus, suggesting a votive or commemorative monument from the imperial period.

EDCS-15400055.translation: Julius, what he had vowed, to Silvanus.
EDCS-15400055.summary: This inscription records a fulfilled vow made by Julius to the god Silvanus, likely indicating a small votive offering.

EDCS-15400056.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Silvanus Domesticus by Aurelius, likely a votive offering for the god of the home or household.

EDCS-15400057.text_edition: Silvano Domestico Marcus Valerius Basssinus Iunior posuit
EDCS-15400057.translation: To Silvanus Domesticus, Marcus Valerius Basssinus Junior placed this.
EDCS-15400057.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Silvanus Domesticus, erected by Marcus Valerius Bassinus Junior, probably for a votive altar or shrine.

EDCS-15400058.text_edition: Deo Soli Lucius Valerius Felix votum solvit
EDCS-15400058.translation: To the god Sol, Lucius Valerius Felix fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15400058.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to the god Sol by Lucius Valerius Felix, suggesting a votive offering or altar dedicated to the sun god.

EDCS-15400059.text_edition: Marcus Aurelius Sila actarius peditum singularium pro se et suos sic votum libens solvit
EDCS-15400059.translation: Marcus Aurelius Sila, actuary of the singular infantry, for himself and his own, thus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15400059.summary: This inscription records a fulfilled vow by Marcus Aurelius Sila, an actuary of the singular infantry, made on behalf of himself and his family, likely for a votive monument.

EDCS-15500075.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publio Aelio Tertio domo Claudia Viruno veterano ex |(centurione) cohortis I Britannicae |(miliariae) civium Romanorum equitatae annorum LX Aelia Aestiva coniunx et heredes posuerunt
EDCS-15500075.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. For Publius Aelius Tertius, from the Claudian tribe, from Virunum, a veteran formerly a centurion of the First Britannic Cohort, a thousand strong, of Roman citizens, cavalry, aged 60. Aelia Aestiva, his wife, and his heirs placed this.
EDCS-15500075.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument dedicated by his wife Aelia Aestiva and his heirs to Publius Aelius Tertius, a veteran centurion of the First Britannic Cohort, who died at the age of 60.

EDCS-15500076.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valeriae Rufinae vixit annos XX et Longiniano filio eius annorum IIII Longinus beneficiarius consularis suis fecit
EDCS-15500076.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. For Valeria Rufina, who lived 20 years, and for her son Longinianus, aged 4 years. Longinus, a beneficiarius consularis, made this for his own.
EDCS-15500076.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument erected by Longinus, a beneficiarius consularis, for his wife Valeria Rufina, who lived 20 years, and their son Longinianus, who lived 4 years.

EDCS-15500084.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iustiniae Eutychiae vixit annos XXXXV Livius Rufus decurio coniugi posuit bene merenti posuit
EDCS-15500084.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. For Iustinia Eutychia, who lived 45 years. Livius Rufus, a decurion, placed this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-15500084.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument dedicated by Livius Rufus, a decurion, to his well-deserving wife, Iustinia Eutychia, who lived 45 years.

EDCS-15500085.text_edition: Aelia Nice vixit annis XC Aelius Victor vixit annis IIII Aelia Mammutio vixit annis XXII Aelius Vitalianus augur NMOO matri et filio neptiae sic bene merentibus
EDCS-15500085.translation: Aelia Nice lived 90 years. Aelius Victor lived 4 years. Aelia Mammutio lived 22 years. Aelius Vitalianus, augur NMOO, [placed this] for his well-deserving mother, son, and grandniece.
EDCS-15500085.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument erected by Aelius Vitalianus, an augur, for his mother Aelia Nice (aged 90), his son Aelius Victor (aged 4), and his grandniece Aelia Mammutio (aged 22).

EDCS-15500086.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Iulio Philippo Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate patri patriae consuli proconsuli res publica municipii Septimi Porolissensium
EDCS-15500086.translation: To the Imperator Caesar Marcus Iulius Philippus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power, father of the country, consul, proconsul, the Republic of the municipium Septimum Porolissensium [set this up].
EDCS-15500086.summary: This inscription is a public dedication by the city of Porolissum to Emperor Philip the Arab, likely celebrating his ascension or a specific benefaction around the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-15500087.text_edition: Dis Manibus Eufemus sic vikarius sic Peregrini vixit nnos XXXV Erastus conservo posuit
EDCS-15500087.translation: To the Shades of the Departed. Euphemus, likewise a deputy, likewise of Peregrinus, lived 35 years. Erastus placed this for his fellow slave.
EDCS-15500087.summary: This is a funerary inscription, set up by Erastus for his fellow slave Euphemus, who died at the age of 35.

EDCS-15500089.text_edition: Marco Iulio Severo Philippo nobilissimo Caesari nostro res publica municipii Septimii Porolissensium
EDCS-15500089.translation: To Marcus Iulius Severus Philippus, our most noble Caesar, the Republic of the municipium Septimum Porolissensium [set this up].
EDCS-15500089.summary: This inscription is a public dedication by the city of Porolissum to Philip II, the son of Philip the Arab, likely dating to the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-15500092.text_edition: Deae Suriae Aurelius Gaianus decurio municipii Porolissensis sacerdos
EDCS-15500092.translation: To the Goddess Syria, Aurelius Gaianus, decurion of the municipium Porolissensis, priest [set this up].
EDCS-15500092.summary: This is a religious dedication by Aurelius Gaianus, a decurion and priest from Porolissum, to the Syrian Goddess.

EDCS-15500093.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus Felix Parthicus maximus pontifex maximus Brittanicus maximus tribunicia potestate XVI imperator II consul IIII pater patriae proconsul fecit
EDCS-15500093.translation: Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus Felix Parthicus Maximus Brittanicus Maximus, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power for the sixteenth time, acclaimed Imperator for the second time, consul for the fourth time, father of the country, proconsul, made this.
EDCS-15500093.summary: This inscription records a construction or dedication made by Emperor Caracalla, detailing his full titulature and dating to approximately 213 AD, during his sixteenth year of tribunician power.

EDCS-15500094.text_edition: Silvano Domestico Iulius Aemilius Felix votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15500094.translation: To Silvanus Domesticus, Iulius Aemilius Felix willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15500094.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Silvanus Domesticus by Iulius Aemilius Felix, fulfilling a vow he made.

EDCS-15600955.translation: To [---] A, of Marcus [---], a vow [---]
EDCS-15600955.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a votive dedication involving a person named Marcus.

EDCS-15600958.text_edition: Tutor et Atticus Augustales coloniae votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-15600958.translation: Tutor and Atticus, Augustales of the colony, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-15600958.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Tutor and Atticus, who were Augustales (priests of the imperial cult) of a Roman colony, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-15600959.text_edition: s librarius legionis XIII geminae  tabula marmorea libus et ordinis  commanunculos suos o et Maximo consulibus
EDCS-15600959.translation: [---] a scribe of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, a marble tablet [---] and of the order [---] their small common places [---] and during the consulship of Maximus.
EDCS-15600959.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly recording a dedication or construction by a scribe of Legio XIII Gemina, mentioning the consulship of Maximus, likely dating it to a specific year when someone named Maximus was consul.

EDCS-15600960.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, indicating a connection to the Thirteenth Legion Gemina.

EDCS-15600966.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely part of a dedicatory text, with very little preserved information about its purpose or origin.

EDCS-15700116.text_edition: Marco Ulpio Apollinari praefecto castrorum legionis XIII geminae conscribti sic et cives Romani consistentes kannabis legionis eiusdem ex pecunia publica
EDCS-15700116.translation: To Marcus Ulpius Apollinaris, prefect of the camp of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion. Enrolled and Roman citizens residing in the military settlement of the same legion dedicated this from public money.
EDCS-15700116.summary: This inscription is a public dedication by Roman citizens residing in a military settlement to Marcus Ulpius Apollinaris, a camp prefect of Legio XIII Gemina.

EDCS-15800001.text_edition: Numini Aesculapi Caius Iulius Metrobianus  coloniae Sarmizegetusae sacerdos dei eiusdem pontifex quinquennalis IIvir coloniae Apuli ex voto
EDCS-15800001.translation: To the Divinity of Aesculapius. Gaius Iulius Metrobianus, priest of the same god in the colony of Sarmizegetusa, pontiff, quinquennial duumvir of the colony of Apulum, dedicated this out of a vow.
EDCS-15800001.summary: This inscription records a votive dedication to the god Aesculapius by Gaius Iulius Metrobianus, a prominent local official and priest from Sarmizegetusa and Apulum.

EDCS-15800002.text_edition: Numini Serapis  Gaius Iulius Metrobianus IIVIII coloniae Zarmizegetusae sacerdos dei Aesculapi ponTtifex#po]ntifex#TT quinquennalis IIvir coloniae Apuli
EDCS-15800002.translation: To the Divinity of Serapis. Gaius Iulius Metrobianus, duumvir of the colony of Sarmizegetusa, priest of the god Aesculapius, pontiff, quinquennial duumvir of the colony of Apulum.
EDCS-15800002.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the god Serapis by Gaius Iulius Metrobianus, who held several prominent religious and political offices in Sarmizegetusa and Apulum.

EDCS-15800003.text_edition: Numini Aesculapi et Hygiae Publius Aelius Genialis IIviralis coloniae Apulensis
EDCS-15800003.translation: To the Divinity of Aesculapius and Hygia. Publius Aelius Genialis, former duumvir of the colony of Apulum.
EDCS-15800003.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Aesculapius and Hygia by Publius Aelius Genialis, a former duumvir of the colony of Apulum.

EDCS-15800004.text_edition: Aesculapio et Hygiae Publius Aelius Rufinus decurio municipii et patronus collegii fabrum coloniae Apulensis pro salute sua et Ulpiae Helpidiae coniugis et Aeli Urbani fili ex iusso sic fecit porticum per pedes XXXX
EDCS-15800004.translation: To Aesculapius and Hygia. Publius Aelius Rufinus, decurion of the municipality and patron of the college of craftsmen of the colony of Apulum, for his own health and that of Ulpia Helpidia, his wife, and Aelius Urbanus, his son, made this portico, forty feet long, as commanded.
EDCS-15800004.summary: This inscription records the construction of a forty-foot portico by Publius Aelius Rufinus, a municipal decurion and patron, dedicated to Aesculapius and Hygia for the health of his family, in fulfillment of a divine command.

EDCS-15800005.text_edition: Aesculapio et Hygiae Publius Aelius Syrus Augustalis municipii Septimii Apulensis et Valeria Severa coniux et Aelia Syra filia ex voto porticum per pedes XXX fecerunt
EDCS-15800005.translation: To Aesculapius and Hygia. Publius Aelius Syrus, Augustalis of the municipality of Septimius Apulensis, and Valeria Severa, his wife, and Aelia Syra, his daughter, made this portico, thirty feet long, out of a vow.
EDCS-15800005.summary: This inscription records the construction of a thirty-foot portico by Publius Aelius Syrus, an Augustalis, along with his wife and daughter, as a votive offering to Aesculapius and Hygia.

EDCS-15800006.text_edition: Aesculapio et Hygiae sacrum Aelius Moschos votum solvit libens
EDCS-15800006.translation: Sacred to Aesculapius and Hygia. Aelius Moschos willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800006.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Aelius Moschos to Aesculapius and Hygia, indicating a fulfilled vow.

EDCS-15800007.text_edition: Numini Aesculapi et Hygiae pro salute dominorum nnostrorum Auggstorum Androclus ex dispensatore posuit
EDCS-15800007.translation: To the Divinity of Aesculapius and Hygia, for the health of our lords, the Augusti. Androclus, former dispenser, set this up.
EDCS-15800007.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Androclus, a former dispenser, to Aesculapius and Hygia for the well-being of the reigning emperors.

EDCS-15800008.text_edition: Deo Aesculapio et Hygiae Aurelius Aeternalis votum solvit
EDCS-15800008.translation: To the god Aesculapius and Hygia. Aurelius Aeternalis fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800008.summary: This inscription records a fulfilled vow by Aurelius Aeternalis to the gods Aesculapius and Hygia.

EDCS-15800009.text_edition: Aesculapio et Hygiae pro salute sua suorumque Carpion Augusti libertus tabularius provincciae Apulensis
EDCS-15800009.translation: To Aesculapius and Hygia, for his own health and that of his family, Carpion, freedman of Augustus, archivist of the province of Apulia.
EDCS-15800009.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Carpion, an imperial freedman and provincial archivist, to Aesculapius and Hygia for the well-being of himself and his household.

EDCS-15800010.text_edition: Aesculapio et Hygiae Tiberius Claudius Valerianus (centurio) legionis XIII geminae et legionis I adiutricis voto posuit
EDCS-15800010.translation: To Aesculapius and Hygia, Tiberius Claudius Valerianus, centurion of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion and of the First Adiutrix Legion, placed this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-15800010.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Tiberius Claudius Valerianus, a centurion who served in two different legions, to Aesculapius and Hygia.

EDCS-15800011.text_edition: Aesculapio et Hygiae Caius Pomponius Fuscus IIvir coloniae Apulensis votum libens solvit
EDCS-15800011.translation: To Aesculapius and Hygia, Gaius Pomponius Fuscus, duumvir of the colony of Apulia, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800011.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Aesculapius and Hygia by Gaius Pomponius Fuscus, a duumvir of the Apulian colony.

EDCS-15800012.text_edition: Aesculapio et Hygiae Lucius Septimius Nigrinus patronus collegii fabrorum coloniae Apulensis pro salute sua et suorum posuit
EDCS-15800012.translation: To Aesculapius and Hygia, Lucius Septimius Nigrinus, patron of the college of craftsmen of the colony of Apulia, placed this for his own health and that of his family.
