EDCS-15800012.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Aesculapius and Hygia by Lucius Septimius Nigrinus, a patron of a craftsmen's college, for his and his family's health.

EDCS-15800013.text_edition: Numini Aesculapi et Hygiae Marcus Ulpius Valens IIII viralis municipii Septimii Apulensis pro salute sua suorumque votum libens solvit
EDCS-15800013.translation: To the Divinity of Aesculapius and Hygia, Marcus Ulpius Valens, an ex-duumvir of the Septimian Apulian municipality, willingly fulfilled his vow for his own health and that of his family.
EDCS-15800013.summary: This inscription records Marcus Ulpius Valens, an ex-duumvir, fulfilling a vow to the divinities of Aesculapius and Hygia for his own and his family's health.

EDCS-15800015.text_edition: Auribus Aesculapi et Hygiae et Apollini et Dianae Cai Iulii Dius Farnax Irenicus fratres Augustales municipii Aureli Apuli pro salute sua posuerunt Praesente II et Condiano consulibus
EDCS-15800015.translation: To the ears of Aesculapius and Hygia and Apollo and Diana, Gaius Iulius Dius, Farnax, [and] Irenicus, brothers and Augustales of the Aurelian Apulian municipality, placed this for their own health, in the consulship of Praesens II and Condianus.
EDCS-15800015.summary: This inscription, dated to 217 AD, records a dedication by three brothers, Augustales of a local municipality, to multiple deities for their health, implying a communal or family offering.

EDCS-15800016.text_edition: Aesculapio et Hygiae ceterisque diis deabusque huiusque loci salutaribus Caius Iulius Frontonianus veteranus ex beneficiario consularis legionis V Macedonicae piae redditis sibi luminibus gratias agens ex viso pro se et Carteia Maxima coniuge et Iulia Frontina filia votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15800016.translation: To Aesculapius and Hygia and the other salutary gods and goddesses of this place, Gaius Iulius Frontonianus, a veteran who was an ex-beneficiary of a consular official of the Fifth Macedonian Legion 'Pia', having had his eyesight restored, gives thanks from a vision, for himself and Carteia Maxima, his wife, and Iulia Frontina, his daughter, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800016.summary: This inscription records a veteran, Gaius Iulius Frontonianus, fulfilling a vow to Aesculapius, Hygia, and local deities in gratitude for the restoration of his eyesight, dedicated for himself and his family.

EDCS-15800017.text_edition: Virtutibus deii Aeterni Lucius Aquila Ambrosius posuit
EDCS-15800017.translation: To the powers of the Eternal God, Lucius Aquila Ambrosius placed this.
EDCS-15800017.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Lucius Aquila Ambrosius to the powers of an 'Eternal God'.

EDCS-15800018.translation: To the Eternal God, Lucius Aquila Ambrosius.
EDCS-15800018.summary: This short inscription records a dedication to an 'Eternal God' by Lucius Aquila Ambrosius.

EDCS-15800019.text_edition: Numini Apollini Marcus Aurelius Marinus veteranus legionis XIII geminae ex voto posuit
EDCS-15800019.translation: To the divinity of Apollo, Marcus Aurelius Marinus, a veteran of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, placed this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-15800019.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Marcus Aurelius Marinus, a veteran of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, to the divinity of Apollo.

EDCS-15800020.text_edition: Ex iussu dei Apollinis fontem Aeterni Ulpius Proculinus speculator legionis XIII geminae Gordianae a solo restituit
EDCS-15800020.translation: By order of the god Apollo, Ulpius Proculinus, a scout of the Thirteenth Gemina Gordian legion, restored the spring of Aeternus from its foundation.
EDCS-15800020.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a spring dedicated to Aeternus by Ulpius Proculinus, a military scout, at the command of the god Apollo, likely dating to the Gordianic period (mid-3rd century AD).

EDCS-15800021.text_edition: Deo Apollini praestantissimo Aurelius Vitalis pro se suisque voto libens posuit
EDCS-15800021.translation: To the most excellent god Apollo, Aurelius Vitalis willingly fulfilled his vow for himself and his kin.
EDCS-15800021.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to the god Apollo by Aurelius Vitalis on behalf of himself and his family.

EDCS-15800022.text_edition: Numini Caelesti Marcianus Augggustorum nnnostrorum verna librarius
EDCS-15800022.translation: To the Celestial Divinity, Marcianus, a native slave and scribe of our Augusti.
EDCS-15800022.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Celestial Divinity by Marcianus, a slave and scribe serving the emperors, indicating imperial patronage.

EDCS-15800023.text_edition: Caelesti Augustae et Aesculapio Augusto et Genio Carthaginis et Genio Daciarum Olus sic Terentius Pudens Uttedianus legatus Auggustorum legionis XIII geminae legatus Auggustorum pro praetore provinciae Raetiae
EDCS-15800023.translation: To Celestial Augusta and to Aesculapius Augustus and to the Genius of Carthage and to the Genius of the Dacias, Olus, thus Terentius Pudens Uttedianus, legate of the Augusti, of the Thirteenth Gemina legion, legate of the Augusti pro praetore of the province of Raetia.
EDCS-15800023.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Olus Terentius Pudens Uttedianus, a high-ranking imperial legate, to a pantheon of deities including Celestial Augusta, Aesculapius Augustus, and the Genii of Carthage and the Dacias.

EDCS-15800024.translation: To Cautus, Gaius Herennius Hermes, by vow.
EDCS-15800024.summary: This inscription is a votive offering made by Gaius Herennius Hermes to Cautus, likely a local deity or a deified hero.

EDCS-15800025.text_edition: Daciis tribus et Genio legionis XIII geminae Marcus Caelius Iulianus tribunus laticlavius donum dedit
EDCS-15800025.translation: To the three Dacias and to the Genius of the Thirteenth Gemina legion, Marcus Caelius Iulianus, a tribunus laticlavius, gave this gift.
EDCS-15800025.summary: This inscription records a gift given by Marcus Caelius Iulianus, a military tribune, to the three Dacias (provinces) and the guardian spirit of the Thirteenth Gemina legion.

EDCS-15800028.text_edition: Dianae sacrum Quintus Paetinius Senilis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15800028.translation: Sacred to Diana, Quintus Paetinius Senilis willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800028.summary: This inscription is a votive tablet dedicated to Diana by Quintus Paetinius Senilis, who fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-15800029.text_edition: Dianae sacrum pro salute Auli Tapetii Antonini Tenax votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15800029.translation: Sacred to Diana, for the well-being of Aulus Tapetius Antoninus, Tenax willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800029.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to Diana made by Tenax for the health of Aulus Tapetius Antoninus.

EDCS-15800030.text_edition: Dianae sacrum COMMUN Veteranus ULP miles legionis XIII geminae et C filius
EDCS-15800030.translation: Sacred to Diana, COMMUN Veteranus, a soldier of the Thirteenth Gemina legion, and C, his son.
EDCS-15800030.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Diana by COMMUN Veteranus, a soldier of the Thirteenth Gemina legion, and his son C.

EDCS-15800031.text_edition: Dianae Augustae Aelius Iulianus Augustalis coloniae Apulensis votum libens solvit
EDCS-15800031.translation: To Diana Augusta, Aelius Iulianus, an Augustalis of the colony of Apulum, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800031.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to Diana Augusta by Aelius Iulianus, a priest of the imperial cult from the colony of Apulum.

EDCS-15800032.translation: Sacred to Diana Mellifica. Comatius Super.
EDCS-15800032.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Comatius Super to Diana Mellifica.

EDCS-15800033.text_edition: Dianae Reginae Caius Valerius Sarapio votum libens posuit
EDCS-15800033.translation: To Diana Regina, Caius Valerius Sarapio willingly placed this vow.
EDCS-15800033.summary: This inscription records a vow willingly made by Caius Valerius Sarapio to Diana Regina.

EDCS-15800034.text_edition: Domino Aeterno pro salute Imperatoris Nervae Traiani Caesaris Augusti Germanici Dacici Lucius Antonius Apollinaris veteranus legionis I adiutricis piae fidelis
EDCS-15800034.translation: To the Eternal Lord, for the safety of the Emperor Nerva Trajan Caesar Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, Lucius Antonius Apollinaris, a veteran of the First Adiutrix Pia Fidelis legion.
EDCS-15800034.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Lucius Antonius Apollinaris, a veteran, to an Eternal Lord for the well-being of Emperor Trajan, dating to between 102 and 117 AD.

EDCS-15800035.text_edition: Mestrius Martinus pictor constituit pro salute sua et suorum fanum Dominarum
EDCS-15800035.translation: Mestrius Martinus, a painter, established a shrine to the Dominas for his own safety and that of his family.
EDCS-15800035.summary: This inscription records the establishment of a shrine to the Dominas by the painter Mestrius Martinus for the safety of himself and his family.

EDCS-15800036.text_edition: Fortunae Augustae sacrum Publius Aelius Gemellus vir clarissimus perfecto a solo balneo consacravit
EDCS-15800036.translation: Sacred to Fortuna Augusta. Publius Aelius Gemellus, a man of senatorial rank, consecrated a bath completed from its foundations.
EDCS-15800036.summary: This inscription records the consecration of a newly built bath by Publius Aelius Gemellus, a man of senatorial rank, to Fortuna Augusta.

EDCS-15800037.text_edition: Fortunae Augustae sacrum Publius Calpurnius Proculus legatus Auggustorum pro praetore
EDCS-15800037.translation: Sacred to Fortuna Augusta. Publius Calpurnius Proculus, imperial legate with propraetorian power.
EDCS-15800037.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Fortuna Augusta by Publius Calpurnius Proculus, an imperial legate.

EDCS-15800038.text_edition: Fortunae Augustae sacrum et Genio canabensium Lucius Silius Maximus veteranus legionis I adiutricis piae fidelis magistrans primus in canabis donum dedit et Silia Ianuaria et Silius Firminus
EDCS-15800038.translation: Sacred to Fortuna Augusta and to the Genius of the canabenses. Lucius Silius Maximus, a veteran of the First Adiutrix Pia Fidelis legion, as the first magistrate in the canabae, gave this gift, and Silia Ianuaria and Silius Firminus (also gave it).
EDCS-15800038.summary: This inscription records a gift given to Fortuna Augusta and the Genius of the canabenses by Lucius Silius Maximus, a veteran and the first magistrate of the canabae, along with Silia Ianuaria and Silius Firminus.

EDCS-15800039.text_edition: Fortunae bonae domesticae sacrum Quintus Paetinius Senilis votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-15800039.translation: Sacred to good domestic Fortuna. Quintus Paetinius Senilis willingly, happily, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800039.summary: This inscription records Quintus Paetinius Senilis fulfilling a vow to good domestic Fortuna.

EDCS-15800040.text_edition: Fortunae Publicae Vitalis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15800040.translation: To Public Fortuna. Vitalis willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800040.summary: This inscription records Vitalis fulfilling a vow to Public Fortuna.

EDCS-15800041.text_edition: Fortunae Reduci Quintus Caecilius Laetus legatus Augusti legionis XIII geminae votum libens solvit
EDCS-15800041.translation: To Fortuna Redux. Quintus Caecilius Laetus, legate of Augustus for the Thirteenth Gemina legion, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800041.summary: This inscription records Quintus Caecilius Laetus, legate of the Legio XIII Gemina, willingly fulfilling a vow to Fortuna Redux.

EDCS-15800042.text_edition: Genio legionis XIII geminae Quintus Caecilius Laetus legatus legionis eiusdem votum libens solvit
EDCS-15800042.translation: Quintus Caecilius Laetus, legate of the Thirteenth Gemina legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the Genius of the same legion.
EDCS-15800042.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by a legionary legate, Quintus Caecilius Laetus, to the Genius of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, likely dating to the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-15800043.text_edition: Soli Invicto Quintus Caecilius Laetus legatus Augusti legionis XIII geminae votum libens solvit
EDCS-15800043.translation: Quintus Caecilius Laetus, imperial legate of the Thirteenth Gemina legion, willingly fulfilled his vow to Sol Invictus.
EDCS-15800043.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by the imperial legate Quintus Caecilius Laetus to Sol Invictus, likely dating to the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-15800045.translation: Tiberius Claudius Mansuetus, optio, to the Genius of the centuria.
EDCS-15800045.summary: This is a brief dedicatory inscription by an optio (a military officer's assistant) named Tiberius Claudius Mansuetus to the Genius of his centuria.

EDCS-15800046.text_edition: Pro salute Auggustorum Genio fabrum Marcus Aurelius Timon Augustalis coloniae Apulensis et magister collegii supra scripti pecunia sua fecit loco dato decurionum decreto
EDCS-15800046.translation: For the welfare of the Augusti, Marcus Aurelius Timon, Augustalis of the colony of Apulum and master of the above-written college of craftsmen, made this at his own expense in a place granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-15800046.summary: This inscription records the construction of a monument for the welfare of the emperors by Marcus Aurelius Timon, an Augustalis and master of the college of craftsmen, funded privately on land granted by the decurions of Colonia Apulensis, likely dating to the 3rd century AD due to the plural 'Augusti'.

EDCS-15800047.text_edition: Genio Imperatoris Gordiani Pii Felicis invicti Augusti Petronius Polianus vir clarissimus legatus legionis XIII geminae Gordianae legatus Augusti pro praetore provinciae Belgicae
EDCS-15800047.translation: To the Genius of Imperator Gordianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, Petronius Polianus, a most distinguished man, legate of the Thirteenth Gemina Gordiana legion, imperial legate pro praetore of the province of Belgica.
EDCS-15800047.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Genius of Emperor Gordian III, made by Petronius Polianus, a high-ranking official who was legate of the Thirteenth Gemina Gordiana legion and also imperial legate of Belgica, dating to the reign of Gordian III (238-244 AD).

EDCS-15800048.text_edition: Genio loci et Fortunae? magnae Q(uintus) Appianius? Maternus pro se et Marco Vettio Bellatori ex iusso sic libens merito posuit
EDCS-15800048.translation: Quintus Appianius Maternus, for himself and Marcus Vettius Bellator, by divine command, willingly and deservedly placed this to the Genius of the place and to Great Fortuna?
EDCS-15800048.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Genius Loci and possibly Fortuna by Quintus Appianius Maternus for himself and Marcus Vettius Bellator, fulfilling a divine command.

EDCS-15800049.text_edition: Genio praetorii huius Marcus Valerius Longinus vir clarissimus legatus legionis XIII geminae Severianae cum suis votum solvit
EDCS-15800049.translation: Marcus Valerius Longinus, a most distinguished man, legate of the Thirteenth Gemina Severiana legion, with his household, fulfilled his vow to the Genius of this praetorium.
EDCS-15800049.summary: This is a votive inscription by Marcus Valerius Longinus, legate of the Thirteenth Gemina Severiana legion, and his household, dedicated to the Genius of the praetorium, likely dating to the early 3rd century AD during the Severan dynasty.

EDCS-15800050.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Conservatori Marcus Valerius Longinus vir clarissimus legatus legionis XIII geminae Severianae cum suis ex voto
EDCS-15800050.translation: Marcus Valerius Longinus, a most distinguished man, legate of the Thirteenth Gemina Severiana legion, with his household, [dedicated this] by vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Conservator.
EDCS-15800050.summary: This is a votive inscription by Marcus Valerius Longinus, legate of the Thirteenth Gemina Severiana legion, and his household, dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Conservator, likely dating to the early 3rd century AD during the Severan dynasty.

EDCS-15800051.text_edition: Glyconi Marcus Antonius Onesas iusso dei libens posuit
EDCS-15800051.translation: Marcus Antonius Onesas willingly placed this to Glycon by command of the god.
EDCS-15800051.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Glycon, a snake-god, by Marcus Antonius Onesas, fulfilling a divine command.

EDCS-15800052.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Iunoni Reginae Titus Claudius Anicetus Augustalis coloniae Sarmizegetusae templum vetustate conlapsum a solo restituit
EDCS-15800052.translation: Titus Claudius Anicetus, Augustalis of the colony of Sarmizegetusa, restored from its foundations a temple that had collapsed due to old age, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno Regina.
EDCS-15800052.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a temple to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno Regina, which had collapsed from age, by Titus Claudius Anicetus, an Augustalis of Colonia Sarmizegetusa, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-15800053.text_edition: Glyconi Marcus Aurelius Theodotus iusso dei posuit
EDCS-15800053.translation: To Glycon, Marcus Aurelius Theodotus placed this by command of the god.
EDCS-15800053.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Marcus Aurelius Theodotus to Glycon, likely a deity, at the command of a god, probably in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-15800055.text_edition: Deo  Herculi Iulia Maximina voti sui compos
EDCS-15800055.translation: To the god Hercules, Iulia Maximina, having achieved her vow.
EDCS-15800055.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Iulia Maximina to the god Hercules, commemorating the fulfillment of a vow, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-15800056.text_edition: Herculi Augusto sacrum Aurelius Vetustinus votum solvit
EDCS-15800056.translation: Sacred to Hercules Augustus, Aurelius Vetustinus fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800056.summary: This inscription records Aurelius Vetustinus fulfilling a vow to Hercules Augustus, indicating a religious dedication typical of the Imperial Roman era.

EDCS-15800057.text_edition: Herculi conservatori Geminius Aelianus beneficiarius legati votum libens solvit
EDCS-15800057.translation: To Hercules the preserver, Geminius Aelianus, a beneficiarius of the legate, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800057.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Geminius Aelianus, a beneficiarius legati, to Hercules Conservator, reflecting military piety likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-15800058.text_edition: Herculi conservatori Marcus Lucilius Threptio donum dedit amicis
EDCS-15800058.translation: To Hercules the preserver, Marcus Lucilius Threptio gave this gift to his friends.
EDCS-15800058.summary: This inscription documents a gift dedicated to Hercules Conservator by Marcus Lucilius Threptio, seemingly for the benefit of his friends, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-15800059.text_edition: Herculi Invicto pro salute Cai Iuli Metrobiani et Aeliae Bonae coniugis Adamas ex voto
EDCS-15800059.translation: To Hercules the Unconquered, for the well-being of Gaius Iulius Metrobianus and Aelia Bona his wife, Adamas, according to his vow.
EDCS-15800059.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication by Adamas to Hercules Invictus for the health and safety of Gaius Iulius Metrobianus and his wife Aelia Bona, likely from the Imperial era.

EDCS-15800060.text_edition: Herculi Invicto pro salute Iuliorum Callistus eorum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15800060.translation: To Hercules the Unconquered, for the well-being of the Iulii, Callistus willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-15800060.summary: This inscription records Callistus fulfilling a vow on behalf of the Iulii to Hercules Invictus for their well-being, suggesting a dedication by a freedman or dependent, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-15800061.text_edition: Iunoni Reginae Caius Aelius Primus decurio coloniae
EDCS-15800061.translation: To Juno Regina, Gaius Aelius Primus, decurion of the colony.
EDCS-15800061.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Gaius Aelius Primus, a decurion of a Roman colony, to the goddess Juno Regina, typical of municipal elites in the Imperial period.

EDCS-15800062.text_edition: Iovi Maximo Crispius Saturninus beneficiarius legati voto libens posuit
EDCS-15800062.translation: To Jupiter Maximus, Crispius Saturninus, a beneficiarius of the legate, willingly placed this in accordance with his vow.
EDCS-15800062.summary: This inscription records a votive dedication by Crispius Saturninus, a beneficiarius of a legate, to Jupiter Maximus, indicating a military-religious context likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-15800064.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Bussumaro Atpatinius Rufi votum libens solvit merito
EDCS-15800064.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Bussumarus, Atpatinius Rufi willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800064.summary: This inscription documents Atpatinius Rufi fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Bussumarus, a local Celticized form of Jupiter, reflecting a dedication with provincial religious syncretism, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-15800065.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Aelius Gaius miles legionis XIII geminae votum solvit
EDCS-15800065.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Publius Aelius Gaius, soldier of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800065.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a soldier of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina.

EDCS-15800066.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Publi Aelii Caesidius et Iulianus cum operariis suis voto libentes posuerunt
EDCS-15800066.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Publius Aelius Caesidius and Iulianus, with their workers, willingly placed this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-15800066.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by two individuals and their workers, made willingly in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-15800067.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Aelius Fidus pro suis votum solvit
EDCS-15800067.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Lucius Aelius Fidus fulfilled his vow on behalf of his family.
EDCS-15800067.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Lucius Aelius Fidus on behalf of his family.

EDCS-15800068.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Avonius Tertullus veteranus legionis XIII geminae [Ale]xandr[ianae] votum solvit
EDCS-15800068.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Gaius Avonius Tertullus, a veteran of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina Alexandriana, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800068.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a veteran of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina Alexandriana, likely dating to the early 3rd century AD due to the honorific 'Alexandriana'.

EDCS-15800069.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Aurelius Crescentinus immunis legionis XIII geminae Antoninianae pro se et suos posuit
EDCS-15800069.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Marcus Aurelius Crescentinus, an exempt soldier of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina Antoniniana, placed this for himself and his family.
EDCS-15800069.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by an exempt soldier of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina Antoniniana for himself and his family, likely dating to the early 3rd century AD due to the honorific 'Antoniniana'.

EDCS-15800070.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo S--- Caecilius Saturninus et Iulius Rufus beneficiarii consulares votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-15800070.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, S[---] Caecilius Saturninus and Iulius Rufus, consular beneficiaries, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-15800070.summary: This inscription records the willing and deserved fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by two consular beneficiaries.

EDCS-15800071.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Sextus mmaps Clemens beneficiarius consularis
EDCS-15800071.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Sextus [---] Clemens, a consular beneficiary.
EDCS-15800071.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Sextus Clemens, a consular beneficiary.

EDCS-15800072.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Cornelius Primus veteranus legionis XIII geminae quot votum fecit pro se et suorumque posuit
EDCS-15800072.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Cornelius Primus, a veteran of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, placed this for himself and his family, just as he made a vow.
EDCS-15800072.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a veteran of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina for himself and his family, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-15800073.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Flavius Silvanus et Titus Flavius Iustinus milites legionis XIII geminae votum solverunt libentes merito Victorino consulari
EDCS-15800073.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Titus Flavius Silvanus and Titus Flavius Iustinus, soldiers of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow, with Victorinus as consul.
EDCS-15800073.summary: This inscription records the willing and deserved fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by two soldiers of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, dated to the consulship of Victorinus, likely in the 3rd century AD.

EDCS-15800074.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute imperii et collegii fabrum Tiberius Iulius Bubalus ex decuria XI donum dedit
EDCS-15800074.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, for the well-being of the empire and the guild of craftsmen, Tiberius Iulius Bubalus, from the eleventh decuria, gave this gift.
EDCS-15800074.summary: This inscription records a gift dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Tiberius Iulius Bubalus, a member of the eleventh decuria, for the well-being of the Roman Empire and the guild of craftsmen.

EDCS-15800075.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Iulius Celer Isauria |(centurio) legionis IIII Scythicae et XVI Flaviae firmae et XIII geminae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15800075.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Gaius Julius Celer from Isauria, centurion of the Fourth Scythian Legion, and of the Sixteenth Flavian Loyal, and of the Thirteenth Gemina, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800075.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Gaius Julius Celer, a centurion who served in three different legions, likely dating to the late 1st or early 2nd century AD.

EDCS-15800076.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iulius Primus |(centurio) legionis XIII geminae votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-15800076.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Julius Primus, centurion of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800076.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Julius Primus, a centurion of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, fulfilling a vow, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-15800077.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iustia Secundina ex voto posuit
EDCS-15800077.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Justia Secundina placed this according to her vow.
EDCS-15800077.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Justia Secundina to Jupiter Optimus Maximus in fulfillment of a vow, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800078.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marius Cerdo votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15800078.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Marius Cerdo willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800078.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marius Cerdo, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800079.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Omulius Crescens votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15800079.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Omulius Crescens willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800079.summary: This inscription records a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Omulius Crescens, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800080.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Marcus Postumius Tertius
EDCS-15800080.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Best and Greatest, Marcus Postumius Tertius (dedicated this).
EDCS-15800080.summary: This inscription is a sacred dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marcus Postumius Tertius, likely a private individual, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800081.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Sempronius Valentinus beneficiarius legati votum libens solvit
EDCS-15800081.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Sempronius Valentinus, beneficiarius of the legate, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800081.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Sempronius Valentinus, a beneficiarius (a privileged soldier serving as an assistant) of a legate, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-15800082.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Sentius Anicetus decurio coloniae Sarmizegetusae patronus collegii fabrum primus municipii Septimii Apulensis votum solvit libens merito Auggustis nnostris Imperatoribus Antonino II et [Geta] consulibus (ante diem) X Kalendas Iunias luna XVIII die Iovis
EDCS-15800082.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Gaius Sentius Anicetus, decurion of the colony of Sarmizegetusa, patron of the college of craftsmen, first man of the municipium Septimium Apulensis, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow. In the consulship of our August Emperors Antoninus II and Geta, ten days before the Kalends of June, on the 18th day of the moon, on a Thursday.
EDCS-15800082.summary: This inscription is a detailed votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Gaius Sentius Anicetus, a prominent municipal official, precisely dated to May 23, 209 AD, during the co-reign of Caracalla and Geta.

EDCS-15800083.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Septimius Pius miles legionis XIII geminae ex voto posuit
EDCS-15800083.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Septimius Pius, soldier of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, placed this according to his vow.
EDCS-15800083.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Septimius Pius, a soldier of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-15800084.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Terentius Iuvenalis NV
EDCS-15800084.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Terentius Juvenalis NV.
EDCS-15800084.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Terentius Juvenalis, with an unclear abbreviation at the end, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800085.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Gaius Valerius Sarapio a Carpis liberatus pro salute sua et suorum voto libens posuit
EDCS-15800085.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Gaius Valerius Sarapio, freed from the Carpi, willingly placed this vow for his own safety and that of his family.
EDCS-15800085.summary: This is a votive altar dedicated by Gaius Valerius Sarapio to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, giving thanks for his liberation from the Carpi and for the safety of himself and his family.

EDCS-15800086.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute CII  Caius Valerius Secundus donum dedit
EDCS-15800086.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the safety of CII (?). Gaius Valerius Secundus gave this gift.
EDCS-15800086.summary: This inscription records a gift dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Gaius Valerius Secundus, likely for the safety of someone or something abbreviated as CII.

EDCS-15800087.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Lucius Veratius Speratus beneficiarius praefecti legionis XIII geminae
EDCS-15800087.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Lucius Veratius Speratus, beneficiarius of the prefect of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion.
EDCS-15800087.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Lucius Veratius Speratus, a beneficiarius (an assistant or privileged soldier) of the prefect of Legio XIII Gemina, likely on a votive altar or monument.

EDCS-15800088.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo ex visu Sex(us) Virius - f(ilius) Postumus votum solvit
EDCS-15800088.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, based on a vision. Sextus Virius Postumus, son of (?), fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800088.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Sextus Virius Postumus, inspired by a vision, suggesting a votive monument.

EDCS-15800089.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Victorinus  legionis XIII geminae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15800089.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Victorinus of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800089.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Victorinus, a soldier of Legio XIII Gemina, willingly fulfilling his vow.

EDCS-15800090.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo T AMUS Vindex benef(iciarius) consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15800090.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. T. AMUS Vindex, a consular beneficiarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800090.summary: This inscription records a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by T. AMUS Vindex, a beneficiarius serving a provincial governor (consular), who willingly fulfilled his vow.

EDCS-15800091.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo ceterisque diis deabusque immortalibus Titus Iulius Gaianus decurio coloniae Sarmizegetusae pro salute sua suorumque omnium posuit
EDCS-15800091.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and all other immortal gods and goddesses. Titus Iulius Gaianus, decurion of the colony of Sarmizegetusa, placed this for his own safety and that of all his family.
EDCS-15800091.summary: This is a dedication by Titus Iulius Gaianus, a decurion of the colony of Sarmizegetusa, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and other immortal deities, placed for the safety of himself and his entire family.

EDCS-15800092.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et consessui deorum dearumque pro salute imperii Romani et virtute legionis XIII geminae sub Marco Statio Prisco consule designato demonstrantibus ipsis aquas aperiendas per Lucium Aurelium Trophimum ponentem signum Iovis et aram pecunia sua fecit
EDCS-15800092.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the assembly of gods and goddesses, for the safety of the Roman Empire and the valor of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, under Marcus Statius Priscus, consul designate, with the gods themselves indicating that waters should be opened through Lucius Aurelius Trophimus, who placed the statue of Jupiter and built the altar with his own money.
EDCS-15800092.summary: This complex inscription records the dedication of an altar and a statue of Jupiter by Lucius Aurelius Trophimus, made at his own expense for the safety of the Roman Empire and Legio XIII Gemina, during the consulship of Marcus Statius Priscus, with the gods themselves indicating the opening of waters, likely for a public utility or sacred spring.

EDCS-15800093.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo ceteris dis deabusque us Pus(?) legatus Augusti legionis XIII geminae votum libens solvit
EDCS-15800093.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the other gods and goddesses. [---]us Pus (?), legate of Augustus of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800093.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and other deities by a legate of Augustus from Legio XIII Gemina, whose name is partially missing, willingly fulfilling his vow.

EDCS-15800094.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et ceteris diis deabusque immortalibus et Daciae pro salute domini nostri Marci Aurelii Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti nostri Caius Aurelius Sigillius tribunus legionis XIII geminae Antoninianae pridie Nonas Apriles Laeto II et Cerialae consulibus
EDCS-15800094.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and all other immortal gods and goddesses, and to Dacia, for the safety of our lord Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, our emperor. Gaius Aurelius Sigillius, tribune of the Thirteenth Gemina Antoniniana Legion, on the day before the Nones of April, in the consulship of Laetus II and Cerialis.
EDCS-15800094.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, other immortal gods, and Dacia, made by Gaius Aurelius Sigillius, a tribune of Legio XIII Gemina Antoniniana, for the safety of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (Caracalla), dated to April 4, 215 AD.

EDCS-15800095.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo dis et deabus Tiberius Claudius Rufus flamen coloniae
EDCS-15800095.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, and to the gods and goddesses, Tiberius Claudius Rufus, flamen of the colony.
EDCS-15800095.summary: This is a religious dedication to Jupiter and other deities by Tiberius Claudius Rufus, a flamen of a colony.

EDCS-15800096.text_edition: Libero Patri Tiberius Claudius Rufus decurio coloniae et flamen municipii Apulensis
EDCS-15800096.translation: To Liber Pater, Tiberius Claudius Rufus, decurion of the colony and flamen of the municipium of Apulum.
EDCS-15800096.summary: This is a religious dedication to Liber Pater by Tiberius Claudius Rufus, a decurion and flamen from the community of Apulum.

EDCS-15800097.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo ceterisque diis Caius? Pescennius Niger? consularis
EDCS-15800097.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and to the other gods, Caius? Pescennius Niger?, consular.
EDCS-15800097.summary: This is a religious dedication to Jupiter and other gods by Caius Pescennius Niger, a person of consular rank.

EDCS-15800098.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Pomponius Victorinus veteranus ex duplario legionis XIII geminae voto libens posuit
EDCS-15800098.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, and to Juno, Pomponius Victorinus, veteran from a duplarius of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, willingly placed this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-15800098.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter and Juno by Pomponius Victorinus, a veteran of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina.

EDCS-15800099.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae Titus Aurelius Narcissus negotiator pro se et suorum libens merito votum solvit
EDCS-15800099.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, to Juno Regina, Titus Aurelius Narcissus, merchant, for himself and his family, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800099.summary: This is a religious dedication to Jupiter and Juno Regina, made as a fulfilled vow by Titus Aurelius Narcissus, a merchant, on behalf of himself and his family.

EDCS-15800100.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Iunoni Reginae pro salute Imperatoris Marci Aureli Antonini Pii Augusti et Iuliae Augustae matris Augusti Marcus Ulpius Mucianus miles legionis XIII geminae horologiarium templum a solo ex voto fecit Falcone et Claro consulibus
EDCS-15800100.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and to Juno Regina, for the well-being of the Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus and Julia Augusta, mother of the Augustus, Marcus Ulpius Mucianus, soldier of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, built a sundial temple from its foundations, in fulfillment of a vow, during the consulship of Falcon and Claros.
EDCS-15800100.summary: This inscription records the construction of a sundial temple from its foundations by Marcus Ulpius Mucianus, a soldier of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, dedicated to Jupiter and Juno for the emperor and his mother, and is dated to the consulship of Falcon and Claros (likely 239 CE).

EDCS-15800101.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  Iunoni Reginae Minervae Lucius Annius Italicus Honoratus legatus Augusti legionis XIII geminae Antoninianae praefectus aerarii militaris sodalis Hadrianalis cum Gavidia Torquata sua et Anniis Italico et Honorato et Italica filiis
EDCS-15800101.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, to Juno Regina, to Minerva, Lucius Annius Italicus Honoratus, imperial legate of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina Antoniniana, prefect of the military treasury, Hadrianic sodalis, with his wife Gavidia Torquata and his children Annius Italicus, Annius Honoratus, and Annia Italica.
EDCS-15800101.summary: This is a religious dedication to Jupiter, Juno Regina, and Minerva by Lucius Annius Italicus Honoratus, a high-ranking official including legate and prefect, together with his wife and children.

EDCS-15800102.text_edition: Victoriae Antonini Augusti Lucius Annius Italicus Honoratus legatus Augusti legionis XIII geminae Antoninianae praefectus aerarii militaris sodalis Hadrianali cum Gavidia Torquata sua et Anniis Italico et Honorato et Italica filiis
EDCS-15800102.translation: To the Victory of Antoninus Augustus, Lucius Annius Italicus Honoratus, imperial legate of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina Antoniniana, prefect of the military treasury, Hadrianic sodalis, with his wife Gavidia Torquata and his children Annius Italicus, Annius Honoratus, and Annia Italica.
EDCS-15800102.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Victory of Emperor Antoninus Augustus by Lucius Annius Italicus Honoratus, a prominent military and financial official, together with his family.

EDCS-15800104.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Caerellius Sabinus legatus Augusti legionis XIII geminae et Fufidia Pollitta eius voto
EDCS-15800104.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Caius Caerellius Sabinus, imperial legate of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, and his wife Fufidia Pollitta, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-15800104.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter by Caius Caerellius Sabinus, an imperial legate of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, and his wife Fufidia Pollitta.

EDCS-15800105.text_edition: Iunoni Reginae Populoniae deae patriae Caius Caerellius Sabinus legatus Augusti legionis XIII geminae et Fufidia Pollitta eius voto
EDCS-15800105.translation: To Juno Regina of Populonia, the goddess of the homeland, Caius Caerellius Sabinus, imperial legate of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, and his wife Fufidia Pollitta, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-15800105.summary: This is a votive dedication to Juno Regina of Populonia, the goddess of the homeland, by Caius Caerellius Sabinus, an imperial legate, and his wife Fufidia Pollitta.

EDCS-15800106.text_edition: Minervae Iovis consiliorum participi Caius Caerellius Sabinus legatus Augusti legionis XIII geminae et Fufidia Pollitta eius voto
EDCS-15800106.translation: To Minerva, sharer in the counsels of Jupiter, Gaius Caerellius Sabinus, legate of Augustus for the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, and Fufidia Pollitta, his wife, in fulfillment of their vow.
EDCS-15800106.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Minerva by a legionary legate and his wife, likely from the late 1st or early 2nd century AD.

EDCS-15800107.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae Minervae Quintus Cornelius Silvanus |(centurio) legionis V Macedonicae piae constantis votum solvit
EDCS-15800107.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, to Juno Queen, to Minerva, Quintus Cornelius Silvanus, centurion of the Fifth Legion Macedonica, pious and constant, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800107.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to the Capitoline Triad by a centurion of the Fifth Legion Macedonica.

EDCS-15800108.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae Minervae veterani legionis XIII geminae missi honesta missione per Iulium Bassum legatum Augusti pro praetore Idibus Decembribus Pontiano et Atiliano consulibus milites facti
EDCS-15800108.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, to Juno Queen, to Minerva, the veterans of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, discharged with honorable service by Julius Bassus, legate of Augustus with propraetorian power, on the Ides of December, with Pontianus and Atilianus as consuls, having become soldiers.
EDCS-15800108.summary: This inscription records a dedication by veterans of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina to the Capitoline Triad, celebrating their honorable discharge and their enrollment as soldiers, dated to December 13th, 224 AD based on the consuls.

EDCS-15800109.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Minervae et Aesculapio domino Septimius Asclepius Hermes libertus numinis Aesculapi habens ornamenta decurionalia coloniae Apulensis et Augustalis coloniae eiusdem voto posuit
EDCS-15800109.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, to Juno, to Minerva, and to Aesculapius the lord, Septimius Asclepius Hermes, freedman of the divinity Aesculapius, holding the ornaments of a decurion of the colony of Apulum and an Augustalis of the same colony, placed this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-15800109.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Capitoline Triad and Aesculapius by a freedman who held prominent local civic and religious offices in Apulum.

EDCS-15800110.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Marti Lucius Arrius Probus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15800110.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and to Mars, Lucius Arrius Probus, a beneficiarius consularis, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800110.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter and Mars by a beneficiarius consularis.

EDCS-15800111.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Dis Penatibus Scaurianus
EDCS-15800111.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and to the Household Gods, Scaurianus [made this dedication].
EDCS-15800111.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Penates by an individual named Scaurianus.

EDCS-15800112.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Aeterno  Titus Flavius Flavianus Augustalis municipii Septimii Apulensis et decurio collegii fabrum una cum Aelia Vindia coniuge sua quod pro se et Flaviis Restuto Flavino Ianuario Flaviano filiis suis vovetant votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-15800112.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the Eternal, Titus Flavius Flavianus, Augustalis of the municipium Septimium Apulense and decurion of the guild of craftsmen, together with Aelia Vindia, his wife, willingly and deservedly fulfilled the vow they had made for themselves and for their sons Flavius Restutus, Flavius Flavinus, Flavius Ianuarius, and Flavius Flavianus.
EDCS-15800112.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Aeternus by a civic and guild official and his wife, made on behalf of themselves and their four sons.

EDCS-15800113.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Aeterno Caius Iulius Valentinus IIIIvir primus annualis municipii Septimii Apulensis et patronus collegii fabrum municipii supra scripti ex voto posuit
EDCS-15800113.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the Eternal, Gaius Julius Valentinus, first annual quattuorvir of the municipium Septimium Apulense and patron of the guild of craftsmen of the aforementioned municipium, placed this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-15800113.summary: This inscription records a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Aeternus by a prominent local magistrate and patron of a craftsmen's guild in the municipium Septimium Apulense.

EDCS-15800114.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Conservatori Lucius Antonius Secundus Augustalis coloniae Apulensis in his aedibus natus cum Celsina filia votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15800114.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the Preserver, Lucius Antonius Secundus, Augustalis of the colony of Apulum, born in this house, with his daughter Celsina, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800114.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Conservator by a local Augustalis and his daughter, possibly made at his birthplace in Apulum.

EDCS-15800115.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Conservatori Callistus Augusti nostri verna dispensator et Cornelia eius pro se et suis
EDCS-15800115.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the Preserver, Callistus, our emperor's home-born slave and dispenser, and Cornelia, his wife, for themselves and their family.
EDCS-15800115.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Conservator by an imperial slave holding the position of dispensator and his wife, made on behalf of their household.

EDCS-15800116.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo divo fulgurali sacrum Iuliano et Crispino consulibus   consularis
EDCS-15800116.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, divine thunderer, sacred. In the consulship of Julianus and Crispinus. A man of consular rank.
EDCS-15800116.summary: This is a sacred dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a consular official, dated to the consulship of Julianus and Crispinus (likely 224 or 227 CE).

EDCS-15800117.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Statori Caius Valerius Valerianus ex visu posuit
EDCS-15800117.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Stator. Gaius Valerius Valerianus placed this according to a vision.
EDCS-15800117.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Stator by Gaius Valerius Valerianus, made in response to a divine vision.

EDCS-15800118.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Taviano et dis deabusque pro salute et victoria domini nostri sanctissimi Tavianus Augusti libertus sub procurator aurariarum votum solvit animo libenter
EDCS-15800118.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Taviano and to all gods and goddesses, for the health and victory of our most sacred lord, Augustus. Tavanius, a freedman of Augustus, assistant procurator of the gold mines, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800118.summary: This inscription is a vow made by Tavanius, an Augustan freedman and assistant procurator of the gold mines, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Taviano and all gods and goddesses for the health and victory of the emperor Augustus.

EDCS-15800119.text_edition: Iovi Statori Quintus Aburnius Caedicianus legatus Augusti
EDCS-15800119.translation: To Jupiter Stator. Quintus Aburnius Caedicianus, legate of Augustus.
EDCS-15800119.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Stator by Quintus Aburnius Caedicianus, a legate of Augustus.

EDCS-15800121.text_edition: Libero Patri Publius Aelius Fronto veteranus ex beneficiario consularis et Aelia Frontonia coniux eius pro salute sua suorumque omnium
EDCS-15800121.translation: To Liber Pater. Publius Aelius Fronto, a veteran, formerly a beneficiary of a consular, and Aelia Frontonia, his wife, for their own health and that of all their kin.
EDCS-15800121.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Liber Pater by Publius Aelius Fronto, a veteran and former beneficiary of a consular, and his wife Aelia Frontonia, made for the health of themselves and their family.

EDCS-15800122.text_edition: Libero Patri sacrum pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Commodi Antonini Augusti Pii patris patriae Lucius Calvisius Luci filius Velina Secundus Falerione primus pilus legionis XIII geminae donum dedit sub Vespronio Candido cons(sulari) dedicante Caerellio Sabino legato
EDCS-15800122.translation: Sacred to Liber Pater, for the health of Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Commodus Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of his country. Lucius Calvisius, son of Lucius, of the Velina tribe, Secundus, from Falerii, primus pilus of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, gave this gift under Vespronius Candidus, consular, with Caerellius Sabinus, legate, dedicating it.
EDCS-15800122.summary: This is a sacred dedication to Liber Pater by Lucius Calvisius Secundus, a primus pilus of the Legio XIII Gemina, made for the health of Emperor Commodus, and overseen by consular Vespronius Candidus and legate Caerellius Sabinus.

EDCS-15800123.text_edition: Libero Patri et Liberae Claudius Atteius Celer veteranus legionis XIII geminae INI decurio canabensium cum suis votum libens solvit loco dato decurionum decreto
EDCS-15800123.translation: To Liber Pater and Libera. Claudius Atteius Celer, a veteran of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, decurion of the canabenses, with his family, willingly fulfilled his vow, in a place granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-15800123.summary: This inscription records a vow to Liber Pater and Libera by Claudius Atteius Celer, a veteran of Legio XIII Gemina and a decurion of the canabenses, which he fulfilled with his family on land granted by the decurions.

EDCS-15800124.text_edition: Libero Patri et Liberae Caius Maximius Iulianus optio praetorii legionis V Macedonicae visu monitus pro salute sua et suorum libens posuit
EDCS-15800124.translation: To Liber Pater and Libera. Gaius Maximius Iulianus, optio of the praetorium of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, admonished by a vision, willingly placed this for his own health and that of his family.
EDCS-15800124.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Liber Pater and Libera by Gaius Maximius Iulianus, an optio of Legio V Macedonica, made in response to a vision for his and his family's health.

EDCS-15800125.text_edition: Libero Patri et Liberae  ius tus CF --- eq(uo) publico  Marcus Aurelius Comatius Super antistes
EDCS-15800125.translation: To Liber Pater and Libera. [---]ius Tus, C.F. [---] with public horse. Marcus Aurelius Comatius Super, priest.
EDCS-15800125.summary: This is a dedication to Liber Pater and Libera, involving an individual with a public horse and Marcus Aurelius Comatius Super, a priest.

EDCS-15800126.text_edition: Pro salute sua et suorum Marcus Aurelius Comatius Super decurio antistes Marcus Comatius Exsuperatus Petronia Celerina mater Herennia Heuresis eius filiorum Superiani Exsuperantiani Superstitis Superes sic cryptam cum porticibus et apparatorio et exedra pecunia sua fecerunt libenter
EDCS-15800126.translation: For their own health and that of their kin, Marcus Aurelius Comatius Super, decurion and priest, Marcus Comatius Exsuperatus, Petronia Celerina, mother, Herennia Heuresis, his daughter, Superianus, Exsuperantianus, Superstes, Superes, thus willingly made this crypt with porticoes and an antechamber and exedra with their own money.
EDCS-15800126.summary: This inscription records the construction of a crypt with associated structures by Marcus Aurelius Comatius Super (a decurion and priest), Marcus Comatius Exsuperatus, Petronia Celerina, Herennia Heuresis, and their children, built with their own funds for the health of themselves and their family.

EDCS-15800127.text_edition: Lunae Luciferae Caius Iulius Varianus magister donum dedit
EDCS-15800127.translation: To Luna Lucifera, Gaius Iulius Varianus, a master, gave this gift.
EDCS-15800127.summary: This inscription records a gift dedicated to the goddess Luna Lucifera by Gaius Iulius Varianus, who held the title of master.

EDCS-15800128.text_edition: Marti et Victoriae Publius Aelius Crescens duplicarius posuit
EDCS-15800128.translation: To Mars and Victoria, Publius Aelius Crescens, a duplicarius, set this up.
EDCS-15800128.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Mars and Victoria by Publius Aelius Crescens, who was a duplicarius (a soldier receiving double pay).

EDCS-15800129.text_edition: Marti et Victoriae Publius Aelius Crescens duplicarius posuit
EDCS-15800129.translation: To Mars and Victoria, Publius Aelius Crescens, a duplicarius, set this up.
EDCS-15800129.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Mars and Victoria by Publius Aelius Crescens, who was a duplicarius (a soldier receiving double pay).

EDCS-15800130.text_edition: Pro salute Auggustorum Marti conservatori Publius Aelius Rufinus quod adiutor officii rationum vovit cornicularius praefecti kastrorum legionis XIII geminae voto libens posuit
EDCS-15800130.translation: For the health of the Augusti, to Mars the preserver, Publius Aelius Rufinus, assistant in the office of accounts and cornicularius of the camp prefect of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, gladly fulfilled his vow as he had promised.
EDCS-15800130.summary: This inscription records a votive dedication to Mars Conservator for the health of the emperors, made by Publius Aelius Rufinus, a military administrator in the Thirteenth Gemina Legion.

EDCS-15800131.text_edition: Magnae Deum Matri sacrum pro se et suis Gaius Statius Pollio veteranus legionis XIII geminae
EDCS-15800131.translation: Sacred to the Great Mother of the Gods, for himself and his family, Gaius Statius Pollio, a veteran of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion.
EDCS-15800131.summary: This inscription records a sacred dedication to the Magna Mater by Gaius Statius Pollio, a veteran of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, on behalf of himself and his family.

EDCS-15800132.text_edition: Magnae Deum Matri Umbricia Maxima pro se suisque votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15800132.translation: To the Great Mother of the Gods, Umbricia Maxima willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow for herself and her family.
EDCS-15800132.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the Magna Mater by Umbricia Maxima, on behalf of herself and her family, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-15800133.text_edition: Invicto Mythirae sic Chrestion votum solvit
EDCS-15800133.translation: To the Unconquered Mithras, Chrestion thus fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800133.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the god Mithras, made by Chrestion, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-15800134.text_edition: Deo Mercurio Caius -imius Veranus voto libens posuit
EDCS-15800134.translation: To the god Mercury, Gaius [-]imius Veranus gladly set this up in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-15800134.summary: This inscription records a votive dedication to the god Mercury by Gaius [-]imius Veranus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-15800135.text_edition: Minervae Publi Aeli Antipater Iulianus Genialis equo publico decuriones coloniae Apuli Q
EDCS-15800135.translation: To Minerva, Publius Aelius Antipater, Iulianus, Genialis, with a public horse, decurions of the colony of Apulum, Q[...].
EDCS-15800135.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Minerva by several decurions of the colonia Apulum, specifically Publius Aelius Antipater, Iulianus, and Genialis, who held a public horse.

EDCS-15800136.text_edition: Minervae Tiberius Iulius Novianus librarius legati posuit
EDCS-15800136.translation: To Minerva, Tiberius Iulius Novianus, a scribe of the legate, set this up.
EDCS-15800136.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Minerva by Tiberius Iulius Novianus, who served as a scribe for a legate.

EDCS-15800137.text_edition: Minervae sanctae Celerinius Valerianus cornicularius consularis
EDCS-15800137.translation: To holy Minerva, Celerinius Valerianus, a consular cornicularius (aide).
EDCS-15800137.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Minerva by Celerinius Valerianus, a military administrative officer, likely dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-15800139.text_edition: Deo Soli Hieribolo Aurelius Bassinus decurio coloniae Aequensis sacerdos numinum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15800139.translation: To the god Sol Hieribolus, Aurelius Bassinus, a decurion of the colony of Aequum and priest of the deities, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800139.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Sol Hieribolus by Aurelius Bassinus, a municipal official and priest, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-15800140.text_edition: Deo Soli Invicto Mithrae  pro salute et incolumitate Marci Aureli Thimothei et Aureli Maximi votum nuncupavit solvitque Euthices eorum libertus
EDCS-15800140.translation: To the Unconquered god Sol Mithras, for the health and safety of Marcus Aurelius Thimotheus and Aurelius Maximus, Euthices, their freedman, vowed and fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800140.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Sol Invictus Mithras by Euthices, a freedman, on behalf of his former masters, for their health and safety.

EDCS-15800141.text_edition: Deo Soli Invicto Mithrae  pro salute (et) incotlumitate Marci Aureli Timotei et Aureli Maximi votum nuncupavit solvitque Euthyces eorum libertus retulit
EDCS-15800141.translation: To the Unconquered god Sol Mithras, for the health and safety of Marcus Aurelius Timoteus and Aurelius Maximus, Euthyces, their freedman, vowed, fulfilled, and reported his vow.
EDCS-15800141.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Sol Invictus Mithras by Euthyces, a freedman, for the health and safety of Marcus Aurelius Timoteus and Aurelius Maximus.

EDCS-15800142.text_edition: Soli Invicto aedem restituit Caius Caerellius Sabinus legatus Augusti legionis XIII geminae
EDCS-15800142.translation: To Sol Invictus, Caius Caerellius Sabinus, legate of Augustus for the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, restored a temple.
EDCS-15800142.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a temple to Sol Invictus by Caius Caerellius Sabinus, a high-ranking military official, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-15800143.text_edition: Invicto Mythrae sic Dioscorus Marci votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15800143.translation: To Unconquered Mithras, thus Dioscorus, son of Marcus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800143.summary: This inscription is a personal dedication to Mithras by Dioscorus, son of Marcus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-15800144.text_edition: invicto pro salute imperii populique Romani et ordinis coloniae Apulensis Caius Iulius Valens haruspex coloniae supra scriptae et antises huiusque loci voto libens posuit
EDCS-15800144.translation: To the Unconquered, for the health of the empire, the Roman people, and the order of the colony of Apulum, Caius Iulius Valens, soothsayer of the aforesaid colony and priest of this place, willingly placed this by vow.
EDCS-15800144.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication by Caius Iulius Valens, a haruspex and priest, for the well-being of the Roman Empire, people, and the local colony of Apulum.

EDCS-15800145.text_edition: Veneri Victrici pro salute imperii et senatus populique Romani et ordinis coloniae Apulensis Caius Iulius Valens haruspex coloniae supra scriptae et antistes huiusce loci voto libens posuit
EDCS-15800145.translation: To Venus Victrix, for the health of the empire and the senate and the Roman people and the order of the colony of Apulum, Caius Iulius Valens, soothsayer of the aforesaid colony and priest of this place, willingly placed this by vow.
EDCS-15800145.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Venus Victrix by Caius Iulius Valens, a haruspex and priest, for the health of the empire, senate, Roman people, and the colony of Apulum.

EDCS-15800148.text_edition: Soli Quintus Marcius Victor Felix Maximillianus legatus Auggustorum legionis XIII geminae et Pullaiena Caeliana clarissima femina eius et Publius Marcius Victor Maximillianus clarissimus puer filius voto
EDCS-15800148.translation: To Sol, Quintus Marcius Victor Felix Maximillianus, legate of the Augusti for the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, and Pullaiena Caeliana, his most distinguished wife, and Publius Marcius Victor Maximillianus, his most distinguished son, by vow.
EDCS-15800148.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Sol by a prominent family, including a senatorial legate, his wife, and son, likely for the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-15800149.text_edition: Invicto Mithrae Lucius Octavius Gratus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15800149.translation: To Unconquered Mithras, Lucius Octavius Gratus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800149.summary: This inscription is a personal dedication to Mithras by Lucius Octavius Gratus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-15800150.text_edition: Deo invicto Mithrae  Euhemerus ex voto posuit
EDCS-15800150.translation: To the unconquered god Mithras, Euhemerus dedicated this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-15800150.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Euhemerus to the god Mithras, fulfilling a vow, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800151.text_edition: Soli Invicto Mithrae Turranius Marcellinus et Antonius Senecio Iunior conductores armamentarii ex voto posuerunt
EDCS-15800151.translation: To Sol Invictus Mithras, Turranius Marcellinus and Antonius Senecio Junior, contractors of the arsenal, dedicated this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-15800151.summary: This inscription commemorates a dedication by two contractors of an arsenal, Turranius Marcellinus and Antonius Senecio Junior, to Sol Invictus Mithras, fulfilling a vow, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800152.text_edition: Soli Invicto Mithrae Marcus Valerius Maximianus legatus Augusti votum solvit
EDCS-15800152.translation: To Sol Invictus Mithras, Marcus Valerius Maximianus, imperial legate, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800152.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Sol Invictus Mithras by Marcus Valerius Maximianus, an imperial legate, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800153.text_edition: S(oli) Invicto Mithrae signum Secundinus ex voto posuit
EDCS-15800153.translation: To Sol Invictus Mithras, Secundinus set up this statue in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-15800153.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a statue by Secundinus to Sol Invictus Mithras, fulfilling a vow, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800154.text_edition: Deae Nemesi Aurelius Marius optio signiferorum legionis XIII geminae cum Severia Secundina coniuge pro salute sua et Marianae Bonosae et Mariniani liberti
EDCS-15800154.translation: To the goddess Nemesis, Aurelius Marius, 'optio signiferorum' of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, with Severia Secundina his wife, for their own well-being and that of Mariana Bonosa and their freedman Marinianus.
EDCS-15800154.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the goddess Nemesis by Aurelius Marius, an officer of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, and his wife, for the health and well-being of themselves and their family members, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800155.text_edition: Deae Nemesi sive Fortunae Pistorius Rugianus vir clarissimus legatus legionis XIII geminae Gordianae
EDCS-15800155.translation: To the goddess Nemesis or Fortuna, Pistorius Rugianus, a most distinguished man, legate of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina Gordiana.
EDCS-15800155.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the goddess Nemesis or Fortuna by Pistorius Rugianus, a distinguished legate of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina Gordiana, likely from the Gordian period in the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-15800156.translation: To the most attentive Nemesis, Claudius Marcellus, Augustalis.
EDCS-15800156.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Nemesis by Claudius Marcellus, an Augustalis (priest of the imperial cult), likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800157.text_edition: Numinibus Auggustorum Severi et Antonini et Getae Caesaris et deae Dianae Caius Iulius Maximinus legatus Auggustorum pro praetore
EDCS-15800157.translation: To the Deities of the Augusti Severus and Antoninus, and of Geta Caesar, and to the goddess Diana, Caius Iulius Maximinus, imperial legate with propraetorian power.
EDCS-15800157.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the imperial cult (Severus, Antoninus, Geta) and the goddess Diana, made by Caius Iulius Maximinus, an imperial legate, dating to the early 3rd century AD during the reign of Septimius Severus and his sons.

EDCS-15800159.text_edition: Pro salute domini nostri sanctissimi Antonini Pii Augusti Nymphis novis sacrum Rufrius Sulpicianus legatus legionis XIII geminae Antoninianae
EDCS-15800159.translation: For the well-being of our most sacred lord Antoninus Pius Augustus, sacred to the New Nymphs, Rufrius Sulpicianus, legate of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina Antoniniana.
EDCS-15800159.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the New Nymphs by Rufrius Sulpicianus, a legate of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina Antoniniana, for the welfare of the emperor Antoninus Pius Augustus, dating to the Roman Imperial period (most likely Caracalla, who also bore the name Antoninus Pius).

EDCS-15800160.translation: To the Good Boy God, Phosphorus.
EDCS-15800160.summary: This inscription is a dedication to a deity named the Good Boy God, Phosphorus, likely a syncretic or local divinity, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800161.text_edition: Bono Puero Aurelius Chrestus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15800161.translation: To the Good Boy, Aurelius Chrestus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800161.summary: This inscription records Aurelius Chrestus fulfilling a vow to the 'Good Boy,' likely a local deity, on a votive monument.

EDCS-15800162.text_edition: Bono Puero ex voto ARO DIVS Alexandri posuit
EDCS-15800162.translation: To the Good Boy, from a vow, ARO DIVS, son of Alexander, placed this.
EDCS-15800162.summary: This inscription commemorates ARO DIVS, son of Alexander, placing an offering in fulfillment of a vow to the 'Good Boy,' a deity.

EDCS-15800163.text_edition: Bono Deo Puero Phosphoro Titus Flavius Italicus primus IIIIvir municipii Aureli Apulensis cum Statilia Lucia coniuge et suis ex voto
EDCS-15800163.translation: To the Good God Boy Phosphoro, Titus Flavius Italicus, the first quattuorvir of the municipium Aureli Apulensis, with Statilia Lucia his wife and their family, (dedicated this) from a vow.
EDCS-15800163.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication by Titus Flavius Italicus, a prominent municipal official, his wife Statilia Lucia, and their family, to the syncretic deity Good God Boy Phosphoro, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800164.text_edition: Deo Bono Puero Phosphoro Apollini Pythio Titus Flavius Titus et Titus Flavius Philetus pro salute sua suorumque
EDCS-15800164.translation: To the Good God Boy Phosphoro, Pythian Apollo, Titus Flavius Titus and Titus Flavius Philetus (dedicated this) for their own well-being and that of their kin.
EDCS-15800164.summary: This inscription records Titus Flavius Titus and Titus Flavius Philetus dedicating a monument to the syncretic deities Good God Boy Phosphoro and Pythian Apollo for the health and safety of themselves and their family.

EDCS-15800165.text_edition: Iulia Secunda Puero Phosphoro D O M donum dedit dedicavitque
EDCS-15800165.translation: Julia Secunda gave and dedicated this gift to the Boy Phosphoro, to the Greatest and Best God.
EDCS-15800165.summary: This inscription records Julia Secunda dedicating a gift to the deity Boy Phosphoro, also invoking Jupiter Optimus Maximus, on a votive monument.

EDCS-15800166.text_edition: Bono Puero Phosphoro Laellia Curillia pro salute sua suorumque votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15800166.translation: To the Good Boy Phosphoro, Laellia Curillia willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow for her own well-being and that of her kin.
EDCS-15800166.summary: This inscription records Laellia Curillia fulfilling a vow to the Good Boy Phosphoro for the health of herself and her family, likely on a votive altar or tablet.

EDCS-15800167.text_edition: Deo Bono Phosphoro Apollini Pythio Decimus Iulius Rusonius custos armorum pro salute sua et suorum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15800167.translation: To the Good God Phosphoro, Pythian Apollo, Decimus Iulius Rusonius, keeper of arms, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for his own well-being and that of his kin.
EDCS-15800167.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication by Decimus Iulius Rusonius, a 'keeper of arms,' to the syncretic deities Good God Phosphoro and Pythian Apollo, made for the health of himself and his family.

EDCS-15800168.text_edition: Priepo Pantheo PPublii Aelii Ursio et Antonianus aediles coloniae Apulensis dicaverunt Severo et Quintiano consulibus
EDCS-15800168.translation: To Priapus Pantheus, Publius Aelius Ursio and Antonianus, aediles of the colony of Apulum, dedicated this in the consulship of Severus and Quintianus.
EDCS-15800168.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument to Priapus Pantheus by two aediles, Publius Aelius Ursio and Antonianus, from the colony of Apulum, precisely dated to the year 235 AD (during the consulship of Severus and Quintianus).

EDCS-15800169.text_edition: Quadriviis sacrum Flavia Pulchra pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15800169.translation: Sacred to the crossroads, Flavia Pulchra willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow for herself and her family.
EDCS-15800169.summary: This inscription records Flavia Pulchra fulfilling a vow to the deities of the crossroads (Quadriviae) for her own well-being and that of her family, likely on a small altar.

EDCS-15800170.text_edition: Silvano  Publius Aelius Publi filius Fabianus augur pontifex IIvirir decurio coloniae Sarmizegetusae decurio coloniae Napocae augur coloniae eiusdem decurio municipii Apulensis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15800170.translation: To Silvanus, Publius Aelius Fabianus, son of Publius, augur, pontifex, duumvir, decurion of the colony of Sarmizegetusa, decurion of the colony of Napoca, augur of the same colony, decurion of the municipium of Apulum, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800170.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to the god Silvanus by Publius Aelius Fabianus, a man of significant religious and political standing holding multiple offices in several Roman colonies and municipia, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800171.text_edition: Silvano Marcus Caecilius Rufinus Marianus legatus legionis XIII geminae
EDCS-15800171.translation: To Silvanus, Marcus Caecilius Rufinus Marianus, legate of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion.
EDCS-15800171.summary: This is a dedication to the god Silvanus by a high-ranking military officer, likely a votive offering from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-15800172.text_edition: Silvano Marcus Chrestus pro salute Marci Antoni Florentini fili mei votum solvi libens merito
EDCS-15800172.translation: To Silvanus, Marcus Chrestus willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow for the health of my son, Marcus Antonius Florentinus.
EDCS-15800172.summary: This is a private dedication by a father, Marcus Chrestus, to Silvanus, made in fulfillment of a vow for his son's well-being, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-15800173.text_edition: Silvano sacrum Philippus ex viso posuit
EDCS-15800173.translation: Sacred to Silvanus, Philippus placed this as a result of a vision.
EDCS-15800173.summary: This is a personal dedication to Silvanus by Philippus, prompted by a divine vision, indicating a votive offering.

EDCS-15800174.text_edition: Deo Silvano Ulpius Salvinus quaestor voto libens posuit
EDCS-15800174.translation: To the god Silvanus, Ulpius Salvinus, quaestor, willingly placed this in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-15800174.summary: This is a public dedication to the god Silvanus by a Roman official, Ulpius Salvinus, serving as a quaestor, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-15800175.text_edition: Marcus Q Donatus Silvano Augusto sacrum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15800175.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus, Marcus Q. Donatus willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-15800175.summary: This is a dedication to Silvanus, explicitly linked with the imperial cult (Augustus), made by Marcus Q. Donatus in fulfillment of a vow, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-15800178.text_edition: Silvano Domestico pro salute Titi Flavi Crescentis praefecti numeri Maurorum Hispanorum Titus Flavius Felicianus domus illius alumnus
EDCS-15800178.translation: To Silvanus Domesticus, Titus Flavius Felicianus, alumnus of that household, for the health of Titus Flavius Crescens, prefect of the Mauri Hispani cavalry unit.
EDCS-15800178.summary: This is a votive dedication to Silvanus Domesticus, made by a household alumnus for the health of his master, a military prefect, highlighting the domestic and protective aspects of the deity and likely dating to the Imperial era.

EDCS-15800179.text_edition: Silvano Domestico Caius Iulius Valens IIvir coloniae Apulensis pro salute sua suorumque posuit
EDCS-15800179.translation: To Silvanus Domesticus, Gaius Iulius Valens, duumvir of the colony of Apulum, placed this for his own health and that of his family.
EDCS-15800179.summary: This is a dedication to Silvanus Domesticus by a municipal magistrate, Gaius Iulius Valens, for the well-being of himself and his family, attesting to his public office in the colony of Apulum.

EDCS-15800180.text_edition: Silvano Domestico Marcus Opellius Secundus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15800180.translation: To Silvanus Domesticus, Marcus Opellius Secundus willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-15800180.summary: This is a straightforward votive dedication to Silvanus Domesticus by Marcus Opellius Secundus, marking the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-15800181.text_edition: Silvano Domestico Terrae Matri Herculi sacrum pro salute sua suorumque Iulius Gaianus libens posuit
EDCS-15800181.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Domesticus, Terra Mater, and Hercules, Iulius Gaianus willingly placed this for his own health and that of his family.
EDCS-15800181.summary: This is a multiple dedication by Iulius Gaianus to Silvanus Domesticus, Terra Mater, and Hercules, made for the health of himself and his family, showing a broader range of protective deities, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-15800182.text_edition: Silvano Sancto Lucius Aemilius Carus legatus Augusti pro praetore III Daciarum
EDCS-15800182.translation: To Silvanus Sanctus, Lucius Aemilius Carus, Imperial legate with propraetorian power, governor of the Three Dacias.
EDCS-15800182.summary: This is an official dedication to Silvanus Sanctus by Lucius Aemilius Carus, a provincial governor of the three Dacias, indicating a significant votive offering from a high-ranking official, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-15800183.text_edition: Silvano Silvestri et Dianae Marcus Aurelius Comatius Super decurio antistes pro salute sua et Comatiorum Superiani Superes Exsuperantiani Superstitis filiorum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15800183.translation: To Silvanus Silvester and Diana, Marcus Aurelius Comatius Super, a decurion and priest, for his own well-being and that of his sons Comatius Superianus, Superes Exsuperantianus, and Superstes, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800183.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Marcus Aurelius Comatius Super, a decurion and priest, to Silvanus Silvester and Diana for the health of himself and his sons, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-15800184.text_edition: Deo Silvano Silvestri Caius Longius Aquilinus veteranus legionis XIII geminae pro se et suis voto libens posuit
EDCS-15800184.translation: To the god Silvanus Silvester, Gaius Longius Aquilinus, a veteran of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, willingly placed this for himself and his family, according to his vow.
EDCS-15800184.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Gaius Longius Aquilinus, a veteran of Legio XIII Gemina, to the god Silvanus Silvester for himself and his family, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-15800185.text_edition: Suleviis Flavius Attalus votum libens solvit
EDCS-15800185.translation: To the Suleviae, Flavius Attalus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800185.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Suleviae by Flavius Attalus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-15800186.text_edition: Veneri Augustae Fabius Pulcher Aug3  coloniae votum? solvit
EDCS-15800186.translation: To Venus Augusta, Fabius Pulcher, augur of the colony, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800186.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Venus Augusta by Fabius Pulcher, an augur of a Roman colony, in fulfillment of a vow.
