EDCS-15800187.text_edition: Victoriae Augustae Lucius Iulius Luci Galeria Leuganus Clunia veteranus legionis XIIII geminae Martiae victricis aedis custos civium Romanorum legionis XIII geminae nomine suo et Cai Iuli Paterni fili sui donum dedit
EDCS-15800187.translation: To Victoria Augusta, Lucius Julius, son of Lucius, of the Galerian tribe, Leuganus from Clunia, a veteran of the Fourteenth Legion Gemina Martia Victrix, custodian of the temple of the Roman citizens of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, gave this gift in his own name and that of his son Gaius Julius Paternus.
EDCS-15800187.summary: This inscription records a gift to Victoria Augusta by Lucius Julius Leuganus, a veteran of Legio XIIII Gemina Martia Victrix and temple custodian, on behalf of himself and his son.

EDCS-15800188.text_edition: Virtuti Romanae et Genio Imperatoris Gordiani Pii Felicis Augusti
EDCS-15800188.translation: To Roman Virtue and to the Genius of the Emperor Gordian Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-15800188.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Roman Virtue and the Genius of Emperor Gordian Pius Felix Augustus.

EDCS-15800189.text_edition: ] ibus mu--- Publius Aelius Crescens duplicarius singularis consularis equitum singularium per quem templum factum est posuit
EDCS-15800189.translation: ...Publius Aelius Crescens, a duplicarius of the governor's cavalry guard, through whom the temple was built, placed this.
EDCS-15800189.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a temple by Publius Aelius Crescens, a duplicarius of the governor's cavalry guard.

EDCS-15800190.text_edition: reginae Auggustae dedicarunt XIII Kalendas Octobres
EDCS-15800190.translation: They dedicated this to the Augustan Queen on the 13th day before the Kalends of October.
EDCS-15800190.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument to an 'Augustan Queen' on September 19th.

EDCS-15800191.text_edition: C(aius)? Valerius Salvius Augustalis coloniae Apulensis pro salute sua et Flaviae Syrae coniugis et Cai Valerii Flaviani filii et omnium suorum ex voto posuit
EDCS-15800191.translation: Gaius Valerius Salvius, Augustalis of the colony of Apulum, placed this in accordance with a vow for his own well-being and that of Flavia Syra, his wife, and Gaius Valerius Flavianus, his son, and all his household.
EDCS-15800191.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Gaius Valerius Salvius, an Augustalis of Colonia Apulensis, for the health and well-being of himself, his wife, his son, and his entire family, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-15800194.translation: Sacred to Nutia, Caecilia, daughter of Sextus.
EDCS-15800194.summary: This inscription records a sacred dedication to Nutia by Caecilia, daughter of Sextus.

EDCS-15800195.text_edition: Marcus Marcellus librarius legati votum solvit merito
EDCS-15800195.translation: Marcus Marcellus, the legate's scribe, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800195.summary: This is a votive inscription by a scribe named Marcus Marcellus, dedicated in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-15800196.text_edition: Silvano  sacrum Marcus Ulpius Terentius ex viso posuit
EDCS-15800196.translation: Sacred to Silvanus. Marcus Ulpius Terentius set this up in consequence of a vision.
EDCS-15800196.summary: This is a votive dedication to the god Silvanus, set up by Marcus Ulpius Terentius following a vision.

EDCS-15800200.text_edition: Imperatore Caesare Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto III et Imperatore Caesare Lucio Aurelio Vero Augusto II consulibus per legionem XIII geminam sub cura Publi Furi Saturnini legati pro praetore consulis designati
EDCS-15800200.translation: When Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus was consul for the third time and Imperator Caesar Lucius Aurelius Verus Augustus for the second time, through the Thirteenth Twin Legion, under the care of Publius Furius Saturninus, legate pro praetore, consul-designate.
EDCS-15800200.summary: This inscription records an activity undertaken by the Thirteenth Twin Legion under the command of Publius Furius Saturninus, dated to the consulate of Marcus Aurelius (III) and Lucius Verus (II), which corresponds to 161 CE.

EDCS-15800201.text_edition: propro Imperatoris Commodi Augusti Pii Felicis veteranis legionis XIII geminae militibus factis Pudente et Pollione consulibus missis honesta missione per Manilium Fuscum legatum Augusti Aproniano et Bradua consulibus Acutius Quintinus contiro conveteranis suis columnam donum dedit
EDCS-15800201.translation: For Imperator Commodus Augustus Pius Felix, to the veterans of the Thirteenth Twin Legion, made soldiers when Pudens and Pollio were consuls, honorably discharged when Manilius Fuscus was legate of Augustus, when Apronianus and Bradua were consuls, Acutius Quintinus, a fellow veteran, gave this column as a gift to his fellow veterans.
EDCS-15800201.summary: This inscription records Acutius Quintinus dedicating a column as a gift to his fellow veterans of the Thirteenth Twin Legion, in honor of Emperor Commodus, noting their recruitment in 167 CE and honorable discharge in 191 CE.

EDCS-15800203.text_edition: Pro salute Augggustorum Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis et Marci Aurelii Antonini Impperatorum et Publi Septimi Getae Caesaris collegium centonariorum scholam cum aetoma pecunia sua fecit dedicante Lucio Pomponio Liberale consulari Daciarum III
EDCS-15800203.translation: For the welfare of the Augusti, Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, Emperors, and Publius Septimius Geta Caesar, the guild of centonarii built a collegium hall with a pediment at their own expense, with Lucius Pomponius Liberalis, consular governor of the Three Dacias, dedicating it.
EDCS-15800203.summary: This inscription records the construction of a collegium hall with a pediment by a guild of centonarii, dedicated by Lucius Pomponius Liberalis, for the welfare of the imperial family (Septimius Severus, Caracalla, and Geta), dating to between 198 and 209 CE.

EDCS-15800204.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Caio Messio Quinto Traiano Decio Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate II consuli II patri patriae restitutori Daciarum colonia nova Apulensis
EDCS-15800204.translation: To Imperator Caesar Gaius Messius Quintus Traianus Decius Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the second time, consul for the second time, father of his country, restorer of the Dacias, the new colony of Apulum.
EDCS-15800204.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription from the new colony of Apulum to Emperor Trajan Decius, recognizing him as restorer of Dacia, likely dating to 250 CE.

EDCS-15800205.text_edition: Lucio Mario Perpetuo consulari Daciarum III legato Augusti pro praetore provinciae Moesiae Superioris curatori rerum publicarum Urbis item Tusculanorum praesidi provinciae Arabiae laticlavio legionis IIII Scythicae praesidi iustissimo Marcus Ulpius Caius |(centurio) legionis III Italicae Antoninianae
EDCS-15800205.translation: To Lucius Marius Perpetuus, consular governor of the Three Dacias, legate of Augustus pro praetore of the province of Upper Moesia, curator of public works in the City and also of Tusculum, governor of the province of Arabia, laticlavius of the Fourth Scythian Legion, most just governor, Marcus Ulpius Gaius, centurion of the Third Italic Antoninian Legion [dedicated this].
EDCS-15800205.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Marcus Ulpius Gaius, a centurion, to Lucius Marius Perpetuus, listing his extensive imperial career, including several high-ranking provincial governorships and military commands.

EDCS-15800207.text_edition: Publio Aelio Antipatro Marcello equiti Romano decurioni coloniae Apulensis filio Publi Aeli Antipatri a militiis et IIviri coloniae supra scriptae et adoptivo Publi Aeli Marcelli viri egregii ex praefecto legionum VII Claudiae et I adiutricis Dades et Filetus actores
EDCS-15800207.translation: To Publius Aelius Antipater Marcellus, Roman knight, decurion of the colony of Apulum, son of Publius Aelius Antipater, from military service, and duumvir of the above-written colony, and adopted son of Publius Aelius Marcellus, most distinguished man, former prefect of the Seventh Claudia and First Adiutrix Legions, Dades and Filetus, agents [dedicated this].
EDCS-15800207.summary: This inscription is a dedication by two agents, Dades and Filetus, to Publius Aelius Antipater Marcellus, a Roman knight and decurion of Apulum, detailing his family connections and his adoptive father's distinguished military career.

EDCS-15800208.text_edition: Publiae Aeliae Iulianae Marcellae splendidissimae puellae filiae Publi Aeli Iuliani equitis Romani flaminis et IIviralis coloniae Apulensis et adoptivae Publi Aeli Marcelli viri egregii ex praefecto leggionum VII Claudiae et I adiutricis Dades et Filetus actores
EDCS-15800208.translation: To Publia Aelia Iuliana Marcella, most splendid girl, daughter of Publius Aelius Iulianus, Roman knight, flamen and duumviral of the colony of Apulum, and adopted daughter of Publius Aelius Marcellus, most distinguished man, former prefect of the Seventh Claudia and First Adiutrix Legions, Dades and Filetus, agents [dedicated this].
EDCS-15800208.summary: This inscription is a dedication by two agents, Dades and Filetus, to Publia Aelia Iuliana Marcella, an honorific dedication to a distinguished young girl, outlining her notable family lineage.

EDCS-15800209.text_edition: Lucius Aelius Silvanus beneficiarius consularis libens posuit
EDCS-15800209.translation: Lucius Aelius Silvanus, a consular beneficiarius, willingly set this up.
EDCS-15800209.summary: This is a votive or commemorative inscription set up by Lucius Aelius Silvanus, who held the military position of beneficiarius consularis.

EDCS-15800210.text_edition: librarius  benef(iciarius) consularis    Aurelius Iustus Aurelius Lucilius agrimensor Valerius Romulus Valerius Sabinus Septimius Magnus Septimius Victor Aurelius Senecio duplarius Iulius Militaris Por Iulianus tesserarius cohortis IIII Ulpius Surus
EDCS-15800210.translation: Aurelius Iustus, a bookseller and consular beneficiary; Aurelius Lucilius, a land surveyor; Valerius Romulus; Valerius Sabinus; Septimius Magnus; Septimius Victor; Aurelius Senecio, a duplicarius; Julius Militaris; Porcius Iulianus, a tesserarius of the Fourth Cohort; Ulpius Surus.
EDCS-15800210.summary: This inscription lists various military and administrative personnel, including a bookseller, a land surveyor, and officers from the Fourth Cohort, likely documenting individuals serving in a particular unit or administration.

EDCS-15800211.text_edition: ADIZ   candidatus s candidatus inus duplicarius ianus duplicarius arius secutor tribuni mus beneficiarius tribuni  candidatus    DI Septimius Carpianus Septimius Statilianus Septimius Castus Septimius Celsus candidatus Septimius Alexander candidatus Septimius Nepos candidatus Claudius Alexander beneficiarius consularis
EDCS-15800211.translation: ADIZ... candidate, candidate... duplicarius... duplicarius... secutor of the tribune, beneficiary of the tribune... candidate. Septimius Carpianus, Septimius Statilianus, Septimius Castus, Septimius Celsus (candidate), Septimius Alexander (candidate), Septimius Nepos (candidate), Claudius Alexander (consular beneficiary).
EDCS-15800211.summary: This fragmented inscription enumerates military personnel, including candidates, duplicarii, a tribune's attendant, and beneficiaries, possibly detailing a unit's roster or a promotion list.

EDCS-15800212.text_edition: Marco Ulpio Caio praefecto legionum III Flaviae et XIII geminae
EDCS-15800212.translation: To Marcus Ulpius Gaius, prefect of the Third Flavian and Thirteenth Gemina legions.
EDCS-15800212.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Marcus Ulpius Gaius, commemorating his service as prefect of two Roman legions, the III Flavia and XIII Gemina.

EDCS-15800213.text_edition: Publio Aelio Publi filio Papiria Geniali decurioni et pontifici coloniae Apulensis patrono collegii centonariorum Publius Aelius Euthymus libertus
EDCS-15800213.translation: To Publius Aelius Genialis, son of Publius, of the Papirian tribe, decurion and pontiff of the colony Apulensis, patron of the college of rag-dealers. Publius Aelius Euthymus, freedman, [set this up].
EDCS-15800213.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Publius Aelius Euthymus, a freedman, to Publius Aelius Genialis, honoring his public offices in Apulum and his patronage of the college of rag-dealers.

EDCS-15800214.text_edition: Publio Aelio Publi filio Papiria Strenuo equo publico sacerdoti arae Augusti auguri et IIvirali coloniae Sarmizegetusae auguri coloniae Apulensis decurioni coloniae Drobetensis patrono collegiorum fabrum centonariorum et nautarum conductori pascui salinarum et commerciorum Rufinus eius
EDCS-15800214.translation: To Publius Aelius Strenuus, son of Publius, of the Papirian tribe, holder of the public horse, priest of the Altar of Augustus, augur and duumvir of the colony Sarmizegetusa, augur of the colony Apulensis, decurion of the colony Drobetensis, patron of the colleges of artisans, rag-dealers, and sailors, contractor for the pasturage of the saltworks and trade. Rufinus, his [servant/freedman, set this up].
EDCS-15800214.summary: This extensive inscription is a dedication to Publius Aelius Strenuus, detailing his illustrious career, numerous priesthoods, civic offices across several colonies, and his patronage of various collegia and business ventures.

EDCS-15800215.text_edition: Marcus Aurelius Chrestus ob honorem patronatus collegii fabrum coloniae Apulensis ad exstructionem aetomae dedit HS nummorum VIsex milia
EDCS-15800215.translation: Marcus Aurelius Chrestus, for the honor of the patronage of the college of artisans of the colony Apulensis, gave six thousand sesterces for the construction of a pediment.
EDCS-15800215.summary: This inscription records Marcus Aurelius Chrestus's donation of 6,000 sesterces for the construction of a pediment, made to honor his patronage of the college of artisans in Apulum.

EDCS-15800216.text_edition: instantibus Ulpio Artemidoro et Aurelio Maximo quaestoribus
EDCS-15800216.translation: At the urging of Ulpius Artemidorus and Aurelius Maximus, quaestors.
EDCS-15800216.summary: This inscription records that a public work or dedication was undertaken at the instigation of Ulpius Artemidorus and Aurelius Maximus, who held the office of quaestor.

EDCS-15800218.summary: This inscription simply identifies Aurelius Maximillianus by his name and profession as a mensor or surveyor.

EDCS-15800221.text_edition: Deo Aeterno lius Gallicanus veteranus ex beneficiario consularis pro salute sua suorumque posuit
EDCS-15800221.translation: To the Eternal God, [---]lius Gallicanus, a veteran and former consular beneficiary, placed this for his own safety and that of his family.
EDCS-15800221.summary: This inscription is a religious dedication to the Eternal God, set up by a veteran named Gallicanus, a former consular beneficiary, for the well-being of himself and his household.

EDCS-15800222.text_edition: Aeterno Clodia Maxima et FElavia#Flavia#ELA Valeria
EDCS-15800222.translation: To the Eternal [God], Clodia Maxima and Flavia Valeria.
EDCS-15800222.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Eternal God by Clodia Maxima and Flavia Valeria, likely for a religious purpose or commemoration.

EDCS-15800224.text_edition: Caius Nummius Verus eques Romanus IIviralis coloniae Apulensis et sacerdos numinis Aesculapi consecravit
EDCS-15800224.translation: Gaius Nummius Verus, a Roman knight, duumvir of the colony of Apulum, and priest of the divinity of Aesculapius, consecrated this.
EDCS-15800224.summary: This inscription records the consecration of a monument by Gaius Nummius Verus, a Roman knight and priest, in honor of Aesculapius.

EDCS-15800225.text_edition: Asclepio et Hygiae Caius Fabricius Dexter votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15800225.translation: To Asclepius and Hygia, Gaius Fabricius Dexter willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800225.summary: This inscription is a votive offering by Gaius Fabricius Dexter to Asclepius and Hygia.

EDCS-15800227.text_edition: Dianae Augustae Caius Iulius Valerius veteranus legionis XIII geminae decurio coloniae Sarmizegetusae missus  ex beneficiario consularis
EDCS-15800227.translation: To Diana Augusta, Gaius Julius Valerius, a veteran of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, decurion of the colony of Sarmizegetusa, dispatched after serving as a consular beneficiarius.
EDCS-15800227.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Diana Augusta by Gaius Julius Valerius, a veteran and municipal officer from Sarmizegetusa.

EDCS-15800229.text_edition: Dianae ex voto Ulpius Vindex votum posuit libens
EDCS-15800229.translation: To Diana, in accordance with a vow, Ulpius Vindex willingly placed this vow.
EDCS-15800229.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to Diana by Ulpius Vindex, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-15800231.text_edition: Deis deabusque Publius Fabius Herclianus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15800231.translation: To the gods and goddesses, Publius Fabius Herclianus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800231.summary: This inscription is a general votive offering by Publius Fabius Herclianus to the gods and goddesses.

EDCS-15800233.translation: To the god Bussumarus, Sentius Alexander willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800233.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Sentius Alexander to the god Bussumarus.

EDCS-15800235.text_edition: Eponae Reginae sanctae Caius [] S[] legatus Augusti pro praetore consularis Daciarum III   V IENTI
EDCS-15800235.translation: To Epona the Holy Queen, Gaius [---], legate of Augustus with pro-praetorian power, consular of the three Dacias, V IENTI.
EDCS-15800235.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Epona by Gaius [---], a high-ranking imperial official responsible for the province of Dacia.

EDCS-15800236.text_edition: Herculi Augusto  Reginus sacerdos institutus ab Helvio Pertinace cons(ulari)
EDCS-15800236.translation: To Hercules Augustus, Reginus, priest appointed by Helvius Pertinax, the consular.
EDCS-15800236.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Hercules Augustus by Reginus, a priest appointed by the consular Helvius Pertinax, likely indicating a late 2nd-century AD date.

EDCS-15800237.translation: Sacred to Hercules, for the well-being of the Emperor.
EDCS-15800237.summary: This inscription is a sacred dedication to Hercules, made on behalf of the Emperor's health.

EDCS-15800238.text_edition: Iunoni Semliae Iulius Alexander actarius legati legionis XIII geminae et Aelia Vincentia libentes posuerunt
EDCS-15800238.translation: To Juno Semlia, Julius Alexander, the records clerk of the legate of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, and Aelia Vincentia willingly placed this.
EDCS-15800238.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Juno Semlia, set up by Julius Alexander, a military administrative official, and Aelia Vincentia.

EDCS-15800239.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo veterani legionis XIII geminae milites facti Serviano II et Sura et Traiano V consulibus NVM
EDCS-15800239.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the veterans of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina became soldiers when Servianus II and Sura and Trajan V were consuls.
EDCS-15800239.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by veterans of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, commemorating their enlistment during specific consulates in the early 2nd century AD.

EDCS-15800240.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Aurelius Marinus Bassus et Aurelius Castor Polydi circumstantes viderunt numen aquilae descidisse monte supra draconem res validavit supstrinxit sic aquilam hi supra scripti aquilam de periculo liberaverunt voto libentes merito posuerunt
EDCS-15800240.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Aurelius Marinus Bassus and Aurelius Castor Polydi, standing by, saw the divine power of an eagle descend from the mountain above a dragon. The event strengthened and bound them. Thus, these aforementioned persons freed the eagle from danger. They gladly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-15800240.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aurelius Marinus Bassus and Aurelius Castor Polydi, commemorating a miraculous vision of an eagle and a dragon, which they interpreted as a divine intervention.

EDCS-15800241.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iulius Memnon votum reddidit donum dedit
EDCS-15800241.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Iulius Memnon rendered his vow and gave a gift.
EDCS-15800241.summary: This inscription is a simple dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Iulius Memnon, fulfilling a vow and presenting a gift.

EDCS-15800244.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno pro salute Imperatorum Aelius Valentinus veteranus sacerdos templum impendio suo restituit
EDCS-15800244.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, for the health of the Emperors, Aelius Valentinus, a veteran priest, restored the temple at his own expense.
EDCS-15800244.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a temple to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus by Aelius Valentinus, a veteran priest, at his own expense for the well-being of the emperors.

EDCS-15800245.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Aurelii Alexander et Flavus Suri negotiatores ex voto libentes posuerunt
EDCS-15800245.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, Aurelii Alexander and Flavus Suri, merchants, gladly erected this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-15800245.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus by two merchants, Aurelii Alexander and Flavus Suri, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-15800250.text_edition: Nemesi Reginae Caius Spedius Valerianus decurio collegii fabrum pro salute sua suorumque omnium votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15800250.translation: To Queen Nemesis, Gaius Spedius Valerianus, a decurion of the college of craftsmen, willingly and deservedly paid his vow for his own health and that of all his kin.
EDCS-15800250.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Nemesis by Gaius Spedius Valerianus, a decurion of a craftsmen's guild, fulfilling a vow for his and his family's health.

EDCS-15800251.text_edition: Sarapi Augusto sacrum Tiberius Iulius Flaccinus legatus Augusti pro praetore
EDCS-15800251.translation: Sacred to Sarapis Augustus. Tiberius Iulius Flaccinus, imperial legate with propraetorian power.
EDCS-15800251.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Sarapis Augustus by Tiberius Iulius Flaccinus, an imperial legate.

EDCS-15800253.text_edition: Sarapi et Isidi Lucius Iunius Rufinus Proculianus tribunus lati clavius militum legionis XIII geminae
EDCS-15800253.translation: To Sarapis and Isis. Lucius Iunius Rufinus Proculianus, tribune laticlavius of the soldiers of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina.
EDCS-15800253.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Sarapis and Isis by Lucius Iunius Rufinus Proculianus, a high-ranking military tribune of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina.

EDCS-15800254.text_edition: Sarapidi Iovi Soli Isidi Lunae Dianae dis deabusque conservatoribus Lucius Aemilius Carus legatus Augusti pro praetore III Daciarum
EDCS-15800254.translation: To Sarapis, Jupiter, Sol, Isis, Luna, Diana, and to the preserving gods and goddesses, Lucius Aemilius Carus, imperial legate with propraetorian power of the Three Dacias.
EDCS-15800254.summary: This inscription is a dedication to a pantheon of gods and goddesses, including Sarapis, Jupiter, Sol, Isis, Luna, and Diana, by Lucius Aemilius Carus, an imperial legate governing the Three Dacias.

EDCS-15800255.text_edition: Silvano merita libens posuit Titus Annius Tertius
EDCS-15800255.translation: To Silvanus, Titus Annius Tertius willingly and deservedly placed this.
EDCS-15800255.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Silvanus by Titus Annius Tertius, expressing his gratitude.

EDCS-15800256.text_edition: Silvano Domestico Marcus Lucilius Philoctemon IIvir coloniae Aureliae Apulensis voto libens posuit
EDCS-15800256.translation: To Silvanus Domesticus, Marcus Lucilius Philoctemon, duumvir of the colony Aurelia Apulensis, willingly set this up in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-15800256.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Silvanus Domesticus by Marcus Lucilius Philoctemon, a duumvir of the colony Aurelia Apulensis, made in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-15800257.text_edition: Silvano Domestico Lucius A Lucianus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15800257.translation: To Silvanus Domesticus, Lucius A. Lucianus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800257.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled to Silvanus Domesticus by Lucius A. Lucianus.

EDCS-15800258.text_edition: Soli Invicto Mithrae Caius Nummius Amandus qui et templum
EDCS-15800258.translation: To Sol Invictus Mithras, Gaius Nummius Amandus, who also [made] the temple.
EDCS-15800258.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Sol Invictus Mithras by Gaius Nummius Amandus, who also appears to have been responsible for the temple.

EDCS-15800259.text_edition: Invicto Mythrae Sextius Syntrofus votum solvit libens
EDCS-15800259.translation: To Invictus Mythras, Sextius Syntrofus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800259.summary: This inscription records a vow willingly fulfilled to Invictus Mithras by Sextius Syntrofus.

EDCS-15800260.translation: To the god Mithras, I, Lucanus, brought back my vows.
EDCS-15800260.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of vows to the god Mithras by Lucanus.

EDCS-15800264.text_edition: LLO TIANI ILVM Uplius Vitalis |(centurio) legionis III Italicae voto libens posuit
EDCS-15800264.translation: To LLO TIANI ILVM, Uplius Vitalis, centurion of the Third Italic Legion, willingly set this up in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-15800264.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Uplius Vitalis, a centurion of the Third Italic Legion, made willingly in fulfillment of a vow to a poorly preserved recipient.

EDCS-15800265.text_edition: Aurelius Mucianus ex voto strator consularis posuit
EDCS-15800265.translation: Aurelius Mucianus, consular groom, set this up in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-15800265.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Aurelius Mucianus, a consular groom, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-15800266.text_edition: D decurio equitum singularium ex voto posuit
EDCS-15800266.translation: D., decurion of the singular cavalry, set this up in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-15800266.summary: This inscription records a dedication made in fulfillment of a vow by D., a decurion of the singular cavalry.

EDCS-15800267.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Aelius Primus decurio coloniae
EDCS-15800267.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Gaius Aelius Primus, decurion of the colony.
EDCS-15800267.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Gaius Aelius Primus, a decurion of the colony.

EDCS-15800268.translation: Aurelius Tzolotus set this up in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-15800268.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Aurelius Tzolotus in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-15800269.summary: This is a brief dedicatory inscription recording Arrius Hermes' fulfillment of a vow, likely to a deity.

EDCS-15800270.text_edition: Deo Commag(eno) sacrum per Antiochum sacerdos sic loci
EDCS-15800270.translation: Sacred to the Commagenian God, by Antiochus, priest thus of the place.
EDCS-15800270.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to a Commagenian god, made by Antiochus, who served as a local priest, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800271.text_edition: optio legionis XIII geminae Antoninianae pater et
EDCS-15800271.translation: An optio of the Thirteenth Gemina Antoniniana Legion, father and...
EDCS-15800271.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription mentioning an 'optio' (junior officer) of the Legio XIII Gemina Antoniniana, indicating a military context from the Severan period.

EDCS-15800272.text_edition: TINUS I-- et Marcus Antonius Sabinus decurio coloniae votum solverunt
EDCS-15800272.translation: Tinus I[---] and Marcus Antonius Sabinus, a decurion of the colony, fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-15800272.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by two individuals, one of whom was Marcus Antonius Sabinus, a decurion (local councilor) of a colony, indicating a civic and religious dedication.

EDCS-15800275.text_edition: qquaestionarii Longius Orestus Iulius Victorinus qqaestionarii Ulpius Valentinus Iulius Seneca qqaestionarii Aelius Caesianus Aelius Valerius
EDCS-15800275.translation: Interrogators Longius Orestus, Iulius Victorinus, interrogators Ulpius Valentinus, Iulius Seneca, interrogators Aelius Caesianus, Aelius Valerius.
EDCS-15800275.summary: This inscription lists several individuals, including Longius Orestus, Iulius Victorinus, Ulpius Valentinus, Iulius Seneca, Aelius Caesianus, and Aelius Valerius, all identified as 'quaestionarii' (interrogators or investigators), likely indicating a professional or collegial monument.

EDCS-15800276.text_edition: Familiaricum a solo Prosmoni ex suo fecerunt per Aurelium Statium et Ulpium Paulum quaestores
EDCS-15800276.translation: The Family members built from the ground up for Prosmonius at their own expense, through Aurelius Statius and Ulpius Paulus, quaestors.
EDCS-15800276.summary: This inscription records the construction of a monument or building for Prosmonius by his family, funded privately and overseen by Aurelius Statius and Ulpius Paulus, who served as quaestors (financial officials).

EDCS-15800277.text_edition: Marcus Licinnius sic Moesicus et Marcus Licinius Euangelus fratres
EDCS-15800277.translation: Marcus Licinnius Moesicus and Marcus Licinius Euangelus, brothers.
EDCS-15800277.summary: This is a simple inscription listing the names of two brothers, Marcus Licinnius Moesicus and Marcus Licinius Euangelus, likely from a tombstone or dedicatory monument.

EDCS-15800466.text_edition: Dis Manibus P-VLSTI V SVA VLI ius Iilino IMP
EDCS-15800466.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. P[---] Vlsti V. S. Uliius Iilino Imp.
EDCS-15800466.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes (spirits of the departed), likely for an individual named P. Vlsti, with other names partially legible.

EDCS-15800469.text_edition: Publius Aelius Athenodorus vixit nnos III diebus XL
EDCS-15800469.translation: Publius Aelius Athenodorus lived 3 years and 40 days.
EDCS-15800469.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Aelius Athenodorus, recording his very young age at death, likely from a tombstone.

EDCS-15800470.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelius Crescens Ulpio Lupo filio carissimo vixit annis III mensibus VI bene merenti posuit
EDCS-15800470.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aelius Crescens placed this for Ulpius Lupus, his dearest son, who lived for 3 years and 6 months, well-deserving.
EDCS-15800470.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aelius Crescens to his beloved son, Ulpius Lupus, who died at the age of three and a half, expressing affection and respect.

EDCS-15800471.translation: To the husband of Publius Aelius Fortunatus, Aelia [set this up].
EDCS-15800471.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Aelia for her husband, Publius Aelius Fortunatus.

EDCS-15800472.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelio Geniali ex singulari vixit annis XXXII vexillarius Valerius librarius primus heres et Atticus secundus heres bene merenti posuit
EDCS-15800472.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Aelius Genialis, who lived 32 years, a singular standard-bearer. Valerius, the first clerk and heir, and Atticus, the second heir, set this up for the well-deserving.
EDCS-15800472.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Aelius Genialis, a standard-bearer, set up by his two heirs.

EDCS-15800473.translation: To Aelia, daughter of Publius GP.
EDCS-15800473.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription for Aelia, identified as the daughter of Publius.

EDCS-15800474.text_edition: Aelius Niger veteranus nnorum LXX cohortis I Alpinorum equitatae Saturninae sic Saturnini filia coniugi pientissimo posuit et sibi vivae
EDCS-15800474.translation: Aelius Niger, a veteran aged 70, of the First Cohort of Alpine Mounted Troops. Saturnina, daughter of Saturninus, set this up for her most devoted husband and for herself while living.
EDCS-15800474.summary: This is a funerary monument for Aelius Niger, a veteran, set up by his wife Saturnina, also prepared for herself during her lifetime.

EDCS-15800475.text_edition: Publius Aelius Proclus veteranus legionis XIII geminae vixit nnis LV et Aelia Claudia vixit annis XL Publius Aelius Proclinus et Aelia Procla parentibus pientissimis posuerunt
EDCS-15800475.translation: Publius Aelius Proclus, a veteran of the Thirteenth Twin Legion, lived 55 years, and Aelia Claudia lived 40 years. Publius Aelius Proclinus and Aelia Procla set this up for their most devoted parents.
EDCS-15800475.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Publius Aelius Proclinus and Aelia Procla in honor of their deceased parents, Publius Aelius Proclus, a veteran, and Aelia Claudia.

EDCS-15800476.text_edition: Publio Aelio Publi filio Papiria Silvano IIvirali et sacerdotali coloniae Apulensis equiti Romano egregiae memoriae viro Fabia Lucilla egregiae memoriae viri filia mater collegiorum fabrum et centonariorum coloniae supra scriptae socero sui amantissimo
EDCS-15800476.translation: To Publius Aelius Silvanus, son of Publius, of the Papirian tribe, a duumvir and priest of the colony of Apulum, a Roman knight, a man of excellent memory. Fabia Lucilla, daughter of a man of excellent memory, mother of the guilds of artisans and cloth-workers of the aforementioned colony, [set this up] for her most beloved father-in-law.
EDCS-15800476.summary: This is an honorific inscription dedicated by Fabia Lucilla to her father-in-law, Publius Aelius Silvanus, who held several public and religious offices in the colony of Apulum.

EDCS-15800477.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publius Aelius Valerianus patronus et decurio collegii fabrum vixit annos LX deccuriones et principales collegii supra scriptiae re conlato secundum decretum ipsorum faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-15800477.translation: To the Divine Manes. Publius Aelius Valerianus, patron and decurion of the guild of artisans, lived 60 years. The decurions and leading members of the aforementioned guild, having collected contributions according to their decree, had this made.
EDCS-15800477.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to Publius Aelius Valerianus, a patron and decurion of the artisan's guild, commissioned by the guild's members.

EDCS-15800478.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publius Aelius Vetustinus miles legionis XIII geminae stipendiorum V vixit annis
EDCS-15800478.translation: To the Divine Manes. Publius Aelius Vetustinus, soldier of the Thirteenth Twin Legion, with 5 years of service, lived [---] years.
EDCS-15800478.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Publius Aelius Vetustinus, a soldier of the Thirteenth Twin Legion, whose age is partially lost.

EDCS-15800480.text_edition: Dis Manibus quinque hic annorum aetatis conditur infans Aemilius Hermes hanc generavit matris de nomine dixit Plotiam patris praenomine Aemiliam vixit rapuit quam mors inlimine vitae
EDCS-15800480.translation: To the Divine Manes. Here lies a child of five years old, Aemilius Hermes, whom his mother generated and named Plotia, and his father named Aemilia, whom death snatched away on the threshold of life.
EDCS-15800480.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely an epitaph in verse, for a five-year-old child named Aemilius Hermes, noting the names given by his parents.

EDCS-15800481.text_edition: Aemilia Hermias liberta et heres patron- bene merenti fecit
EDCS-15800481.translation: Aemilia Hermias, freedwoman and heir, made this for her well-deserving patron.
EDCS-15800481.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Aemilia Hermias, a freedwoman and heir, for her patron.

EDCS-15800482.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Gaius Aesernus.
EDCS-15800482.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicating a monument to Gaius Aesernus.

EDCS-15800483.text_edition: Dis Manibus - Annius Firmus vixit annis L Domitia Iusta coniux et filii  bene merenti posuerunt hic situs est
EDCS-15800483.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Annius Firmus lived for 50 years. Domitia Iusta, his wife, and his children placed this for the well-deserving. He is buried here.
EDCS-15800483.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Annius Firmus, placed by his wife Domitia Iusta and their children, noting his age and burial place.

EDCS-15800484.text_edition: Dis Manibus Antonius Marci filius Geminianus miles cohortis Ubiorum vixit annos XXXX militavit annis XVIII
EDCS-15800484.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Antonius Geminianus, son of Marcus, a soldier of the cohort of the Ubii, lived for 40 years and served for 18 years.
EDCS-15800484.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Antonius Geminianus, a soldier, detailing his age and years of military service.

EDCS-15800485.text_edition: Caio Antonio Cai filio Papiria Valentino quondam decurioni coloniae Apulensis Caius Antonius Agrippinus a militiis decurio coloniae Napocensis et municipii Potaissensis filius et Gaii Antonii Marcellus Agrippinus decuriones coloniae Apulensis Marcella et Agrippina nepotes eius
EDCS-15800485.translation: To Gaius Antonius Papiria Valentinus, son of Gaius, formerly decurion of the colony of Apulum. Gaius Antonius Agrippinus, son, decurion in military service of the colony of Napoca and the municipality of Potaissa; and Gaius Antonius Marcellus Agrippinus, decurion of the colony of Apulum; and his granddaughters Marcella and Agrippina.
EDCS-15800485.summary: This is a commemorative inscription for Gaius Antonius Papiria Valentinus, a former decurion, set up by his son, another decurion, and his granddaughters.

EDCS-15800486.text_edition: vixit annis LX  Atilius pupillus  cum BEL T
EDCS-15800486.translation: He lived for 60 years. Atilius Pupillus, with BEL T.
EDCS-15800486.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Atilius Pupillus, indicating his age at death.

EDCS-15800487.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucius Atius At CeFleFia#Celeia#CFLFIA veteranus vixit annos LXXXI C
EDCS-15800487.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Atius, a veteran from Celeia, lived for 81 years.
EDCS-15800487.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Atius, a veteran from Celeia, stating his age.

EDCS-15800488.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caius Atrius Cai filius Crustumina Crescens Carsulis miles legionis XIII geminae vixit annis XXXV Valentius Demetrius merenti posuit
EDCS-15800488.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Gaius Atrius Crescens, son of Gaius, of the Crustumina tribe, from Carsulae, a soldier of the Thirteenth Twin Legion, lived for 35 years. Valentius Demetrius placed this for the deserving.
EDCS-15800488.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Atrius Crescens, a soldier of the Thirteenth Legion, placed by Valentius Demetrius.

EDCS-15800492.text_edition: Aureliae Apolloniae Titus Aelius Lupus eques Romanus pontifex et IIviralis coloniae Apulensis matri carissimae
EDCS-15800492.translation: To Aurelia Apollonia, his dearest mother, from Titus Aelius Lupus, Roman knight, pontifex, and duumvir of the colony of Apulum.
EDCS-15800492.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription for Aurelia Apollonia, set up by her son Titus Aelius Lupus, a prominent official in Apulum.

EDCS-15800493.text_edition: Aureliae Placentinae vixit annis LX Titus Aurelius Stephanus libertus patronae bene merenti posuit
EDCS-15800493.translation: To Aurelia Placentina, who lived for 60 years. Titus Aurelius Stephanus, her freedman, placed this for his well-deserving patroness.
EDCS-15800493.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelia Placentina, placed by her freedman Titus Aurelius Stephanus, indicating her age at death.

EDCS-15800494.text_edition: Aureliae Priscillae coniugi pietate et castitate incomparabili Aurelius Sabinus primipilaris
EDCS-15800494.translation: To Aurelia Priscilla, his wife of incomparable piety and chastity, from Aurelius Sabinus, primipilaris.
EDCS-15800494.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription for Aurelia Priscilla, set up by her husband Aurelius Sabinus, a primipilaris, praising her virtues.

EDCS-15800498.text_edition: Dis Manibus Cassiae Saturninae vixit annis L Cassia Ponticilla secundum voluntatem testamenti patronae bene meritae fecit arbitratu Publicii Ianuarii coniugis et Publicii Severi
EDCS-15800498.translation: To the Divine Manes. Cassia Ponticilla, according to the will of her well-deserving patroness, Cassia Saturnina, who lived for 50 years, made this at the discretion of Publicius Ianuarius, her husband, and Publicius Severus.
EDCS-15800498.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the erection of a monument by Cassia Ponticilla for her deceased patroness, Cassia Saturnina, likely around the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-15800499.text_edition: Lucius Caventius Clemens  legionis XIII geminae Severianae vixit annis V Lucius Caventius Apollinaris heres posuit
EDCS-15800499.translation: Lucius Caventius Clemens, of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina Severiana, lived for five years. Lucius Caventius Apollinaris, his heir, set this up.
EDCS-15800499.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Lucius Caventius Apollinaris for Lucius Caventius Clemens, a very young member of the Legio XIII Gemina Severiana, likely dating to the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-15800501.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Censorio Gai filio Sereno Flavia Solva |(centurioni) legionis XIIII geminae Fuscius Successus et Censorius Fortunatus heredes faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-15800501.translation: To the Divine Manes. Fuscius Successus and Censorius Fortunatus, his heirs, took care to have this made for Gaius Censorius Serenus, son of Gaius, from Flavia Solva, a centurion of the Fourteenth Legion Gemina.
EDCS-15800501.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by his heirs, Fuscius Successus and Censorius Fortunatus, to Gaius Censorius Serenus, a centurion of Legio XIIII Gemina from Flavia Solva.

EDCS-15800503.text_edition: Claudiae Marcianae Ulpii Domitianus et Philetus patronae optimae
EDCS-15800503.translation: To Claudia Marciana, Ulpius Domitianus and Philetus, her most excellent patrons.
EDCS-15800503.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription made by Ulpius Domitianus and Philetus to their patroness, Claudia Marciana.

EDCS-15800505.text_edition: Dis Manibus Claudianae Candidae vixit annos XXX Aelius Firminus coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-15800505.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aelius Firminus set this up for his well-deserving wife, Claudiana Candida, who lived for 30 years.
EDCS-15800505.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Aelius Firminus for his wife, Claudiana Candida, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-15800506.text_edition: Cocceiae Valeriae Publius Tenacius Gemellinus Augustalis coloniae Apulensis ex testamento Publi Tenaci Vindicis |(centurionis) legionis Primigeniae XXII loco dato decreto decurionum
EDCS-15800506.translation: To Cocceia Valeria. Publius Tenacius Gemellinus, an Augustalis of the Colonia Apulensis, set this up from the will of Publius Tenacius Vindex, a centurion of the Twenty-Second Legion Primigenia, on a plot of land given by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-15800506.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cocceia Valeria, erected by Publius Tenacius Gemellinus according to the will of Publius Tenacius Vindex, a centurion, on a plot granted by the decurions of Colonia Apulensis.

EDCS-15800507.text_edition: Crescenti Platoris filio vixit annis XXXV Vibius Rufinus signifer legionis XIII geminae heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-15800507.translation: For Crescens, son of Plator, who lived for 35 years. Vibius Rufinus, standard-bearer of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, his heir, took care to have this made.
EDCS-15800507.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Vibius Rufinus, a standard-bearer and heir, to Crescens, son of Plator, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-15900477.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caius Valerius Cai filius domo Claudia Viruni Silvanus veteranus legionis XIII geminae vixit annos LXX Titus Flavius Valerius Valens miles legionis XIII geminae patri piissimo posuit
EDCS-15900477.translation: To the Divine Manes. Gaius Valerius Silvanus, son of Gaius, from the Claudia tribe, from Virunum, a veteran of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, lived for 70 years. Titus Flavius Valerius Valens, a soldier of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, set this up for his most dutiful father.
EDCS-15900477.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Titus Flavius Valerius Valens, a soldier, for his father, Gaius Valerius Silvanus, a veteran of Legio XIII Gemina from Virunum, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-15900479.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcus Mociuncius Valentinus librarius legati legionis XIII geminae vixit annis XXVIIII posuit Valerius Constans veteranus legionis XIII geminae et Floria Ingenua mater filio bene merenti
EDCS-15900479.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Mociuncius Valentinus, a scribe of the legate of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, lived for 29 years. Valerius Constans, a veteran of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, and Floria Ingenua, his mother, set this up for their well-deserving son.
EDCS-15900479.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Valerius Constans and Floria Ingenua for their son, Marcus Mociuncius Valentinus, a scribe of the legate of Legio XIII Gemina, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-15900480.text_edition: Dis Manibus Mucasenus Censorini aeques sic ex singulari consularis vixit annis XX Rescuturme Soiae coniux pientissima posuit  bene merenti
EDCS-15900480.translation: To the Divine Manes. Mucasenus Censorini, a cavalryman, thus from the singular consul, lived for 20 years. Rescuturme Soiae, his most devoted wife, set this up for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-15900480.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Rescuturme Soiae for her husband, Mucasenus Censorini, a cavalryman, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-15900481.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Sentio Cai filio Sulpicia Flacco Antiquaria veterano legionis XIIII geminae Martiae victricis decurioni coloniae Dacicae Sarmizegetusae vixit annos LXXV Caius Sentius Flaccinus filius et heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-15900481.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Gaius Sentius Flaccus Antiquarius, son of Gaius, of the Sulpicia tribe, a veteran of the Fourteenth Legion Gemina Martia Victrix, a decurion of the Dacian colony of Sarmizegetusa. He lived for 75 years. Gaius Sentius Flaccinus, his son and heir, had this monument made.
EDCS-15900481.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Sentius Flaccus Antiquarius, a veteran and decurion, erected by his son and heir, Gaius Sentius Flaccinus, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-15900482.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tutor Silvani eques alae Bosporanorum ex numero Illyricorum stipendiorum XXIII vixit nnos XLIII hic situs est
EDCS-15900482.translation: To the Divine Manes. Tutor Silvani, a cavalryman of the Bosporan wing, from the Illyrian contingent, served for 23 years. He lived for 43 years. He lies here.
EDCS-15900482.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tutor Silvani, a cavalryman of Illyrian origin who served in the Bosporan cavalry, probably from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-15900483.text_edition: Tito Varenio Titi filio Pappiria Sabiniano equiti Romano flamini Laurentino item flamini coloniae Sarmizegetusae decurioni coloniarum Sarmizegetusae et Apulensis egregiae memoriae viro omnibus equestribus militiis perfuncto Cornelia Lucilla coniux pila extructa sarcofagum in quo Varenia Probina quondam Sabiniani soror condita erat etiam eius corpore conlocato superposuit
EDCS-15900483.translation: To Titus Varenius Sabinianus, son of Titus, of the Pappiria tribe, a Roman Knight, flamen of Laurentum, also flamen of the colony of Sarmizegetusa, decurion of the colonies of Sarmizegetusa and Apulum, a man of outstanding memory, having completed all equestrian military service. Cornelia Lucilla, his wife, erected this pillar and placed the sarcophagus in which Varenia Probina, once Sabinianus's sister, had been interred, also placing his body within.
EDCS-15900483.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Varenius Sabinianus, a Roman Knight and prominent local official, erected by his wife Cornelia Lucilla, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-15900485.text_edition: Dis Manibus Sextus Viaeus Sergia Victor Iader eques legionis XIII geminae Sex(tus) Ingenuvius IVR
EDCS-15900485.translation: To the Divine Manes. Sextus Viaeus Victor, of the Sergia tribe, from Iader, a cavalryman of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina. Sextus Ingenuvius [dedicant or relative].
EDCS-15900485.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sextus Viaeus Victor, a cavalryman of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina from Iader, with Sextus Ingenuvius as a potential dedicant or relative, probably from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-15900486.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Ulpio Marci filio Flavino optioni signiferoum legionis XIII geminae stipendiorum XXV vixit annis  Titia Maximilla coniugi piissimo faciendum curavit
EDCS-15900486.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Marcus Ulpius Flavius, son of Marcus, an optio (junior officer) of the standard-bearers of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, who served for 25 years. He lived for [---] years. Titia Maximilla, his most devoted wife, had this monument made.
EDCS-15900486.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Ulpius Flavius, a junior officer of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, erected by his devoted wife Titia Maximilla, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-15900487.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ulpiae Constantinae quae vixit annis XVI mensibus VIIII Ulpius Congonius veteranus ex decurione et Aelia Valeria parentes infelicissimi filiae pientissimae
EDCS-15900487.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Ulpia Constantina, who lived for 16 years and 9 months. Ulpius Congonius, a veteran and former decurion, and Aelia Valeria, her most unhappy parents, [erected this] for their most devoted daughter.
EDCS-15900487.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ulpia Constantina, a young woman who died at 16, erected by her grieving parents, Ulpius Congonius (a veteran and former decurion) and Aelia Valeria, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-15900488.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caius Urbicius Condunus miles legionis XIII geminae stipendiorum IX vixit annis XXX Urbicia Ingenua vixit annis XX Urbicius Senilis vixit annos X posuit Urbicius Spectatus filiis bene merentibus
EDCS-15900488.translation: To the Divine Manes. Gaius Urbicius Condunus, a soldier of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, served for 9 years. He lived for 30 years. Urbicia Ingenua lived for 20 years. Urbicius Senilis lived for 10 years. Urbicius Spectatus placed this for his well-deserving children.
EDCS-15900488.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Urbicius Condunus, a soldier, and two other family members, Urbicia Ingenua and Urbicius Senilis, erected by Urbicius Spectatus for his deserving children, probably from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-15900489.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tito Fabio Ibliomaro domo Augusta Treverorum quondam decurioni kanabarum vixit annis LX Fabii Pulcher Romana Aquileiensis per tutores suos posuerunt
EDCS-15900489.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Titus Fabius Ibliomarus, from Augusta Treverorum, formerly a decurion of the canabarii. He lived for 60 years. Fabii Pulcher and Romana Aquileiensis, through their guardians, placed this.
EDCS-15900489.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Fabius Ibliomarus, a former decurion of the canabarii from Augusta Treverorum, erected by Fabii Pulcher and Romana Aquileiensis through their guardians, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-15900490.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Rustio Finito decurioni coloniae vixit annos L Rustia Censorina
EDCS-15900490.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Lucius Rustius Finitus, a decurion of the colony. He lived for 50 years. Rustia Censorina [dedicated this].
EDCS-15900490.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Rustius Finitus, a decurion of an unnamed colony, likely set up by Rustia Censorina, probably from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-15900491.text_edition: Verzoviae Saturninae equitis Romani filiae Caius Nummius Certus eques Romanus augur coloniae Apulensis patronus collegiorum fabrum et dendrophorum coloniae supra scriptae suadente adfectione matri posuit
EDCS-15900491.translation: To Verzovia Saturnina, daughter of a Roman Knight. Gaius Nummius Certus, a Roman Knight, augur of the colony of Apulum, patron of the guilds of craftsmen and dendrophori of the aforesaid colony, prompted by affection, placed this for his mother.
EDCS-15900491.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Gaius Nummius Certus, a Roman Knight and prominent official in Apulum, in honor of his mother, Verzovia Saturnina, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-15900492.text_edition: Sisiata Vaussi filia Claudia Savaria vixit annis XXXV Iestinius Super coniugi bene merenti hic sita est
EDCS-15900492.translation: Sisiata Claudia Savaria, daughter of Vaussus, lived for 35 years. Iestinius Super to his well-deserving wife. She is buried here.
EDCS-15900492.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Sisiata Claudia Savaria, dedicated by her husband Iestinius Super, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-15900493.text_edition: Dis Manibus Isidorae domo Asiae vixit nnis XVIII mensibus III diebus XIII Primus Augusti despensatoris vikarius sic bene merenti
EDCS-15900493.translation: To the Divine Manes of Isidora from Asia, who lived for 18 years, 3 months, and 13 days. Primus, the deputy dispenser of Augustus, to his well-deserving (servant/colleague).
EDCS-15900493.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Isidora, likely a slave or freedwoman from Asia, made by Primus, a vicarius (deputy) in the Imperial administration, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-15900494.text_edition: Dis Manibus Sertoriae Feliclae domi Aequo vixsit sic annos LX posuit Marcus Vaternius Primigenius bene merenti matri
EDCS-15900494.translation: To the Divine Manes of Sertoria Felicla from Aequum, who lived for 60 years. Marcus Vaternius Primigenius placed this for his well-deserving mother.
EDCS-15900494.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Marcus Vaternius Primigenius to his mother, Sertoria Felicla, who lived for 60 years and was from Aequum, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-15900495.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription preserving parts of two names, Domitius and Luctatius, preventing a full interpretation of its purpose or date.

EDCS-15900496.text_edition: Luci Ianuari Romuli vixit nnis VIIII et Ianuariae Pupae vixit nnis XIII Ianuaria Titia mater filiis posuit
EDCS-15900496.translation: To Lucius Ianuarius Romulus, who lived for 9 years, and to Ianuaria Pupa, who lived for 13 years. Ianuaria Titia, the mother, placed this for her children.
EDCS-15900496.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a mother, Ianuaria Titia, for her two deceased children, Lucius Ianuarius Romulus and Ianuaria Pupa, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-15900497.text_edition: Ingenuus Gai vixit annis LXXV Gaius et Apollodorus posuerunt patri carissimo
EDCS-15900497.translation: Ingenuus, son of Gaius, lived for 75 years. Gaius and Apollodorus placed this for their dearest father.
EDCS-15900497.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Gaius and Apollodorus to their father, Ingenuus, who lived to the age of 75, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-15900498.translation: Iulius Censorinus had this made for his father.
EDCS-15900498.summary: This is an inscription indicating a funerary monument commissioned by Iulius Censorinus for his father, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-15900499.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulia Valeria vixit annis XXX Iulia Iunilla libertae etnon coniugi bene merenti hic siti s(unt)
EDCS-15900499.translation: To the Divine Manes of Iulia Valeria, who lived for 30 years. Iulia Iunilla to her well-deserving freedwoman and also wife. They are buried here.
EDCS-15900499.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Iulia Iunilla for Iulia Valeria, described as both her freedwoman and wife, who lived for 30 years, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-15900501.text_edition: Dis Manibus Memmio Hel vixit annos XLV Iunia Beronice coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-15900501.translation: To the Divine Manes of Memmius Hel[---], who lived for 45 years. Iunia Beronice to her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-15900501.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Iunia Beronice to her husband, Memmius Hel[---], who lived for 45 years, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-15900502.translation: Restituta made this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-15900502.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating a monument made by Restituta for her husband, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-15900503.text_edition: Dis Manibus Seccia Cupiti filia vixit annos LX filii et coniux bene merenti posuerunt hic sita est
EDCS-15900503.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Seccia, daughter of Cupitus, lived 60 years. Her sons and husband placed this for their well-deserving wife/mother. She is buried here.
EDCS-15900503.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Seccia, daughter of Cupitus, erected by her sons and husband, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-15900506.text_edition: Tuticiae Adrastillae vixit nnos XVIII menses II dies XX Tuticia Victoria filia et heres matri karissimae
EDCS-15900506.translation: To Tuticia Adrastilla, who lived 18 years, 2 months, 20 days. Tuticia Victoria, her daughter and heir, [placed this] for her dearest mother.
EDCS-15900506.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tuticia Adrastilla, placed by her daughter and heir, Tuticia Victoria, commemorating her short life.

EDCS-15900507.text_edition: Dis Manibus Vellecii filia Ursina vixit annos XII dies
EDCS-15900507.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Ursina, daughter of Vellecius, lived 12 years, [and a certain number of] days.
EDCS-15900507.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to Ursina, daughter of Vellecius, indicating her young age at death.

EDCS-15900508.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Victorinus lived [a certain number of] years.
EDCS-15900508.summary: This is a very fragmentary funerary inscription for Victorinus, only noting his name and partial lifespan.

EDCS-15900509.text_edition: Dis Manibus Viria Publia Virii Publii filia vixit annis XXI Virius
EDCS-15900509.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Viria Publia, daughter of Virius Publius, lived 21 years. Virius [placed this].
EDCS-15900509.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Viria Publia, daughter of Virius Publius, who died at age 21, likely erected by a male relative named Virius.

EDCS-15900510.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ulpia Flava vixit annis LX Herennia Lucia et Herennia Secundina matri pientissimae posuerunt
EDCS-15900510.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Ulpia Flava lived 60 years. Herennia Lucia and Herennia Secundina placed this for their most devoted mother.
EDCS-15900510.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ulpia Flava, erected by her daughters, Herennia Lucia and Herennia Secundina, in honor of their pious mother.

EDCS-15900511.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ulpiae Renatae vixit nnis XXXV Aurelius Mac
EDCS-15900511.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Ulpia Renata, who lived 35 years. Aurelius Mac[...].
EDCS-15900511.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Ulpia Renata, who died at age 35, and was likely dedicated by Aurelius Mac[something].

EDCS-15900512.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ulpia Rustica vixit annis XXV IPXIITC
EDCS-15900512.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Ulpia Rustica lived 25 years. IPXIITC.
EDCS-15900512.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ulpia Rustica, who died at age 25, with an unclear dedicatory formula or abbreviation following her age.

EDCS-15900513.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ursulus vixit annis XXIIII Lupula fratri bene merenti posuit
EDCS-15900513.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Ursulus lived 24 years. Lupula placed this for her well-deserving brother.
EDCS-15900513.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ursulus, who died at age 24, erected by his sister Lupula, indicating a sibling bond.

EDCS-15900516.text_edition: Publio Tenacio Publi filio Papiria Vindici |(centurioni) legionis XXII Primigeniae buleutae civitatis Nicopolitanorum Publius Tenacius Gemellinus Augustalis coloniae Apulensis libertus testamentum ponendum curavit locus datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-15900516.translation: To Publius Tenacius, son of Publius, of the Papirian tribe, Vindix, centurion of the XXII Primigenia Legion, councilor of the city of Nicopolis. Publius Tenacius Gemellinus, Augustalis of the colony of Apulum, his freedman, took care of placing this testament. The ground was given by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-15900516.summary: This is an honorary and funerary inscription for Publius Tenacius Vindix, a military officer and civic dignitary, placed by his freedman Publius Tenacius Gemellinus, with the burial plot granted by municipal decree, likely dating to the High Imperial period.

EDCS-15900517.text_edition: Dis Manibus Fabricius Iucundus vixit annis L Fabricius Iucundus heres et Fabricii Silvanus  et Licinianus fratres cum collegio fabrorum titulum posuerunt fretri pientissimo
EDCS-15900517.translation: To the Divine Manes. Fabricius Iucundus lived for 50 years. Fabricius Iucundus, the heir, and Fabricii Silvanus and Licinianus, his brothers, together with the college of craftsmen, set up this monument for their most devoted brother.
EDCS-15900517.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by the heir and brothers of Fabricius Iucundus, along with a college of craftsmen, to their deceased brother, Fabricius Iucundus, who lived for 50 years.

EDCS-15900518.text_edition: Dasanti Scenobarbi equiti alae Batavorum ex numero singularium stipendiorum Vannorum XXXI Bersius Ingenuus decurio ex numero eodem herres sic posuit
EDCS-15900518.translation: To Dasas Scenobarbus, a knight of the Batavian ala from the special corps, who served 31 years among the Vannians. Bersius Ingenuus, a decurion from the same corps, his heir, erected this as such.
EDCS-15900518.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Bersius Ingenuus, a decurion, to Dasas Scenobarbus, a knight of the Batavian cavalry unit, who served 31 years in a special corps.

EDCS-15900519.text_edition: Caio Cervonio Papiria Sabino quinquennali coloniae Dacicae decurioni municipii Apulensis patrono collegii fabrum coloniae et municipii supra scriptorum patrono causarum piissimo amico rarissimo Sextus Sentinas Maximus anno primo facti municipii posuit  ob cuius statuae dedicationem Lucia Iulia uxor Cervonii per omnes balneas populo publice oleum posuit loco dato decurionum decreto
EDCS-15900519.translation: To Gaius Cervonius Papiria Sabinus, quinquennial magistrate of the Dacian colony, decurion of the municipality of Apulum, patron of the college of craftsmen of the above-mentioned colony and municipality, a most devoted patron of legal cases, a most rare friend. Sextus Sentinas Maximus erected this in the first year of the municipality's establishment. For the dedication of whose statue, Lucia Iulia, wife of Cervonius, publicly provided oil for the people in all the baths, by a place given by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-15900519.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Sextus Sentinas Maximus to Gaius Cervonius Papiria Sabinus, a distinguished local official and patron, celebrating the dedication of his statue and the public benefaction by his wife, Lucia Iulia, in the first year of the municipality's founding.

EDCS-15900520.text_edition: Marco Firmio Bassiano et Marco Firmio Severo vixerunt
EDCS-15900520.translation: Marcus Firmius Bassianus and Marcus Firmius Severus lived.
EDCS-15900520.summary: This appears to be a very brief, possibly incomplete, funerary inscription for Marcus Firmius Bassianus and Marcus Firmius Severus.

EDCS-15900521.text_edition: D(is) Manibus M(arcus) Horatius Marci filius
EDCS-15900521.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Horatius, son of Marcus.
EDCS-15900521.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription dedicated to Marcus Horatius, son of Marcus.

EDCS-15900522.text_edition: Dis Manibus Mammulo vixit annis XXV Ulpio Clono vixit mense I Ulpius Iuvenalis Mammulo annorum XII Mammulo piissimo
EDCS-15900522.translation: To the Divine Manes. Mammulo lived for 25 years. Ulpius Clono lived for 1 month. Ulpius Iuvenalis (for) Mammulo, 12 years old. To the most devoted Mammulo.
EDCS-15900522.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Mammulo, who lived for 25 years, Ulpius Clono, who lived for 1 month, and a 12-year-old Mammulo, likely erected by Ulpius Iuvenalis for the latter.

EDCS-15900523.translation: To the Divine Manes. Is Diofanis.
EDCS-15900523.summary: This is a concise funerary inscription dedicated to Diofanis.

EDCS-15900526.translation: a freedman, and Flavius Silvanus erected this.
EDCS-15900526.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription indicating that a freedman and Flavius Silvanus were the dedicants of a monument.

EDCS-15900529.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulia Serena vixit annos LXXXI Gaius Bellicius Quintianus amitae piissimae fecit
EDCS-15900529.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulia Serena lived for 81 years. Gaius Bellicius Quintianus made this for his most devoted aunt.
EDCS-15900529.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Gaius Bellicius Quintianus for his most devoted aunt, Iulia Serena, who lived for 81 years.

EDCS-15900531.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcianus Avitianus vixerunt annos V posuit l Restuta nepotibus
EDCS-15900531.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcianus and Avitianus lived for 5 years. Restuta placed this for her nephews.
EDCS-15900531.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Restuta to her nephews, Marcianus and Avitianus, who both lived for 5 years.

EDCS-15900533.text_edition: Dis Manibus Mucatra Brasi miles numeri Palmyrenorum Tibiscensium vixit annis XXXVIIII Mucapor Mucatralis heres contubernali carissimo posuit
EDCS-15900533.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Mucatra Brasi, a soldier of the unit of Palmyrenes from Tibiscum, lived for 39 years. Mucapor Mucatralis, his heir, set this up for his dearest comrade.
EDCS-15900533.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Mucatra Brasi, a Palmyrene soldier, erected by his heir and comrade Mucapor Mucatralis, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-15900539.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caius Secundinius Eutyches vixit annos XX-- Vibia Annia coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-15900539.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Gaius Secundinius Eutyches lived for 20 years. Vibia Annia set this up for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-15900539.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Gaius Secundinius Eutyches, erected by his wife Vibia Annia, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-15900540.translation: Severus, his heir, saw to its erection.
EDCS-15900540.summary: This fragment suggests a funerary monument whose erection was overseen by an heir named Severus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-15900544.text_edition: LII vixit annos LV hic situs est Valeria Verecunda coniux et filii pientissimi posuerunt
EDCS-15900544.translation: LII lived for 55 years. He is buried here. Valeria Verecunda, his wife, and his most devoted children set this up.
EDCS-15900544.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an individual whose name is partially lost, but who lived 55 years and was buried by his wife Valeria Verecunda and his devoted children, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-15900545.text_edition: ius Vindex Fabricio Apollinari liberto  G adstante e liberto
EDCS-15900545.translation: --- Vindex to Fabricius Apollinaris, his freedman, with G. --- present, from his freedman.
EDCS-15900545.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly recording a legal or dedicatory act involving a freedman named Fabricius Apollinaris and his patron, and another freedman, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-15900546.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ulpiae Maxsimillae posuit Artorius Victor coniugi bene merenti vixit annis XXV
EDCS-15900546.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Ulpia Maximilla. Artorius Victor set this up for his well-deserving wife. She lived for 25 years.
EDCS-15900546.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ulpia Maximilla, who lived 25 years, erected by her husband Artorius Victor, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-15900547.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ulpiae Victorinae omni obsequio maritali Caius Iulius Iulianus a militiis maritus et Iulia Ursula filia
EDCS-15900547.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Ulpia Victorina, with all marital devotion, Gaius Julius Julianus, her husband from military service, and Julia Ursula, her daughter.
EDCS-15900547.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ulpia Victorina, dedicated by her husband Gaius Julius Julianus, a veteran, and her daughter Julia Ursula, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-15900556.translation: of the legion... Can...? decurion... VaA...
EDCS-15900556.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning a decurion of a legion, suggesting a military context and likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-15900572.text_edition: libertus Qua et coniux  et pia filia
EDCS-15900572.translation: freedman Qua... and his wife... and devoted daughter...
EDCS-15900572.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, mentioning a freedman, his wife, and his devoted daughter, suggesting a family monument from the Imperial period.

EDCS-15900576.text_edition: vixit annis X Proc  E IO
EDCS-15900576.translation: lived for 10 years. Proc... E IO...
EDCS-15900576.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, indicating an individual who lived for 10 years, and includes a partially preserved name or title, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-15900587.translation: To Marcus Clapius Onicus, in Sarmizegetusa.
EDCS-15900587.summary: This is a brief inscription, likely a dedication or epitaph, for Marcus Clapius Onicus in Sarmizegetusa, though its precise monument type is not specified.

EDCS-16000076.text_edition: Caio Iulio Cai filio Theveste Corinthiano praefecto cohortis VII Gallorum tribuno cohortis I Brittannicae item vexillationis Dacorum Parthicae cui ob virtutem suam sacratissimi imperatores coronam muralem hastam puram et vexilum argento insignem dederunt praefecto alae Campagonum idem |(miliariae) vixit annis XXXVIIII Marcius Arrianus et Iulii Clinias et Pisonianus heredes faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-16000076.translation: To Gaius Julius, son of Gaius, a Corinthian from Theveste, prefect of the Seventh Cohort of Gauls, tribune of the First Cohort of Britons, also of a vexillation of Dacians and Parthians, to whom, for his valor, the most sacred emperors gave a mural crown, a pure spear, and a standard distinguished with silver, prefect of the milliary Ala Campagonum. He lived for 39 years. Marcius Arrianus and Iulius Clinias and Pisonianus, his heirs, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-16000076.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the military career and honors of Gaius Julius, a distinguished Roman officer from Theveste, erected by his heirs after his death at 39 years old, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-16000080.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription mentioning the name Alexander, with the rest of the text missing or illegible, making its original purpose uncertain.

EDCS-16000081.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription bearing the name Marcus Ulpius Secundus, likely part of a larger dedication, epitaph, or ownership mark.

EDCS-16000082.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, only preserving a few letters and part of the name Ulpius, making its original content and purpose entirely unclear.

EDCS-16000095.summary: This is a highly fragmentary inscription with only a few letters preserved, making its content and purpose completely undeterminable.

EDCS-16000109.summary: This inscription names two personified winds, Zephyr and Eurus, and is likely part of a larger monument, perhaps a sundial, a weather vane, or a decorative architectural element.

EDCS-16000110.text_edition: Dis Paternis Tiberius Claudius Crispinus pro salute sua suorumque ex voto posuit
EDCS-16000110.translation: To Dis Pater, Tiberius Claudius Crispinus, for his own well-being and that of his family, placed this according to a vow.
EDCS-16000110.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Dis Pater (god of the underworld) by Tiberius Claudius Crispinus for his and his family's welfare, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-16000111.text_edition: Diis Patriis Lucius Valerius Aristio pro salute sua et suorum voto libens posuit
EDCS-16000111.translation: To the Ancestral Gods, Lucius Valerius Aristio willingly placed this for his own well-being and that of his family, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-16000111.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Ancestral Gods by Lucius Valerius Aristio, made willingly to fulfill a vow for the well-being of himself and his family.

EDCS-16000121.text_edition: Invicto Deo pro salute Cai Iuli Rufini liberorumque eius Spatalus servus actor votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-16000121.translation: To the Unconquered God, for the well-being of Gaius Iulius Rufinus and his children, Spatalus, a slave and agent, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-16000121.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to an unnamed 'Unconquered God' by Spatalus, a slave and agent, on behalf of Gaius Iulius Rufinus and his children, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-16000122.summary: This is a simple titulus recording the name and military rank of Publius Aelius Valerianus, a speculator.

EDCS-16000123.summary: This fragmentary inscription provides two names, Maximius and Aelius Gaius, with the initial letters 'IIC' whose meaning is unclear.

EDCS-16000497.text_edition: Silvano Domestico Aurelius Titus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-16000497.translation: To Silvanus Domesticus, Aurelius Titus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-16000497.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Aurelius Titus to Silvanus Domesticus, likely a domestic protective deity, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-16000499.text_edition: Libero Patri et Liberae Conservatoribus Aelius Superi sic augur municipii Septimi Potaissensium voto libens posuit
EDCS-16000499.translation: To Liber Pater and Libera Conservatrix, Aelius Super, thus an augur of the municipium of Septimum Potaissensium, willingly placed this in accordance with his vow.
EDCS-16000499.summary: This is a votive dedication made by Aelius Super, an augur of the municipium of Septimum Potaissensium, to Liber Pater and Libera Conservatrix, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-16100179.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning a procurator Augusti, an imperial financial administrator, but the rest of the text is lost.

EDCS-16100180.translation: Aphrodisia, as a result of a vow.
EDCS-16100180.summary: This is a short votive inscription dedicated by Aphrodisia, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-16100188.text_edition: Marius Aurelianus princeps numeri porticum deum stravit
EDCS-16100188.translation: Marius Aurelianus, princeps numeri, paved the portico of the gods.
EDCS-16100188.summary: This inscription records that Marius Aurelianus, a military officer (princeps numeri), funded the paving of a portico dedicated to the gods.

EDCS-16100253.text_edition: Silvano Silvestri posuit Fortunatus Theupropi votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-16100253.translation: To Silvanus Silvestris, Fortunatus Theupropi placed this; he willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-16100253.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Fortunatus Theupropi to Silvanus Silvestris, a sylvan deity, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-16100254.text_edition: Libero Patri Marcus ValeIririus#Valeririus#VALIRIRIUS Victor decurio coloniae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-16100254.translation: To Liber Pater, Marcus Valerius Victor, a decurion of the colony, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-16100254.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Marcus Valerius Victor, a decurion of a Roman colony, to Liber Pater, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-16100322.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelia Prisosta vixit nnos XXXIII Aurelia Bassina filia vixit nnos VII Aurelius Denzi vixit nnos LXXX Aurelius Bassus signifer numeri M O coniugi et filiis bene merentibus posuit
EDCS-16100322.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Aelia Prisosta lived 33 years. Aurelia Bassina, daughter, lived 7 years. Aurelius Denzi lived 80 years. Aurelius Bassus, standard-bearer of the numerus M. O., placed this for his well-deserving wife and children.
EDCS-16100322.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Aurelius Bassus, a military standard-bearer, for his wife Aelia Prisosta, his daughter Aurelia Bassina, and Aurelius Denzi, likely a family member, commemorating their lives.

EDCS-16100743.text_edition: Dis Manibus Quinto Canio Quinti filio Claudiae Celeiae Restituto veterano legionis XIII decurioni coloniae Sarmizegetusae vixit annos LXXX Canius Respectus et Cania Atticilla filii item Ulpia Admata coniunx posuerunt bene merenti
EDCS-16100743.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Quintus Canius Restitutus, son of Quintus, of the Claudia tribe, a veteran of the Thirteenth Legion, a decurion of the colony of Sarmizegetusa, who lived 80 years. Canius Respectus and Cania Atticilla, his children, and also Ulpia Admata, his wife, placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-16100743.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Canius Respectus, Cania Atticilla, and Ulpia Admata for Quintus Canius Restitutus, a veteran and decurion of Sarmizegetusa, who died at the age of 80.

EDCS-16100853.text_edition: Aesculapio et Hygiae Marcus Antonius Saturninus decurio coloniae votum solvit libens merito  Aesculapio et Hygiae Marcus Antonius Saturninus decurio coloniae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-16100853.translation: To Aesculapius and Hygia, Marcus Antonius Saturninus, decurion of the colony, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow. To Aesculapius and Hygia, Marcus Antonius Saturninus, decurion of the colony, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-16100853.summary: This is a votive inscription to Aesculapius and Hygia, dedicated by Marcus Antonius Saturninus, a decurion of the colony, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-16100854.text_edition: Dianae Marcus Antonius Saturninus decurio coloniae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-16100854.translation: To Diana, Marcus Antonius Saturninus, decurion of the colony, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-16100854.summary: This is a votive inscription to Diana, dedicated by Marcus Antonius Saturninus, a decurion of the colony, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-16100855.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Antonius Saturninus decurio coloniae votum solvit libens merito  Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Antonius Saturninus votum solvit libens merito
