EDCS-16100855.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Marcus Antonius Saturninus, decurion of the colony, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow. To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Marcus Antonius Saturninus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-16100855.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, dedicated by Marcus Antonius Saturninus, a decurion of the colony, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-16100857.text_edition: Terrae Matri Marcus Antonius Saturninus decurio coloniae votum solvit libens merito  Terrae Matri Marcus Antonius Saturninus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-16100857.translation: To Mother Earth, Marcus Antonius Saturninus, decurion of the colony, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow. To Mother Earth, Marcus Antonius Saturninus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-16100857.summary: This is a votive inscription to Mother Earth, dedicated by Marcus Antonius Saturninus, a decurion of the colony, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-16100858.text_edition: Deo aeterno Zmaragdus Augusti libertus tabularius et Aurelia Urbica coniux et Matrona filia votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-16100858.translation: To the Eternal God, Zmaragdus, freedman of Augustus and tabularius, and Aurelia Urbica, his wife, and Matrona, their daughter, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-16100858.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to an unnamed Eternal God by Zmaragdus, an Augustan freedman and clerk, his wife Aurelia Urbica, and their daughter Matrona, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-16100860.text_edition: Dianae sacrum pro salute Antoniae Rufae Victorinus servus
EDCS-16100860.translation: Sacred to Diana, for the health of Antonia Rufa, by Victorinus, a slave.
EDCS-16100860.summary: This is a votive dedication to Diana, offered by Victorinus, a slave, on behalf of Antonia Rufa's well-being.

EDCS-16100861.text_edition: Domino et Dominae Satrius Felix beneficiarius procuratoris pro se et suos sic votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-16100861.translation: To the Lord and Lady, Satrius Felix, a beneficiarius of the procurator, for himself and his people, thus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-16100861.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to an unspecified Lord and Lady by Satrius Felix, a beneficiarius of the procurator, on behalf of himself and his family, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-16100863.text_edition: Fortunae Augustae Publi Aelii Victorinus Victor Fabianus Victorinus pater cum filiis
EDCS-16100863.translation: To Fortuna Augusta, Publius Aelius Victorinus Victor, Fabianus Victorinus, father with his sons.
EDCS-16100863.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Fortuna Augusta by Publius Aelius Victorinus Victor and his sons, Fabianus Victorinus and another Victorinus.

EDCS-16100864.text_edition: Herculi Invicto sacrum Aelius Theseus aurarius  libens laetus posuit
EDCS-16100864.translation: Sacred to Hercules Invictus, Aelius Theseus, a goldsmith, willingly and joyfully placed this.
EDCS-16100864.summary: This is a votive inscription to Hercules Invictus, dedicated by Aelius Theseus, a goldsmith.

EDCS-16100865.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  [[]] [[]] [[]] []R ductum ma domo IIviri et ordo Ampelensium Caio Aurelio Salviano procuratore Augusti nostri curante
EDCS-16100865.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. [---] R was led from the house of the duumvir, and the order of the Ampelenses, under the care of Gaius Aurelius Salvianus, procurator of our Augustus.
EDCS-16100865.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an aqueduct or similar structure to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by the local council of Ampelum, overseen by an imperial procurator, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-16100866.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Aurelius Alexander miles numeri Maurorum Hispanorum Antoniniani ex iussu dei fecit
EDCS-16100866.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Marcus Aurelius Alexander, soldier of the Mauri Hispani Antoniniani unit, made this by order of the god.
EDCS-16100866.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, dedicated by a soldier from a Moorish Spanish unit, acting on a divine command, likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-16100867.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Aurelius Diocles beneficiarius procuratoris votum solvit libens Imperatoribus Augustis consulibus
EDCS-16100867.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Titus Aurelius Diocles, beneficiarius of the procurator, willingly fulfilled his vow when the Augusti were consuls.
EDCS-16100867.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, dedicated by an imperial administrative assistant, noting that the vow was fulfilled during the consulate of the emperors (Augusti), likely in the 3rd century AD.

EDCS-16100868.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Iunius Nedymus
EDCS-16100868.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Junius Nedymus.
EDCS-16100868.summary: This is a simple dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Junius Nedymus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-16100869.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Neptunalis Augusti libertus tabularius aurariarum Dacicarum votum bene merenti posuit
EDCS-16100869.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Neptunalis, freedman of Augustus, archivist of the Dacian gold mines, placed this vow for a well-deserving recipient.
EDCS-16100869.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, dedicated by an imperial freedman working in the Dacian gold mines, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-16100870.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Semppronius Urbanus procurator Augusti
EDCS-16100870.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Gaius Sempronius Urbanus, procurator of Augustus.
EDCS-16100870.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by an imperial procurator, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-16100871.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Titi Aeli Hadriani Antonini Augusti Pii patris patriae Marcus Statius Priscus legatus eius pro praetore
EDCS-16100871.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. For the well-being of the Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of his country, Marcus Statius Priscus, his legate acting as propraetor.
EDCS-16100871.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, made on behalf of Emperor Antoninus Pius by his imperial legate, Marcus Statius Priscus, dating to the mid-2nd century AD.

EDCS-16100872.text_edition: Inferis Dis Manibus MMORTM AVG RPI MS Mercurius Caesaris n(ostri) servus RIRMIRO
EDCS-16100872.translation: To the Infernal Manes Gods. [---] Mercurius, slave of our Caesar [---].
EDCS-16100872.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead, mentioning Mercurius, a slave of the emperor, likely from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-16100873.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo aeterno conservatori Callistus Augusti nostri dispensator pro salute sua et suorum ex voto posuit
EDCS-16100873.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, eternal preserver. Callistus, dispenser of our Augustus, placed this according to a vow for his own health and that of his family.
EDCS-16100873.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, dedicated by an imperial financial administrator for his own and his family's well-being, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-16100874.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Commagenorum Aeterno Marinus Mariani Bassus sacerdos Iovis Optimi Maximi Dolicheni pro salute sua suorumque omnium votum VH
EDCS-16100874.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus of the Commagenians, the Eternal. Marinus, son of Marianus Bassus, priest of Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, fulfilled his vow for his own safety and that of all his people.
EDCS-16100874.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, specifically identified with the Commagenian and Dolichenian cults, made by a priest for his and his family's well-being, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-16100875.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno et deo Commagenorum Aurelius Marinus et Adde Barsemei et Oceanus Socratis sacerdotes votum libentes posuerunt
EDCS-16100875.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Dolichenus, and to the god of Commagene, Aurelius Marinus and Adde Barsemei and Oceanus Socratis, priests, willingly placed this vow.
EDCS-16100875.summary: This inscription records a dedication by three priests, Aurelius Marinus, Adde Barsemei, and Oceanus Socratis, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus and the god of Commagene, likely a votive offering.

EDCS-16100876.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Dolichenus, Modestius [dedicates this].
EDCS-16100876.summary: This inscription is a simple dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus by an individual named Modestius.

EDCS-16100877.text_edition: Libero Patri et Liberae Herculianis et cervabus Romanus Augusti nostri et AuYrelia#Aurelia#AYR Chreste votum  libenter posuerunt
EDCS-16100877.translation: To Father Liber and Libera, Herculean, and to the stags, Romanus, freedman of our Augustus, and Aurelia Chreste willingly placed this vow.
EDCS-16100877.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Romanus, an imperial freedman, and Aurelia Chreste, to Father Liber and Libera Herculean, and to the stags, indicating a cultic dedication.

EDCS-16100878.text_edition: Nemesi Augustae Vitalis Cai Aponi votum solvit
EDCS-16100878.translation: To Nemesis Augusta, Vitalis, slave of Gaius Aponius, fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-16100878.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Vitalis, a slave of Gaius Aponius, to Nemesis Augusta, suggesting a dedication to the imperial goddess of retribution.

EDCS-16100879.text_edition: Silvano sacrum Leonas Augusti libertus adiutor tabularii pro salute sua suorumque omnium ex voto libens posuit
EDCS-16100879.translation: Sacred to Silvanus. Leonas, freedman of Augustus, assistant of the record-office, willingly placed this from a vow for his own health and that of all his kin.
EDCS-16100879.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Silvanus by Leonas, an imperial freedman and an assistant in the tabularium, for his and his family's health.

EDCS-16100880.text_edition: Deo Silvan=MIo#Silvano#SILVMIO Domestico Senti Aper et Philumenus votum libentes posuerunt
EDCS-16100880.translation: To the domestic god Silvanus, Sentius Aper and Philumenus willingly placed this vow.
EDCS-16100880.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Sentius Aper and Philumenus to the domestic god Silvanus.

EDCS-16100882.text_edition: Anniae Lucillae Augustae Imperatoris Veri Augusti Armeniaci Parthici maximi liberti et familia et leguli aurariarum
EDCS-16100882.translation: To Annia Lucilla Augusta, wife of Emperor Verus Augustus Armeniacus Parthicus Maximus, the freedmen and household and agents of the gold mines [dedicate this].
EDCS-16100882.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Annia Lucilla Augusta, likely from her freedmen, household, and the agents of the gold mines, acknowledging her imperial status as the wife of Emperor Verus.

EDCS-16100884.text_edition: Pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Antonini Pii Felicis patris patriae  eius  legatus Augusti pro praetore  ETLBD
EDCS-16100884.translation: For the health of Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix, father of his country, the imperial legate with propraetorian power [dedicates this].
EDCS-16100884.summary: This inscription is a dedication for the health of Emperor Caracalla (Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix) by an imperial legate with propraetorian power, characteristic of official imperial cult dedications.

EDCS-16100885.text_edition: I(ovi)? O(ptimo) Maximo Lucius Macrius Macer procurator Auggustorum
EDCS-16100885.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Lucius Macrius Macer, procurator of the Augusti [dedicates this].
EDCS-16100885.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Lucius Macrius Macer, a procurator in service of the emperors, likely in a mining region.

EDCS-16100886.text_edition: Memoriae Papirii Rufi FA PINAPQ procuratoris aurariarum liberti et heredes patrono pientissimo fecerunt et sibi
EDCS-16100886.translation: To the memory of Papirius Rufus, procurator of the gold mines, his freedmen and heirs made this for their most devoted patron and for themselves.
EDCS-16100886.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument dedicated to Papirius Rufus, a procurator of the gold mines, erected by his freedmen and heirs for their patron and for themselves, indicating a collective remembrance.

EDCS-16100887.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Ulpio Augusti liberto Hermiae procuratori aurariarum cuius reliquiae ex indulgentia Augusti nostri Romam latae sunt Salonia Palestrice coniunx et Diogenes libertus bene merenti fecerunt vixit nnos LV
EDCS-16100887.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Marcus Ulpius Hermias, freedman of Augustus, procurator of the gold mines, whose remains were brought to Rome by the indulgence of our Augustus. Salonia Palestrice, his wife, and Diogenes, his freedman, made this for the well-deserving. He lived 55 years.
EDCS-16100887.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a freedman of Augustus who served as a procurator, set up by his wife and freedman, likely dating to the 2nd century AD based on the imperial names.

EDCS-16100888.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ulpiae Marci libertae Trophimae coniugi sanctissimae Neptunalis Augusti nostri libertus
EDCS-16100888.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Ulpia Trophima, freedwoman of Marcus, most sacred wife. Neptunalis, freedman of our Augustus.
EDCS-16100888.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Neptunalis, an imperial freedman, to his most sacred wife, Ulpia Trophima, who was a freedwoman of Marcus, probably Marcus Aurelius, suggesting a mid-2nd century AD date.

EDCS-16100889.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iustinus Caesaris nostri verna subsequens librariorum vixit annis XV Tertius verna valdenio pientissimo bene merenti posuit
EDCS-16100889.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iustinus, home-born slave of our Caesar, a junior clerk, lived for 15 years. Tertius, a home-born slave, placed this for his most devoted, well-deserving Valdenio.
EDCS-16100889.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iustinus, a young imperial slave who worked as a clerk, erected by another slave, Tertius, likely dating to the Imperial period given the mention of 'Caesar'.

EDCS-16100890.text_edition: Dis Manibus Sossia Sabina vixit nnos XXVI et fecit in matrimonio nnos XI menses X sine ulla querella Fuscinus verna ab instrumentis tabularii coniugi bene merenti fecit
EDCS-16100890.translation: To the Divine Manes. Sossia Sabina lived 26 years and spent 11 years and 10 months in marriage without any complaint. Fuscinus, a home-born slave from the archive instruments, made this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-16100890.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Fuscinus, a slave working in a public or imperial archive, for his wife, Sossia Sabina, commemorating her life and blameless marriage, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-16100892.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marci Aureli Antonini militis legionis XIII geminae vixit nnis XXII mensibus XI diebus II militavit nnis V librarius Aurelius Marcianus et Valeria Valentina filio pientissimo
EDCS-16100892.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, soldier of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, who lived 22 years, 11 months, 2 days; he served for 5 years as a librarius. Aurelius Marcianus and Valeria Valentina for their most devoted son.
EDCS-16100892.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young soldier, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, who served as a clerk in the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, erected by his parents, likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD due to the 'Aurelius' nomen.

EDCS-16100893.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publio Helvio Primano militi legionis XIII geminae librario cons(ularis) Publius Helvius Primus pater filio IVNL
EDCS-16100893.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Publius Helvius Primanus, soldier of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, clerk to the consular. Publius Helvius Primus, father, for his son.
EDCS-16100893.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Publius Helvius Primus to his son, Publius Helvius Primanus, a soldier and consular clerk in the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, likely from the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-16100895.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcus Valerianus vixit annos LX et mensem I Menonia Gemellina vixit annos LXV Marcus Aurelius Maximinus veteranus legionis XIII geminae vixit annos L Menonnianus bene meritis
EDCS-16100895.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Valerianus lived 60 years and 1 month. Menonia Gemellina lived 65 years. Marcus Aurelius Maximinus, veteran of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, lived 50 years. Menonnianus for the well-deserving.
EDCS-16100895.summary: This is a collective funerary inscription set up by Menonnianus for three individuals: Marcus Valerianus, Menonia Gemellina, and Marcus Aurelius Maximinus, a veteran of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, probably from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-16100896.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publius Aelius Iulianus vixit mensibus V diebus XXV Publius Aelius Provincialis decurio coloniae filio posuit
EDCS-16100896.translation: To the Divine Manes. Publius Aelius Iulianus lived 5 months, 25 days. Publius Aelius Provincialis, decurion of the colony, placed this for his son.
EDCS-16100896.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an infant, Publius Aelius Iulianus, dedicated by his father, Publius Aelius Provincialis, a decurion of an unnamed colony, likely from the Hadrianic or Antonine period.

EDCS-16100897.text_edition: Dis Manibus Titus Aurelius Afer Delmata princeps adsignato sic ex municipio Splono vixit nnos XXX Aurelius Sattara libertus patrono optimo posuit
EDCS-16100897.translation: To the Divine Manes. Titus Aurelius Afer Delmata, 'princeps adsignato' from the municipality of Splonum, lived 30 years. Aurelius Sattara, freedman, placed this for his best patron.
EDCS-16100897.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Aurelius Afer Delmata, a 'princeps adsignato' from Splonum, dedicated by his freedman, Aurelius Sattara, likely dating to the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-16100898.text_edition: Publio Celsenio Constanti decurioni coloniae Delmatiae sic Claudia Aequo item decurioni coloniae Dacicae vixit annos XXX Marcus Opellius Adiutor IIvir coloniae Dacicae heres testamento vivus posuit
EDCS-16100898.translation: To Publius Celsenius Constans, decurion of the colony of Dalmatia, and likewise to Claudia Aequo, also decurion of the Dacic colony, who lived 30 years. Marcus Opellius Adiutor, duumvir of the Dacic colony, heir by testament, placed this during his lifetime.
EDCS-16100898.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Celsenius Constans and Claudia Aequo, both decurions of colonies, dedicated by Marcus Opellius Adiutor, a duumvir and heir, who prepared it during his lifetime, probably from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-16100899.text_edition: Inferis Dis Manibus Asclepius et Asclepiades cives Bithinum sic vixit annos XXX Affia coniunx bene merenti posuit
EDCS-16100899.translation: To the Infernal Gods, the Manes. Asclepius and Asclepiades, citizens of Bithynium. He lived for 30 years. Affia, his wife, placed this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-16100899.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Affia, the wife, to her husband, who was a citizen of Bithynium and lived 30 years, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16100902.text_edition: Dis Manibus Calaudianus posuit filio Claudio Maximo annorum septem AV S bene merenti
EDCS-16100902.translation: To the Manes. Calaudianus placed this for his son, Claudius Maximus, who lived for seven years. A.V.S. to the well-deserving.
EDCS-16100902.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Calaudianus for his seven-year-old son, Claudius Maximus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16100903.text_edition: Dis Manibus Claudia Tertullina vixit annos IIII Claudia Vera mater
EDCS-16100903.translation: To the Manes. Claudia Tertullina lived for 4 years. Claudia Vera, her mother, [placed this].
EDCS-16100903.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Claudia Tertullina, aged four, placed by her mother, Claudia Vera, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16100905.text_edition: Dis Manibus Opellia Victorina vixit annos X menses III et Marcus Opilius Crispinus vixit annos IIII menses V dies XIII et Marcus Opilius Firminus vixit annos
EDCS-16100905.translation: To the Manes. Opellia Victorina lived for 10 years and 3 months, and Marcus Opilius Crispinus lived for 4 years, 5 months, and 13 days, and Marcus Opilius Firminus lived for [an unknown number of] years.
EDCS-16100905.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating three individuals, Opellia Victorina and two boys named Marcus Opilius, likely placed by their family during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16100906.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valerius Rufinus vixit nnos L et Opelia Calityche vixit annos LXVIII Valerius Crescens libertus eorum bene merentibus posuit
EDCS-16100906.translation: To the Manes. Valerius Rufinus lived for 50 years, and Opelia Calityche lived for 68 years. Valerius Crescens, their freedman, placed this for his well-deserving patrons.
EDCS-16100906.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Valerius Crescens, a freedman, to his former master and mistress, Valerius Rufinus and Opelia Calityche, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16100907.text_edition: Dis Manibus Victorina quae vixit annis plus minus -- posuit pater Herculan(us)
EDCS-16100907.translation: To the Manes. Victorina, who lived for more or less [a certain number of] years. Her father, Herculanus, placed this.
EDCS-16100907.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Herculanus for his daughter, Victorina, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16100911.text_edition: Dis Manibus IV DVP vixit annos V MP FRNI DASNI VCISE TVSRV Aureli- C
EDCS-16100911.translation: To the Manes. I.V.D.V.P. lived for 5 years. M.P.F.R.N.I. D.A.S.N.I. V.C.I.S.E. T.V.S.R.V. Aurelius C. [---].
EDCS-16100911.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for an individual who lived for five years, with other largely illegible text, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16200025.text_edition: Iuliae Augustae matri sanctissimi piissimique Antonini Augusti et castrorum senatusque ac patriae cohors I Brittanica |(miliaria) Antoniniana
EDCS-16200025.translation: To Julia Augusta, mother of the most holy and pious Emperor Antoninus, and of the camps, the Senate, and the fatherland. The First Cohort of Britons, one thousand strong, Antoniniana.
EDCS-16200025.summary: This is a military dedication made by the First Cohort of Britons (Cohors I Brittanica miliaria Antoniniana) to Julia Augusta, likely during the reign of Caracalla (Antoninus Augusti), honoring her role as mother of the emperor, army, senate, and fatherland.

EDCS-16300044.text_edition: Dianae et Silvano sacrum Publius Aelius Atilianus decurio ex singulari consularis fecit
EDCS-16300044.translation: Sacred to Diana and Silvanus. Publius Aelius Atilianus, decurion, made this according to the special instruction of the consular.
EDCS-16300044.summary: This is a religious dedication to the gods Diana and Silvanus, made by Publius Aelius Atilianus, a decurion, acting under specific orders from a consular, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16300047.translation: Legio XIII Gemina. [Property/possession] of Annaeus Saturninus.
EDCS-16300047.summary: This is an ownership inscription indicating a possession belonging to Annaeus Saturninus, likely a soldier or officer of the Legio XIII Gemina, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16300421.text_edition: A N  ex voto posuit Silvanno sic Libero Patri
EDCS-16300421.translation: A.N. placed this as a vow to Silvanus, and also to Liber Pater.
EDCS-16300421.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Silvanus and Liber Pater by an individual identified as A.N.

EDCS-16400035.translation: Lucretius Aquila of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina.
EDCS-16400035.summary: This fragment records the name Lucretius Aquila and his association with the Thirteenth Legion Gemina.

EDCS-16400367.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Fulguratori Publius Aelius Maximianus augur coloniae votum solvit
EDCS-16400367.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus the Thunderer. Publius Aelius Maximianus, augur of the colony, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-16400367.summary: This inscription records a fulfilled vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Fulgurator by Publius Aelius Maximianus, an augur of a colony.

EDCS-16400369.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Petronius Marcianus votum libens solvit
EDCS-16400369.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Petronius Marcianus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-16400369.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, recording that Petronius Marcianus willingly fulfilled his vow.

EDCS-16700204.text_edition: Timocleti Caesaris nostri ab instrumentis vixit annos XXXV Bona Caesaris nostri serva nepoti posuit
EDCS-16700204.translation: Timocletus, our Caesar's assistant for instruments, lived for 35 years. Bona, our Caesar's slave, set this up for her grandson.
EDCS-16700204.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Timocletus, an assistant to a Caesar, set up by his grandmother Bona, a slave of the same Caesar.

EDCS-16700298.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Marcus Aurelius Ex[...]
EDCS-16700298.summary: This is a fragmentary votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marcus Aurelius Ex[...].

EDCS-18300738.text_edition: Legio XIII gemina Aeli Brogitarus IIC
EDCS-18300738.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina. Aeli Brogitarus, IIC.
EDCS-18300738.summary: This fragment mentions the Thirteenth Legion Gemina and an individual named Aeli Brogitarus with an unclear abbreviation IIC.

EDCS-20400534.text_edition: Lucius Flavius Valens ob honorem flamonii Bono Puero dedit
EDCS-20400534.translation: Lucius Flavius Valens gave this to Bonus Puer on account of the honor of the flamonium.
EDCS-20400534.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Lucius Flavius Valens to Bonus Puer, likely related to his holding of a flamonium priesthood.

EDCS-20400535.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Antonio Gordiano Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate II consuli patri patriae colonia Ulpia Traiana Augusta Dacica Sarmizegetusa metropolis dicatissima numini maiestatique eius
EDCS-20400535.translation: To Imperator Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the second time, consul, father of the fatherland. The most devoted colony Ulpia Traiana Augusta Dacica Sarmizegetusa, metropolis, dedicates this to his divinity and majesty.
EDCS-20400535.summary: This is a monumental dedication from the colony of Ulpia Traiana Augusta Dacica Sarmizegetusa to Emperor Gordian III, dating to his second year of tribunician power (240 CE).

EDCS-20400536.text_edition: Publio Furio Saturnino legato Augusti pro praetore consuli colonia Dacica Sarmizegetusa praesidi dignissimo
EDCS-20400536.translation: To Publius Furius Saturninus, legate of Augustus with propraetorian power, consul, most worthy governor. The colony Dacica Sarmizegetusa [dedicates this].
EDCS-20400536.summary: This is an honorific inscription dedicated by the colony Dacica Sarmizegetusa to Publius Furius Saturninus, who served as a legate and governor and held the rank of consul.

EDCS-20900025.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Lucius vixit annis X Aurelius Iulianus pater filio posuit
EDCS-20900025.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelius Lucius lived for 10 years. Aurelius Julianus, his father, placed this for his son.
EDCS-20900025.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the short life of Aurelius Lucius, erected by his father Aurelius Julianus, likely dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-20900029.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio collegii pomarensium  Valerius Ianuarius pater et Flavia Crescentina mater pro salute filiorum suorum cultores Iovis Optimi Maximi ex promisso fecerunt  Numini aeterno Iovis Optimi Maximi cultores pomarensium Valerius Valerianus I Octavius Cassianus  Iulius Silvanus Iulius Dignus Valerius Ianuarius IARSIA
EDCS-20900029.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and to the Genius of the guild of fruit-sellers, Valerius Januarius, father, and Flavia Crescentina, mother, for the well-being of their sons, worshipers of Jupiter Optimus Maximus, made this as promised. To the eternal divine will of Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the worshipers of the fruit-sellers: Valerius Valerianus I, Octavius Cassianus, Julius Silvanus, Julius Dignus, Valerius Januarius, IARSIA.
EDCS-20900029.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by parents and members of a guild of fruit-sellers to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and their guild's Genius, made for the well-being of their sons as a promise fulfilled, and lists several individuals involved in the dedication, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-20900032.translation: Comitialis made this for the Iovenii.
EDCS-20900032.summary: This is a brief dedicatory or funerary inscription recording that Comitialis made something for the Iovenii family or group, typical of Roman epitaphs or small monuments.

EDCS-23500001.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Iulius Herculanus decurio scholae fabrum iimaginifer vixit nnos LXXX Iulia Vivenia coniux Iulius Marcianus filius immaginifer scholae fabrum vixit nnos XXVII Aurelius Iulius miles cohortis I sagittariorum immaginifer vixit nnos XXX Iulius Marcellinus filius vexillarius scholae fabrum vixit nnos XXV Iulia Marcia filia vixit nnos XIIII Iulia Heraclia filia vixit nnos VIIII Iulia Marcellina nepos vixit nnos IIII Vivenia mater se viva fecit
EDCS-23500001.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Julius Herculanus, decurion of the college of craftsmen, standard-bearer, lived for 80 years. Julia Vivenia, his wife, Julius Marcianus, son, standard-bearer of the college of craftsmen, lived for 27 years. Aurelius Julius, soldier of the First Cohort of Archers, standard-bearer, lived for 30 years. Julius Marcellinus, son, vexillarius of the college of craftsmen, lived for 25 years. Julia Marcia, daughter, lived for 14 years. Julia Heraclia, daughter, lived for 9 years. Julia Marcellina, granddaughter, lived for 4 years. Vivenia, the mother, made this while alive.
EDCS-23500001.summary: This extensive funerary inscription records multiple generations of a family, including men who served in military roles and a college of craftsmen, with their ages at death, and was set up by Vivenia, likely during the Imperial period.

EDCS-24100202.text_edition: Divo Vero Parthico maximo fratri Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Antonini Augusti Armeniaci Medici Germanici Parthici maximi tribuniciae potestatis XXVI imperatoris V patris patriae consulis III proconsulis colonia Ulpia Traiana Augusta Dacica Sarmizegetusa
EDCS-24100202.translation: To the deified Verus, greatest Parthian victor, brother of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, Armeniacus, Medicus, Germanicus, greatest Parthian victor, holding tribunician power for the 26th time, imperator for the 5th time, father of his country, consul for the 3rd time, proconsul, the colony Ulpia Traiana Augusta Dacica Sarmizegetusa.
EDCS-24100202.summary: This is a public dedication from the colony Ulpia Traiana Augusta Dacica Sarmizegetusa, honoring the deified Lucius Verus as the brother of Emperor Marcus Aurelius and listing the emperor's numerous titles and accomplishments, likely dating to Marcus Aurelius's reign (around 172-175 AD).

EDCS-24400843.text_edition: Placidae Reginae equites voto libentes posuerunt per Proculo sic  principe et Gaio optione
EDCS-24400843.translation: To Placida Regina, the cavalrymen willingly placed this as a vow through Proculus, thus, the princeps, and Gaius, the optio.
EDCS-24400843.summary: This is a votive inscription by a group of cavalrymen dedicated to Placida Regina, indicating their fulfillment of a vow, likely overseen by military officials Proculus and Gaius, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-24400844.text_edition: Isidi et Serapi Caius Iulius Antigonus |(centurio)  legionis V Macedonicae piae cO(onstantis)#c(onstantis)#O et Flavia Apollinaria eius votum libentes solverunt
EDCS-24400844.translation: To Isis and Serapis, Gaius Julius Antigonus, centurion of the Fifth Legion Macedonica, Pia Constantis, and Flavia Apollinaria, his wife, willingly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-24400844.summary: This is a votive inscription to Isis and Serapis, made by Gaius Julius Antigonus, a centurion of the Fifth Legion Macedonica, and his wife Flavia Apollinaria, indicating the fulfillment of a religious promise, likely dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-24400845.text_edition: Isidi myrionimae sic Caius Iulius Martialis pater et Lucius Livius Victorinus quaestor collegio Isidis dono dederunt
EDCS-24400845.translation: To Isis of countless names, Gaius Julius Martialis, father, and Lucius Livius Victorinus, quaestor, gave this as a gift to the college of Isis.
EDCS-24400845.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Isis, referring to her 'countless names', given by Gaius Julius Martialis and Lucius Livius Victorinus (a quaestor) as a gift to a college dedicated to Isis, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-24500528.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno pro salute et incolumitate Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Antoni Gordiani Pii Felicis Augusti et cohortis III Campestris Marcus Aurelius Flavus IIIIvir municipii Septimi Porolissensis Marcus Antonius Maximus veteranus et decurio ornatus ornamentis IIIIviralibus municipii supra scripti et Aurelius Flavus decurio municipii vegesimarius  sic sacerdotes dei et cohortis supra scriptae templum cum tabernis aere suo fecerunt
EDCS-24500528.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, for the health and safety of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus and of the Third Campestrian Cohort, Marcus Aurelius Flavus, quattuorvir of the Municipium Septimium Porolissense, Marcus Antonius Maximus, veteran and decurion honored with quattuorviral ornaments of the aforesaid municipium, and Aurelius Flavus, decurion of the municipium, vigesimarius, priests of the god and of the aforesaid cohort, built the temple with shops at their own expense.
EDCS-24500528.summary: This is a grand dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, for the well-being of Emperor Gordian and the Third Campestrian Cohort, with multiple municipal and military officials acting as priests of the god and cohort funding the construction of a temple with shops at their own expense, dating to the reign of Gordian III (238-244 AD).

EDCS-24500530.text_edition: Dis Manibus Terentio Liccai filio vixit annos X posuit Licinius fratri bene merenti
EDCS-24500530.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Terentius, son of Liccai, who lived for 10 years. Licinius placed this for his well-deserving brother.
EDCS-24500530.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Terentius, son of Liccai, who died at age 10, erected by his brother Licinius, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-24500532.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription marking the ownership or dedication to Publius Labius Rufinus.

EDCS-26600722.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Fulguratori pro salute sua et suorum Marcus Aurelius Decoratus decurio coloniae Aequi flamen aedilis et Marcus Aurelius Philippianus decurio coloniae Aequi quaestor  votum solverunt libentes merito Perpetuo et Corneliano consulibus VIII Idus Iunias
EDCS-26600722.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Fulgurator, for his own health and that of his family, Marcus Aurelius Decoratus, decurion of the colony of Aequum, flamen and aedile, and Marcus Aurelius Philippianus, decurion of the colony of Aequum, quaestor, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow. On the 8th day before the Ides of June, when Perpetuus and Cornelianus were consuls.
EDCS-26600722.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Fulgurator by two local magistrates, Marcus Aurelius Decoratus and Marcus Aurelius Philippianus, fulfilling a vow for their health and that of their families, dated to June 6th, 237 CE.

EDCS-26600723.text_edition: Iunoni Marcus Aurelius Rogatianus pro salute sua et suorum votum libens merito solvit
EDCS-26600723.translation: To Juno, Marcus Aurelius Rogatianus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for his own health and that of his family.
EDCS-26600723.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Juno by Marcus Aurelius Rogatianus, fulfilling a vow for the well-being of himself and his family.

EDCS-26600724.text_edition: Mercurio sacrum Marcus Aurelius Rogatianus pro salute sua et suorum ex viso
EDCS-26600724.translation: Sacred to Mercury. Marcus Aurelius Rogatianus [made this offering] for his own health and that of his family, in consequence of a vision.
EDCS-26600724.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Mercury by Marcus Aurelius Rogatianus, made in response to a divine vision, for the health of himself and his family.

EDCS-26600725.text_edition: Terrae Matri Marcus Aurelius Rogatianus pro salute sua et suorum votum libens merito solvit
EDCS-26600725.translation: To Mother Earth, Marcus Aurelius Rogatianus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for his own health and that of his family.
EDCS-26600725.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Mother Earth by Marcus Aurelius Rogatianus, fulfilling a vow for the health of himself and his family.

EDCS-26600726.text_edition: Marti Patri conservatori et bonae Victoriae Lucius Aelius Rufinus pro se et suos voto libens posuit
EDCS-26600726.translation: To Father Mars the preserver and to good Victory, Lucius Aelius Rufinus willingly placed this according to his vow for himself and his family.
EDCS-26600726.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Mars Pater Conservator and Bona Victoria by Lucius Aelius Rufinus, fulfilling a vow for himself and his family.

EDCS-26600727.text_edition: pro salute dominorum nn[nostrorum] Luci Septimi Severi et Marci Aureli Antonini et [Publi Septimi Getae] Caesaris Augg[gustorum] cultores Iovis  dedicatum VIII Kalendas Augustas Muciano et Fabiano consulibus
EDCS-26600727.translation: For the health of our lords Lucius Septimius Severus and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus and Publius Septimius Geta Caesar, the Augusti, the worshipers of Jupiter dedicated this on the 8th day before the Kalends of August, when Mucianus and Fabianus were consuls.
EDCS-26600727.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the worshipers of Jupiter for the health of the emperors Septimius Severus, Caracalla, and Geta, dated to July 25th, 201 CE.

EDCS-26600728.text_edition: Dis Manibus Scauriani Caesaris nostri servus villicus sic vixit annos XXIII Fuscianus fratri bene merenti posuit
EDCS-26600728.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Scaurianus, our Caesar's slave, the bailiff, thus lived 23 years. Fuscianus placed this for his well-deserving brother.
EDCS-26600728.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Fuscianus for his brother Scaurianus, a slave and bailiff of 'our Caesar,' who lived for 23 years.

EDCS-26600729.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Romanius Encolpus ex voto
EDCS-26600729.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Marcus Romanius Encolpus, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-26600729.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marcus Romanius Encolpus, made in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-26600730.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno ex praecepto numinis Aesculapi somno monitus Veturius Marcianus veteranus legionis XIII geminae pro salute sua suorumque
EDCS-26600730.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, by the command of the deity Aesculapius, warned in a dream, Veturius Marcianus, veteran of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, for his own health and that of his family.
EDCS-26600730.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus by Veturius Marcianus, a veteran of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, prompted by a dream from Aesculapius, for the health of himself and his family.

EDCS-26600732.translation: I, Zenovius, placed this vow for Christ.
EDCS-26600732.summary: This is a brief Christian votive inscription made by an individual named Zenovius, likely dating to the late Roman period.

EDCS-26600733.text_edition: Marti Gradivo cohors II Flavia Commagenorum cui praeest Caius Vettius Sabinianus praefectus
EDCS-26600733.translation: To Mars Gradivus, the Second Flavian Cohort of Commagenians, commanded by Gaius Vettius Sabinianus, its prefect.
EDCS-26600733.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the god Mars Gradivus made by a military unit, the Cohors II Flavia Commagenorum, under the command of its prefect Gaius Vettius Sabinianus.

EDCS-26600734.text_edition: Aureliae Ianuariae filiae piissimae quae vixit annis IIII mensibus VII diebus XVI Romanus Augusti libertus procurator ACAD pater
EDCS-26600734.translation: To Aurelia Ianuaria, his most devoted daughter, who lived for 4 years, 7 months, 16 days. Romanus, freedman of Augustus, imperial procurator of the ACAD, her father.
EDCS-26600734.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelia Ianuaria, a young daughter who died at the age of four, erected by her father Romanus, an imperial freedman and procurator.

EDCS-26600737.text_edition: pro Herennio Gemellino viro egregio procuratore Auggustorum nnostrorum agente vice praesidis marito suo libens solvit
EDCS-26600737.translation: For Herennius Gemellinus, an excellent man, procurator of our Augusti, acting vice-governor, his wife willingly paid this.
EDCS-26600737.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription made by a wife for her husband, Herennius Gemellinus, who served as an imperial procurator and acting vice-governor.

EDCS-26600742.text_edition: Deae Nemesi Aelius Diogenes et Silia Valeria pro salute sua et filiorum suorum mater et pater ex voto a solo templum ex suo fecerunt collegio utriculariorum
EDCS-26600742.translation: To the Goddess Nemesis, Aelius Diogenes and Silia Valeria, for their own well-being and that of their children, the mother and father, made this temple from its foundation at their own expense, according to a vow, as part of the Guild of Utricularii (Bargemen).
EDCS-26600742.summary: This inscription records the construction of a temple to the goddess Nemesis by Aelius Diogenes and Silia Valeria, a husband and wife, for their health and their children's, as part of their vow and their association with the guild of bargemen.

EDCS-26600743.text_edition: Libero Patri pro salute Marci Turrani Dii et Flaviae Aeliae Nices Marcus Turranius Patroclus ex voto
EDCS-26600743.translation: To Liber Pater, for the health of Marcus Turranius Dii and Flavia Aelia Nices, Marcus Turranius Patroclus, according to a vow.
EDCS-26600743.summary: This is a votive inscription to Liber Pater, made by Marcus Turranius Patroclus for the health of Marcus Turranius Dii and Flavia Aelia Nices, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-26600744.text_edition: Soli Invicto Numini Mithrae pro salute Publi Aeli Mari Hermadio actor Turrani Dii votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-26600744.translation: To the Unconquered Sun, the Divinity Mithras, for the health of Publius Aelius Marius, Hermadius, agent of Turranius Dii, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26600744.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Sol Invictus Mithras, made by Hermadius, an agent of Turranius Dii, for the well-being of Publius Aelius Marius, fulfilling his vow willingly and deservedly.

EDCS-26600745.text_edition: Corneliae Saloninae Augustae coniugi Gallieni Augusti nostri ordo municipii Tibiscensium devotus numini maiestatique eius
EDCS-26600745.translation: To Cornelia Salonina Augusta, wife of our Augustus Gallienus, the order of the municipality of Tibiscum, devoted to her divinity and majesty.
EDCS-26600745.summary: This inscription is a civic dedication by the municipal council of Tibiscum, expressing their devotion to Cornelia Salonina Augusta, the empress and wife of Emperor Gallienus, dating it to the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-26600746.text_edition: Quinto Marcio Turboni Frontoni Publicio Severo praefecto praetorio Imperatoris Caesaris Aelii Traiani Hadriani Augusti patris patriae colonia Ulp=II(ia)#Ulp(ia)#UII Trainana Augusta Dacica Sarmizegetusa
EDCS-26600746.translation: To Quintus Marcius Turbo Frontonianus Publicius Severus, praetorian prefect of the Emperor Caesar Aelius Traianus Hadrianus Augustus, father of the fatherland, by the colony Ulpia Traiana Augusta Dacica Sarmizegetusa.
EDCS-26600746.summary: This inscription is a public dedication by the colony of Ulpia Traiana Augusta Dacica Sarmizegetusa to Quintus Marcius Turbo Frontonianus Publicius Severus, a praetorian prefect under Emperor Hadrian, indicating a date in the first half of the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-26600747.text_edition: D(is) M(anibus)  Publi Aeli Ulpi veterani ex decurione hanc sedem longo placuit sacrare labori hanc requiem fessos tandem qua conderet artus Ulpius emeritis longaevi muneris annis ipse suo curam titulo dedit ipse sepulcri arbiter hospitium membris fatoque paravit
EDCS-26600747.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Publius Aelius Ulpius, a veteran, formerly a decurion. This seat, by long labor, he chose to consecrate; this rest, where at last he might lay his weary limbs. Ulpius, with the long years of his service fulfilled, himself cared for this inscription, himself prepared this sepulcher, an arbiter of fate, a resting place for his limbs.
EDCS-26600747.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Aelius Ulpius, a veteran and former decurion, who personally prepared and dedicated his own tomb as a final resting place.

EDCS-26600749.text_edition: Iarse Caes(aris vixit nnos L posuit Iulia Succcessa coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-26600749.translation: Iarse, a slave of Caesar, lived for 50 years. Iulia Successa placed this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-26600749.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Iulia Successa to her husband Iarse, a slave of Caesar, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-26600752.text_edition: Dis Manibus Flavia Ingenua vixit nnos XX Iulius Flavianus coniugi moestissimae bene merenti posuit
EDCS-26600752.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Flavia Ingenua lived for 20 years. Iulius Flavianus placed this for his most sorrowful and well-deserving wife.
EDCS-26600752.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Iulius Flavianus for his wife, Flavia Ingenua, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-26600754.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publio Aelio Ariorto IIIIviro annuali municipii Drobetae interfecto a latronibus vixit annos L Ulpia Digna coniugi pientissimo et Publius Aelius filius et Publii Aelii Valens et Audarus nepotes bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-26600754.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Publius Aelius Ariortus, annual quattuorvir of the municipality of Drobeta, killed by robbers. He lived for 50 years. Ulpia Digna, his most devoted wife, and Publius Aelius, his son, and Publii Aelii Valens and Audarus, his grandsons, placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-26600754.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Publius Aelius Ariortus, a municipal official in Drobeta, who was killed by robbers, erected by his wife, son, and grandsons.

EDCS-26600755.text_edition: Diis magnis et bonis Aesculapio et Hygiae Marcus Aurelius Veteranus praefectus legionis XIII geminae Gallienianae votum libens merito posuit
EDCS-26600755.translation: To the great and good gods, Aesculapius and Hygia, Marcus Aurelius Veteranus, prefect of the Thirteenth Gemina Gallieniana Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26600755.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Aesculapius and Hygia by Marcus Aurelius Veteranus, a legionary prefect, likely dating to the reign of Gallienus in the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-26600756.text_edition: Aesculapio et Hygiae pro salute Iuniae Cyrillae quod a longa infirmitate virtute aquarum numinis sui revocaverunt Titus B A eius votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-26600756.translation: To Aesculapius and Hygia, for the health of Iunia Cyrilla, because they recalled her from a long illness by the power of the waters of their divine will, Titus B. A. willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-26600756.summary: This is a votive inscription to Aesculapius and Hygia, made on behalf of Iunia Cyrilla for her recovery from illness, fulfilled by Titus B. A.

EDCS-26600757.text_edition: Dis et Numinibus Aquarum Ulpius Secundinus Marius Valens Pomponius Haemus Iulius Carus Valerius Valens legati Romam ad consulatum Severiani clarissimi viri missi incolumes reversi ex voto e a
EDCS-26600757.translation: To the Gods and Numen of the Waters. Ulpius Secundinus, Marius Valens, Pomponius Haemus, Iulius Carus, Valerius Valens, envoys sent to Rome for the consulship of the most distinguished man Severianus, returned safely from their vow.
EDCS-26600757.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the local water deities by a group of envoys who safely returned from a mission to Rome concerning the consulship of Severianus.

EDCS-26600759.text_edition: Herculi pro salute Imperatorum Severi et Antonini fili conservatori Augustorum dominorum nostrorum Claudius Gallus clarissimus vir legatus eorum pro praetore cum suis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-26600759.translation: To Hercules, for the safety of the Emperors Severus and Antoninus, his son, the preserver of our lords the Augusti, Claudius Gallus, a most distinguished man, their legate pro praetore, with his retinue, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26600759.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Hercules for the well-being of the emperors Septimius Severus and Caracalla (Antoninus), set up by their legate Claudius Gallus, likely dating to the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-26600760.text_edition: Herculi pro salute Imperatoris Marci Aureli Antonini Pii Augusti et Iuliae Domnae Augustae matri Augusti et castrorum sub cura Iuli Paterni procuratis Syntrophus vilicus
EDCS-26600760.translation: To Hercules, for the safety of the Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus and Iulia Domna Augusta, mother of the Augustus and of the camps, under the care of Iulius Paternus, procurator, Syntrophus, bailiff.
EDCS-26600760.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Hercules for the welfare of the emperor Caracalla (Marcus Aurelius Antoninus) and his mother Iulia Domna, set up by Syntrophus, a bailiff, under the authority of procurator Iulius Paternus.

EDCS-26600761.text_edition: Herculi Genio loci fontibus calidis Calpurnius Iulianus vir clarissimus legatus legionis V Macedonicae legatus Augusti pro praetore provinciae Daciae superioris votum libens solvit
EDCS-26600761.translation: To Hercules, the Genius of the Place, and the warm springs, Calpurnius Iulianus, a most distinguished man, legate of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, legate of Augustus pro praetore of the province of Dacia Superior, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26600761.summary: This is a votive inscription by Calpurnius Iulianus, a Roman governor of Dacia Superior and legate of Legio V Macedonica, dedicated to Hercules, the local genius, and the thermal springs.

EDCS-26600762.text_edition: Herculi et Veneri Mercurius pr EL N cum suis
EDCS-26600762.translation: To Hercules and Venus, Mercurius, praefectus (? / praeses?) E. L. N. with his retinue.
EDCS-26600762.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Hercules and Venus by Mercurius, possibly a prefect or governor, accompanied by his retinue, with some abbreviations remaining unclear.

EDCS-26600763.text_edition: Herculi Augusto sacrum Felix Iuli Saturnini conductoris publici portorii tertiae partis ex privatis stationis Tsiernensis IIII Idibus Decembribus anno XI Barbaro et Regulo consulibus ex voto posuit
EDCS-26600763.translation: Sacred to Hercules Augustus. Felix, an employee of Julius Saturninus, a public contractor of the third part of the portorium, from private stations of Tsiernensis, placed this on the 4th Ides of December in the 11th year, in the consulship of Barbarus and Regulus, as vowed.
EDCS-26600763.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Felix, an employee of a tax collector, to Hercules Augustus, fulfilling a vow on December 10th in a year corresponding to the consulship of Barbarus and Regulus, likely around 215 or 217 CE, given the 'year 11' likely referring to an emperor's tribunician power count which would place it in Caracalla's reign.

EDCS-26600764.text_edition: Herculi invicto Titus Aurelius Geminianus veteranus legionis XIII geminae Antoninianae ex voto posuit
EDCS-26600764.translation: To Hercules the Unconquered. Titus Aurelius Geminianus, a veteran of the Thirteenth Gemina Antoniniana Legion, placed this as vowed.
EDCS-26600764.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Hercules the Unconquered by Titus Aurelius Geminianus, a veteran of the Legio XIII Gemina Antoniniana, made in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-26600765.text_edition: Herculi invicto Publius Claudius Iulius decurio coloniae Drubetae votum solvit
EDCS-26600765.translation: To Hercules the Unconquered. Publius Claudius Julius, a decurion of the colony of Drubeta, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26600765.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Hercules the Unconquered by Publius Claudius Julius, a decurion from the colony of Drubeta.

EDCS-26600766.text_edition: Herculi invicto Lucius Pompeius Celer praefectus cohortis I Ubiorum votum solvit
EDCS-26600766.translation: To Hercules the Unconquered. Lucius Pompeius Celer, prefect of the First Cohort of Ubii, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26600766.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Hercules the Unconquered by Lucius Pompeius Celer, a prefect of the Cohors I Ubiorum.

EDCS-26600767.text_edition: Herculi salutifero Quintus Vibius Amillus Augustalis coloniae Dacicae pro salute Iuliani fili sui
EDCS-26600767.translation: To Hercules the Health-bringing. Quintus Vibius Amillus, Augustalis of the colony of Dacica, for the health of his son Julianus.
EDCS-26600767.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Hercules Salutifer by Quintus Vibius Amillus, an Augustalis of the colony of Dacica, made for the health of his son Julianus.

EDCS-26600768.text_edition: Herculi sancto Simonius Iulianus vir clarissimus praeses Daciarum
EDCS-26600768.translation: To Holy Hercules. Simonius Julianus, a most distinguished man, governor of the Dacias.
EDCS-26600768.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Holy Hercules by Simonius Julianus, a vir clarissimus and governor of the Dacias, likely dating to the late 3rd or 4th century CE.

EDCS-26600769.text_edition: Herculi sancto Eutyches actor Publi Aeli Antipatri ex voto posuit
EDCS-26600769.translation: To Holy Hercules. Eutyches, agent of Publius Aelius Antipater, placed this as vowed.
EDCS-26600769.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Holy Hercules by Eutyches, an agent of Publius Aelius Antipater, made in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-26600770.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Antestius Gaius pro salute sua et suorum votum libens posuit
EDCS-26600770.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Antestius Gaius willingly placed this vow for his own health and that of his family.
EDCS-26600770.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Antestius Gaius, who willingly fulfilled a vow for his own health and that of his family.

EDCS-26600771.text_edition: Deo Sucello pro salute Marci Sedati Severiani legati Augusti
EDCS-26600771.translation: To the god Sucellus, for the health of Marcus Sedatius Severianus, imperial legate.
EDCS-26600771.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Sucellus made for the health of Marcus Sedatius Severianus, an imperial legate, likely dating to the mid-2nd century CE.

EDCS-26600772.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari divi Hadriani filio divi Traiani nepoti divi Nervae pronepoti Tito Aelio Hadriano Antonino Augusto Pio pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XXIII consuli IIII
EDCS-26600772.translation: To Imperator Caesar, son of the deified Hadrian, grandson of the deified Trajan, great-grandson of the deified Nerva, Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 23rd time, consul for the 4th time.
EDCS-26600772.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Emperor Antoninus Pius, detailing his imperial titles and honors, specifically dating to the 23rd year of his tribunician power and his 4th consulship, which corresponds to 160 CE.

EDCS-26600773.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Publio Licinio Gallieno Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate consuli III proconsuli cohors III Delmatarum Valeriana Galliena |(miliaria) eqquitata civium Romanorum pia fidelis devota numini maiestatique eorum sic
EDCS-26600773.translation: To the Imperator Caesar Publius Licinius Gallienus, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power, consul for the third time, proconsul: the Third Cohort of Delmatians, Valeriana Galliena, one thousand strong, mounted, of Roman citizens, pious, faithful, devoted to their divinity and majesty, thus (dedicated this).
EDCS-26600773.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the Third Cohort of Delmatians to Emperor Gallienus, likely for a monument or public work, dating to approximately 257-260 AD when Gallienus held his third consulate.

EDCS-26600774.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Peditianus nondum plenos annos tres vixit annis duobus mensibus VI diebus XIIII qui et a innocentia parentum suorum sua manu cecidit Aurelius Peditus et Aurelia Donata filio innocenti
EDCS-26600774.translation: To the Shades of the Departed. Aurelius Peditianus lived not yet three full years, (but) two years, six months, fourteen days. Aurelius Peditus and Aurelia Donata, his innocent parents, laid him to rest with their own hands due to his innocence.
EDCS-26600774.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Aurelius Peditus and Aurelia Donata for their young son, Aurelius Peditianus, who died at the age of two and a half years.

EDCS-26600775.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Iulio Luci filio Sergia Basso decurioni municipii Drobetae quaestori interfecto a latronibus vixit annos XXXX Iulii Iulianus et Bassus patri piissimo et Iulius Valerianus frater mortem eius exsecutus faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-26600775.translation: To the Shades of the Departed. To Lucius Iulius Bassus, son of Lucius, of the Sergian tribe, decurion of the municipium of Drobeta, quaestor, killed by bandits. He lived forty years. Iulius Iulianus and Bassus, his most devoted father, and Iulius Valerianus, his brother, who avenged his death, saw to its erection.
EDCS-26600775.summary: This is a funerary monument for Lucius Iulius Bassus, a municipal official in Drobeta who was killed by bandits, erected by his father and brother who avenged his death.

EDCS-26600776.text_edition: Paulus decurio coloniae coniugi carissimae posuit
EDCS-26600776.translation: Paulus, decurion of the colony, placed this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-26600776.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Paulus, a decurion of a colony, dedicated to his beloved wife.

EDCS-26600777.text_edition: Genio Turmazgadae Aurelius Dionisius sic curator posuit
EDCS-26600777.translation: To the Genius of Turmazgada, Aurelius Dionisius, thus (as) curator, placed this.
EDCS-26600777.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the local deity (Genius) of Turmazgada by Aurelius Dionisius, acting in his capacity as a curator.

EDCS-26600778.text_edition: Herculi Augusto sacrum Lucilius Felix et Domitius Herculanus magistri cultorum Herculis donum dederunt
EDCS-26600778.translation: Sacred to Hercules Augustus. Lucilius Felix and Domitius Herculanus, masters of the cult of Hercules, gave this gift.
EDCS-26600778.summary: This inscription records a dedication or gift to Hercules Augustus made by Lucilius Felix and Domitius Herculanus, who were masters of the cult of Hercules.

EDCS-26600780.text_edition: Isidi ex voto templum fecit Domitia Varenii Pudentis Vettia  et Au
EDCS-26600780.translation: Domitia Vettia, daughter of Varenus Pudens, and Au[---], built a temple to Isis in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-26600780.summary: This inscription records the construction of a temple to the goddess Isis by Domitia Vettia and another individual, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-26600783.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo ala I Bosporanorum cui praeest Caius Valerius Gracilis praefectus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-26600783.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. The First Ala of Bosporanians, commanded by Gaius Valerius Gracilis, prefect, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-26600783.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by the First Ala of Bosporanians, led by their prefect Gaius Valerius Gracilis, offered willingly and deservedly.

EDCS-26600785.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus CocceiLus#Cocceius#COCCLUS Themo votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-26600785.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Marcus Cocceius Themo willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26600785.summary: This is a votive inscription by Marcus Cocceius Themo, dedicating something to Jupiter Optimus Maximus in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-26600786.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Crispus Luci |(centurio) cohortis II Commagenorum votum solvit
EDCS-26600786.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Crispus, son of Lucius, centurion of the Second Cohort of Commagenians, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26600786.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Crispus, a centurion of the Second Cohort of Commagenians, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-26600789.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae pro salute Miciensium Lucius Atilius Faustinus et Marcus Ulpius Romanus magistri votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-26600789.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, to Juno the Queen, for the welfare of the people of Micia, Lucius Atilius Faustinus and Marcus Ulpius Romanus, masters, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-26600789.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter and Juno, dedicated by two masters, Lucius Atilius Faustinus and Marcus Ulpius Romanus, on behalf of the inhabitants of Micia.

EDCS-26600790.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Terrae Daciae et Genio populi Romani et commerci Felix Caesaris nostri servus vilicus stationis Pontis Augusti promotus ex statione Micia ex vicario
EDCS-26600790.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, to the Land of Dacia, and to the Genius of the Roman people, Felix, our Caesar's slave, overseer of the station at Pons Augusti, promoted from the station at Micia, from being a deputy.
EDCS-26600790.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter, the land of Dacia, and the Genius of the Roman people by Felix, a slave of the emperor, recording his promotion within the imperial administration.

EDCS-26600791.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo veterani et cives Romani per Marcum Ulpium Quintum magistrum
EDCS-26600791.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the veterans and Roman citizens, through Marcus Ulpius Quintus, master.
EDCS-26600791.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Best and Greatest, made by veterans and Roman citizens, facilitated by Marcus Ulpius Quintus as master.

EDCS-26600792.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Heliopolitano Quintus Licinius Macrinus |(centurio) legionis IIII Flaviae Felicis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-26600792.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest of Heliopolis, Quintus Licinius Macrinus, a centurion of the Fourth Flavian Felix Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26600792.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Heliopolitanus, dedicated by Quintus Licinius Macrinus, a centurion of Legio IIII Flavia Felix, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-26600793.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Heliopolitano LI(ucius)#L(ucius)#I LiLcinius#Licinius#LLCINIUS Messalinus |(centurio) legionis XIII geminae
EDCS-26600793.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest of Heliopolis, Lucius Licinius Messalinus, a centurion of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion.
EDCS-26600793.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Heliopolitanus by Lucius Licinius Messalinus, a centurion of Legio XIII Gemina.

EDCS-26600794.text_edition: Libero Patri sacrum Aelius Sossius decurio cohortis II Flaviae Commagenorum ex voto libens posuit
EDCS-26600794.translation: Sacred to Liber Pater, Aelius Sossius, decurion of the Second Flavian Commagenian cohort, willingly placed this out of a vow.
EDCS-26600794.summary: This is a votive monument to Liber Pater, erected by Aelius Sossius, a decurion of Cohors II Flavia Commagenorum, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-26600796.translation: To the Unconquered Sun Mithras, Aurelius.
EDCS-26600796.summary: This is a dedication to Sol Invictus Mithras by Aurelius.

EDCS-26600800.text_edition: Silvano Domestico Catullus immunis votum solvit libens
EDCS-26600800.translation: To Silvanus Domesticus, Catullus, being exempt, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26600800.summary: This is a votive inscription to Silvanus Domesticus, dedicated by Catullus, who was exempt (from certain duties).

EDCS-26600801.text_edition: Silvano Domestico Publius Aelius Euphorus pro salute Publi Aeli Mari conductoris pascui et salinarum lI(ibens)#l(ibens)#I votum vovit
EDCS-26600801.translation: To Silvanus Domesticus, Publius Aelius Euphorus willingly vowed this for the welfare of Publius Aelius Marius, contractor of pasture and saltworks.
EDCS-26600801.summary: This is a votive inscription to Silvanus Domesticus, dedicated by Publius Aelius Euphorus for the well-being of Publius Aelius Marius, a contractor of pasturage and saltworks.

EDCS-26600803.text_edition: Victoriae Augustae et Genio collegii eiius sic Marcus Cocceius Lucius lapidarius donum dedit
EDCS-26600803.translation: To Victoria Augusta and to the Genius of this college, Marcus Cocceius Lucius, a stonemason, gave this gift.
EDCS-26600803.summary: This is a dedication to Victoria Augusta and the Genius of a college, made by Marcus Cocceius Lucius, a stonemason.

EDCS-26600804.text_edition: Dianae aeternae sanctissimeis sic Statilia Bassa filia Pergamiani praefecti equitum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-26600804.translation: To Diana, eternal and most holy, Statilia Bassa, daughter of Pergamenus, prefect of the cavalry, willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-26600804.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made to Diana by Statilia Bassa, daughter of a cavalry prefect.

EDCS-26600805.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Terentius Augustalis, likely a freedman of the emperor.

EDCS-26600806.text_edition: N EB Rufinus IIviralis coloniae votum solvit
EDCS-26600806.translation: Rufinus, duumvir of the colony, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26600806.summary: This inscription records a fulfilled vow by Rufinus, a municipal magistrate (duumvir) of a colony.

EDCS-26600807.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari divi Traiani Parthici filio divi Nervae nepoti Traiano Hadriano Augusto patri patriae consuli III cohors II Flavia Commagenorum
EDCS-26600807.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Traianus Hadrianus Augustus, son of the divine Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the divine Nerva, father of his country, consul for the third time, the Second Flavian Cohort of Commagenians [set this up].
EDCS-26600807.summary: This inscription is a dedication by a military unit, the Second Flavian Cohort of Commagenians, to Emperor Hadrian, dating to approximately 119 AD.

EDCS-26600808.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari divi Antonini filio divi Hadriani nepoti divi Traiani Parthici pronepoti divi Nervae abnepoti Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto Armeniaco tribunicia potestate XVIII consuli III cohors II Flavia Commagenorum
EDCS-26600808.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Armeniacus, son of the divine Antoninus, grandson of the divine Hadrian, great-grandson of the divine Trajan Parthicus, great-great-grandson of the divine Nerva, holding tribunician power for the eighteenth time, consul for the third time, the Second Flavian Cohort of Commagenians [set this up].
EDCS-26600808.summary: This inscription is a dedication by a military unit, the Second Flavian Cohort of Commagenians, to Emperor Marcus Aurelius, dating to approximately 164 AD.

EDCS-26600809.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari divi Antonini filio divi Hadriani nepoti divi Traiani Parthici pronepoti divi Nervae abnepoti Lucio Aurelio VNero#Vero#NERO Augusto Armeniaco tribunicia potestate IIII consuli II proconsuli cohors II Flavia Commagenorum
EDCS-26600809.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Lucius Aurelius Verus Augustus Armeniacus, son of the divine Antoninus, grandson of the divine Hadrian, great-grandson of the divine Trajan Parthicus, great-great-grandson of the divine Nerva, holding tribunician power for the fourth time, consul for the second time, proconsul, the Second Flavian Cohort of Commagenians [set this up].
EDCS-26600809.summary: This inscription is a dedication by a military unit, the Second Flavian Cohort of Commagenians, to Emperor Lucius Verus, dating to approximately 164 AD.

EDCS-26600810.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus consul balneas cohortis II Flaviae Commagenorum vetustate dilabsas sic restituit sub Polo Terentiano consulari III Daciarum curante Sexto Boebio Scribonio Casto praefecto cohortis
EDCS-26600810.translation: The Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus, consul, restored the baths of the Second Flavian Cohort of Commagenians, which had fallen into ruin through age, under Polus Terentianus, consular governor of the Three Dacias, with Sextus Boebius Scribonius Castus, prefect of the cohort, overseeing the work.
EDCS-26600810.summary: This inscription records the restoration of the baths of the Second Flavian Cohort of Commagenians by Emperor Septimius Severus, dating to approximately 202 AD.

EDCS-26600811.text_edition: Impperatoribus Lucio Septimio Severo Pio Pertinaci et Marco Aurelio Antonino Auggustis et [Lucio Septimio Getae Caesari] Caius Antonius Crispinus veteranus ex decurione alae Pannoniorum donum dedit
EDCS-26600811.translation: To the Emperors Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augusti, and to [Lucius Septimius Geta Caesar], Gaius Antonius Crispinus, a veteran formerly a decurion of the Pannonian cavalry wing, gave this gift.
EDCS-26600811.summary: This inscription records a gift given by a veteran, Gaius Antonius Crispinus, to the co-ruling emperors Septimius Severus, Caracalla, and Geta, dating to the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-26600813.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi filio divi Marci Antonini Pii Germanici Sarmatici nepoti divi Antonini Pii pronepoti divi Hadriani abnepoti divi Traiani Parthici et divi Nervae adnepoti Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto equites alae Campagonum dedicante Mevio Suro consulari
EDCS-26600813.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, son of Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, grandson of the divine Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, great-grandson of the divine Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the divine Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the divine Trajan Parthicus and the divine Nerva, the cavalrymen of the Ala Campagonum dedicated this, with Mevius Sura, consular governor, dedicating it.
EDCS-26600813.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the cavalrymen of the Ala Campagonum to Emperor Caracalla, installed under the authority of Mevius Sura, a consular governor, dating to the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-26600815.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Iulio Philippo Pio Felici invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate II pater sic patriae cons(uli) proconsuli cohors II Flavia Commagenorum Philippiana devota numini maiestatique eius ex quaestura
EDCS-26600815.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Iulius Philippus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the second time, father of his country, consul, proconsul, the Second Flavian Cohort of Commagenians, designated 'Philippiana,' devoted to his divinity and majesty, [set this up] from the quaestorship.
EDCS-26600815.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the Second Flavian Cohort of Commagenians (now named 'Philippiana') to Emperor Philip the Arab, dating to approximately 245 AD.

EDCS-26600816.text_edition: Marco Iulio Philippo nobilissimo Caesari principi iuventutis filio Imperatoris Marci Iuli Philippi invicti Augusti ala I Hispanorum Campagonum Philippiana devoti sic numini maiestatique
EDCS-26600816.translation: To Marcus Julius Philippus, most noble Caesar, prince of youth, son of the Emperor Marcus Julius Philippus, invincible Augustus. The First Ala of Campagonian Spaniards, Philippiana, devoted to his divinity and majesty.
EDCS-26600816.summary: This inscription is a dedication by an auxiliary cavalry unit (Ala I Hispanorum Campagonum Philippiana) to Marcus Julius Philippus, son of Emperor Philip the Arab, likely dating to the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-26600817.text_edition: stipendiorum XII vixit annos hic situs est Aurelius Primus et Valerius Maximus et Valerius Valerianus heredes et Aelia Philippis mater bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-26600817.translation: He served for 12 years; he lived for years. Aurelius Primus is buried here, and Valerius Maximus and Valerius Valerianus, his heirs, and Aelia Philippis, his mother, placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-26600817.summary: This is a funerary inscription, erected by heirs and a mother (Aelia Philippis) for Aurelius Primus, noting his 12 years of service.

EDCS-26600818.text_edition: Dis Manibus memoriae Cai Licini Collina Caesarea Pontici veterano alae I Augustae Ituraeorum  vixit annos L  Licinius Euprepes libertus patrono fecit
EDCS-26600818.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead, to the memory of Gaius Licinius Ponticus, of the Collina tribe, from Caesarea, a veteran of the First Augustan Ala of Ituraeans. He lived for 50 years. Licinius Euprepes, his freedman, made this for his patron.
EDCS-26600818.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by a freedman (Licinius Euprepes) to his patron, Gaius Licinius Ponticus, a veteran of an auxiliary cavalry unit, who lived to age 50.

EDCS-26600819.text_edition: Caius Valerius Ursus vixit annos XX Caius Valerius Antestius veteranus ex decurione filio fecit
EDCS-26600819.translation: Gaius Valerius Ursus lived for 20 years. Gaius Valerius Antestius, a veteran formerly a decurion, made this for his son.
EDCS-26600819.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Gaius Valerius Antestius, a veteran and former decurion, for his son, Gaius Valerius Ursus, who died at age 20.

EDCS-26600821.text_edition: Dis Manibus Cai Bovi PAL Symphori Augustalis coloniae Dacicae Sarmizegetusae qui vixit nnos XXV
EDCS-26600821.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead, of Gaius Bovius Symphorus, Augustalis of the colony of Dacian Sarmizegetusa, who lived for 25 years.
EDCS-26600821.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Bovius Symphorus, an Augustalis of the Roman colony of Sarmizegetusa, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-26600822.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelius Ianuarius vixit annos XVIII Publius Aelius Aelianus filio pientissimo posuit
EDCS-26600822.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Aelius Ianuarius lived for 18 years. Publius Aelius Aelianus placed this for his most devoted son.
EDCS-26600822.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Publius Aelius Aelianus for his son, Aelius Ianuarius, who passed away at 18 years old.

EDCS-26600823.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Augus vixit annos IIIcianus Valer
EDCS-26600823.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Aurelius Augus[tianus] lived for 3 years. Valer[ius].
EDCS-26600823.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Aurelius Augustianus, who lived only three years, likely set up by a Valerius, perhaps his father.

EDCS-26600824.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caius Iulius Sosipater vixit annos LXX et Caius Iulius Antipater vixit annos XVI et Caius Iulius Valentinus vixit annos XXIIII Iulius II Euhodus  coniugi et filiis bene merentibus posuit
EDCS-26600824.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Gaius Julius Sosipater lived for 70 years, and Gaius Julius Antipater lived for 16 years, and Gaius Julius Valentinus lived for 24 years. Julius Euhodus, the second, placed this for his well-deserving wife and sons.
EDCS-26600824.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Julius Euhodus (the second) for his wife, Gaius Julius Sosipater, and his two sons, Gaius Julius Antipater and Gaius Julius Valentinus.

EDCS-26600825.text_edition: Avita vixit annos L Ulpius Provincialis coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-26600825.translation: Avita lived for 50 years. Ulpius Provincialis placed this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-26600825.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Ulpius Provincialis for his wife, Avita, who lived to the age of 50.

EDCS-26600826.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ulpius Sabinus vixit annos XIIII Ulpia Maxima mater filio bene merenti posuit
EDCS-26600826.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Ulpius Sabinus lived for 14 years. Ulpia Maxima, his mother, placed this for her well-deserving son.
EDCS-26600826.summary: This is a funerary inscription placed by Ulpia Maxima for her son, Ulpius Sabinus, who died at the age of 14.

EDCS-26600827.text_edition: quondam Pomponi?  et in bellis corpo    edem iuveni eris condidit
EDCS-26600827.translation: Formerly, to Pomponius and in wars, a body... he founded a shrine for the youth.
EDCS-26600827.summary: This fragmentary inscription appears to record the founding of a shrine for a youth, possibly in honor of someone named Pomponius and referencing military service.

EDCS-26600828.text_edition: Fortunae pro salute Auggustorum nnnostrorum Lucius Octavius Iulianus consularis III Daciarum fieri iussit instante Aelio Germano praefecto alae AsturumB
EDCS-26600828.translation: To Fortuna, for the well-being of our Augusti, Lucius Octavius Iulianus, consular governor of the Three Dacias, ordered this to be made, with Aelius Germanus, prefect of the ala Asturum B, urging it on.
EDCS-26600828.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Fortuna for the health of the emperors, commissioned by Lucius Octavius Iulianus, a provincial governor, at the instigation of Aelius Germanus, a military officer.

EDCS-26600829.text_edition: Herculi invicto pro sal(ute) Inperatoris sic collegium GCalatarum#Galatarum#CALATARUM Lucius Livius Marcellinus donum dedit dedicavit
EDCS-26600829.translation: To Hercules the unconquered, for the well-being of the Emperor, thus the college of Galatian guards, Lucius Livius Marcellinus gave and dedicated this gift.
