EDCS-26600829.summary: This inscription records a gift dedicated to Hercules by Lucius Livius Marcellinus on behalf of the college of Galatian guards for the emperor's well-being.

EDCS-26600830.text_edition: Hanc tibi marmoreo caesam de monte dicavi regina undarum Nympha decus nemorum voto damnasti perfecto quem prece Bassus moenitae propter moenia Germisarae
EDCS-26600830.translation: To you, Queen of the waves, Nymph, glory of the groves, I have dedicated this, carved from a marble mountain. Bassus, having fulfilled a vow, dedicated it with prayer, near the walls of Germisara.
EDCS-26600830.summary: This inscription is a poetic dedication to a Nymph, Queen of the waves, carved from marble, offered by Bassus after fulfilling a vow, located near Germisara.

EDCS-26600831.text_edition: Nymphis sanctissimis Publius Aelius Marcellinus signifer et quaestor numeri Brittonum mortis periculo liberatus votum solvit libens merito Imperatore Commodo Augusto Felice V et Glabrione II consulibus
EDCS-26600831.translation: To the most sacred Nymphs, Publius Aelius Marcellinus, standard-bearer and quaestor of the unit of Britons, freed from the danger of death, willingly and deservedly paid his vow, in the fifth consulship of Emperor Commodus Augustus Felix and the second of Glabrio.
EDCS-26600831.summary: This inscription records Publius Aelius Marcellinus, a standard-bearer and quaestor of a British unit, fulfilling a vow to the Nymphs for being saved from death, dated to 189 CE during the consulship of Commodus and Glabrio.

EDCS-26600832.text_edition: Nymphis salutiferis sacrum Marcus Lucilius Lucilianus Augustalis coloniae pro salute sua et Luci Antisti Onesimi Augustalis coloniae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-26600832.translation: Sacred to the health-giving Nymphs, Marcus Lucilius Lucilianus, Augustalis of the colony, for his own well-being and that of Lucius Antistius Onesimus, Augustalis of the colony, willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-26600832.summary: This inscription records Marcus Lucilius Lucilianus, an Augustalis, fulfilling a vow to the health-giving Nymphs for his own well-being and that of another Augustalis, Lucius Antistius Onesimus.

EDCS-26600834.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Aurelio Crescenti Auggustorum liberto vixit annos LXI et Aureliae Florae filiae eius defunctae Poetovio vixit annis XXIII mensibus XI diebus XXX Aelius Iulianus socero et coniugi pientissimis bene merentibus posuit
EDCS-26600834.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Marcus Aurelius Crescens, freedman of the Augusti, who lived 61 years, and for Aurelia Flora, his daughter, who died in Poetovio, and lived 23 years, 11 months, 30 days. Aelius Iulianus erected this for his most devoted father-in-law and wife, who deserved it well.
EDCS-26600834.summary: This funerary inscription was erected by Aelius Iulianus to his father-in-law, Marcus Aurelius Crescens, a freedman of the emperors, and his wife, Aurelia Flora, who died in Poetovio, commemorating their lives.

EDCS-26600835.text_edition: Dis Manibus Quintus Ingenuvius Attio vixit annis XV Ingenuvia Marcella filio pientissimo
EDCS-26600835.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Quintus Ingenuvius Attius, who lived 15 years. Ingenuvia Marcella to her most devoted son.
EDCS-26600835.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Ingenuvia Marcella to her deceased son, Quintus Ingenuvius Attius, who lived to be 15 years old.

EDCS-26600836.text_edition: Dis Manibus Mansotiniae sic Spectatae vixit nnos XX filiae pientissimae posuit mater
EDCS-26600836.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Mansotinia Spectata, who lived 20 years. Her mother placed this for her most devoted daughter.
EDCS-26600836.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a mother to her most devoted daughter, Mansotinia Spectata, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-26600837.text_edition: INV posuerunt coniux et filii patri suo bene merenti hic situs est et Severia Fida vixit annos LXVII coniux eius
EDCS-26600837.translation: His wife and children placed this for their well-deserving father. He is buried here. And Severia Fida, his wife, lived 67 years.
EDCS-26600837.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by a wife and children for their deceased father, also commemorating the wife, Severia Fida, who lived 67 years.

EDCS-26600838.text_edition: AESVI REG FABI V leg MP XIX I
EDCS-26600838.translation: To Aesvus, King of the Fabii, five legions. Nineteen miles from the milestone, one [mile].
EDCS-26600838.summary: This inscription, likely a milestone or a dedication related to a milestone, records a distance associated with Aesvus, King of the Fabii, and refers to five legions and specific mile markers.

EDCS-26600839.text_edition: Sil=ACvaIno#Silvano#ACVINO Domestico  V Saturninus votum libens solvit
EDCS-26600839.translation: To Silvanus Domesticus, V. Saturninus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26600839.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the god Silvanus Domesticus, made by V. Saturninus as a fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-26600840.text_edition: Fortunae Augustae Quintus Decius Vindex procurator
EDCS-26600840.translation: To Augustan Fortuna, Quintus Decius Vindex, procurator.
EDCS-26600840.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Fortuna Augusta by Quintus Decius Vindex, who held the office of procurator.

EDCS-26600841.text_edition: Genio pagi Miciae Titus Aurelius Primanus magister pagi eiusdem ex suo fecit libens merito
EDCS-26600841.translation: To the Genius of the pagus Micia, Titus Aurelius Primanus, master of the same pagus, made this from his own funds, willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-26600841.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Titus Aurelius Primanus, a master of the pagus Micia, to the Genius of that pagus, funded by himself to commemorate a local deity.

EDCS-26600842.text_edition: Herculi Marcus Iulius Proclianus veteranus votum libens solvit
EDCS-26600842.translation: To Hercules, Marcus Julius Proclianus, a veteran, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26600842.summary: This is a votive inscription to Hercules, dedicated by Marcus Julius Proclianus, a veteran, as a fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-26600843.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute dominorum nostrorum Caius Iulius Marcianus decurio coloniae praefectus pagi Aquensis Genio eorum posuit
EDCS-26600843.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the well-being of our lords, Gaius Julius Marcianus, decurion of the colony, prefect of the pagus Aquensis, placed this to their Genius.
EDCS-26600843.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the health of the emperors, made by Gaius Julius Marcianus, a decurion and prefect, who also dedicated it to the Genius of the emperors.

EDCS-26600844.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Flavius Gratus votum solvit libens
EDCS-26600844.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Titus Flavius Gratus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26600844.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Titus Flavius Gratus, marking the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-26600847.text_edition: Libero Patri Ulpius Nepos votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-26600847.translation: To Liber Pater, Ulpius Nepos willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26600847.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Liber Pater by Ulpius Nepos.

EDCS-26600848.text_edition: Dis Faventibus et Concordia Imperatorum evenit quod a primo adventu suo Publius Furius Saturninus legatus Augustorum donec provincia decederet ita singulos universosque benignitate sua tractarit oneribus etiam relevaverit ut felicissima et praecipuis virtutibus eiius sic obstricta simul et devota provincia ei gratias agat   GERMP
EDCS-26600848.translation: With the favoring gods and the harmony of the emperors, it came to pass that from his first arrival, Publius Furius Saturninus, legate of the Augusti, until he departed from the province, treated individuals and everyone with such kindness and even relieved them of burdens, that the most fortunate province, thus bound and devoted by his outstanding virtues, gives thanks to him. Germanicus.
EDCS-26600848.summary: This elaborate inscription is a public expression of gratitude from a province to Publius Furius Saturninus, the legate of the Augusti, for his benevolent governance and relief of burdens, likely dating to the time of Germanicus.

EDCS-26600849.summary: This inscription is a simple artisan's signature, indicating that Claudius Saturninus was the sculptor of the monument it was carved on.

EDCS-26600851.text_edition: Apollini Augusto Lucius Aemilius Carus legatus Augusti pro praetore III Daciarum
EDCS-26600851.translation: To Apollo Augustus, Lucius Aemilius Carus, legate of Augustus with pro-praetorian power, governing the three Dacias.
EDCS-26600851.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Apollo Augustus by Lucius Aemilius Carus, a Roman provincial governor, likely dating to the early-mid 3rd century AD.

EDCS-26600852.text_edition: Victoriae Augustae pro salute Imperatoris Antonini Augusti Marcus Statius Priscus legatus eius pro praetore
EDCS-26600852.translation: To Victory Augusta, for the welfare of the Emperor Antoninus Augustus, Marcus Statius Priscus, his legate with pro-praetorian power.
EDCS-26600852.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Victoria Augusta for the emperor's well-being by Marcus Statius Priscus, a provincial governor, probably dating to the mid-2nd century AD during the reign of Antoninus Pius or Marcus Aurelius.

EDCS-26600853.text_edition: Numini Aesculapi et Hygiae Aelia Venusta votum solvit
EDCS-26600853.translation: To the Divinity of Aesculapius and Hygia, Aelia Venusta fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-26600853.summary: This inscription records a fulfilled vow to the divinities Aesculapius and Hygia by an individual named Aelia Venusta.

EDCS-26600854.text_edition: Aesculapio Pergameno et Hygiae sacrum Caius Spedius Hermias flamen coloniae Sarmizegetusae posuit
EDCS-26600854.translation: Sacred to Aesculapius of Pergamum and Hygia, Gaius Spedius Hermias, flamen of the colony of Sarmizegetusa, set this up.
EDCS-26600854.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Aesculapius Pergamene and Hygia by Gaius Spedius Hermias, a priest from the colony of Sarmizegetusa.

EDCS-26600855.text_edition: Dianae sanctae Potentinus nummularius votum solvit
EDCS-26600855.translation: To sacred Diana, Potentinus, the money-changer, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26600855.summary: This inscription records a fulfilled vow to Diana by Potentinus, who held the profession of money-changer.

EDCS-26600859.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae Minervae omnibus dis immortalibus Quintus Axius Aelianus vir egregius procurator Augg(ustorum) et Aelia Romana eius Ioni
EDCS-26600859.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Juno Regina, Minerva, and all the immortal gods, Quintus Axius Aelianus, an excellent man, procurator of the Augusti, and Aelia Romana, his wife, to Ioni.
EDCS-26600859.summary: This inscription is a dedication to a pantheon of Roman deities and "Ioni" by Quintus Axius Aelianus, a procurator, and his wife Aelia Romana, likely dating to a period with co-emperors.

EDCS-26600860.text_edition: Genio collegii fabrum Aurelius Ingenuus decurio coloniae Sarmizegetusae
EDCS-26600860.translation: To the Genius of the guild of craftsmen, Aurelius Ingenuus, decurion of the colony of Sarmizegetusa.
EDCS-26600860.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Genius (protective spirit) of a craftsmen's guild by Aurelius Ingenuus, a decurion from Sarmizegetusa.

EDCS-26600861.text_edition: Genio ordinis quem Ulpius Domitius Hermes Augustalis coloniae ob honorem ornamentorum decurionalium promiserat Valerius Threptus et Domitii Regulus Hipponicus Hermes Onesimus Augustales coloniae heredes ponendum curaverunt locus datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-26600861.translation: To the Genius of the order, which Ulpius Domitius Hermes, Augustalis of the colony, had promised on account of the honor of decurional ornaments, Valerius Threptus and the Domitii Regulus, Hipponicus, Hermes, Onesimus, Augustales of the colony, as heirs, took care to set it up; the place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-26600861.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Genius of a specific order, promised by Ulpius Domitius Hermes upon receiving decurional honors, and erected posthumously by his heirs with official approval.

EDCS-26600862.text_edition: Minervae Augustae Ulpius Domitius Hermes Augustalis coloniae ornatus ornamentis decurionalibus testamento poni iussit Valerius Threptus et Domitii Regulus Hipponicus Hermes Onesimus Augustales coloniae heredes ponendum curaverunt loco dato decreto decurionum
EDCS-26600862.translation: To Minerva Augusta, Ulpius Domitius Hermes, Augustalis of the colony, adorned with decurional ornaments, ordered this to be set up in his will; Valerius Threptus and the Domitii Regulus, Hipponicus, Hermes, Onesimus, Augustales of the colony, as heirs, took care to set it up, the place having been granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-26600862.summary: This inscription records a testamentary dedication to Minerva Augusta by Ulpius Domitius Hermes, an Augustalis, which was executed by his heirs with the official permission of the decurions.

EDCS-26600863.text_edition: Aesculapio et Hygiae Augustis  miles legionis V votum solvit
EDCS-26600863.translation: To Aesculapius and Hygia Augusti, a soldier of the Fifth Legion fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26600863.summary: This inscription records a fulfilled vow to Aesculapius and Hygia Augusti by a soldier from the Fifth Legion.

EDCS-26600864.text_edition: Deae Isidi Priscianus Augustalis coloniae Sarmizegetusae metropolis et Aurelia Fortunata liberta eius
EDCS-26600864.translation: To the Goddess Isis, Priscianus Augustalis of the colony Sarmizegetusa Metropolis, and Aurelia Fortunata, his freedwoman, (dedicated this).
EDCS-26600864.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Isis by Priscianus, an Augustalis from Sarmizegetusa Metropolis, and his freedwoman Aurelia Fortunata.

EDCS-26600866.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Antonius Priscus ex voto
EDCS-26600866.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Antonius Priscus, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-26600866.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Antonius Priscus.

EDCS-26600867.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno pro salute Marci Bassi Aquilae et Gai Gaiani ex collegio fabrum decuriarum IIII votum solutum libenter merito
EDCS-26600867.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, for the health of Marcus Bassus Aquila and Gaius Gaianus from the college of craftsmen of the fourth decuria, a vow willingly and deservedly fulfilled.
EDCS-26600867.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus by members of a craftsmen's college for the health of Marcus Bassus Aquila and Gaius Gaianus.

EDCS-26600869.text_edition: pro salute Bassi Aquilini et Bassi Flaviani filiorum suorum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-26600869.translation: For the health of Bassus Aquilinus and Bassus Flavianus, his sons, he willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26600869.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow made for the health of the dedicant's two sons, Bassus Aquilinus and Bassus Flavianus.

EDCS-26600870.text_edition: Marti Augusto pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Antoni Gordiani Pii Felicis Augusti Marcus Antonius Valentinus eques Romanus decurio municipii Apulensis sacerdos arae Augusti nostri coronatus Daciarum III donum dedit
EDCS-26600870.translation: To Mars Augustus, for the health of Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus, Marcus Antonius Valentinus, a Roman eques, decurion of the municipium Apulensis, priest of our Altar of Augustus, crowned for the three Dacias, gave this gift.
EDCS-26600870.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Mars Augustus for the health of Emperor Gordian III, made by Marcus Antonius Valentinus, a Roman eques and decurion, who held the significant priestly office of 'coronatus' for the three Dacias, dating to the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-26600871.text_edition: Caius Iavolenus Modestus |(centurio) legionis XIII geminae Mercurio Augusto sacrum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-26600871.translation: Gaius Iavolenus Modestus, centurion of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled a sacred vow to Mercury Augustus.
EDCS-26600871.summary: This is a votive inscription to Mercury Augustus by Gaius Iavolenus Modestus, a centurion of Legio XIII Gemina.

EDCS-26600872.text_edition: Mercurio Augusto sacrum Caius Sextius Atta pro salute filiorum
EDCS-26600872.translation: Sacred to Mercury Augustus, Gaius Sextius Atta, for the health of his sons.
EDCS-26600872.summary: This is a sacred dedication to Mercury Augustus by Gaius Sextius Atta for the well-being of his sons.

EDCS-26600873.text_edition: Soli Invicto Mithrae Aniceto Hermadio votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-26600873.translation: To Sol Invictus Mithras Anicetus Hermadius, he willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26600873.summary: This is a votive inscription to the syncretic deity Sol Invictus Mithras Anicetus Hermadius.

EDCS-26600875.text_edition: Deae Nemesi Reginae Cornelius Cornelianus defensor lecticariorum et Iulia Bessa coniux eius ex voto posuerunt
EDCS-26600875.translation: To the Goddess Nemesis Regina, Cornelius Cornelianus, protector of the litter-bearers, and Iulia Bessa, his wife, placed this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-26600875.summary: This inscription records a votive dedication to the goddess Nemesis Regina by Cornelius Cornelianus, a 'defensor lecticariorum', and his wife Iulia Bessa.

EDCS-26600877.text_edition: Quadribis! Claudius Anicetus Augustalis coloniae Sarmizegetusae metropolis ex voto
EDCS-26600877.translation: To the Quadribiae! Claudius Anicetus, Augustalis of the colony Sarmizegetusa Metropolis, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-26600877.summary: This is a votive dedication to the Quadribiae by Claudius Anicetus, an Augustalis from Sarmizegetusa Metropolis.

EDCS-26600878.text_edition: Deo Silvano Lucius Coelius Aelianus decurio coloniae Sarmizegetusae metropolis aedilicius ex voto
EDCS-26600878.translation: To the god Silvanus, Lucius Coelius Aelianus, decurion of the colony of Sarmizegetusa, a metropolis, having held the office of aedile, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26600878.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to the god Silvanus by Lucius Coelius Aelianus, a decurion and former aedile of the colony of Sarmizegetusa, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-26600881.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely part of a larger dedication, mentioning the emperor Trajan (early 2nd century CE).

EDCS-26600882.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari divi Traiani Parthici filio divi Nervae nepoti Traiano Hadriano Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate II consuli patri patriae colonia Ulpia Traiana Augusta Dacica Sarmizegetusa
EDCS-26600882.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the deified Nerva, Trajan Hadrian Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the second time, consul, father of his country, the colony Ulpia Traiana Augusta Dacica Sarmizegetusa [dedicated this].
EDCS-26600882.summary: This inscription is a monumental dedication by the colony Ulpia Traiana Augusta Dacica Sarmizegetusa to the emperor Hadrian, dating to the early years of his reign (c. 118 CE).

EDCS-26600883.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari divi Traiani Parthici filio divi Nervae nepoti Traiano Hadriano Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XVI consuli III patri patriae aqua inducta coloniae Dacicae Sarmizegetusae per Cnaeum Papirium Aelianum legatum eius pro praetore
EDCS-26600883.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the deified Nerva, Trajan Hadrian Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the sixteenth time, consul for the third time, father of his country, because water was brought to the colony Dacica Sarmizegetusa by Gnaeus Papirius Aelianus, his legate pro praetore.
EDCS-26600883.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the emperor Hadrian, likely commemorating the completion of an aqueduct for the colony Dacica Sarmizegetusa, dating to around 132-133 CE during his sixteenth tribunician power.

EDCS-26600885.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari divi Hadriani filio divi Traiani Parthici nepoti divi Nervae pronepoti Tito Aelio Hadriano Antonino Augusto Pio pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate V consuli III patri patriae Quintus Aurelius Quinti filius Tertius Papiria Sarmizegetusa decurio coloniae ob honorem flamoni et HS LXXXmilia nummum ad annonam dedit locus datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-26600885.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Hadrian, grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-grandson of the deified Nerva, Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the fifth time, consul for the third time, father of his country. Quintus Aurelius Tertius, son of Quintus, of the Papirian voting tribe, from Sarmizegetusa, a decurion of the colony, for the honor of the flamonate, gave 80,000 sesterces for the grain supply. The site was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-26600885.summary: This inscription commemorates a generous donation of 80,000 sesterces for the grain supply by Quintus Aurelius Tertius, a decurion of Sarmizegetusa, to the emperor Antoninus Pius in exchange for the honor of the flamonate, dating to around 142 CE.

EDCS-26600886.text_edition: Faustinae Augustae divi Pii filiae colonia Sarmizegetusa
EDCS-26600886.translation: To Faustina Augusta, daughter of the deified Pius, the colony Sarmizegetusa [dedicated this].
EDCS-26600886.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the colony Sarmizegetusa to Empress Faustina the Younger, daughter of Antoninus Pius, likely from the mid-2nd century CE.

EDCS-26600887.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus maximus et Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus et Publius Septimius Geta nobilissimus Caesar fecerunt
EDCS-26600887.translation: The Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, and the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus, and Publius Septimius Geta, the most noble Caesar, made this.
EDCS-26600887.summary: This inscription records a construction or restoration project undertaken by the co-emperors Septimius Severus, Caracalla (Marcus Aurelius Antoninus), and Geta (as Caesar), dating to the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-26600888.text_edition: Divo Severo Pio colonia Ulpia Traiana Augusta Dacica Sarmizegetusa
EDCS-26600888.translation: To the deified Severus Pius, the colony Ulpia Traiana Augusta Dacica Sarmizegetusa [dedicated this].
EDCS-26600888.summary: This inscription is a posthumous dedication by the colony Ulpia Traiana Augusta Dacica Sarmizegetusa to the deified emperor Septimius Severus, made shortly after his death (after 211 CE).

EDCS-26600890.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Antonio Gordiano Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate IIII consuli II patri patriae proconsuli concilium provinciarum Daciarum III devoti numini maiestatique eius
EDCS-26600890.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the fourth time, consul for the second time, father of his country, proconsul, the council of the three Dacian provinces, devoted to his divinity and majesty [dedicated this].
EDCS-26600890.summary: This inscription is a provincial dedication by the council of the three Dacian provinces to the emperor Gordian III, dating to around 241 CE during his fourth tribunician power.

EDCS-26600892.text_edition: Quinto Axio Quinti filio Palatina Aeliano equiti Romano Laurenti Lavinati curatori ad populum viarum Traianae et Aureliae et Aeclanensis procuratori ad alimenta per Apuliam Calabriam Lucaniam et Bruttios procuratori rationis privatae provinciae Mauretaniae Caesariensis item per Belgicum et duas Germanias procuratori provinciae Daciae Apulensis bis vice praesidis ordo coloniae Sarmizegetusae metropolis patrono
EDCS-26600892.translation: To Quintus Axius Aelianus, son of Quintus, of the Palatina voting tribe, a Roman knight, Laurentian Lavinate, curator for the people of the Via Traiana, Via Aurelia, and Via Aeclanensis, procurator for the alimenta in Apulia, Calabria, Lucania, and Bruttium, procurator of the private treasury for the province of Mauretania Caesariensis, likewise for Belgica and the two Germaniae, procurator of the province of Dacia Apulensis twice, acting as governor, the order of the colony Sarmizegetusa, a metropolis, to its patron.
EDCS-26600892.summary: This inscription is a testimonial dedication by the colony of Sarmizegetusa to Quintus Axius Aelianus, a prominent Roman knight who held numerous imperial administrative posts, including procurator of Dacia Apulensis, recognizing him as their patron, likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-26600893.text_edition: Marco Claudio Tiberi filio Quirina Frontoni consuli legato Augusti pro praetore trium Daciarum et Moesiae superioris comiti divi Veri Augusti donato donis militaribus bello Armeniaco et Parthico ab Imperatore Antonino Augusto et a divo Vero Augusto corona murali item vallari item classica item aurea item hastis puris IIII item vexillis curatori operum locorumque publicorum legato legionis I Minerviae legato legionis XI Claudiae praetori aedili curuli ab actis senatus quaestori urbano decemviro stlitibus iudicandis colonia Ulpia Traiana Augusta Dacica Sarmizegetusa patrono fortissimo duci amplissimo praesidi
EDCS-26600893.translation: To Marcus Claudius Fronton, son of Tiberius, of the Quirina tribe, consul, legate of Augustus with propraetorian power for the three Dacias and Upper Moesia, companion of the deified Emperor Verus, awarded military decorations in the Armenian and Parthian War by Emperor Antoninus Augustus and by the deified Emperor Verus: a mural crown, also a palisade crown, also a naval crown, also a golden crown, also four honorary spears, also vexilla; curator of public works and places, legate of Legion I Minervia, legate of Legion XI Claudia, praetor, curule aedile, recorder of senatorial acts, urban quaestor, decemvir for judging lawsuits. The colonia Ulpia Traiana Augusta Dacica Sarmizegetusa dedicates this to its bravest patron, most distinguished leader, and protector.
EDCS-26600893.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the colonia Ulpia Traiana Augusta Dacica Sarmizegetusa to Marcus Claudius Fronton, detailing his extensive military and civilian career, including numerous awards and magistracies, likely from the mid to late 2nd century AD.

EDCS-26600894.text_edition: Caio Curtio Cai filio Pollia Iusto cons(uli) IIIIviro viarum curandarum  quaestori urbano adlecto inter tribunicios a divo Hadriano praetori peregrino IIIIIIviro equitum Romanorum turmis ducendis praefecto frumenti  dandi curatori viarum Clodiae Anniae Cassiae Ciminiae legato Imperatoris Antonini Augusti Pii legionis XX Valeriae Victricis proconsuli provinciae COLLIAE legato pro praetore Imperatoris Antonini Augusti Pii provinciae Daciae colonia Ulpia Traiana Dacica Sarmizegetusa patrono
EDCS-26600894.translation: To Gaius Curtius Iustus, son of Gaius, of the Pollia tribe, consul, quattuorvir for supervising roads, urban quaestor, adlected among the tribunes by the deified Hadrian, praetor peregrinus, sevir of Roman knights for leading squadrons, prefect for grain distribution, curator of the Clodia, Annia, Cassia, and Ciminia roads, legate of Emperor Antoninus Augustus Pius for Legion XX Valeria Victrix, proconsul of the province COLLIAE, legate of Emperor Antoninus Augustus Pius with propraetorian power for the province of Dacia. The colonia Ulpia Traiana Dacica Sarmizegetusa dedicates this to its patron.
EDCS-26600894.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the colonia Ulpia Traiana Dacica Sarmizegetusa to Gaius Curtius Iustus, outlining his distinguished senatorial career, including military commands and provincial governorships, likely dating to the mid-2nd century AD.

EDCS-26600895.text_edition: Caio Curtio Cai filio Pollia Rufino tribuno laticlavio legionis XIII geminae IIIviro aere argento auro flando feriundo colonia Ulpia Traiana Dacica Sarmizetegusa
EDCS-26600895.translation: To Gaius Curtius Rufinus, son of Gaius, of the Pollia tribe, laticlavian tribune of Legion XIII Gemina, tresvir for minting bronze, silver, and gold. The colonia Ulpia Traiana Dacica Sarmizegetusa dedicates this.
EDCS-26600895.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the colonia Ulpia Traiana Dacica Sarmizegetusa to Gaius Curtius Rufinus, detailing the initial stages of his senatorial career, likely dating to the mid-2nd century AD.

EDCS-26600896.text_edition: Publio Furio Saturnino legato Augusti pro praetore consuli colonia Dacica Sarmizegetusa
EDCS-26600896.translation: To Publius Furius Saturninus, legate of Augustus with propraetorian power, consul. The Dacian colonia Sarmizegetusa dedicates this.
EDCS-26600896.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the Dacian colonia Sarmizegetusa to Publius Furius Saturninus, acknowledging his consular rank and service as an imperial legate, likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-26600898.text_edition: Quinto Marcio Turboni Frontoni Publicio Severo praefecto praetorio Imperatoris Caesaris Traiani Hadriani Augusti patris patriae colonia Ulpia Traiana Augusta Dacica Sarmizegetusa
EDCS-26600898.translation: To Quintus Marcius Turbo Fronton Publicius Severus, praetorian prefect of Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, Father of the Fatherland. The colonia Ulpia Traiana Augusta Dacica Sarmizegetusa dedicates this.
EDCS-26600898.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the colonia Ulpia Traiana Augusta Dacica Sarmizegetusa to Quintus Marcius Turbo Fronton Publicius Severus, recognizing his important role as praetorian prefect under Hadrian, dating to the early-mid 2nd century AD.

EDCS-26600899.text_edition: Decimo Terentio Gentiano tribuno militum quaestori tribuno plebis praetori legato Augusti consuli pontifici censori provinciae Macedoniae colonia Ulpia Traiana Augusta Dacica Sarmizegetusa patrono
EDCS-26600899.translation: To Decimus Terentius Gentianus, military tribune, quaestor, tribune of the plebs, praetor, legate of Augustus, consul, pontiff, censor of the province of Macedonia. The colonia Ulpia Traiana Augusta Dacica Sarmizegetusa dedicates this to its patron.
EDCS-26600899.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the colonia Ulpia Traiana Augusta Dacica Sarmizegetusa to Decimus Terentius Gentianus, detailing his extensive senatorial career, including various magistracies and provincial service, likely dating to the early 2nd century AD.

EDCS-26600900.text_edition: Ulpio --- v(iro) e(gregio) procuratori Auggustorum provinc(iae) Daciae Apulensis agenti vice praesidis item procuratori provinciae Porolissensis subpraefecto nnae sacrae urbis praeposito legionis VII geminae Philippianae item procuratori stationis privatae per Tusciam et Picenum item procuratori ad bona Plautiani tribuno militum legionis II Parthicae Philippianae praeposito vexillationis auxiliariorum nnoniae inferioris praefecto cohortis VII Breucorum Siscius Valerius |(centurio) legionis XIII geminae Philippianae patrono optimo
EDCS-26600900.translation: To Ulpius [---], vir egregius, procurator of our Augusti for the province of Dacia Apulensis, acting as vice-governor, also procurator of the province of Porolissensis, sub-prefect of the sacred annona of the city, commander of Legion VII Gemina Philippiana, also procurator of the private customs station throughout Tuscia and Picenum, also procurator for the estates of Plautianus, military tribune of Legion II Parthica Philippiana, commander of an auxiliary vexillation of Lower Pannonia, prefect of Cohort VII Breucorum. Siscius Valerius, centurion of Legion XIII Gemina Philippiana, dedicates this to his excellent patron.
EDCS-26600900.summary: This inscription is a dedication by a centurion, Siscius Valerius, to his excellent patron, Ulpius [---], outlining the latter's equestrian career with numerous procuratorial and military appointments, likely from the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-26600902.text_edition: Dis Manibus Alliano Augusti nostri servo vernae vixit annis VI mensibus IX diebus XXII Constantianus Augusti libertus adiutor tabularii pater
EDCS-26600902.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Allianus, our Augustus's slave, born in the household, who lived 6 years, 9 months, 22 days. Constantianus, freedman of Augustus, assistant record-keeper, his father, placed this.
EDCS-26600902.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Allianus, a young imperial slave, erected by his father, Constantianus, an imperial freedman and assistant record-keeper, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-26600903.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Aurelio Onesimo Carpion Augusti libertus tabularius filio dulcissimo p(osuit)
EDCS-26600903.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Marcus Aurelius Onesimus. Carpion, freedman of Augustus, record-keeper, placed this for his dearest son.
EDCS-26600903.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Aurelius Onesimus, erected by his father, Carpion, an imperial freedman and record-keeper, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-26600904.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aureliae Respectae rarissimae feminae Herculanus Auggustorum nnostrorum verna adiutor tabularii coniugi
EDCS-26600904.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Aurelia Respecta, a most rare woman. Herculanus, our Augusti's household-born slave, assistant record-keeper, [placed this for his] wife.
EDCS-26600904.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelia Respecta, erected by her husband, Herculanus, an imperial slave and assistant record-keeper, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-26600905.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aureliae Victoriae incomparabili feminae Fortunatus Augusti libertus adiutor tabularii coniugi karissimae hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur
EDCS-26600905.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Aurelia Victoria, an incomparable woman. Fortunatus, freedman of Augustus, assistant record-keeper, dedicated this monument to his dearest wife. This monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-26600905.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Fortunatus, an imperial freedman and assistant record-keeper, to his wife Aurelia Victoria, with a clause preventing it from being inherited.

EDCS-26600907.text_edition: Publius Aelius Septimius Audeo qui et Maximus veteranus ex |(centurione) numeri Palmyrenorum Optatianensium vixit nnis LX Aelius Septimius Romanus miles legionis XIII geminae adiutor officii corniculariorum et Septimia Septimina quae et Revocata filii et Cornelia Antonia uxor heredes ponendum curaverunt curam agente Septimio Asclepiade Augustali coloniae liberto eius
EDCS-26600907.translation: Publius Aelius Septimius Audeo, also known as Maximus, a veteran ex-centurion of the Palmyrene Optatianensian unit, lived for 60 years. Aelius Septimius Romanus, soldier of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, assistant of the cornicularii office, and Septimia Septimina, also known as Revocata, his children, and Cornelia Antonia, his wife, arranged for this to be set up. The care was managed by Septimius Asclepiades, Augustalis of the colony, his freedman.
EDCS-26600907.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Aelius Septimius Audeo, a veteran centurion, set up by his children and wife, with his freedman overseeing the work.

EDCS-26600908.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publio Aelio Publi filio Papiria  Theimeti veterano et IIvirali coloniae Sarmizegetusae vixit annos LXXXVIIII et Aeliae  vixit annos LXXI et Publio  legionis XX Victricis  Publius Aelius Senecio gener
EDCS-26600908.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Publius Aelius, son of Publius, of the Papirian tribe, Theimetus, a veteran and duumvir of the colony of Sarmizegetusa, who lived for 89 years. And for Aelia, who lived for 71 years. And for Publius, of the Twentieth Legion Victrix. Publius Aelius Senecio, his son-in-law, dedicated this.
EDCS-26600908.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Publius Aelius Senecio to his father-in-law Publius Aelius Theimetus, a veteran and local magistrate in Sarmizegetusa, and to Aelia and another Publius from the Twentieth Legion.

EDCS-26600909.text_edition: Dis Manibus quod Aelia Adiuta mater sibi et Cominio Celerino pontifici coloniae equiti Romano tribuno legionis facere constituerat Cominius Quintus pontifex et quinquennalis et Cominia Sperata et Cominia Caecilia filii consummaverunt
EDCS-26600909.translation: To the Divine Manes. What Aelia Adiuta, mother, had decided to make for herself and for Cominius Celerinus, pontiff of the colony, Roman knight, tribune of a legion, Cominius Quintus, pontiff and quinquennalis, and Cominia Sperata and Cominia Caecilia, her children, completed.
EDCS-26600909.summary: This inscription records the completion of a funerary monument by Cominius Quintus, Cominia Sperata, and Cominia Caecilia for their mother Aelia Adiuta and Cominius Celerinus, fulfilling their mother's original intention.

EDCS-26600910.text_edition: Dis Manibus Antoniae Bonosae vixit annis XXXVII Aurelio Constantio equiti Romano decurioni coloniae Viminacii vixit annis VL Publio Antonio Viatori militi legionis VI victricis frumentario vixit nnis XVIIII Publius Antonius Super infelix pater  Publius Antonius Super tribunus cohortis III Delmatarum vixit annis LXXXIIII infelix pater
EDCS-26600910.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Antonia Bonosa, who lived 37 years. For Aurelius Constantius, Roman knight, decurion of the colony of Viminacium, who lived 55 years. For Publius Antonius Viator, soldier of the Sixth Legion Victrix, frumentarius, who lived 19 years. Publius Antonius Super, unhappy father, [dedicated this]. Publius Antonius Super, tribune of the Third Cohort of Dalmatians, who lived 84 years, unhappy father.
EDCS-26600910.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Publius Antonius Super, a tribune, for his deceased family members: Antonia Bonosa, Aurelius Constantius, and Publius Antonius Viator.

EDCS-26600911.text_edition: Dis Manibus Claudia Serena vixit nnos LXXI Iulius Priscus veteranus coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-26600911.translation: To the Divine Manes. Claudia Serena lived for 71 years. Iulius Priscus, a veteran, set this up for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-26600911.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Iulius Priscus, a veteran, to his wife Claudia Serena.

EDCS-26600912.text_edition: item Lucio Dasumio Prisco veterano legionis XIII geminae vixit annos LX
EDCS-26600912.translation: Also for Lucius Dasumius Priscus, veteran of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, who lived for 60 years.
EDCS-26600912.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Dasumius Priscus, a veteran of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina.

EDCS-26600913.text_edition: Dis Manibus Quinto Manlio Papiria Vero veterano legionis XV signiferi decurioni coloniae vixit annos LXXV Caius Iulius Macer |(centurio) heres fecit
EDCS-26600913.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Quintus Manlius, of the Papirian tribe, Verus, veteran of the Fifteenth Legion, standard-bearer, decurion of the colony, who lived for 75 years. Gaius Iulius Macer, a centurion, as heir, made this.
EDCS-26600913.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Gaius Iulius Macer, as heir, to Quintus Manlius Verus, a veteran standard-bearer and decurion of a colony.

EDCS-26600914.text_edition: Dis Manibus Maria Secundina vixit annos LIII et Aurelia Saturnina vixit nnos XVIII Aurelius Ingenuus miles legionis XIII geminae Antoninianae matri et sorori posuit
EDCS-26600914.translation: To the Divine Manes. Maria Secundina lived for 53 years, and Aurelia Saturnina lived for 18 years. Aurelius Ingenuus, a soldier of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina Antoniniana, set this up for his mother and sister.
EDCS-26600914.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Aurelius Ingenuus, a soldier, to his mother Maria Secundina and his sister Aurelia Saturnina.

EDCS-26600915.text_edition: Sextus Pilonius Sexti filius Stellatina Modestus Benevento |(centurio) legionis IIII Flaviae Felicis III hastatus posterior nnorum XXXVII ordinem accepit ex equite Romano militavit in legione VII Claudia pia fideli et VIII Augusta XI Claudia pia fideli I Minervia pia fideli stipendiis centurionicis XVIIII hic situs est sit tibi terra levis
EDCS-26600915.translation: Sextus Pilonius, son of Sextus, of the Stellatina tribe, Modestus, from Beneventum, centurion of the Fourth Legion Flavia Felix, third hastatus posterior, 37 years old. He received his rank from a Roman knight. He served in the Seventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis, and the Eighth Augusta, the Eleventh Claudia Pia Fidelis, the First Minervia Pia Fidelis, with 19 years of centurion's pay. He is buried here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-26600915.summary: This is a funerary monument for Sextus Pilonius Modestus, a centurion from Beneventum, detailing his military career and various legionary assignments.

EDCS-26600916.text_edition: Tito Varenio Probo IIvirali item quinquennali coloniae Varenius Pudens flamen coloniarum equo publico praefectus cohortis patri ob stratam ab eo aream dimidiam in honorem suum ex SS Lmilibus nummum loco dato decreto decurionum
EDCS-26600916.translation: To Titus Varenius Probus, duumvir and quinquennial of the colony, Varenius Pudens, flamen of the colonies, a public horseman, prefect of a cohort, to his father, for the half-area he paved at his own expense in his honor, with the given space by decree of the decurions, having spent 50,000 sesterces.
EDCS-26600916.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Varenius Pudens to his father, Titus Varenius Probus, honoring his public service and generosity in paving an area, with the sum of 50,000 sesterces contributed at his own expense and with the approval of the decurions.

EDCS-26600917.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Verpidio Silvano equiti alae II Pannoniorum Valerius Proculus secundus heres ponendum curavit
EDCS-26600917.translation: To the Divine Manes. Valerius Proculus, his second heir, had this placed for Marcus Verpidius Silvanus, a horseman of the Second Pannonian Wing.
EDCS-26600917.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Marcus Verpidius Silvanus, a cavalryman, placed by his second heir, Valerius Proculus.

EDCS-26600919.text_edition: Ulpiae Procillae vixit annos XXVI Lucius Valerius Rufus veteranus ex beneficiario consularis decurio coloniae coniugi bene merenti posuit Lucio Valerio Rufo veterano legionis XIII geminae ex beneficiario consularis decurioni coloniae quaestori IIviro vixit annos LXV Valerii Rufinus et Proculeianus filii statuendum curaverunt
EDCS-26600919.translation: To Ulpia Procilla, who lived 26 years. Lucius Valerius Rufus, a veteran from a consular beneficiarius, decurion of the colony, placed this for his well-deserving wife. For Lucius Valerius Rufus, veteran of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, from a consular beneficiarius, decurion of the colony, quaestor, duumvir, who lived 65 years, his sons Valerii Rufinus and Proculeianus had this statue set up.
EDCS-26600919.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Ulpia Procilla, set up by her husband Lucius Valerius Rufus, and also commemorates Lucius Valerius Rufus himself, with his military and civil career, set up by his sons Rufinus and Proculeianus.

EDCS-26600920.text_edition: Tito Varenio Titi filio Papiria Pudenti equo publico a militiis decurioni et flamini coloniae Sarmizegetusae quinquennali coloniae supra scriptae decurioni municipii Apulensis patrono municipii Porolissensis et collegiorum itemque causarum
EDCS-26600920.translation: To Titus Varenius Pudens, son of Titus, of the Papirian tribe, a public horseman from military service, decurion and flamen of the colony of Sarmizegetusa, quinquennial of the above-mentioned colony, decurion of the municipium Apulensis, patron of the municipium Porolissensis and of collegia and also of legal cases.
EDCS-26600920.summary: This inscription honors Titus Varenius Pudens, detailing his extensive public career including military service, various civic offices in multiple Roman settlements, and patronage of collegia.

EDCS-26600922.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aeliae Andenae Aelius Macrinus Epidianus qui et Epidius Augustalis coloniae et Macrinia Marcia filiae posuerunt
EDCS-26600922.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aelius Macrinus Epidianus, also known as Epidius, Augustalis of the colony, and his daughter Macrinia Marcia, placed this for Aelia Andena.
EDCS-26600922.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aelia Andena, erected by Aelius Macrinus Epidianus, an Augustalis of the colony, and his daughter Macrinia Marcia.

EDCS-26600923.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Antonio Papiria Prisco vixit nnos LXII Antonius Rufus decurio coloniae et Antonia Priscilla patri
EDCS-26600923.translation: To the Divine Manes. Antonius Rufus, decurion of the colony, and Antonia Priscilla, to their father Lucius Antonius Priscus of the Papirian tribe, who lived 62 years.
EDCS-26600923.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Lucius Antonius Priscus, placed by his children, Antonius Rufus, a decurion, and Antonia Priscilla.

EDCS-26600924.text_edition: Dis Manibus Quintiniae Magnillae vixit annos XXX Antonius Rufus IIviralis coloniae maritus et Antonii Priscus Rufus Rufinus decuriones coloniae filii
EDCS-26600924.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Quintinia Magnilla, who lived 30 years. Antonius Rufus, duumviral of the colony, her husband, and Antonii Priscus, Rufus, Rufinus, decurions of the colony, her sons.
EDCS-26600924.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintinia Magnilla, erected by her husband Antonius Rufus, a duumviral, and her sons Priscus, Rufus, and Rufinus, all decurions of the colony.

EDCS-26600925.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Antonio Cai filio Papiria Ursino decurioni coloniae Dacicae Sarmizegetusae vixit nnos XXVIII Terentia Hermione mater infelicissima bene merenti posuit
EDCS-26600925.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Gaius Antonius Ursinus, son of Gaius, of the Papirian tribe, decurion of the Dacian colony of Sarmizegetusa, who lived 28 years. Terentia Hermione, his most unfortunate mother, placed this for her well-deserving son.
EDCS-26600925.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Antonius Ursinus, a decurion, placed by his grieving mother Terentia Hermione.

EDCS-26600927.text_edition: Collegio fabrum decuriae III Marcus Aurelius Fortunatus ob duplam sportulam collatam sibi
EDCS-26600927.translation: To the Collegium of Smiths, Third Decuria. Marcus Aurelius Fortunatus, for the double sportula he received.
EDCS-26600927.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Marcus Aurelius Fortunatus to the Collegium of Smiths (Third Decuria) in thanks for a double distribution (sportula) he received, likely a benefactor's gift for the guild.

EDCS-26600928.translation: Aurelius Theophilus, thus according to his vow.
EDCS-26600928.summary: This is a votive inscription by Aurelius Theophilus, indicating that he fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-26600929.text_edition: ? Valerio Cai filio Papiria Valentino decurioni coloniae Samizegetusae metropolis aedilicio IIvirali et quinquennali praefecto collegii fabrum et patrono eorundem Aurelii Theophilus et Castor deccuriones municipii Porolissensis amico fidissimo locus datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-26600929.translation: To Valerius, son of Gaius, of the Papirian tribe, Valentinus, decurion of the colony of Sarmizegetusa Metropolis, an aedilician duumvir and quinquennial duumvir, prefect of the guild of craftsmen and their patron. Aurelii Theophilus and Castor, decurions of the municipality of Porolissum, to their most faithful friend. The plot was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-26600929.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument dedicated to Valerius Valentinus, a prominent civic official in Sarmizegetusa, erected by decurions from Porolissum, with the burial plot granted by decree of the decurions.

EDCS-26600930.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelia Lucilla vixit nnos XXXVIII Caius Clodius Secundinus IIviralis coloniae coniugi pientissimae posuit
EDCS-26600930.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelia Lucilla lived 38 years. Gaius Clodius Secundinus, duumvir of the colony, placed this for his most devoted wife.
EDCS-26600930.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Gaius Clodius Secundinus, a duumvir of the colony, for his wife, Aurelia Lucilla, who died at the age of 38.

EDCS-26600931.text_edition: Marco Cominio Marci filio Papiria Quinto pontifici coloniae Sarmizegetusae et praefecto quinquennali pro Antonino Imperatore patrono collegii fabrum equo publico ordo coloniae ob merita ipsius
EDCS-26600931.translation: To Marcus Cominius, son of Marcus, of the Papirian tribe, Quintus, priest of the colony of Sarmizegetusa and quinquennial prefect for Antoninus Imperator, patron of the guild of craftsmen, with a public horse. The order of the colony honors his merits.
EDCS-26600931.summary: This inscription honors Marcus Cominius Quintus, a priest, quinquennial prefect, and patron of craftsmen in Sarmizegetusa, for his merits, by the colonial order, and indicates he was granted a public horse.

EDCS-26600932.text_edition: Dis Manibus Corneliae Faustinae undeviginti annos natae Caius Spedius Clemens IIvir coloniae coniugis memoriae erga se dignissimae fecit
EDCS-26600932.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Cornelia Faustina, who lived nineteen years. Gaius Spedius Clemens, duumvir of the colony, made this for his wife, most worthy of his remembrance.
EDCS-26600932.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Gaius Spedius Clemens, a duumvir of the colony, for his wife, Cornelia Faustina, who died at the age of nineteen.

EDCS-26600933.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Corneliae Victorinae filiae Marcus Cornelius Primus Augustalis coloniae
EDCS-26600933.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Cornelia Victorina, his daughter. Marcus Cornelius Primus, Augustalis of the colony.
EDCS-26600933.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Marcus Cornelius Primus, an Augustalis of the colony, for his daughter, Cornelia Victorina.

EDCS-26600934.text_edition: Crasso Macrobio negotiatores provinciae Apulensis defensori optimo locus datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-26600934.translation: To Crassus Macrobius, most excellent defender of the merchants of the province of Apulia. The plot was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-26600934.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to Crassus Macrobius, honored as a defender by Apulian merchants, with his burial plot granted by decree of the decurions.

EDCS-26600935.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Flavio Valenti vixit annos LXXX collegium fabrum patrono posuit
EDCS-26600935.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Lucius Flavius Valens, he lived 80 years. The guild of craftsmen placed this for their patron.
EDCS-26600935.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by the guild of craftsmen for their patron, Lucius Flavius Valens, who lived to be 80 years old.

EDCS-26600936.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aulo Frisenio Papiria Vero decurioni coloniae Sarmizegetusae vixit annos XXXX et Lucio Clodio Papiria Etr<u=I>sco#Etr<u>sco#ETRISCO decurioni coloniae
EDCS-26600936.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Aulus Frisenius, of the Papirian tribe, Verus, decurion of the colony of Sarmizegetusa, he lived 40 years. And to Lucius Clodius, of the Papirian tribe, Etruscus, decurion of the colony.
EDCS-26600936.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to two decurions of the colony of Sarmizegetusa, Aulus Frisenius Verus and Lucius Clodius Etruscus.

EDCS-26600937.text_edition: Quinto Ianuario Quinti filio Collina Rufo Tavio flamini quinquennali primo pro Imperatore ordo coloniae Ulpiae Traianae Dacicae Sarmizegetusae
EDCS-26600937.translation: To Quintus Ianuarius, son of Quintus, of the Collina tribe, Rufus Tavius, quinquennial flamen, first for the Emperor. The order of the colony Ulpia Traiana Dacica Sarmizegetusa honors him.
EDCS-26600937.summary: This inscription honors Quintus Ianuarius Rufus Tavius, a quinquennial flamen for the Emperor, by the order of the colony Ulpia Traiana Dacica Sarmizegetusa.

EDCS-26600938.text_edition: Dis Manibus Quinto Ianuario Zosimo vixit nnos XXXV et Ianuariae Candidae vixit nnos XXX Quintus Ianuarius Agathangelus filiis piissimis posuit ad funus sic autem Zosimi et titulo contulerunt collegium fabrum |(denarios) CCCC Quintus Ianuarius
EDCS-26600938.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Quintus Ianuarius Zosimus, he lived 35 years, and to Ianuaria Candida, she lived 30 years. Quintus Ianuarius Agathangelus placed this for his most devoted children. For the funeral, as well as for Zosimus, and for the title, the guild of craftsmen contributed 400 denarii. Quintus Ianuarius [provided the rest].
EDCS-26600938.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Quintus Ianuarius Agathangelus to his children, Quintus Ianuarius Zosimus and Ianuaria Candida, with the guild of craftsmen contributing funds for the funeral and title.

EDCS-26600939.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Iulio Marco ex collegio fabrum vixit annos LX collegium supra scriptum
EDCS-26600939.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Gaius Iulius Marcus from the college of craftsmen. He lived for 60 years. The above-written college [set this up].
EDCS-26600939.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by the college of craftsmen for one of its members, Gaius Iulius Marcus, who died at the age of 60, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-26600940.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Iulio Victori Augustali coloniae Sarmizetegusae metropolis vixit annos LXXVI Iulia Maxima coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-26600940.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Marcus Iulius Victor, Augustalis of the colony of Sarmizegetusa, the metropolis. He lived for 76 years. Iulia Maxima placed this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-26600940.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Iulia Maxima to her husband, Marcus Iulius Victor, an Augustalis of Sarmizegetusa, who lived to be 76 years old, probably from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-26600941.text_edition: In memoriam  Laurentianus collegio fabrum vixit annos XXXVI Iulia
EDCS-26600941.translation: In memory of Laurentianus from the college of craftsmen. He lived for 36 years. Iulia [set this up].
EDCS-26600941.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Laurentianus, a member of the college of craftsmen, who died at 36 years old, likely set up by Iulia, probably from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-26600942.text_edition: Dis Manibus   Marcio Augustalis coloniae Sarmizegetusae heres coniugi piissimae fecit
EDCS-26600942.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Marcius, Augustalis of the colony of Sarmizegetusa. His heir made this for his most devoted wife.
EDCS-26600942.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcius, an Augustalis of Sarmizegetusa, dedicated by his heir for his most devoted wife, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-26600944.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caius Togernius Ingenuus Augustalis coloniae Sarmizegetusae metropolis vixit nnos XXX Petilia Victorina mater posuit
EDCS-26600944.translation: To the Divine Shades. Gaius Togernius Ingenuus, Augustalis of the colony of Sarmizegetusa, the metropolis. He lived for 30 years. Petilia Victorina, his mother, placed this.
EDCS-26600944.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Petilia Victorina for her son, Gaius Togernius Ingenuus, an Augustalis of Sarmizegetusa, who died at the age of 30, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-26600945.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Turranio Frontoni decurioni coloniae vixit nnos XXII et Fanniae Maximae vixit annos XX et Turraniis Antonino vixit annos XX et Maximo decurioni coloniae vixit annos XXX Turranius Iustus decurio coloniae Metropolis et Maxima eius filiis posuerunt
EDCS-26600945.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Marcus Turranius Fronto, decurion of the colony, who lived for 22 years; and to Fannia Maxima, who lived for 20 years; and to Turranius Antoninus, who lived for 20 years; and to Maximus, decurion of the colony, who lived for 30 years. Turranius Iustus, decurion of the colony of Metropolis, and his wife Maxima placed this for their children.
EDCS-26600945.summary: This is a multi-generational funerary inscription for several family members: Marcus Turranius Fronto, Fannia Maxima, Turranius Antoninus, and Maximus, all children of Turranius Iustus (a decurion of Sarmizegetusa Metropolis) and his wife Maxima, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-26600946.text_edition: Valeria Cara vixit annos XXIX Titus Flavius Aper scriba coloniae Sarmizegetusae coniugi rarissimae
EDCS-26600946.translation: Valeria Cara lived for 29 years. Titus Flavius Aper, scribe of the colony of Sarmizegetusa, [made this] for his most uncommon wife.
EDCS-26600946.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Titus Flavius Aper, a scribe of Sarmizegetusa, for his beloved wife, Valeria Cara, who died at the age of 29, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-26600947.text_edition: Cominiae coniugi Titi Vareni Probi quinquennali coloniae Sarmizegetusae Marcus Cominius Quintus pontifex et bis quinquennalis coloniae equo publico sacerdos arae Augusti sorori pientissimae loco dato decreto decurionum
EDCS-26600947.translation: To Cominia, wife of Titus Varenus Probus, quinquennalis of the colony of Sarmizegetusa. Marcus Cominius Quintus, pontifex and twice quinquennalis of the colony, with public horse, priest of the Altar of Augustus, [set this up] for his most devoted sister, on a plot of land granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-26600947.summary: This is a funerary or honorary inscription dedicated by Marcus Cominius Quintus (a high-ranking priest and official) to his sister Cominia, wife of Titus Varenus Probus (also a high-ranking official), on land granted by the city council, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-26600949.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caius Venetius Privatus Augustalis coloniae Sarmizegetusae metropolis vixit annis L Ulpia Patricia coniux
EDCS-26600949.translation: To the Divine Shades. Gaius Venetius Privatus, Augustalis of the colony of Sarmizegetusa, the metropolis. He lived for 50 years. Ulpia Patricia, his wife [set this up].
EDCS-26600949.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Ulpia Patricia to her husband, Gaius Venetius Privatus, an Augustalis of Sarmizegetusa, who died at the age of 50, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-26600950.text_edition: Marcus Veponius Maximinus Augustalis coloniae Sarmizegetusae porticum tectum ex solo de suo fecit
EDCS-26600950.translation: Marcus Veponius Maximinus, Augustalis of the colony of Sarmizegetusa, made the covered portico from his own land at his own expense.
EDCS-26600950.summary: This is an honorary or building inscription recording that Marcus Veponius Maximinus, an Augustalis of Sarmizegetusa, constructed a covered portico at his own expense on his own land, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-26600951.text_edition: Publius Vibidius - f(ilius) Valentinus aedilis et quinquennalis S honorem M pecunia sua loco publico
EDCS-26600951.translation: Publius Vibidius, son of ---, Valentinus, aedile and quinquennial censor, to S. (or: to his honor), at his own expense in a public place.
EDCS-26600951.summary: This inscription records the public dedication of a monument (or statue) by Publius Vibidius Valentinus, who held the magistracies of aedile and quinquennial censor, funded by himself in a public location.

EDCS-26600952.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aureliae feminae vixit annos XXV Marcus Ulpius Iustinus decurio coloniae coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-26600952.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Ulpius Iustinus, decurion of the colony, placed this for Aurelia, a woman who lived for 25 years, his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-26600952.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a tombstone dedicated by Marcus Ulpius Iustinus, a decurion, to his beloved wife Aurelia, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-26600953.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Marco Ulpio Maio decurioni coloniae Sarmizegetusae metropolis vixit annos XXX
EDCS-26600953.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Marcus Ulpius Maius, decurion of the colony Sarmizegetusa Metropolis, who lived for 30 years.
EDCS-26600953.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Marcus Ulpius Maius, a decurion of the city Sarmizegetusa Metropolis, who passed away at the age of 30.

EDCS-26600954.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Ulpio Restuto IIviro coloniae Sarmizegetusae Ulpiae Marcellina et Restuta filiae et heredes titulum ponendum curaverunt
EDCS-26600954.translation: To the Divine Manes. Ulpia Marcellina and Restuta, daughters and heirs, took care to have this title placed for Marcus Ulpius Restutus, duumvir of the colony Sarmizegetusa.
EDCS-26600954.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Ulpius Restutus, a duumvir of the colony Sarmizegetusa, dedicated by his daughters and heirs, Ulpia Marcellina and Restuta.

EDCS-26600958.text_edition: Dis Manibus Cai Iuli Cai fili Rufi domo Viminacio nnorum XX Iulia Gemellina fratri pientissimo et Marco Aurelio Marci filio Maximo mensium VIII Aurelius Helico et Iulia Gemellina parentes hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur
EDCS-26600958.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelius Helico and Iulia Gemellina, parents, placed this monument for Gaius Iulius Rufus, son of Gaius, from Viminacium, 20 years old, his most devoted brother, and for Marcus Aurelius Maximus, son of Marcus, 8 months old. This monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-26600958.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Aurelius Helico and Iulia Gemellina to their son Marcus Aurelius Maximus, who died at 8 months old, and to Gaius Iulius Rufus, Iulia's most devoted brother, who died at 20 years old and was from Viminacium, with a stipulation that the monument is not to be inherited.

EDCS-26600959.text_edition: Dis Manibus Antonia Sisiata vixit annos LX Caius Iulius Rusticinus vixit annos XL Antonia Protogenia vixit annos XXVI Caius Iulius Rusticus matri fratri et coniugi karissimis
EDCS-26600959.translation: To the Divine Manes. Antonia Sisiata lived 60 years. Gaius Iulius Rusticinus lived 40 years. Antonia Protogenia lived 26 years. Gaius Iulius Rusticus [erected this] for his dearest mother, brother, and wife.
EDCS-26600959.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Gaius Iulius Rusticus to his mother Antonia Sisiata (aged 60), his brother Gaius Iulius Rusticinus (aged 40), and his wife Antonia Protogenia (aged 26).

EDCS-26600960.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription possibly indicating ownership or dedication by someone named Aurelius Mucianus.

EDCS-26600961.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aureliae Proculae vixit annis III mensibus II Bubalus Augusti libertus filiae innocentissimae posuit
EDCS-26600961.translation: To the Divine Manes. Bubalus, freedman of Augustus, placed this for his most innocent daughter Aurelia Procula, who lived for 3 years and 2 months.
EDCS-26600961.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Bubalus, a freedman of Emperor Augustus, to his innocent daughter Aurelia Procula, who died at a young age of 3 years and 2 months.

EDCS-26600962.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ulpiae Candidae vixit annos XXV Ulpius Eucharistus coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-26600962.translation: To the Divine Manes. Ulpius Eucharistus placed this for Ulpia Candida, his well-deserving wife, who lived for 25 years.
EDCS-26600962.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ulpia Candida, who lived 25 years, dedicated by her husband Ulpius Eucharistus, acknowledging her as his well-deserving wife.

EDCS-26600963.text_edition: Dis Manibus Castena Nice vixit annis XXVII testamento poni sibi iussit arbitratu Herenniae Maximae
EDCS-26600963.translation: To the Divine Manes. Castena Nice lived for 27 years. She ordered in her will that this be placed for herself, at the discretion of Herennia Maxima.
EDCS-26600963.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Castena Nice, who lived 27 years, placed according to her will and under the direction of Herennia Maxima.

EDCS-26600964.text_edition: Maximo Fan annos XXX Domitia Secundina patrono pientissimo merito fecit
EDCS-26600964.translation: Domitia Secundina deservedly made this for Maximus Fannus, her most devoted patron, who lived for 30 years.
EDCS-26600964.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a dedication by Domitia Secundina to her patron, Maximus Fannus, who died at age 30.

EDCS-26600965.text_edition: Dis Manibus Hygiae Imperatorum nostrorum vixit annos XLVII Augustalis eorundem coniugi bene merenti posuit et ipse hic situs est vixit annos L
EDCS-26600965.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Hygia, Augustalis of our Emperors, who lived 47 years. Her husband, also a Augustalis of the same Emperors, placed this for his well-deserving wife, and he himself is buried here, having lived 50 years.
EDCS-26600965.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by an Augustalis of the Emperors to his wife, Hygia, who was also an Augustalis, and records both their deaths and his eventual burial at the same location.

EDCS-26600966.text_edition: Dis Manibus Cnaeo  Ianuario vixit annos XXI A
EDCS-26600966.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. For Gnaeus Januarius, who lived 21 years. A.
EDCS-26600966.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gnaeus Januarius, who died at the age of 21.

EDCS-26600967.text_edition: Dis Manibus Liciniae Valentinae vixit annos LX et Aureliae Maximinae vixit annos XX Aurelius Maximus et Aurelius Pusinnus coniugibus dignissimis
EDCS-26600967.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. For Licinia Valentina, who lived 60 years, and for Aurelia Maximina, who lived 20 years. Aurelius Maximus and Aurelius Pusinnus [made this] for their most worthy wives.
EDCS-26600967.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelius Maximus and Aurelius Pusinnus to their two wives, Licinia Valentina and Aurelia Maximina.

EDCS-26600968.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tito Lisinio Amabili vixit annos XL Lisinia Callityche coniunx bene merenti posuit
EDCS-26600968.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Titus Lisinius Amabilis, who lived 40 years. His wife Lisinia Callityche placed this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-26600968.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Lisinia Callityche to her husband, Titus Lisinius Amabilis, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-26600969.text_edition: Dis Manibus Gaio Longinio Maximo vixit annos LVIII Iulia Afrodisia coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-26600969.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Gaius Longinius Maximus, who lived 58 years. Iulia Afrodisia placed this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-26600969.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Iulia Afrodisia to her husband, Gaius Longinius Maximus, who died at the age of 58.

EDCS-26600970.text_edition: Dis Manibus hic pietatis honos haec sunt pia dona mariti cui multum dilecta fui ego Marcellina pro merita cernis quae mihi solus coniux Aelius qCuoque#quoque#COQUE post obitum memor amoris dicat
EDCS-26600970.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Here is the honor of piety, these are the pious gifts of a husband to whom I, Marcellina, was much beloved. You see what I deserve, which Aelius, my only husband, dedicates after my death, mindful of his love.
EDCS-26600970.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Aelius, the husband of Marcellina, dedicated to her memory, expressing his enduring love and her worthiness.

EDCS-26600971.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aeliae Modestae  vixit annis LV  decurio coniugi pientissimae   Dis Manibus Valeriae Felete sic vixit annis LII
EDCS-26600971.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Aelia Modesta, who lived 55 years. A decurion [dedicated this] to his most devoted wife. To the Spirits of the Dead. Thus lived Valeria Felete for 52 years.
EDCS-26600971.summary: This inscription comprises two distinct funerary dedications: one by a decurion to his wife Aelia Modesta, and another to Valeria Felete.

EDCS-26600972.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Octavio Nepoti vixit annos LXX Iulia Valentina heres coniugi pientissimo faciendum procuravit hic situs est
EDCS-26600972.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Gaius Octavius Nepos, who lived 70 years. Iulia Valentina, his heir, ensured this was made for her most devoted husband. He is buried here.
EDCS-26600972.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Octavius Nepos, who died at age 70, commissioned by his heir, Iulia Valentina, who was also his devoted wife.

EDCS-26600973.text_edition: Dis Manibus Papiriae Quintillae vixit nnis III mensibus XI diebus VII Papirius Quintinus filiae
EDCS-26600973.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Papiria Quintilla, who lived 3 years, 11 months, 7 days. Papirius Quintinus [made this] for his daughter.
EDCS-26600973.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Papirius Quintinus to his young daughter, Papiria Quintilla, who died just before her fourth birthday.

EDCS-26600974.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age of the deceased, Severianus.

EDCS-26600979.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Aurelius Maximus legulus votum libens animo solvit
EDCS-26600979.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Marcus Aurelius Maximus, an ore-picker, willingly and gladly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26600979.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marcus Aurelius Maximus, an ore-picker, in fulfillment of a vow, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD given the Aurelian name.

EDCS-26600980.text_edition: Libero Patri sacrum Atrius Maximi votum libens merito solvit
EDCS-26600980.translation: Sacred to Liber Pater. Atrius, son of Maximus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26600980.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Liber Pater by Atrius, son of Maximus, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-26600981.text_edition: D(is) M(anibus) Avilliae Pietatis domo Aequo vixit annis XXXX Dasas Suttinis coniugi piissimae faciendum curavit
EDCS-26600981.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Avillia Pietas from Aequum, who lived for 40 years. Dasas, son of Suttinis, arranged for this to be made for his most devoted wife.
EDCS-26600981.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Avillia Pietas, who lived 40 years and was from Aequum, set up by her husband Dasas, son of Suttinis.

EDCS-26600982.text_edition: Dis Manibus Arria Mamma vixit nnos XXV posuit Beucus servus coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-26600982.translation: To the Divine Manes. Arria Mamma lived for 25 years. Beucus, a slave, placed this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-26600982.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Arria Mamma, who lived 25 years, erected by her slave husband Beucus for his well-deserving wife.

EDCS-26600983.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Attio Saturnino vixit annos X Marcus Attius Primitivos sic nepoti posuit
EDCS-26600983.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Marcus Attius Saturninus, who lived for 10 years. Marcus Attius Primitivus thus placed this for his grandson.
EDCS-26600983.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Attius Saturninus, who lived 10 years, placed by his grandfather Marcus Attius Primitivus.

EDCS-26600984.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcus Aurelius Scenobarbus vixit nnos C Marcus Aurelius Si
EDCS-26600984.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Aurelius Scenobarbus lived for 100 years. Marcus Aurelius Si[...]
EDCS-26600984.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Marcus Aurelius Scenobarbus, who lived 100 years, likely erected by a relative named Marcus Aurelius Si[...], probably dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-26600985.text_edition: Dis Manibus Cassiae Peregrinae Integrae vixit annos XXVII fecit Bisius Scenobarbi Sardias coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-26600985.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Cassia Peregrina Integra, who lived for 27 years. Bisius, son of Scenobarbus, from Sardinia, made this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-26600985.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cassia Peregrina Integra, who lived 27 years, made by her husband Bisius, son of Scenobarbus, from Sardinia.

EDCS-26600986.text_edition: Dis Manibus Domitiae Chlone quae vixit annos XXX filia- mat-- bene merenti posuit
EDCS-26600986.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Domitia Chlone, who lived for 30 years. Her daughter-maternal-beneficiary placed this.
EDCS-26600986.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Domitia Chlone, who lived 30 years, placed by her daughter, presumably her well-deserving daughter.

EDCS-26600987.text_edition: Dis Manibus Maximae Luri vixit nnos XXV Rufus et Tertia fili-- piissim--
EDCS-26600987.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Maxima Luri, who lived for 25 years. Rufus and Tertia, her most devoted children.
EDCS-26600987.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Maxima Luri, who lived 25 years, erected by her most devoted children, Rufus and Tertia.

EDCS-26600988.text_edition: Dis Manibus Plaetoria Maxima vixit annos III Lavius Verzonis neptiae bene merenti posuit
EDCS-26600988.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Plaetoria Maxima lived for 3 years. Lavius Verzonis placed this for his well-deserving granddaughter.
EDCS-26600988.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Plaetoria Maxima, set up by her grandfather Lavius Verzonis, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26600989.text_edition: Dis Manibus Planio Baezi qui et Magistro vixit annos L Maxima coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-26600989.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Planius Baezi, also known as Magister, who lived 50 years. Maxima (placed this) for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-26600989.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Maxima to her husband Planius Baezi, also called Magister, who lived to be 50 years old.

EDCS-26600990.text_edition: Dis Manibus Platinonis Verzonis kastello Anso vixit nnos LIIII Rufus Platoris coniugi karissimae pro pietate faciendum curavit
EDCS-26600990.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Platinon Verzonis, from the fort of Ansó, who lived 54 years. Rufus Platoris took care to have this made for his dearest wife, out of piety.
EDCS-26600990.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Rufus Platoris for his beloved wife Platinon Verzonis, noting her origin from the fort of Ansó.

EDCS-26600991.text_edition: Dis Manibus Gaio Valerio Viatori annorum XVI Andes Titi et Valeria Terentia filio bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-26600991.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Gaius Valerius Viator, 16 years old. Andes Titi and Valeria Terentia placed this for their well-deserving son.
EDCS-26600991.summary: This is a funerary monument for Gaius Valerius Viator, aged 16, erected by his parents Andes Titi and Valeria Terentia.

EDCS-26600997.text_edition: Dis Manibus  MOCIO Bov hoc missus veteranus vixit annos Calp     MOCVLI Adiutor vixit annos XXIV Terentius filis posuit
EDCS-26600997.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Mocius Bov, a discharged veteran, who lived [---] years. Calp[---] MOCULI Adiutor lived 24 years. Terentius placed this for his sons.
EDCS-26600997.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Terentius for his two sons, Mocius Bov, a veteran, and MOCULI Adiutor, who died at 24.

EDCS-26600998.text_edition: Dis Manibus   veteranus ex beneficiario legionis XIII geminae vixit annos LXX ponendum curavit coniunx infelicissima et filii hic situs est
EDCS-26600998.translation: To the Divine Spirits. [For ---], a veteran from the beneficiarii of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, who lived 70 years. His most unfortunate wife and sons took care to have this placed. He is buried here.
EDCS-26600998.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an unnamed veteran of the Legio XIII Gemina, aged 70, set up by his grieving wife and sons.

EDCS-26600999.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Sextus Licinius Quintus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-26600999.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Sextus Licinius Quintus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26600999.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, dedicated by Sextus Licinius Quintus in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-26601000.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tito Flavio Rufo veterano legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis domo Amasia vixit annos LX Iulia Maxima coniunx et Flavi Venusta et Maximus et Rufinus filii pro pietate posuerunt hic situs est
EDCS-26601000.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Titus Flavius Rufus, a veteran of the Eleventh Claudian Legion, pious and faithful, from Amasia, who lived 60 years. His wife Julia Maxima and sons Flavius Venustus and Maximus and Rufinus placed this out of piety. He is buried here.
EDCS-26601000.summary: This is a funerary monument for Titus Flavius Rufus, a 60-year-old veteran of Legio XI Claudia from Amasia, erected by his wife and sons.

EDCS-27100064.text_edition: Aesculapio et Hygiae Caius Iulius Atianus praefectus equitum ob resti[tut&
EDCS-27100064.translation: To Aesculapius and Hygia. Gaius Julius Atianus, prefect of cavalry, on account of the resti[tution ---].
EDCS-27100064.summary: This is a votive dedication to Aesculapius and Hygia by Gaius Julius Atianus, a cavalry prefect, likely made in gratitude for a restoration (perhaps of health or a building).

EDCS-27100065.text_edition: Apollini sacrum Sola Mucatri veteranus alae Frontonianae votum solvit
EDCS-27100065.translation: Sacred to Apollo. Sola Mucatri, a veteran of the Ala Frontoniana, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27100065.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Apollo by Sola Mucatri, a veteran of the Ala Frontoniana, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-27100067.text_edition: Fortunae Reduci Aelius Celer praefectus equitum alae Frontonianae ob restitutionem balinei posuit
EDCS-27100067.translation: To Fortuna Redux, Aelius Celer, prefect of cavalry of the Ala Frontoniana, placed this on account of the restoration of the bathhouse.
EDCS-27100067.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument to the goddess Fortuna Redux by a cavalry prefect, Aelius Celer, for the restoration of a bathhouse, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27100069.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Aurellius sic Vitellianus sesquiplarius pro se et sorore sua votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-27100069.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Aurellius Vitellianus, a sesquiplarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for himself and his sister.
EDCS-27100069.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a Roman soldier, Aurellius Vitellianus, made on behalf of himself and his sister.

EDCS-27100070.text_edition: Libero Patri et Liberae Montanius Viator
EDCS-27100070.translation: To Liber Pater and Libera, Montanius Viator [dedicates this].
EDCS-27100070.summary: This is a brief dedication by Montanius Viator to the Roman deities Liber Pater and Libera.

EDCS-27100071.text_edition: Marti ala I Tungrorum Frontoniana per Titum Vettulenum Nepotem praefectum equitum votum solvit libens merito
