EDCS-27100071.translation: To Mars, the First Ala of Tungrians, Frontoniana, through Titus Vettulenus Nepos, prefect of cavalry, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-27100071.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Mars by a cavalry unit, the First Ala of Tungrians, Frontoniana, facilitated by their prefect, Titus Vettulenus Nepos.

EDCS-27100073.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurellio sic Antonino Pio Felici Augusto Parthico maximo Britannico maximo pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XVI consuli IIII optimo maximoque principi ala I Tungrorum Frontoniana Antoniniana numini eius semper ac merito devota
EDCS-27100073.translation: To the Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, greatest Parthian, greatest Briton, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power for the sixteenth time, consul for the fourth time, the best and greatest princeps, the First Ala of Tungrians, Frontoniana Antoniniana, always and deservedly devoted to his divinity.
EDCS-27100073.summary: This inscription is a laudatory dedication by a Roman cavalry unit, the First Ala of Tungrians, Frontoniana Antoniniana, to Emperor Caracalla, likely dating to 213 CE based on his titulature.

EDCS-27200002.text_edition: Genio scholae bbeneficiariorum sub Octavio Iuliano legato Augustorum AB APMIHAAN IVTVALN
EDCS-27200002.translation: To the Genius of the school of beneficiarii, under Octavius Julianus, legate of the Augusti. [Partial illegible text: AB APMIHAAN IVTVALN].
EDCS-27200002.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the guardian spirit (Genius) of a beneficiarii's schola, erected under the authority of Octavius Julianus, a legate serving under multiple emperors (Augusti).

EDCS-27200003.text_edition: Herculi invicto pro salute Marci Aureli Antonini Augusti
EDCS-27200003.translation: To Hercules the Unconquered, for the well-being of Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus.
EDCS-27200003.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Hercules Invictus made for the health and safety of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, likely Caracalla.

EDCS-27200004.text_edition: Herculi invicto Quintus Ennius Ianuarius beneficiarius consularis legionis V Macedonicae ponendum curavit
EDCS-27200004.translation: To Hercules the Unconquered, Quintus Ennius Ianuarius, a consular beneficiarius of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, saw to its placement.
EDCS-27200004.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument to Hercules Invictus by Quintus Ennius Ianuarius, a beneficiarius from Legio V Macedonica.

EDCS-27200005.text_edition: Invicto Aurelius Montanus miles legionis V Macedonicae pI(iae)#p(iae)#I constantis felicis libens posuit
EDCS-27200005.translation: To the Unconquered [god], Aurelius Montanus, a soldier of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, Pious and Constant, Fortunate, willingly placed this.
EDCS-27200005.summary: This inscription is a willing dedication to an 'Unconquered' deity, possibly Hercules or Sol Invictus, by Aurelius Montanus, a soldier of Legio V Macedonica.

EDCS-27200006.text_edition: I V P Aurelius Florus votum solvit
EDCS-27200006.translation: I. V. P. [likely Iovi Optimo Maximo] Aurelius Florus fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27200006.summary: This is a brief votive inscription by Aurelius Florus, likely dedicating a monument to Jupiter Optimus Maximus (implied by the common abbreviation I.O.M. for Iovi Optimo Maximo).

EDCS-27200007.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Aurelius Lucifer votum libens posuit
EDCS-27200007.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Aurelius Lucifer willingly placed his vow.
EDCS-27200007.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aurelius Lucifer, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-27200008.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute domini nostri Marci Aureli Antonini Imperatoris Aurelius Rusticus lC(ibens)#l(ibens)#C animo votum posuit
EDCS-27200008.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the health of our lord Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Imperator, Aurelius Rusticus willingly and with good spirit placed his vow.
EDCS-27200008.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aurelius Rusticus, made on behalf of the Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, likely dating to the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27200009.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Aurelius Sedatus immunis librarius votum libens solvit
EDCS-27200009.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Aurelius Sedatus, an exempt clerk, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27200009.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aurelius Sedatus, an immunis librarius, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-27200010.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Aurelius Valentinus praefectus equitum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-27200010.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Aurelius Valentinus, prefect of cavalry, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27200010.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aurelius Valentinus, a praefectus equitum, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-27200011.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iucundius Iuvenalis cornicularius legati votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-27200011.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Iucundius Iuvenalis, cornicularius of the legate, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27200011.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Iucundius Iuvenalis, a cornicularius, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-27200012.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Manlius quaestor votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-27200012.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Manlius, quaestor, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27200012.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Manlius, a quaestor, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-27200013.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo D REB IAS miles legionis I Italicae
EDCS-27200013.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, D. Reb.ias, soldier of the First Italic Legion.
EDCS-27200013.summary: This is an incomplete dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a soldier named D. Reb.ias from Legio I Italica.

EDCS-27200014.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Silvano Publius S C
EDCS-27200014.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Silvanus, Publius S. C.
EDCS-27200014.summary: This is an incomplete dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Silvanus by Publius S.C.

EDCS-27200015.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Valerius Romanus Aurelius Felix votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-27200015.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Valerius Romanus and Aurelius Felix willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-27200015.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by two individuals, Valerius Romanus and Aurelius Felix, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-27200016.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et diis deabusque et Genio loci Optatus praefectus legionis V Macedonicae constantis votum libens solvit merito
EDCS-27200016.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and to all gods and goddesses and to the Genius of the place, Optatus, prefect of the constant Fifth Macedonian Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27200016.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, all gods and goddesses, and the Genius Loci by Optatus, the prefect of Legio V Macedonica, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-27200017.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Aurelius Victor beneficiarius ex  voto posuit
EDCS-27200017.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno, Aurelius Victor, a beneficiarius, set this up from a vow.
EDCS-27200017.summary: This inscription records a dedication by a beneficiarius named Aurelius Victor to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-27200018.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Paterno Aurelius Ulpius adiutor officii corniculariorum ex voto libens merito posuit
EDCS-27200018.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Paternus, Aurelius Ulpius, assistant of the office of the cornicularii, willingly and deservedly set this up from a vow.
EDCS-27200018.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Paternus by Aurelius Ulpius, an assistant in the cornicularii's office, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-27200019.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Statori item Depulsori Iulius Maximianus tribunus militum votum solvit pro sua suorumque salute
EDCS-27200019.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the Stayer, and also the Averter of Evil, Iulius Maximianus, military tribune, fulfilled a vow for his own safety and that of his family.
EDCS-27200019.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Iulius Maximianus, a military tribune, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Stator and Depulsor, fulfilling a vow for his and his family's safety.

EDCS-27200020.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Liber Pater, seemingly originating from Rome or Lucca.

EDCS-27200021.text_edition: Marti amico et consentienti sacrum Hermias dedicavit idemque vovit
EDCS-27200021.translation: Sacred to Mars, the friendly and consenting god. Hermias dedicated this and also vowed it.
EDCS-27200021.summary: This inscription is a sacred dedication to Mars, described as friendly and consenting, made by Hermias.

EDCS-27200022.text_edition: Mercurio consentienti sacrum Hermias dedicavit idemque vovit
EDCS-27200022.translation: Sacred to Mercury, the consenting god. Hermias dedicated this and also vowed it.
EDCS-27200022.summary: This inscription is a sacred dedication to Mercury, described as consenting, made by Hermias.

EDCS-27200023.text_edition: Aelius Maximus miles legionis V Macedonicae votum solvit libens posuit
EDCS-27200023.translation: Aelius Maximus, a soldier of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, willingly fulfilled his vow and set this up.
EDCS-27200023.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Aelius Maximus, a soldier of the Legio V Macedonica, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-27200024.translation: Iulius Iulianus set this up from a vow.
EDCS-27200024.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Iulius Iulianus in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-27200026.text_edition: Deae Nemesi Ulpius Valerius miles legionis V Macedonicae piae Antoninianae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-27200026.translation: To the Goddess Nemesis. Ulpius Valerius, soldier of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, Pious Antoniniana, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27200026.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the goddess Nemesis by Ulpius Valerius, a soldier of the Legio V Macedonica Pia Antoniniana, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-27200027.text_edition: Silvano sacrum Valerius Celsus flamen municipii votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-27200027.translation: Sacred to Silvanus. Valerius Celsus, flamen of the municipality, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27200027.summary: This inscription is a sacred dedication to Silvanus by Valerius Celsus, a municipal flamen, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-27200028.text_edition: SER Aurelius Pat legionis V Macedonicae ex voto
EDCS-27200028.translation: Sergius Aurelius, a member of the V Macedonian Legion, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-27200028.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Sergius Aurelius, a soldier of the V Macedonian Legion, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-27200029.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate III imperator VII consul II proconsul pater patriae legioni V Macedonicae piae cP(onstantis)#c(onstantis)#P dono dedit dedicante Publio Septimio Geta legato Augusti pro praetore curam agente Tiberio Claudio Claudiano legato Augusti
EDCS-27200029.translation: Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, conqueror of Arabia and Adiabene, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the third time, acclaimed imperator seven times, consul twice, proconsul, father of his country, gave this as a gift to the V Macedonian Legion, pious and constant. Dedicated by Publius Septimius Geta, legate of Augustus with propraetorian power, with Tiberius Claudius Claudianus, legate of Augustus, overseeing the work.
EDCS-27200029.summary: This inscription records a gift from Emperor Septimius Severus to the V Macedonian Legion, likely around 195 AD given his third tribunician power, and details the officials responsible for its dedication and oversight.

EDCS-27200030.text_edition: nnos VIII  Aelius Vitalis vixit nnos VIII Aelius Primus [tPubicen]#[tubicen]# legionis supra scriptae et Viria Quarta filiis bene merentibus posuerunt
EDCS-27200030.translation: Aelius Vitalis lived for 8 years. Aelius Primus, trumpeter of the aforementioned legion, and Viria Quarta placed this for their well-deserving sons.
EDCS-27200030.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Aelius Primus, a trumpeter of the V Macedonian Legion, and Viria Quarta for their son Aelius Vitalis, who died at the age of eight.

EDCS-27200031.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aelius Tiiadmes Palmura sic vixit annos VIII Surillio vixit annos XXV Rufina vixit annos XX Aelius Bolhas Bannaei veteranus ex numero Palmurenorum sic et Aelia Domestica coniux aeius posuerant filiae pientissimae et dCulcissimae#dulcissimae#CULCME et liberto et menesteriis sic bene merentibus
EDCS-27200031.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Aelius Tiiadmes Palmura lived thus for 8 years. Surillius lived for 25 years. Rufina lived for 20 years. Aelius Bolhas, son of Bannaeus, a veteran from the Palmyrenes, and his wife Aelia Domestica, placed this for their most devoted and sweetest daughters, and for their freedman and minister, thus well-deserving.
EDCS-27200031.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aelius Bolhas, a Palmyrene veteran, and his wife Aelia Domestica, to the Spirits of the Dead for their daughters, who died at young ages, and also to a freedman and minister.

EDCS-27200032.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Celsus vixit annos LX Aurelius Bassus vixit annos L Aurelius Celsinianus miles cohortis I p p patri et avunculo pientissimis ob merita posuit
EDCS-27200032.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Aurelius Celsus lived for 60 years. Aurelius Bassus lived for 50 years. Aurelius Celsinianus, soldier of the First Cohort, dedicated this for his most devoted father and uncle, in recognition of their merits.
EDCS-27200032.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelius Celsinianus, a soldier, to the Spirits of the Dead for his father Aurelius Celsus and his uncle Aurelius Bassus.

EDCS-27200033.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Victor librarius legionis V Macedonicae vixit annos XXXIIII et Valeria Valentina POI vixit annum Ulpia Flaviana coniugi posuit
EDCS-27200033.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Aurelius Victor, a clerk of the V Macedonian Legion, lived for 34 years. And Valeria Valentina lived for 1 year. Ulpia Flaviana placed this for her husband.
EDCS-27200033.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Ulpia Flaviana to the Spirits of the Dead for her husband Aurelius Victor, a clerk of the V Macedonian Legion, who died at 34, and for Valeria Valentina, who died at age one.

EDCS-27200034.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Statio Prisciano nnorum VIII Marcus Statius Priscianus primus pilus pater faciendum curavit
EDCS-27200034.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. For Marcus Statius Priscianus, 8 years old. Marcus Statius Priscianus, primus pilus, his father, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-27200034.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Dead for Marcus Statius Priscianus, who died at the age of eight, and was set up by his father, Marcus Statius Priscianus, a senior centurion (primus pilus).

EDCS-27200035.text_edition: leia o vixit annos III menses T VAL DEC N M  natis piissimis
EDCS-27200035.translation: Leia lived for 3 years. For the most devoted children.
EDCS-27200035.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Dead for Leia, who lived for three years, and generally for other pious children, with missing information about the dedicant.

EDCS-27200036.text_edition: AII  Aurelius  dus magister Augustalis Napocae Aureliae Felicissimae sorori bene merenti posuit
EDCS-27200036.translation: Aurelius, master of the Augustales in Napoca, placed this for his well-deserving sister Aurelia Felicissima.
EDCS-27200036.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Aurelius, a master of the Augustales at Napoca, for his sister Aurelia Felicissima.

EDCS-27200038.text_edition: ] Flavius Hiero filius AD Antonin  municipii Septimi Potaissae item Ulpia Sabina coniux eius et filii item Claudia Sabina itemcubla item Claudius Maximianus filiorum et suo nomine sua vota restituerunt
EDCS-27200038.translation: ] Flavius Hiero, son of Antoninus, of the municipium Septimum Potaissa, and his wife Ulpia Sabina, and their children, and also Claudia Sabina, and Cubla, and Claudius Maximianus, in their own names and on behalf of their children, restored their vows.
EDCS-27200038.summary: This inscription records the restoration of vows by Flavius Hiero, his wife Ulpia Sabina, their children, Claudia Sabina, Cubla, and Claudius Maximianus, likely to a deity or for a public benefaction within the municipium Septimum Potaissa.

EDCS-27200039.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Crito vixit nnos IIII Aurelius Zosimianus vixit annum ex Moesia superiore Aurelius Zosimus natibus
EDCS-27200039.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelius Crito lived for 4 years. Aurelius Zosimianus lived for 1 year, from Upper Moesia. Aurelius Zosimus to his children.
EDCS-27200039.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely dedicated by Aurelius Zosimus to his deceased children, Aurelius Crito and Aurelius Zosimianus, who originated from Upper Moesia.

EDCS-27200040.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelius Sabinus vixit annos XIII Aelia Tertina vixit annos VI Aelia Meletina vixit annos LX Aelius Ingenuus et Aelia Pudentilla
EDCS-27200040.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aelius Sabinus lived for 13 years. Aelia Tertina lived for 6 years. Aelia Meletina lived for 60 years. Aelius Ingenuus and Aelia Pudentilla (made this).
EDCS-27200040.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Aelius Ingenuus and Aelia Pudentilla for Aelius Sabinus, Aelia Tertina, and Aelia Meletina, who presumably were family members.

EDCS-27200041.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelia Publicia vixit annos XXXX Aurelius Germanus coniugi posuit
EDCS-27200041.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aelia Publicia lived for 40 years. Aurelius Germanus placed this for his wife.
EDCS-27200041.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelius Germanus in memory of his wife, Aelia Publicia.

EDCS-27200042.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aia Nandonis vixit annis LXXX Andrada Bituvantis vixit annis LXXX Bricena vixit annis XL Iusta vixit annis XXX Bedarus vixit XII post obitum ei Herculanus libertus patronae bene merenti
EDCS-27200042.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aia Nandonis lived for 80 years. Andrada Bituvantis lived for 80 years. Bricena lived for 40 years. Iusta lived for 30 years. Bedarus lived for 12 years. After her death, Herculanus, her freedman, (made this) for his well-deserving patroness.
EDCS-27200042.summary: This funerary monument records the deaths of several individuals, with a specific dedication by Herculanus, a freedman, to his well-deserving patroness, Aia Nandonis.

EDCS-27200043.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Aulucmus vixit diem I menses VI annos XXXV Aurelii Basianus et Dasius bene merenti et sibi memoriam faciendam curaverunt
EDCS-27200043.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelius Aulucmus lived for 35 years, 6 months, 1 day. Aurelii Basianus and Dasius took care to have this memorial made for the well-deserving and for themselves.
EDCS-27200043.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Aulucmus, erected by Aurelii Basianus and Dasius, who also intended it as a memorial for themselves.

EDCS-27200044.text_edition: Aurelius Bonosus vixit nnos V menses V Aurelii Viatorinus et Magna filio
EDCS-27200044.translation: Aurelius Bonosus lived for 5 years, 5 months. Aurelii Viatorinus and Magna (made this) for their son.
EDCS-27200044.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelii Viatorinus and Magna in memory of their son, Aurelius Bonosus.

EDCS-27200045.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Epicatius vixit nnis LII Aurelius Viator vi[x](it) vixit annis X Aurelius Corbulo vixit annis XXII
EDCS-27200045.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelius Epicatius lived for 52 years. Aurelius Viator lived for 10 years. Aurelius Corbulo lived for 22 years.
EDCS-27200045.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the deaths of Aurelius Epicatius, Aurelius Viator, and Aurelius Corbulo.

EDCS-27200046.text_edition: vixit annos XXXX Maxima coniux bene merenti
EDCS-27200046.translation: (He) lived for 40 years. Maxima, his wife, (made this) for her well-deserving (husband).
EDCS-27200046.summary: This fragmentary funerary inscription was dedicated by Maxima to her well-deserving husband, who lived for 40 years.

EDCS-27200048.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication or funerary monument, mentioning Ulpius Barbatus and another individual whose name began with Ulpi-.

EDCS-27200049.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ulpius <P=F>roculus#Proculus#FROC vixit annos LX  UlpB(i)#Ulp(i)#ULB PLLI vixit annos L MOC IVS VIXV  vixit annos XXXVII L NA vixit annos XV  HP IT-- v(ixit)? a(nnos) XXX FVIPM vixit annos X nus vixit annos VII  annos II
EDCS-27200049.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Ulpius Proculus lived for 60 years. Ulpius PLLI lived for 50 years. MOCIUS VIXV lived. (Someone) lived for 37 years. L NA lived for 15 years. (Someone) lived for 30 years. FVIPM lived for 10 years. (Someone) lived for 7 years. (Someone lived for) 2 years.
EDCS-27200049.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the ages and names of multiple deceased individuals, including Ulpius Proculus and Ulpius PLLI, suggesting a collective or family burial.

EDCS-27200050.text_edition: AREO IOR IS vixit annos XXX conserva posuit bene merent=TNi#merenti#MERETNI
EDCS-27200050.translation: Areo Ior, who lived for 30 years. Conserva erected this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-27200050.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Conserva for Areo Ior, who lived 30 years.

EDCS-27200051.text_edition: lus VE LO FIL AN patrono pro pietate
EDCS-27200051.translation: Iulus Velofilan, to his patron, out of piety.
EDCS-27200051.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Iulus Velofilan to his patron, motivated by piety.

EDCS-27200058.text_edition: Apollini Atilius Celsianus decurio votum solvit libens merito Apollini
EDCS-27200058.translation: To Apollo, Atilius Celsianus, decurion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Apollo.
EDCS-27200058.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Apollo made by Atilius Celsianus, a decurion, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-27200059.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Sedatus, with Servatus potentially also mentioned.

EDCS-27200061.text_edition: Aurelius Cto tesserarius legionis V Macedonicae vixit nnos XL Aelia Candida
EDCS-27200061.translation: Aurelius Cto, tesserarius of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, lived for 40 years. Aelia Candida [set this up].
EDCS-27200061.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Cto, a tesserarius of Legio V Macedonica, who lived 40 years, presumably erected by Aelia Candida.

EDCS-27200062.text_edition: Marcellina posuit coniugi et marito suo bene merito
EDCS-27200062.translation: Marcellina erected this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-27200062.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Marcellina for her husband.

EDCS-27200066.text_edition: Nymphis Augustis pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Titi Aeli Hadriani Antonini Augusti Pii patris patriae Marcus Statius Priscus legatus Augusti pro praetore votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-27200066.translation: To the Augustan Nymphs, for the health of the Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of his country, Marcus Statius Priscus, Augustan legate with pro-praetorian power, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27200066.summary: This is a votive inscription to the Augustan Nymphs, dedicated by Marcus Statius Priscus, an imperial legate, for the health of Emperor Antoninus Pius, dating to the reign of Antoninus Pius (138-161 CE).

EDCS-27200067.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Imperatoris collegium aurariarum Lucius Calpurnius  donum dedit
EDCS-27200067.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the health of the Emperor, the guild of goldsmiths and Lucius Calpurnius gave this gift.
EDCS-27200067.summary: This inscription records a gift dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the Emperor's health by the guild of goldsmiths and Lucius Calpurnius.

EDCS-27200068.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo ceterisque dis consentibus Marcus Opellius Adiutor IIvir coloniae
EDCS-27200068.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the other consenting gods, Marcus Opellius Adiutor, duumvir of the colony [dedicated this].
EDCS-27200068.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Marcus Opellius Adiutor, a duumvir of a colony, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and other gods.

EDCS-27200069.text_edition: Publio Furio Saturnino consuli Publius Aelius Maximianus decurio coloniae
EDCS-27200069.translation: To Publius Furius Saturninus, consul, Publius Aelius Maximianus, decurion of the colony [dedicated this].
EDCS-27200069.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Publius Aelius Maximianus, a decurion, in honor of the consul Publius Furius Saturninus, likely dating to the time of his consulship.

EDCS-27200071.text_edition: Dianae Augustae sacrum Titus Annius Annianus domo Tipasa praefectus cohortis IIII Hispanorum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-27200071.translation: Sacred to Diana Augusta. Titus Annius Annianus, from Tipasa, prefect of the Fourth Cohort of Spaniards, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27200071.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Diana Augusta by Titus Annius Annianus, a prefect of the Fourth Cohort of Spaniards, made to fulfill a vow, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-27200072.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Iulius Iulianus praefectus cohortis IIII Hispanorum equitatae domo Roma votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-27200072.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Gaius Julius Julianus, prefect of the Fourth Cohort of mounted Spaniards, from Rome, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27200072.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Gaius Julius Julianus, a prefect of a mounted cohort of Spaniards, indicating a religious dedication by a military officer.

EDCS-27200073.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Vettius Severus praefectus cohortis IIII? Hisp(anorum) equitatae Antoninianae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-27200073.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Titus Vettius Severus, prefect of the Fourth? Mounted Cohort of Spaniards, Antoniniana, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27200073.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Titus Vettius Severus, a prefect of a mounted cohort of Spaniards bearing the honorific 'Antoniniana,' likely dating to the Severan dynasty.

EDCS-27200074.text_edition: Deo Marti Titus Aelius Crescentianus praefectus cohortis IIII Hispanorum equitatae [] ex voto posuit
EDCS-27200074.translation: To the god Mars. Titus Aelius Crescentianus, prefect of the Fourth Mounted Cohort of Spaniards, placed this from a vow.
EDCS-27200074.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the god Mars, placed by Titus Aelius Crescentianus, a prefect of the Fourth Mounted Cohort of Spaniards, as a fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-27200076.text_edition: Pro salute victoria Imperatoris domini nostri Marci Aureli Severi Alexandri Pii Felicis III   posuit libens
EDCS-27200076.translation: For the health and victory of our lord Emperor Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander, Pious, Felix, [in his] third [consulship/tribunician power], willingly placed.
EDCS-27200076.summary: This inscription is a dedication for the well-being and victory of Emperor Severus Alexander, indicating it was erected during his reign (222-235 CE), specifically in his third consulship or year of tribunician power.

EDCS-27200077.text_edition: Aesculapio et Hygiae Tiberius Claudius Donatus merito
EDCS-27200077.translation: To Aesculapius and Hygia. Tiberius Claudius Donatus deservedly [placed this].
EDCS-27200077.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the healing deities Aesculapius and Hygia by Tiberius Claudius Donatus, likely for health reasons or in gratitude for recovery.

EDCS-27200080.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto
EDCS-27200080.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus.
EDCS-27200080.summary: This inscription is a dedication or honorific title for Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, likely Caracalla, dating to his reign (198-217 CE).

EDCS-27200081.text_edition: Deo Mithrae Honoratus praefectus cohortis III Gallorum  dono dedit
EDCS-27200081.translation: To the god Mithras. Honoratus, prefect of the Third Cohort of Gauls, gave this as a gift.
EDCS-27200081.summary: This inscription is a gift to the god Mithras, dedicated by Honoratus, a prefect of the Third Cohort of Gauls, indicating the presence of Mithraic worship and military personnel at the site.

EDCS-27200082.text_edition: Caius Suria ri filius  aetatis  EINIM voto posuit bene merenti
EDCS-27200082.translation: Gaius Suria, son of Rius, of the age of [---], placed this from a vow for the well-deserving.
EDCS-27200082.summary: This inscription is a votive offering placed by Gaius Suria, son of Rius, for someone well-deserving, likely a funerary dedication or a grateful commemoration.

EDCS-27200085.text_edition: Dis Manibus Licinia mater vixit  AM MVS
EDCS-27200085.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Licinia, mother, lived [---].
EDCS-27200085.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed for Licinia, a mother, indicating a commemoration of her life and death.

EDCS-27200086.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely part of a tombstone or dedication, mentioning an individual named Cornelius Stephanus or a similar name.

EDCS-27200087.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Suadullus TITVR S vixit annos LXXXXI fecit filius patri bene merenti
EDCS-27200087.translation: To the Divine Manes. Suadullus, TITVR S, lived 91 years. His son made this for his well-deserving father.
EDCS-27200087.summary: This is a funerary inscription, set up by a son for his father, Suadullus, who lived to be 91 years old.

EDCS-27200089.text_edition: Dis Manibus Prima Macri filia annorum L Secund
EDCS-27200089.translation: To the Divine Manes. Prima, daughter of Macer, 50 years old. Secund[...]
EDCS-27200089.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Prima, daughter of Macer, who died at the age of 50, with a mention of Secundus, likely another family member or the dedicator.

EDCS-27200090.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Ulpio Sabino decurioni coloniae Aureliae Napocensium vixit annos L Ulpia Sabina marito Ulpii Maximus Manus patri
EDCS-27200090.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Ulpius Sabinus, decurion of the colonia Aurelia Napocensium, who lived 50 years. Ulpia Sabina for her husband. Ulpius Maximus Manus for his father.
EDCS-27200090.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ulpius Sabinus, a decurion of the colony of Napoca, set up jointly by his wife, Ulpia Sabina, and his son, Ulpius Maximus Manus.

EDCS-27200091.text_edition: Deo Saromando Demetrius Antoni votum libens posuit
EDCS-27200091.translation: To the god Saromandus, Demetrius Antonius willingly and deservedly placed this vow.
EDCS-27200091.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Saromandus by Demetrius Antonius.

EDCS-27200092.summary: This is a highly fragmentary inscription with only a few letters surviving, making its original purpose and content impossible to determine.

EDCS-27200093.text_edition: Dis Manibus Cotu Successi filia cives Norica vixit annos LV Claudius Latinus posuit coniugi bene merenti hic sita est
EDCS-27200093.translation: To the Divine Manes. Cotu, daughter of Successus, a Norican citizen, lived 55 years. Claudius Latinus placed this for his well-deserving wife. She lies here.
EDCS-27200093.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Claudius Latinus for his well-deserving wife, Cotu, a Norican citizen, who died at 55 years of age.

EDCS-27200095.text_edition: Iovi Soli Invicto Deo Genitori rupe nato  Lucius Aelius Hylas XX libertus pro salute et Horientis sic fili sui et Apuleia eius signum numinis cum absidata ex voto posuit
EDCS-27200095.translation: To Jupiter Sol Invictus, the Generating God, born from the rock. Lucius Aelius Hylas, a freedman (twenty years after his manumission), placed this sign of the deity with an apse from a vow for his own well-being and that of his son Horientis, and his wife Apuleia.
EDCS-27200095.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Sol Invictus, the unconquered sun god, by Lucius Aelius Hylas, a freedman, on behalf of himself, his son Horientis, and his wife Apuleia.

EDCS-27200096.text_edition: Iuliae Mamaeae Augustae matri sanctissimi Imperatoris Caesaris Severi Alexandri Augusti et castrorum senatusque ala Frontoniana Alexandriana ex quaestura sua dedicante Iasdio Domitiano legato Augusti pro praetore
EDCS-27200096.translation: To Julia Mamaea Augusta, mother of the most sacred Emperor Caesar Severus Alexander Augustus and of the camps and of the senate, the Ala Frontoniana Alexandriana (a cavalry unit) dedicated this from its own quaestorship, with Iasdius Domitianus, legate of the Augustus with propraetorian power, presiding over the dedication.
EDCS-27200096.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription from the Ala Frontoniana Alexandriana, a cavalry unit, honoring Julia Mamaea, mother of Emperor Severus Alexander, and dedicated under the supervision of the provincial legate Iasdius Domitianus, likely dating to the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200098.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Aelius Quadratus eques alIae#alae#AIAE FrontoniTanae#Frontonianae#FRONTONTANE T VAIANI
EDCS-27200098.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Aelius Quadratus, cavalryman of the Ala Frontoniana, T. Vaiani.
EDCS-27200098.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Aelius Quadratus, a cavalryman in the Ala Frontoniana.

EDCS-27200099.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aponius  eques alae Frontonianae vixit annos L Aponia  et Laelius patri  posuerunt bene merenti
EDCS-27200099.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aponius, a horseman of the Ala Frontoniana, lived for 50 years. Aponia and Laelius placed this for their well-deserving father.
EDCS-27200099.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Aponius, a cavalryman, set up by his children Aponia and Laelius, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200101.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Iustinus eques numeri Palmyrenorum Porolissensium vixit nnos XL Aurelius Maximus frater et heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-27200101.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelius Iustinus, a horseman of the Numerus Palmyrenorum Porolissensium, lived for 40 years. Aurelius Maximus, his brother and heir, took care of having this made.
EDCS-27200101.summary: This funerary monument commemorates Aurelius Iustinus, a cavalryman from a Palmyrene unit stationed at Porolissum, erected by his brother and heir, probably from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200102.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Themaes librarius alae Frontonianae vixit annis L et Aelia Iulia coniuxG#coniux#COIUG et Aelia Pupula vixit annis II Aurelius Thementianus filius parentibus bene merentibus posuit
EDCS-27200102.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelius Themaes, a clerk of the Ala Frontoniana, lived for 50 years, and Aelia Iulia, his wife, and Aelia Pupula, who lived for 2 years. Aurelius Thementianus, their son, placed this for his well-deserving parents.
EDCS-27200102.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument dedicated by Aurelius Thementianus to his parents, Aurelius Themaes, a clerk in the Ala Frontoniana, and Aelia Iulia, and also commemorates his sister Aelia Pupula, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200103.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Bonosa vixit annos XLII Valerius Valerianus veteranus ex decurione alae Frontonianae coniugi pientissimae bene merenti faciendum curavit
EDCS-27200103.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Bonosa lived for 42 years. Valerius Valerianus, a veteran formerly a decurion of the Ala Frontoniana, took care of having this made for his most devoted and well-deserving wife.
EDCS-27200103.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Bonosa, erected by her husband Valerius Valerianus, a veteran cavalry officer, for his wife, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200104.text_edition: Dis Manibus Cocceius Iulius eques alae Frontonianae stipendiorum VII vixit annos XXVII Primus heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-27200104.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Cocceius Iulius, a horseman of the Ala Frontoniana, served for 7 years and lived for 27 years. Primus, his heir, took care of having this made.
EDCS-27200104.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cocceius Iulius, a young cavalryman, dedicated by his heir Primus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200105.text_edition: Dis Manibus Cittius Ioivai eques alae Frontonianae stipendiorum XVI vixit annos XXXVI Iustus eques alae eiusdem heres et frater faciendum curavit
EDCS-27200105.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Cittius Ioivai, a horseman of the Ala Frontoniana, served for 16 years and lived for 36 years. Iustus, a horseman of the same ala, his heir and brother, took care of having this made.
EDCS-27200105.summary: This funerary monument commemorates Cittius Ioivai, a cavalryman, set up by his brother and heir Iustus, also a cavalryman of the same unit, probably from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200106.text_edition: Iulia Mansueta vixit annos XIII Valerius Valerianus  veteranus ex decurione alae Frontonianae
EDCS-27200106.translation: Iulia Mansueta lived for 13 years. Valerius Valerianus, a veteran formerly a decurion of the Ala Frontoniana.
EDCS-27200106.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Mansueta, a young girl, likely erected by Valerius Valerianus, a veteran cavalry officer, who may have been her father or guardian, dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200107.text_edition: eques alae Frontonianae hic situs est vixit annis XXXX Mucapuis coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-27200107.translation: A horseman of the Ala Frontoniana is buried here; he lived for 40 years. Mucapuis placed this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-27200107.summary: This funerary inscription marks the burial of an unnamed cavalryman of the Ala Frontoniana, erected by his wife Mucapuis, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200109.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caius Valerius Vitalis veteranus ex decurione alae Frontonianae vixit nnis L et Valerius Vitalianus filius huius vixit annis II et mensibus VII Valerius Laetillus decurio alae supra scriptae Valerius Saturninus duplicarius decuriae eiusdem parentibus fratrique pientissimis bene merentibus posuerunt
EDCS-27200109.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Caius Valerius Vitalis, a veteran formerly a decurion of the Ala Frontoniana, lived for 50 years, and Valerius Vitalianus, his son, lived for 2 years and 7 months. Valerius Laetillus, a decurion of the aforementioned ala, and Valerius Saturninus, a duplicarius of the same decuria, placed this for their most devoted and well-deserving parents and brother.
EDCS-27200109.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Valerius Laetillus and Valerius Saturninus for their parents, Caius Valerius Vitalis, a veteran cavalry officer, and his unnamed wife (implied by 'parents'), and for their brother Valerius Vitalianus, probably from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200110.text_edition: Ius veteranus  vixit annis LX Aurelius anus filius M
EDCS-27200110.translation: Ius, a veteran, lived for 60 years. Aurelius anus, his son...
EDCS-27200110.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Ius, a veteran, who lived to be 60, started by his son Aurelius, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200113.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelius Mercurius vixit nnis XXXX Aelia Rufilla vixit annis VIIII Aelius Publius vixit nnis VI Nennia Ianuaria
EDCS-27200113.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aelius Mercurius lived for 40 years, Aelia Rufilla lived for 9 years, Aelius Publius lived for 6 years. Nennia Ianuaria (set this up).
EDCS-27200113.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Nennia Ianuaria for Aelius Mercurius, Aelia Rufilla, and Aelius Publius, who were likely family members given the shared 'Aelius' nomen, and likely dates from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200115.text_edition: Dis Manibus Calpurnia Maurina vixit annos XL Aelius Respectus coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-27200115.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Calpurnia Maurina lived for 40 years. Aelius Respectus set this up for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-27200115.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Aelius Respectus for his wife, Calpurnia Maurina, who lived for 40 years, likely dating from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200116.text_edition: Iovinus vixit nnos II menses II Aurelius Castor posuit
EDCS-27200116.translation: Iovinus lived for 2 years and 2 months. Aurelius Castor set this up.
EDCS-27200116.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Aurelius Castor for Iovinus, who lived for a very short period, likely dating from the 2nd or 3rd century AD based on the name Aurelius.

EDCS-27200119.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, mentioning the names Amarantus and Hediste, which are common for freedpersons or slaves and suggests a date from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200131.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Aurelius Moenenus consularis legionis VIII Augustae ISR ANS pro salute sua suorumque omnium votum libens merito
EDCS-27200131.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Marcus Aurelius Moenenus, consular legate of the Eighth Augustan Legion, (made this offering) on behalf of his own safety and that of all his kin, willingly and deservedly fulfilling his vow.
EDCS-27200131.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, made by Marcus Aurelius Moenenus, a high-ranking officer of the Legio VIII Augusta, for his and his family's well-being, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200132.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et dis deabus omnibus Valerius Vibius Valerianus beneficiarius consularis legionis XIII geminae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-27200132.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and all gods and goddesses. Valerius Vibius Valerianus, a consular beneficiarius of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27200132.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and all gods and goddesses by Valerius Vibius Valerianus, a non-commissioned officer of Legio XIII Gemina, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200133.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo ceterisque dis Publius Aelius Sextilianus et Caius  Speratus one et Caius OVII Lposuerunt
EDCS-27200133.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the other gods. Publius Aelius Sextilianus and Gaius Speratus and Gaius OVII L set this up.
EDCS-27200133.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and other gods, set up by Publius Aelius Sextilianus, Gaius Speratus, and a third partially preserved individual, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200134.text_edition: Deae Nemesi sacrarium vetustate dilapsum a solo restituit Cassius Erotianus beneficiarius consularis agens in munere stationis
EDCS-27200134.translation: To the goddess Nemesis. Cassius Erotianus, a consular beneficiarius acting in his station's duty, restored from its foundation a shrine that had fallen into ruin due to old age.
EDCS-27200134.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription recording the restoration of an old shrine to the goddess Nemesis by Cassius Erotianus, a beneficiarius consularis, suggesting a date in the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200135.text_edition: Deae Nemesi Caius Iulius Maximus beneficiarius consularis legionis V Macedonicae fecit
EDCS-27200135.translation: To the goddess Nemesis. Gaius Iulius Maximus, a consular beneficiarius of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, made this.
EDCS-27200135.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the goddess Nemesis, made by Gaius Iulius Maximus, a beneficiarius consularis of Legio V Macedonica, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200136.text_edition: Deae Nemesi Reginae Marcus Valerius Valentinus beneficiarius consularis miles legionis XIII geminae Gordianae aedilis coloniae Napocae agens sub sigC(nis)#sig(nis)#SIC Samum cum regione Ans votum libens merito posuit  Imperatore domino nostro Marco Antonio Gordiano Augusto et Aviola consulibus X
EDCS-27200136.translation: To the goddess Nemesis Regina. Marcus Valerius Valentinus, a consular beneficiarius, a soldier of the Thirteenth Gemina Gordiana Legion, aedile of the colony of Napoca, acting under the standard, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for Samum with the region of Ans, in the consulship of our lord Emperor Marcus Antonius Gordianus Augustus and Aviola, for the tenth time.
EDCS-27200136.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Nemesis Regina by Marcus Valerius Valentinus, a soldier and official of the Legio XIII Gemina Gordiana, dated precisely to AD 241, during the tenth consulship of Emperor Gordian III and Aviola.

EDCS-27200137.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno pro salute domini nostri Augusti  pontifex municipii Septimii Porolissensium et Antiochianus PSAE
EDCS-27200137.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, for the health of our lord Augustus, the pontifex of the municipium Septimium Porolissensium and Antiochianus PSAE (dedicated this).
EDCS-27200137.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Dolichenus for the health of the emperor, made by a pontifex from Porolissum and Antiochianus, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-27200139.text_edition: Genio scholaes sic ordinatorum suc sic curagentibus Lucio Cilio Aeliano et Tiberio Aurelio Ro principibus posuerunt
EDCS-27200139.translation: To the Genius of the guild, thus arranged, thus cared for by Lucius Cilius Aelianus and Tiberius Aurelius Ro, as principes, they dedicated this.
EDCS-27200139.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Genius of a guild, set up by Lucius Cilius Aelianus and Tiberius Aurelius Ro as leading members, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-27200140.text_edition: o vixit nnos LX et Aureli- Aelian bene m(erent---?) ---
EDCS-27200140.translation: ...who lived 60 years, and Aurelius Aelian, well-deserving...
EDCS-27200140.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription, mentioning an individual who lived 60 years and Aurelius Aelian, likely dedicating it to a well-deserving person.

EDCS-27200141.text_edition: Herculi sancto Marcus Veracilius Verus legatus Augusti pro praetore curante nnio Maximo praefecto equitum
EDCS-27200141.translation: To holy Hercules, Marcus Veracilius Verus, legate of Augustus pro praetore, with Gnaeus Maximum, prefect of cavalry, taking care of it.
EDCS-27200141.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Hercules by Marcus Veracilius Verus, a pro-praetorian legate of the emperor, supervised by Gnaeus Maximus, a prefect of cavalry, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-27200142.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead (Di Manes) for a person named Daphnus.

EDCS-27200145.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XX imperator II consul IIII pater patriae amphitheatrum vetustate dilapsum denuo fecit curante Tiberio Claudio Quintiliano procuratore suo
EDCS-27200145.translation: Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the twentieth time, imperator for the second time, consul for the fourth time, father of the country, rebuilt the amphitheater, which had collapsed due to age, under the care of his procurator Tiberius Claudius Quintilianus.
EDCS-27200145.summary: This inscription records the rebuilding of an amphitheater by Emperor Antoninus Pius, who held numerous imperial titles, under the supervision of his procurator Tiberius Claudius Quintilianus, dating precisely to the year he held tribunician power for the twentieth time (156-157 CE).

EDCS-27200146.text_edition: annos X  filio vixit annos V Salmas Rami ex numero Palmyrenorum coniugi et filio bene merentibus
EDCS-27200146.translation: ...years 10, to her son who lived 5 years. Salmas Rami, from the number of Palmyrenes, dedicated this to her well-deserving spouse and son.
EDCS-27200146.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Salmas Rami, a Palmyrene, to her well-deserving spouse (who lived 10 years) and son (who lived 5 years), likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-27200147.text_edition: Tiberius Annius Marcellus et Aurelius Manneus et Mestrinius Firminus et Aurelii Victor et Pacutianus et Maximus dederunt Aurelius Manneus princeps posuit
EDCS-27200147.translation: Tiberius Annius Marcellus and Aurelius Manneus and Mestrinius Firminus and Aurelii Victor and Pacutianus and Maximus gave this. Aurelius Manneus, the princeps, set it up.
EDCS-27200147.summary: This inscription records a dedication by a group of individuals including Tiberius Annius Marcellus, Aurelius Manneus, Mestrinius Firminus, Aurelii Victor, Pacutianus, and Maximus, with Aurelius Manneus, acting as the leading member (princeps), being responsible for its erection.

EDCS-27200148.text_edition: Dis Manibus Cai Campani Vitalis |(centurionis) cohortis I Batavorum |(milliariae) stipendiorum VIIII vixit annos XXVII Florius Virilis veteranus ex decurione nepoti pientissimo ponendum curavit
EDCS-27200148.translation: To the Divine Manes. Caius Campanius Vitalis, centurion of the 1st Batavian Cohort (milliary), served for 9 years, lived 27 years. Florius Virilis, veteran from a decurion, arranged for this to be set up for his most dutiful grandson.
EDCS-27200148.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Caius Campanius Vitalis, a centurion of the 1st Batavian Cohort who died at age 27, and was erected by his grandfather, Florius Virilis, a veteran who had been a decurion, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-27200149.text_edition: SIL vixit annos XLVI ArruntIius#Arruntius#ARRUNIIUS Lucilianus filius vixit annos XVIII ArruntIius#Arruntius#ARRUNIIUS Latinus filius vixit annos V Ulpia Amadusa coniunx bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-27200149.translation: Sil. He lived 46 years. Arruntius Lucilianus, his son, lived 18 years. Arruntius Latinus, his son, lived 5 years. Ulpia Amadusa, his wife, set this up for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-27200149.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Ulpia Amadusa to her well-deserving husband, who lived 46 years, and also commemorates their sons, Arruntius Lucilianus (who lived 18 years) and Arruntius Latinus (who lived 5 years), likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-27200150.text_edition: E BAT o vixit annos IIII et OTVM EI CASTIA OH VICINIDEM FELO ES QVANTINE
EDCS-27200150.translation: E. Bat. lived for 4 years and... Oh, neighbor, you are truly strong! So much for you!
EDCS-27200150.summary: This is a funerary inscription for E. Bat., a child who lived for four years, with an unusual and somewhat poetic address to a neighbor.

EDCS-27200154.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aeliae Maximimiae F vixit annis LXVI Aelius Proculus coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-27200154.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Aelia Maximimima, a woman, who lived 66 years. Aelius Proculus to his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-27200154.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Aelius Proculus to his wife, Aelia Maximimima, who lived 66 years.

EDCS-27200155.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcus Aurelius Dassius vixit annis LXXX Aurelius Vi
EDCS-27200155.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Aurelius Dassius lived for 80 years. Aurelius Vi[...]
EDCS-27200155.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Aurelius Dassius, who lived 80 years, seemingly dedicated in part by an Aurelius.

EDCS-27200156.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Sextus Valerius Saturninus decurio alae Silianae et collegae votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-27200156.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Sextus Valerius Saturninus, decurion of the Ala Siliana, and his colleagues willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-27200156.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, dedicated by Sextus Valerius Saturninus, a decurion of the Ala Siliana, and his colleagues.

EDCS-27200157.text_edition: Dis Manibus Titi Aeli Vet Titi Aeli Pauli veterani ex decurione fili vixit nnos XLII Valerius Festivus pupillus bene merenti posuit hic situs est
EDCS-27200157.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Titus Aelius Vet[eranus], son of Titus Aelius Paulus, a veteran who was formerly a decurion, who lived 42 years. Valerius Festivus, his ward, placed this for his well-deserving [guardian]. He is buried here.
EDCS-27200157.summary: This is a funerary monument for Titus Aelius, a veteran and former decurion, placed by his ward, Valerius Festivus.

EDCS-27200158.text_edition: Aurelius Fabius signifer alae Silianae vixit nnos XXVIIII menses XI Aurelius Reburrus veteranus et Fabia filio pientissimo posuerunt SET et sibi
EDCS-27200158.translation: Aurelius Fabius, standard-bearer of the Ala Siliana, lived for 29 years and 11 months. Aurelius Reburrus, veteran, and Fabia, placed this for their most devoted son, and for themselves.
EDCS-27200158.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Aurelius Reburrus, a veteran, and Fabia to their most devoted son, Aurelius Fabius, a standard-bearer of the Ala Siliana, and also for themselves.

EDCS-27200159.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Carinus cornicularius alI(a)eI#al(a)e#AII Silianae vixit annis  Aurelia Serconiux pientissimo coniugi posuit bene merenti
EDCS-27200159.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelius Carinus, cornicularius of the Ala Siliana, lived for [...] years. Aurelia Ser[gia?], his wife, placed this for her most devoted and well-deserving husband.
EDCS-27200159.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Aurelia Ser[gia?] to her most devoted and well-deserving husband, Aurelius Carinus, a cornicularius of the Ala Siliana.

EDCS-27200160.text_edition: Dis Manibus inius Verecundus veteranus ex duplicario vixit annos LIII Aurelia  Sabina coniugi
EDCS-27200160.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. [...]inius Verecundus, a veteran who was formerly a duplicarius, lived for 53 years. Aurelia Sabina to her husband.
EDCS-27200160.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Verecundus, a veteran and former duplicarius, placed by his wife, Aurelia Sabina.

EDCS-27200161.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ulpius Saturninus eques al(ae) Thraecum sic vixit annos XXXV Ulpius Valentinus fratri bene merenti posuit
EDCS-27200161.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Ulpius Saturninus, an eques of the Thracian Ala, lived for 35 years. Ulpius Valentinus placed this for his well-deserving brother.
EDCS-27200161.summary: This is a funerary monument for Ulpius Saturninus, a cavalryman of the Thracian Ala, dedicated by his brother, Ulpius Valentinus.

EDCS-27200162.text_edition: Aurelius Mucapor vixit annos LIXXX#LXXX#IXXX Aurelius Gemellinus vixit annos  Aurelia Re vixit annos
EDCS-27200162.translation: Aurelius Mucapor lived for 80 years. Aurelius Gemellinus lived for [...] years. Aurelia Re[...] lived for [...] years.
EDCS-27200162.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the ages of Aurelius Mucapor and two other individuals, Aurelius Gemellinus and Aurelia Re[...].

EDCS-27200163.text_edition: Fortunae Augustae Caius Aurelius Atilianus procurator Augusti
EDCS-27200163.translation: To Fortuna Augusta, Gaius Aurelius Atilianus, imperial procurator.
EDCS-27200163.summary: This inscription records a dedication made to the goddess Fortuna Augusta by an imperial procurator, likely on a votive altar or statue base, during the imperial period.

EDCS-27200164.text_edition: Fortunae Augustae Ulpius Masculinus veteranus ex signifero legionis V Macedonicae et de=EDcurio#decurio#EDC coloniae Napocae pro salute sua et suorum posuit dedicata Kalendis Septembribus Cilone II et Libone consulibus
EDCS-27200164.translation: To Fortuna Augusta, Ulpius Masculinus, veteran from the standard-bearer of the Fifth Macedonian Legion and decurion of the colony of Napoca, placed this for his own well-being and that of his family. Dedicated on the Kalends of September, with Cilo the second time and Libo as consuls.
EDCS-27200164.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Fortuna Augusta by a veteran and decurion from Napoca, made for his family's welfare, precisely dated to September 1st, 160 CE.

EDCS-27200165.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo ex visu Publius Aelius Maximus
EDCS-27200165.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, by vision, Publius Aelius Maximus.
EDCS-27200165.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus made by Publius Aelius Maximus, likely in fulfillment of a vow made after a divine vision.

EDCS-27200166.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Publicius Antonius Probus procurator Auggustorum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-27200166.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Gaius Publicius Antonius Probus, procurator of the Augusti, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27200166.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by an imperial procurator, Gaius Publicius Antonius Probus, indicating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-27200167.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Valerius Catullinus procurator Augusti
EDCS-27200167.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Valerius Catullinus, imperial procurator.
EDCS-27200167.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Valerius Catullinus, an imperial procurator, likely on a votive altar or monument.

EDCS-27200168.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae Minervae pro salute ddominorum nnostrorum Marci Antoni Gordiani Pii Felicis Augusti et Sabiniae Tranquillinae Augustae Lucius Laelius Terentianus decurio coloniae aedilis praefectus
EDCS-27200168.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Juno Regina, Minerva, for the well-being of our lords Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus and Sabinia Tranquillina Augusta, Lucius Laelius Terentianus, decurion of the colony, aedile, prefect.
EDCS-27200168.summary: This inscription is a collegial dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Juno Regina, and Minerva, made for the welfare of Emperor Gordian III and his empress Tranquillina by Lucius Laelius Terentianus, a local magistrate holding multiple offices in a Roman colony.

EDCS-27200169.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Eruseno sic Aurelius Alexander votum solvit libens
EDCS-27200169.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Erusenus, Aurelius Alexander willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27200169.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Erusenus by Aurelius Alexander, indicating the fulfillment of a vow to this specific deity.

EDCS-27200170.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Taviano pro salute Imperatoris Antonini et Marci Aureli Caesaris Galatae consistentes municipio posuIerunt#posuerunt#POSIERUNT
EDCS-27200170.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Tavius, for the well-being of the Emperor Antoninus and Marcus Aurelius Caesar, the Galatians residing in the municipality placed this.
EDCS-27200170.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Tavius by a group of Galatians residing in a municipality, made for the well-being of Emperor Antoninus Pius and his adopted son Marcus Aurelius, likely dating to the mid-2nd century CE.

EDCS-27200171.translation: To Mercury, Maessianus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27200171.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the god Mercury by Maessianus, indicating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-27200172.text_edition: Numini Augusti Marcus Ulpius Caecilius Bassianus  Callisth maggistri Augustalis sic coloniae Napocae donum dederunt
EDCS-27200172.translation: To the Numen of the Augustus, Marcus Ulpius Caecilius Bassianus and Callisth, Augustales magistrates of the colony of Napoca, gave this gift.
EDCS-27200172.summary: This inscription records a dedication made to the divine spirit of the emperor by two Augustales, Marcus Ulpius Caecilius Bassianus and Callisth, who were magistrates of the colony of Napoca.

EDCS-27200173.text_edition: Deo Silvano pro salute Aeli Iuliani Securus posuit
EDCS-27200173.translation: To the god Silvanus, for the health of Aelius Iulianus, Securus placed this.
EDCS-27200173.summary: This votive inscription records Securus dedicating an altar or monument to the god Silvanus for the well-being of Aelius Iulianus.

EDCS-27200174.text_edition: Veneri Victrici Aurelius Umbrianus veteranus ex decurione votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-27200174.translation: To Venus Victrix, Aurelius Umbrianus, a veteran, formerly a decurion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27200174.summary: This votive inscription records Aurelius Umbrianus, a veteran and former decurion, dedicating a monument to Venus Victrix in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-27200175.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Lucio Aelio Aurelio Commodo Pio Felici Augusto Sarmatico Germanico maximo Brittannico ob honorem decurionatus Aeli Constantis procuratoris eius et Iuli Pacatiani quondam procuratoris Titus Fl(avius) IanuariusR coloniae et Titus Flavius Germanus decurio coloniae quod decuriones alares promiserunt pecunia sua posuerunt locus datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-27200175.translation: To Imperator Caesar Lucius Aelius Aurelius Commodus Pius Felix Augustus Sarmaticus Germanicus Maximus Britannicus, in honor of the decurion status of Aelius Constans, his procurator, and Iulius Pacatianus, formerly a procurator, Titus Flavius Ianuarius, a Roman citizen of the colony, and Titus Flavius Germanus, a decurion of the colony, erected this with their own money, as the decurions of the cavalry promised, on land granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-27200175.summary: This inscription records Titus Flavius Ianuarius and Titus Flavius Germanus dedicating a monument to Emperor Commodus, likely in the late 2nd century AD, to honor the decurion status of two procurators, funded by their own money on land decreed by the decurions.

EDCS-27200176.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelia Aurelia vixit nnis XVII Aurelius Marinianus filius eius vixit anno I mensibus IIII Aurelius Babus veteranus pater posuit sit tibi terra levis
EDCS-27200176.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelia Aurelia lived 17 years. Aurelius Marinianus, her son, lived 1 year and 4 months. Aurelius Babus, a veteran and father, placed this. May the earth be light for you.
EDCS-27200176.summary: This funerary inscription records Aurelius Babus dedicating a tombstone to his deceased wife, Aurelia Aurelia, and their young son, Aurelius Marinianus, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200177.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publio Aelio Traiano vixit annos XXVIII filio et Aelio Libero alumno vixit annos III Publius Aelius Dacianus decurio et aedilis coloniae faciendum curavit
EDCS-27200177.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Publius Aelius Traianus, who lived 28 years, his son, and for Aelius Liber, his foster son, who lived 3 years, Publius Aelius Dacianus, decurion and aedile of the colony, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-27200177.summary: This funerary inscription records Publius Aelius Dacianus, a decurion and aedile, commissioning a monument for his deceased son, Publius Aelius Traianus, and his foster son, Aelius Liber.

EDCS-27200178.text_edition: iae O DEC    et Aurelius anus A
EDCS-27200178.translation: [---] to O DEC [---] and Aurelius [---] anus A [---]
EDCS-27200178.summary: This fragmentary inscription, with significant lacunae, appears to mention a name, Aurelius, and possibly a reference to a decurion, suggesting a funerary or dedicatory context.

EDCS-27200179.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcus Aurelius Papia Augustalis coloniae Napocae vixit annis LX Aurelia Bona coniux marito perficiendum curavit
EDCS-27200179.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Aurelius Papia, an Augustalis of the colony of Napoca, lived 60 years. Aurelia Bona, his wife, arranged for this to be completed for her husband.
EDCS-27200179.summary: This funerary inscription records Aurelia Bona dedicating a tombstone to her husband, Marcus Aurelius Papia, an Augustalis of Napoca, who died at the age of 60, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200180.text_edition: Severo et Quintiano consulibus nomina Asianorum Germanus sprirarchaes  Tattaro Hermes Dizo Gaius Hyius Zoilus Valerius Zoilianus Crescens Eptala Ulpius Mucianus Carus magister Surius Tzinto Longinianus Ulpianus Iulius Hermescus Nepos Agrippinus Valerianus Curinius Rev Co   Epipodia mater Valentina Augusta Asclepiodote Tiberina Maximina Tzinta Iustina Cornificia Longina Cornelia Vera Hilara Graeca Rufina Victorina Paula  Zoilianus scripsit
EDCS-27200180.translation: During the consulship of Severus and Quintianus, the names of the Asians: Germanus, a spirarch, Tattarus, Hermes, Dizo, Gaius, Hyius, Zoilus, Valerius, Zoilianus, Crescens, Eptala, Ulpius Mucianus, Carus, a master, Surius, Tzinto, Longinianus, Ulpianus, Iulius, Hermescus, Nepos, Agrippinus, Valerianus, Curinius, Rev Co [---] Epipodia, mother, Valentina, Augusta, Asclepiodote, Tiberina, Maximina, Tzinta, Iustina, Cornificia, Longina, Cornelia, Vera, Hilara, Graeca, Rufina, Victorina, Paula. Zoilianus wrote this.
EDCS-27200180.summary: This inscription records a list of names, likely members of an association or guild, including men and women from Asia, dated to the consulship of Severus and Quintianus (likely AD 235), and explicitly states that Zoilianus was the scribe.

EDCS-27200183.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelia Sura vixit annos XXX et Aelius Saturninus vixit annos III et Aelius Valentinus vixit annos III Aelius Siro filiis et coniugi pientissimis
EDCS-27200183.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aelia Sura lived 30 years, and Aelius Saturninus lived 3 years, and Aelius Valentinus lived 3 years. Aelius Siro [placed this] for his most devoted children and wife.
EDCS-27200183.summary: This funerary inscription records Aelius Siro dedicating a tombstone to his deceased wife, Aelia Sura, and their two young sons, Aelius Saturninus and Aelius Valentinus, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27300009.summary: This inscription briefly records the Legio XIII Gemina and a soldier named Aelius Deiotarus, likely indicating a military context such as a gravestone or a property mark.

EDCS-27300068.text_edition: Ammius Crescens Silvano votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-27300068.translation: Ammius Crescens willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Silvanus.
EDCS-27300068.summary: This inscription records a dedicatory offering made by Ammius Crescens to the god Silvanus, likely as a fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-27300069.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo veterani cives Romani Mic(ienses) per Marcum Aurelium Tertium quaestorem votum libentes merito solverunt
EDCS-27300069.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the veteran Roman citizens of Micia, through Marcus Aurelius Tertius, their quaestor, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-27300069.summary: This inscription records a dedicatory vow fulfilled by the veteran Roman citizens of Micia to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, facilitated by their quaestor Marcus Aurelius Tertius.

EDCS-27300083.text_edition: Fortunae Reduci Lari Viali Romae Aeternae Quintus Axius Aelianus vir egregius procurator Auggustorum Ioni
EDCS-27300083.translation: To Fortuna Redux, to the Lares Viales, to Roma Aeterna, Quintus Axius Aelianus, an excellent man, procurator of the Augusti, to Ion.
EDCS-27300083.summary: This inscription is a dedication made by Quintus Axius Aelianus, an imperial procurator, to Fortuna Redux, the Lares Viales, and Roma Aeterna, possibly for someone named Ion.

EDCS-27300084.text_edition: Dis Manibus Cai Iuli Sabini militis legionis XIII geminae librarius sic a rationibus vixit annos XXX Caius Iulius Valens IIvir iure dicundo et Cominia Florentina parentes infelicissimi
EDCS-27300084.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Gaius Iulius Sabinus, soldier of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, a scribe, thus from the accounts, lived 30 years. Gaius Iulius Valens, duumvir with judicial power, and Cominia Florentina, his most unhappy parents, [erected this].
EDCS-27300084.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Sabinus, a 30-year-old soldier and scribe of Legio XIII Gemina, erected by his grieving parents Gaius Iulius Valens, a magistrate, and Cominia Florentina.

EDCS-27300087.summary: This very brief inscription simply records the name Olympia Faustina, likely on a tombstone or personal item, without further context.

EDCS-27300090.summary: This brief inscription records the name of Quintus Aponius Bassus, likely indicating ownership or dedication on an object.

EDCS-27300091.summary: This brief inscription records the name of Quintus Cornelius, with the cognomen possibly being Cornelianus, likely indicating ownership or dedication on an object.

EDCS-27300092.summary: This brief inscription records the name of Lucius Domitius, with the cognomen possibly being Priman, likely indicating ownership or dedication on an object.

EDCS-27300094.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari divi Hadriani filio divi Traiani Parthici nepoti divi Nervae pronepoti Tito Aelio Hadriano Antonino Augusto Pio pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate VIII imperatori II consuli IIII patri patriae res publica municipii Hadriani Drobetensium decreto decurionum
EDCS-27300094.translation: To the Imperator Caesar, son of the deified Hadrian, grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-grandson of the deified Nerva, Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the eighth time, imperator for the second time, consul for the fourth time, father of his country, the republic of the Hadrianic municipality of Drobeta, by decree of the decurions [erected this].
EDCS-27300094.summary: This inscription is a monumental dedication from the municipality of Drobeta, by decree of its decurions, honoring Emperor Antoninus Pius, dating to approximately 145 CE based on his tribunician power and consulships.

EDCS-27300095.text_edition: Matri Deum Magnae Iulia Materna T votum solvit libens merito porticum impendio suo fecit
EDCS-27300095.translation: To the Great Mother of the Gods, Iulia Materna T. willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow, and at her own expense she built the portico.
EDCS-27300095.summary: This inscription records a dedicatory offering made by Iulia Materna T. to the Magna Mater, where she not only fulfilled a vow but also funded the construction of a portico.

EDCS-27300096.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caius Valerius Victorinus veteranus legionis V Macedonicae ex beneficiario tribuni vixit annos LV Caius Valerius Mercurialis generi suo et Aurelia Calliroe coniugi bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-27300096.translation: To the Divine Spirits of the Dead. Gaius Valerius Victorinus, veteran of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, formerly a beneficiarius (aide) to the tribune, lived 55 years. Gaius Valerius Mercurialis, to his son-in-law, and Aurelia Calliroe, to her well-deserving husband, set this up.
EDCS-27300096.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Gaius Valerius Mercurialis and Aurelia Calliroe for Gaius Valerius Victorinus, a veteran of the Legio V Macedonica, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27300097.text_edition: IAC interfecta a latronibus et vindicata Ulcudius Baedari et Sutta Epicadi parentes pientissimae filiae titulum posuerunt Dis Inferis Manibus Ulcudius Baedari vixit annos L
EDCS-27300097.translation: She lies here, killed by bandits and avenged. Ulcudius Baedari and Sutta Epicadi, the parents, set up this monument for their most dutiful daughter. To the Infernal Divine Spirits of the Dead. Ulcudius Baedari lived 50 years.
EDCS-27300097.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument erected by Ulcudius Baedari and Sutta Epicadi for their daughter, who was murdered by bandits, and for Ulcudius Baedari himself, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27300098.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Antonia Calliste vixit nnos LX Diogenes Moschi coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-27300098.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits of the Dead. Antonia Calliste lived 60 years. Diogenes Moschi set this up for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-27300098.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Diogenes Moschi to his wife, Antonia Calliste, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27300099.text_edition: Dis Manibus Vario Maroni vixit annis XLVI
EDCS-27300099.translation: To the Divine Spirits of the Dead. Vario Maro lived 46 years.
EDCS-27300099.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription for Vario Maro, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27300100.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo defensori et tutatori Caius Iulius Sennianus cum Iulia Rufina coniuge votum libens posuit
EDCS-27300100.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, defender and protector. Gaius Julius Sennianus, with his wife Julia Rufina, willingly placed this vow.
EDCS-27300100.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Gaius Julius Sennianus and his wife Julia Rufina, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27300103.text_edition: Dis Manibus Claudia Amba vixit annos XX fecit in Dacia nnos V Claudius Montanus immunis ex numero Surorum sagittariorum pater eius bene merenti fecit
EDCS-27300103.translation: To the Divine Spirits of the Dead. Claudia Amba lived 20 years; she lived 5 years in Dacia. Claudius Montanus, an immunis (exempt from duties) from the unit of Syrian archers, her father, made this for his well-deserving daughter.
EDCS-27300103.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Claudius Montanus, an archer from a Syrian unit, for his daughter Claudia Amba, who lived for 5 years in Dacia, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27300104.translation: To the Divine Spirits of the Dead. Antonius Nicanoris.
EDCS-27300104.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription for Antonius Nicanoris, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27300105.text_edition: Pliciae Var Capitonis Ulpius Pulcher ge=CIner#gener#CINER bene merenti
EDCS-27300105.translation: To Plicia Var Capitonis. Ulpius Pulcher, her son-in-law, [placed this] for the well-deserving.
EDCS-27300105.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Ulpius Pulcher to his mother-in-law, Plicia Var Capitonis, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27300107.text_edition: Aurelius Ciiunus |(centurio) legionis V Macedonicae vixit nnos LIX posuerunt filiaes Aurelia Ascia et Aurelia Marcellina patri pientissimo titulum
EDCS-27300107.translation: Aurelius Ciiunus, centurion of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, lived 59 years. His daughters, Aurelia Ascia and Aurelia Marcellina, set up this monument for their most dutiful father.
EDCS-27300107.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Aurelia Ascia and Aurelia Marcellina for their father, Aurelius Ciiunus, a centurion of the Legio V Macedonica, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27300108.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Calanus vixit annos LI et militavit XXV
EDCS-27300108.translation: To the Divine Spirits of the Dead. Aurelius Calanus lived 51 years and served 25 years.
EDCS-27300108.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Calanus, noting his age and years of military service, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27300109.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Titus F Titi fT(ilius)#f(ilius)#T Sera stipendiorum III vixit annos XXX
EDCS-27300109.translation: To the Divine Shades. Titus, son of Titus, of the tribe Sergia, served three stipends; he lived for thirty years.
EDCS-27300109.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus, a soldier who served three stipends and died at the age of 30, likely erected by his family or comrades.

EDCS-27300110.text_edition: Dis Manibus Sicinius Lucanus veteranus legionis XIII geminae mater pientissima posuit
EDCS-27300110.translation: To the Divine Shades. Sicinius Lucanus, veteran of the Thirteenth Twin Legion. His most devoted mother placed this.
EDCS-27300110.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a grieving mother to her son, Sicinius Lucanus, a veteran of the Legio XIII Gemina.

EDCS-27300111.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valerius Longinus veteranus numeri Campestrorum vixit nnos XXXXVIII Aurelia Pirusi virginio bene merenti posuit M
EDCS-27300111.translation: To the Divine Shades. Valerius Longinus, veteran of the Numerus Campestrorum, lived for 48 years. Aurelia Pirusi placed this for the well-deserving virginio.
EDCS-27300111.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Valerius Longinus, a veteran who lived 48 years, erected by Aurelia Pirusi for a 'well-deserving virginio,' possibly an error or specific term of endearment for Valerius.

EDCS-27300112.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelia Secundina vixit annos XIIII et Aelia Crispina vixit annos IIII Aelii Secundus et Deccia parentes filiis pientissimis et Aelius Consius maritus
EDCS-27300112.translation: To the Divine Shades. Aelia Secundina lived 14 years, and Aelia Crispina lived 4 years. Aelius Secundus and Deccia, parents, placed this for their most devoted daughters, and Aelius Consius, the husband.
EDCS-27300112.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Aelius Secundus and Deccia dedicated to their two young daughters, Aelia Secundina and Aelia Crispina, with Aelius Consius, likely the daughters' maternal uncle or a relative, also mentioned.

EDCS-27300113.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelia Valeria vixit nnos LX et Tiberius Claudius Festus vixit annos XX et Aelius Valerius vixit annos XVI Aelius Saturninus Aelia Valentina liberti patronae bene meritae posuerunt
EDCS-27300113.translation: To the Divine Shades. Aelia Valeria lived 60 years, and Tiberius Claudius Festus lived 20 years, and Aelius Valerius lived 16 years. Aelius Saturninus and Aelia Valentina, freedmen, placed this for their well-deserving patroness.
EDCS-27300113.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by freedmen Aelius Saturninus and Aelia Valentina to their patroness Aelia Valeria, who lived to age 60, and also commemorating Tiberius Claudius Festus (20 years) and Aelius Valerius (16 years), likely her children or close family members.

EDCS-27300114.text_edition: P Ulpianus prob coniugi bene merenti
