EDCS-27300114.translation: P. Ulpianus for his well-deserving spouse.
EDCS-27300114.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by P. Ulpianus for his well-deserving wife, indicating a spousal dedication.

EDCS-27300115.text_edition: canius vixit annis XXXV Aflicta Caesia coniugi bene merito posuit
EDCS-27300115.translation: Canus lived 35 years. Aflicta Caesia placed this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-27300115.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Canus, who lived 35 years, erected by his wife Aflicta Caesia.

EDCS-27300116.text_edition: M Vibus Atticus ex voto - libens solvit
EDCS-27300116.translation: M. Vibius Atticus, in accordance with a vow, willingly fulfilled it.
EDCS-27300116.summary: This is a votive inscription by M. Vibius Atticus, fulfilling a vow he had made, likely to a deity.

EDCS-27300117.text_edition: Dis Manibus Longus et Longinus gemini et Max
EDCS-27300117.translation: To the Divine Shades. Longus and Longinus, twins, and Max...
EDCS-27300117.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Shades, mentioning twin brothers Longus and Longinus, and another individual named Max, whose full name is lost.

EDCS-27300120.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus pontifex maximus tr(ibunicia) potestate XII consul V imperator VI pater patriae fecit per cohortem I Flaviam Ulpiam Hispanorum milliariam civium Romanorum equitatam a Potaissa Napocae milia passuum X
EDCS-27300120.translation: The Emperor Caesar Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the twelfth time, consul for the fifth time, hailed imperator for the sixth time, father of the country, built this through the First Flavian Ulpian Cohort of Spaniards, milliaria, Roman citizens, equestrian, from Potaissa to Napoca, a distance of 10 miles.
EDCS-27300120.summary: This is an honorific and building inscription dating to the reign of Emperor Trajan (circa 108-109 AD, given the titles), recording the construction of a road or similar project over 10 miles by the Cohors I Flavia Ulpia Hispanorum, connecting Potaissa and Napoca.

EDCS-27800805.text_edition: Dis Manibus Severus et P Surus M miles ex Marcia Donata heres coniugi bene merenti P posuit
EDCS-27800805.translation: To the Divine Manes. Severus and P. Surus, a soldier from Marcia Donata, his heir, placed this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-27800805.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to a deceased wife, erected by her husband Severus and P. Surus, identified as a soldier and heir.

EDCS-27800807.translation: [---] of the XXII Primigenia Legion, to his most [---] son.
EDCS-27800807.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, mentioning a soldier of Legio XXII Primigenia and dedicated to his son.

EDCS-27800809.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors IIII Hispanorum equitata cui praeest Publius Didius Italicus praefectus domi sic Mauretania Caesarensi votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-27800809.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the Fourth Cohort of Equitated Spaniards, commanded by Publius Didius Italicus, prefect, having returned home from Mauretania Caesarensis, willingly and deservedly fulfilled its vow.
EDCS-27800809.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, made by the Cohors IIII Hispanorum equitata and its prefect Publius Didius Italicus, upon their return from Mauretania Caesarensis.

EDCS-27800810.text_edition: Aeterno sacrum Aurelia Galla coniux Aureli Decimi  imaginiferi M
EDCS-27800810.translation: Sacred to the Eternal. Aurelia Galla, wife of Aurelius Decimus, the imaginifer.
EDCS-27800810.summary: This is a funerary inscription, dedicated by Aurelia Galla to her husband, Aurelius Decimus, who was an imaginifer (standard-bearer).

EDCS-27800811.text_edition: Dianae  sacrum  Publius CGaesonius#Caesonius#GAESONIUS Celer  votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-27800811.translation: Sacred to Diana. Publius Caesonius Celer willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27800811.summary: This is a votive inscription to Diana, dedicated by Publius Caesonius Celer in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-27800812.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Aulus Tapetius Amethystus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-27800812.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Aulus Tapetius Amethystus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27800812.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, dedicated by Aulus Tapetius Amethystus.

EDCS-27800813.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Aulus Tapetius Epius
EDCS-27800813.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Aulus Tapetius Epius.
EDCS-27800813.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, dedicated by Aulus Tapetius Epius.

EDCS-27800815.text_edition: Obilae et Herculi Marcus D P decurio coloniae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-27800815.translation: To Obila and Hercules. Marcus D. P., a decurion of the colony, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27800815.summary: This is a votive inscription to Obila and Hercules, dedicated by Marcus D. P., a decurion of a colony.

EDCS-27800816.text_edition: Dis Manibus Quintus Valerius Macrinus veteranus legionis V Macedonicae missicius praefecti decurio? coloniae Dacicae Sarmizegetusae
EDCS-27800816.translation: To the Divine Manes. Quintus Valerius Macrinus, a veteran of the V Macedonica Legion, discharged by the prefect, a decurion of the Dacic Colony of Sarmizegetusa.
EDCS-27800816.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Valerius Macrinus, identifying him as a veteran of Legio V Macedonica and a decurion of the colony of Dacian Sarmizegetusa.

EDCS-27800818.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Maturus veteranus cohortis Commagenorum vixit annos L Aurelius Primanus vixit annos XVIIII Aurelius Surus milis sic numeri Maurorum Miciensium vixit annos XX Aurelia Eustina vixit annos BS XVIII Aurelia Surilla mater pro pietate fecit bene merentibus
EDCS-27800818.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelius Maturus, a veteran of the Cohort of Commagenians, lived for 50 years. Aurelius Primanus lived for 19 years. Aurelius Surus, a soldier of the Mician Moorish unit, lived for 20 years. Aurelia Eustina lived for 18 and a half years. Aurelia Surilla, his mother, made this out of piety for her well-deserving relatives.
EDCS-27800818.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelia Surilla to multiple deceased family members: Aurelius Maturus (a veteran), Aurelius Primanus, Aurelius Surus (a soldier), and Aurelia Eustina, all of whom died at various ages.

EDCS-27800819.text_edition: Dis Manibus Quinto Aurelio Quinti filio Papiria Tertio decurioni et flamini coloniae Sarmizegetusae Quintus Aurelius Saturninus Augustalis coloniae et Proshodus et Logismus liberti et heredes faciendum curaverunt  Dis Manibus Quinto Aurelio Quinti filio Papiria Tertio decurioni et flamini coloniae Sarmizegetusae Quintus Aurelius Saturninus Augustalis coloniae et Proshodus et Logismus liberti et heredes faciendum curaverunt  Dis Manibus Quinto Aurelio Quinti filio Papiria Tertio decurioni et flamini coloniae Sarmizegetusae Quintus Aurelius Saturninus Augustalis coloniae et Proshodus et Logismus liberti et heredes faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-27800819.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Quintus Aurelius, son of Quintus, of the Papirian tribe, Tertius, decurion and flamen of the colony of Sarmizegetusa. Quintus Aurelius Saturninus, Augustalis of the colony, and Proshodus and Logismus, his freedmen and heirs, took care to have this made.
EDCS-27800819.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument dedicated to Quintus Aurelius Tertius, a decurion and flamen of Sarmizegetusa, by his freedmen and heirs, Quintus Aurelius Saturninus, Proshodus, and Logismus.

EDCS-27800820.text_edition: Marcus Procilius Marci filius Papiria Niceta IIvir et flamen coloniae Sarmizegetusae item sacerdos Laurentium Lavinatium aedem Augustalibus pecunia sua faciendam instituit eandem Marcus Procilius Regulus decurio coloniae equo publico filius et heres eius perfecit dedicavitque
EDCS-27800820.translation: Marcus Procilius, son of Marcus, of the Papirian tribe, Niceta, a duumvir and flamen of the colony of Sarmizegetusa, also a priest of Laurentium Lavinatium, began to have a temple built for the Augustales with his own money; the same Marcus Procilius Regulus, decurion of the colony with public horse, his son and heir, completed and dedicated it.
EDCS-27800820.summary: This inscription records the initiation of a temple for the Augustales by Marcus Procilius Niceta and its completion and dedication by his son and heir, Marcus Procilius Regulus, both prominent figures in Sarmizegetusa.

EDCS-27800821.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Publio Pontio Secundino vixit annos X menses III dies XXIII Publio Pontio Blando vixit annos VI dies VII Publius Pontius Pontianus filiis et Valeriae Blandinae coniugi vixit nnos LXV
EDCS-27800821.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Publius Pontius Secundinus, who lived 10 years, 3 months, 23 days. For Publius Pontius Blandus, who lived 6 years, 7 days. Publius Pontius Pontianus placed this for his sons and for Valeria Blandina, his wife, who lived 65 years.
EDCS-27800821.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument erected by Publius Pontius Pontianus for his two deceased sons, Publius Pontius Secundinus and Publius Pontius Blandus, and his wife, Valeria Blandina.

EDCS-28400485.text_edition: Silvano Domestico Hermadion votum libens posuit
EDCS-28400485.translation: To Silvanus Domesticus, Hermadion willingly placed his vow.
EDCS-28400485.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Silvanus Domesticus, dedicated by Hermadion, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-28400486.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Valerio Septimio veteranus legionis V Macedonicae piae constantis vixit nnos LVIII Aelia Iulia coniux et heres pio virginio bene de se merito posuit
EDCS-28400486.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Gaius Valerius Septimius, a veteran of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, Pia Constans, who lived 58 years. Aelia Iulia, his wife and heir, placed this for her devoted husband, who well deserved it.
EDCS-28400486.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument dedicated to Gaius Valerius Septimius, a veteran of the Legio V Macedonica, by his wife and heir, Aelia Iulia.

EDCS-28400488.text_edition: Publius Aelius Attil et Aelius Ati Emeritus Severa coniux faciendum curavit
EDCS-28400488.translation: Publius Aelius Attil and Aelius Ati Emeritus, with Severa his wife, took care to have this made.
EDCS-28400488.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument, likely funerary, by Publius Aelius Attil, Aelius Ati Emeritus, and his wife Severa.

EDCS-28400489.text_edition: a filia vixit annos II Aelia Aurelia coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-28400489.translation: From his daughter, who lived 2 years. Aelia Aurelia placed this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-28400489.summary: This is a funerary inscription likely for a husband, placed by Aelia Aurelia, with a mention of a daughter who lived two years, possibly indicating the monument was for both or referenced the daughter's premature death.

EDCS-28400494.text_edition: ANN Iulia Aristaenete coniugi carissimo memoriam posuit
EDCS-28400494.translation: ANN. Julia Aristaenete placed this memorial for her dearest husband.
EDCS-28400494.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a memorial dedicated by Julia Aristaenete to her beloved husband, whose name is not fully preserved.

EDCS-28400499.text_edition: Dis Manibus Titi Flavi Paulli  vixit annos IIII dies XIIII Titus Flavius Pin
EDCS-28400499.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Titus Flavius Paullus, who lived 4 years, 14 days. Titus Flavius Pin[...].
EDCS-28400499.summary: This is a funerary inscription to the Divine Spirits, commemorating Titus Flavius Paullus, a young child, likely placed by Titus Flavius Pin[...], possibly his father.

EDCS-28400507.text_edition: Lucia  vixit annos L Flavia bene merenti posuit
EDCS-28400507.translation: For Lucia, who lived 50 years. Flavia placed this for her well-deserving relative.
EDCS-28400507.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument dedicated to Lucia, who lived 50 years, by Flavia, likely a family member.

EDCS-28400508.text_edition: Aelius Valerianus cum Valeriano filio beneficiario consularis voto libens merito posuit
EDCS-28400508.translation: Aelius Valerianus, with his son Valerianus, a beneficiary of the consular, willingly and deservedly set this up in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-28400508.summary: This inscription records Aelius Valerianus and his son Valerianus dedicating a monument to a deity in fulfillment of a vow, likely dating to the High Imperial period.

EDCS-28400509.text_edition: Dis Manibus Mavida Epicadi filia vixit annis XXXXV maritus conpari posuit hic sita est
EDCS-28400509.translation: To the Divine Manes. Mavida, daughter of Epicadus, lived for 45 years. Her husband set this up for his partner. She is laid here.
EDCS-28400509.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a husband to his wife, Mavida, who died at the age of 45, indicating a burial place.

EDCS-28400512.summary: This brief inscription records Hermes fulfilling a vow, likely dating to the High Imperial period.

EDCS-28400513.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Silius Ingenuus pro salute sua suorum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28400513.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Titus Silius Ingenuus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for his own health and that of his family.
EDCS-28400513.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Titus Silius Ingenuus for the well-being of himself and his family, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-28400516.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely naming a Gaius Iulius and potentially indicating a numeral or additional name, precluding a detailed summary.

EDCS-28400519.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ulpius Te eques alae Sstipendiorum XV vixit annos XXX II V MAC F fratri pientissimo  bene merenti
EDCS-28400519.translation: To the Divine Manes. Ulpius Te[---], an eques of the ala S[---], served 15 years, lived 32 years. V. MAC. F. to a most devoted and well-deserving brother.
EDCS-28400519.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Ulpius Te[---], a cavalryman who served 15 years and died at 32, erected by his brother.

EDCS-28400523.text_edition: Apollini Plator Panentis ex voto castellanis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28400523.translation: To Apollo. Plator, son of Panentis, from a vow, fulfilled the vow willingly and deservedly for the castellanis.
EDCS-28400523.summary: This inscription records Plator, son of Panentis, fulfilling a vow to Apollo on behalf of the inhabitants of a fort (castellanis).

EDCS-28400524.text_edition: Dianae sacrum Celsenius Adiutor magister collegii donum dedit
EDCS-28400524.translation: Sacred to Diana. Celsenius Adiutor, master of the collegium, gave this gift.
EDCS-28400524.summary: This inscription records a gift dedicated to Diana by Celsenius Adiutor, who was a master of a collegium (association or guild).

EDCS-28400525.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Implaius Linsantis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28400525.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Implaius Linsantis willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28400525.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Implaius Linsantis, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-28400526.text_edition: Iano Gemino Aelius Baebius et Beusas Platoris votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-28400526.translation: To Janus Geminus. Aelius Baebius and Beusas, son of Plator, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vows.
EDCS-28400526.summary: This inscription records Aelius Baebius and Beusas, son of Plator, fulfilling their vows to the deity Janus Geminus.

EDCS-28400527.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Verzo Pantonis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28400527.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Best and Greatest. Verzo, son of Panto, willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-28400527.summary: This inscription records a religious dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Verzo, son of Panto, in fulfillment of a vow, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400529.text_edition: Silvano Pla Baotius et collegium donum dederunt
EDCS-28400529.translation: Baotius, son of Pla, and the college gave a gift to Silvanus.
EDCS-28400529.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Silvanus, made by Baotius, son of Pla, and a 'collegium' (association or guild), likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400534.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae Minervae pro salute et victoria et incolumitate Marci Aureli Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti et Iuliae Augustae matris eius et castrorum senatus et patriae Aelius Sostratus procurator
EDCS-28400534.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Juno Queen, Minerva, for the health and victory and safety of Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus and Julia Augusta his mother and the camps, the Senate and the Fatherland, Aelius Sostratus, procurator.
EDCS-28400534.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter, Juno, and Minerva, for the well-being and success of the Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (Caracalla) and his mother Julia Domna, likely dating to the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28400535.text_edition: Isidi pro salute Marci Iuli Apollinaris Verus Augusti nostri Romanus Augusti nostri vernae vilicus posuit sic
EDCS-28400535.translation: To Isis, for the health of Marcus Julius Apollinaris. Verus, our Augustus's Roman servant, the overseer, set this up thus.
EDCS-28400535.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Isis for the health of Marcus Julius Apollinaris, set up by Verus, a Roman overseer and a servant of the emperor, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400536.text_edition: Saturno Marcus Antonius Saturninus decurio coloniae  Saturno Marcus Antonius Saturninus decurio coloniae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28400536.translation: To Saturn, Marcus Antonius Saturninus, decurion of the colony, willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-28400536.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Saturn, made by Marcus Antonius Saturninus, a decurion of a colony, in fulfillment of a vow, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400537.text_edition: V Marcus Antonius Saturninus decurio coloniae
EDCS-28400537.translation: V. Marcus Antonius Saturninus, decurion of the colony.
EDCS-28400537.summary: This inscription is an excerpt from a larger monument, recording the name and title of Marcus Antonius Saturninus, a decurion of a colony, which by itself is incomplete but suggests a dedication or civic act.

EDCS-28400538.text_edition: Silvano Domestico sacrum Marcus Opellius Firminus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28400538.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Domesticus. Marcus Opellius Firminus willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-28400538.summary: This inscription records a religious dedication to Silvanus Domesticus by Marcus Opellius Firminus, in fulfillment of a vow, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400540.text_edition: Dis Manibus Calventia Asella domo verna vixit annos XXXV posuit Calventia Marcellina patrona libertae bene meritae
EDCS-28400540.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Calventia Asella, a home-born slave, lived 35 years. Calventia Marcellina, her patroness, set this up for her well-deserving freedwoman.
EDCS-28400540.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Calventia Asella, a freedwoman who lived 35 years, set up by her patroness Calventia Marcellina, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400541.text_edition: Claudiae Piae domo Lapiaerom vixit nnos XXXXVII et Claudio Firmino filio eius vixit nnos XIIII menses IIII dies X posuit Capito Triti libertus coniugi et filio piissimis bene merentibus hic siti sunt
EDCS-28400541.translation: To Claudia Pia, from Lapiaerom, who lived 47 years, and to her son Claudius Firminus, who lived 14 years, 4 months, 10 days. Capito Triti, a freedman, set this up for his most dutiful wife and most deserving son. They are buried here.
EDCS-28400541.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Claudia Pia and her son Claudius Firminus, set up by Capito Triti, presumably her husband and the boy's father, a freedman, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400543.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors II Flavia Commagenorum cui praeest Caius Pomponius Cassianus praefectus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28400543.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. The Second Cohort of Flavian Commagenians, commanded by Caius Pomponius Cassianus, prefect, willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-28400543.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by the Cohors II Flavia Commagenorum, led by its prefect Caius Pomponius Cassianus, in fulfillment of a vow, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400545.text_edition: Mercurio sacrum Iulius Arcanus praefectus cohortis II Flaviae Commagenorum votum solvit laetus libens merito  Mercurio sacrum Iulius Arcanus praefectus cohortis II Flaviae Commagenorum votum solvit laetus libens merito
EDCS-28400545.translation: Sacred to Mercury. Iulius Arcanus, prefect of the Second Flavian Cohort of Commagenians, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow. Sacred to Mercury. Iulius Arcanus, prefect of the Second Flavian Cohort of Commagenians, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28400545.summary: This is a votive inscription where Iulius Arcanus, a military prefect, dedicates an altar or monument to the god Mercury, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-28400546.text_edition: Deo Mercurio Marcus Antonius Sabinianus princeps votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28400546.translation: To the god Mercury, Marcus Antonius Sabinianus, a princeps, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28400546.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the god Mercury by Marcus Antonius Sabinianus, likely a leading man or 'princeps', in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-28400547.text_edition: Deae Nemesi Augustae Aurelia Epictesis pro sal salute sua et suorum ex voto posuit
EDCS-28400547.translation: To the Augustan goddess Nemesis, Aurelia Epictesis erected this from a vow for her own well-being and that of her family.
EDCS-28400547.summary: This is a votive dedication by Aurelia Epictesis to the goddess Nemesis Augusta, made in fulfillment of a vow for the health and safety of herself and her household.

EDCS-28400548.text_edition: Nymphis Caius Iulius Iulianus |(centurio) legionis XIII geminae votum solvit
EDCS-28400548.translation: To the Nymphs, Caius Iulius Iulianus, a centurion of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28400548.summary: This inscription is a votive offering dedicated to the Nymphs by Caius Iulius Iulianus, a centurion of the Legion XIII Gemina, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-28400551.text_edition: Silvano Domestico et Mercurio sacrum Aurelius Secundus
EDCS-28400551.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Domesticus and Mercury, (from) Aurelius Secundus.
EDCS-28400551.summary: This short inscription records a dedication by Aurelius Secundus to the gods Silvanus Domesticus and Mercury.

EDCS-28400552.text_edition: Silvano Domestico Marcus Veracius Euhodus votum
EDCS-28400552.translation: To Silvanus Domesticus, Marcus Veracius Euhodus (made this) vow.
EDCS-28400552.summary: This fragment records a vow made to Silvanus Domesticus by Marcus Veracius Euhodus, likely indicating a votive offering.

EDCS-28400554.text_edition: Deae Syriae Marcus Ulpius Phoebus libens posuit
EDCS-28400554.translation: To the goddess Syria, Marcus Ulpius Phoebus gladly erected this.
EDCS-28400554.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the goddess Syria by Marcus Ulpius Phoebus, indicating a votive offering.

EDCS-28400555.summary: This is a simple name inscription, likely part of a larger monument, perhaps an ownership mark or a funerary inscription.

EDCS-28400557.text_edition: inus votum solvit libens merito I(ovi) Optimo Maximo
EDCS-28400557.translation: [-]inus gladly and deservedly fulfilled a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-28400557.summary: This fragmented votive inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by an individual whose name ends in '-inus' to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-28400558.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelia Hygia vixit annis XVIII Aelius Valentinus coloniae Apulensis flamen libertae et coniugi gratae quam tempus durum rapuit familiamquae simul Dacia te voluit possedit Micia secum have puella multum adque in aevum vale
EDCS-28400558.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Aelia Hygia lived for 18 years. Aelius Valentinus, flamen of the colony of Apulum, (erected this) for his freedwoman and beloved wife, whom harsh time snatched away, and her family with her. Dacia desired you, Micia possessed you with her. Farewell, girl, much and forever farewell.
EDCS-28400558.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Aelia Hygia by her husband, Aelius Valentinus, a flamen of Apulum, commemorating her life cut short at 18 years and expressing his sorrow, possibly alluding to her origins or resting place in Dacia/Micia.

EDCS-28400559.text_edition: Dis Manibus Antonia Quaeta vixit annos XXXIII et Antonia Valentina filia vixit annos XII Antonius Valentinus filius matri et sorori bene merentibus posuit
EDCS-28400559.translation: To the Divine Manes. Antonia Quaeta lived for 33 years, and Antonia Valentina, her daughter, lived for 12 years. Antonius Valentinus, her son, placed this for his mother and sister, who deserved well.
EDCS-28400559.summary: This is a funerary inscription (likely a tombstone) dedicated by Antonius Valentinus to his mother, Antonia Quaeta, and his sister, Antonia Valentina.

EDCS-28400560.text_edition: Dis Inferis Manibus Censorinus imaginifer alae Campagonum stipendiorum XX vixitII EVT et Valeria Cara filia VIAVI ET Augus eius
EDCS-28400560.translation: To the Infernal Divine Manes. Censorinus, an imaginifer of the Ala Campagonum, served 20 years and lived [approximately 22 years?]. And Valeria Cara, his daughter, lived [approximately 6 years?] and Augusta, his wife.
EDCS-28400560.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Censorinus, a military standard-bearer, and his daughter Valeria Cara, likely set up by his wife Augusta, dating from a period when Roman military personnel were active.

EDCS-28400561.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ulpius Mettius veteranus ex decurione alae Claudiae
EDCS-28400561.translation: To the Divine Manes. Ulpius Mettius, a veteran, formerly a decurion of the Ala Claudia.
EDCS-28400561.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ulpius Mettius, a retired cavalry officer from the Ala Claudia, likely set up by his family or comrades.

EDCS-28400562.text_edition: Dis Manibus is Dassi  numeri Maurorum Miciensium  vixit  hic situs est
EDCS-28400562.translation: To the Divine Manes. [---] Dassi, of the numerus Maurorum Miciensium, lived [---]. He is buried here.
EDCS-28400562.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a member of the numerus Maurorum Miciensium, indicating a military burial, likely from the Imperial period when such units were common.

EDCS-28400564.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lusiae Priscae vixit annos XVII Ulpius Romulus coniugi bene merenti hic sita est
EDCS-28400564.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Lusia Prisca, who lived 17 years. Ulpius Romulus placed this for his well-deserving wife. She is buried here.
EDCS-28400564.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Ulpius Romulus to his wife, Lusia Prisca, who died at the age of 17, likely a tombstone.

EDCS-28400566.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aureli- Mas Aureli-  annorum XXV
EDCS-28400566.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelius Mas Aurelius, of 25 years.
EDCS-28400566.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for a person named Aurelius Mas Aurelius, who died at the age of 25, likely part of a tombstone.

EDCS-28400570.text_edition: Caius Iulius Tato deo aeterno votum posuit
EDCS-28400570.translation: Gaius Julius Tato placed a vow to the eternal god.
EDCS-28400570.summary: This is a votive inscription by Gaius Julius Tato dedicated to an eternal god, indicating a personal religious offering or thanksgiving.

EDCS-28400571.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Romanius Summus decurio coloniae votum solvit merito
EDCS-28400571.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Gaius Romanius Summus, a decurion of the colony, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28400571.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Gaius Romanius Summus, a decurion, fulfilling a vow, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400572.text_edition: Nymphis Marcus Statius Priscus legatus Augusti pro praetore votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28400572.translation: To the Nymphs. Marcus Statius Priscus, imperial legate with pro-praetorian power, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28400572.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Nymphs by Marcus Statius Priscus, a high-ranking imperial official, fulfilling a vow, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400573.text_edition: Genio loci pro salute Imperatoris  legionis V Macedonicae CVRV curante Iulio Euangeliano praefecto
EDCS-28400573.translation: To the Genius of the place for the well-being of the Emperor, [---] of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, with Julius Euangelianus, the prefect, overseeing.
EDCS-28400573.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the Genius Loci (local spirit) for the Emperor's health, made by a unit or individual of the Legio V Macedonica, under the supervision of its prefect, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400577.text_edition: ATIIICOES faciendum curaverunt Aurelia Italica vixit annos LX
EDCS-28400577.translation: Atius and Atiia Coes had this made. Aurelia Italica lived 60 years.
EDCS-28400577.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely for a tomb or monument, indicating that Atius and Atiia Coes commissioned it for Aurelia Italica, who lived to be 60 years old.

EDCS-28400578.text_edition: Firmius Florentinus decurio alae Bosporanorum donum dedit
EDCS-28400578.translation: Firmius Florentinus, decurion of the ala Bosporanorum, gave this gift.
EDCS-28400578.summary: This inscription records a dedication or gift made by Firmius Florentinus, an officer (decurion) of a cavalry unit known as the ala Bosporanorum.

EDCS-28400580.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo A Helico votum libens solvit merito
EDCS-28400580.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, A. Helico willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28400580.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, recording A. Helico's willing and deserved fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-28400582.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Ulpio Tertio decurioni coloniae vixit annos XXI Ulpia Opiava filio piissimo posuit
EDCS-28400582.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Marcus Ulpius Tertius, decurion of the colony, who lived 21 years. Ulpia Opiava set this up for her most devoted son.
EDCS-28400582.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Marcus Ulpius Tertius, a decurion of a colony who died at 21, erected by his mother, Ulpia Opiava, for her pious son.

EDCS-28400583.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Aurelio Daedalo vixit annis LX Valeria Cara vixit annis XXXV coniugi optimo Aurelia Tetula vixit annis L Aurelia Augusta vixit annis XXX Caius Aurelius Carus Caius Aurelius Valerianus veteranus parentibus sororibus fecerunt Flavia Crispina Vetilla  Maxima Butes eorum
EDCS-28400583.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Gaius Aurelius Daedalus, who lived 60 years. Valeria Cara, who lived 35 years, to her most excellent husband. Aurelia Tetula, who lived 50 years. Aurelia Augusta, who lived 30 years. Gaius Aurelius Carus and Gaius Aurelius Valerianus, a veteran, made this for their parents and sisters. Flavia Crispina, Vetilla, Maxima, Butes are theirs.
EDCS-28400583.summary: This complex funerary inscription commemorates multiple family members: Gaius Aurelius Daedalus, Valeria Cara (his wife), Aurelia Tetula, and Aurelia Augusta, set up by Gaius Aurelius Carus and veteran Gaius Aurelius Valerianus for their parents and sisters, also mentioning other family members.

EDCS-28400584.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulius Maximus vixit annos XXV Herennia Valentina coniugi carissimo
EDCS-28400584.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Iulius Maximus lived 25 years. Herennia Valentina [set this up] for her dearest husband.
EDCS-28400584.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Iulius Maximus, who died at 25, erected by his wife Herennia Valentina.

EDCS-28400585.summary: This brief inscription identifies Diogenes as a stonecutter, likely marking his work on a monument or as a personal dedication.

EDCS-28400586.text_edition: Aesculapio et Hygiae Publius Aelius Theimes
EDCS-28400586.translation: To Aesculapius and Hygia, Publius Aelius Theimes [dedicated this].
EDCS-28400586.summary: This is a dedication to the healing deities Aesculapius and Hygia by Publius Aelius Theimes.

EDCS-28400589.text_edition: Aesculapio et Hygiae Quintus Axius Aelianus iunior votum pro patris incolumitate susceptum cum gratulatione libens solvit Ioni Ionius
EDCS-28400589.translation: To Aesculapius and Hygia, Quintus Axius Aelianus Junior willingly and gratefully fulfilled the vow undertaken for his father's well-being. Ioni Ionius.
EDCS-28400589.summary: This is a votive inscription to Aesculapius and Hygia, where Quintus Axius Aelianus Junior gratefully fulfilled a vow for his father's health, also mentioning Ioni Ionius.

EDCS-28400590.text_edition: Deo Aeterno Aurelius Marinus Marini filius vexillarius collegii fabrorum pro salute sua et suorum ex voto posuit
EDCS-28400590.translation: To the Eternal God, Aurelius Marinus, son of Marinus, standard-bearer of the college of craftsmen, set this up by vow for his own safety and that of his household.
EDCS-28400590.summary: This is a votive inscription to the Eternal God by Aurelius Marinus, a standard-bearer for a college of craftsmen, who set it up to fulfill a vow for the well-being of himself and his family.

EDCS-28400591.text_edition: Deo Aeterno Herennius Gemellinus vir egregius procurator Auggustorum nnnostrorum pro Ael(ia) Saturnina coniuge et Herenniis Urso et Gemellino et Supero Saturnino votum libens solvit
EDCS-28400591.translation: To the Eternal God, Herennius Gemellinus, an excellent man, procurator of our emperors, willingly fulfilled his vow for his wife Aelia Saturnina and his sons Herennius Ursus, Herennius Gemellinus, and Herennius Superus Saturninus.
EDCS-28400591.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Herennius Gemellinus, an imperial procurator, to an eternal god on behalf of his family.

EDCS-28400592.text_edition: Dianae Augustae sacrum Ianuarius Auggustorum libertus nummularius ex voto locus datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-28400592.translation: Sacred to Diana Augusta. Ianuarius, freedman of the emperors, a money changer, from a vow. The place was given by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28400592.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Diana Augusta by Ianuarius, an imperial freedman and money changer, indicating the site was granted by municipal decree.

EDCS-28400593.text_edition: Eponabus et Campestribus sacrum Marcus Calventius Viator |(centurio) legionis IIII Flaviae Felicis exerciator equitum singularium Cai Avidi Nigrini legati Augusti pro praetore votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28400593.translation: Sacred to the Eponae and the Campestres. Marcus Calventius Viator, centurion of the Fourth Flavian Felix Legion, trainer of the imperial horse guards of Caius Avidius Nigrinus, imperial legate with pro-praetorian power, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28400593.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Marcus Calventius Viator, a centurion and equestrian trainer, to the goddesses Eponae and Campestres, likely dating to the early-to-mid 2nd century AD given the mention of Avidius Nigrinus.

EDCS-28400594.text_edition: Genio decuriae XIII collegii fabrum Marcus Ulpius Sa
EDCS-28400594.translation: To the Genius of the thirteenth decuria of the college of craftsmen, Marcus Ulpius Sa[---]
EDCS-28400594.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Genius of the thirteenth decuria (a subdivision) of a college of craftsmen, made by Marcus Ulpius Sa[---].

EDCS-28400595.text_edition: Genio libertorum et servorum Publius Publicius Anthus et Publicius Cletus donum dederunt dedicaverunt
EDCS-28400595.translation: To the Genius of the freedmen and slaves, Publius Publicius Anthus and Publicius Cletus gave and dedicated this gift.
EDCS-28400595.summary: This inscription records a gift dedicated to the Genius of freedmen and slaves by Publius Publicius Anthus and Publicius Cletus.

EDCS-28400596.text_edition: Deae Reginae Marcus Cominius Quintus equo publico pontifex et quinquennalis coloniae et Antonia Valentina eius pro salute Claudiae Valentinae  templum a solo fecerunt
EDCS-28400596.translation: To the Goddess Regina, Marcus Cominius Quintus, a pontifex and quinquennalis of the colony with a public horse, and Antonia Valentina, his wife, built this temple from its foundations for the health of Claudia Valentina.
EDCS-28400596.summary: This inscription records the construction of a temple to the Goddess Regina by Marcus Cominius Quintus, a municipal official, and his wife Antonia Valentina, for the well-being of Claudia Valentina.

EDCS-28400597.text_edition: Deae Reginae Aelia Primitiva ex voto pro Aelio Metrodoro patrono suo votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28400597.translation: To the Goddess Regina, Aelia Primitiva willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow from a vow for her patron Aelius Metrodorus.
EDCS-28400597.summary: This inscription records Aelia Primitiva fulfilling a vow to the Goddess Regina on behalf of her patron, Aelius Metrodorus.

EDCS-28400598.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Antonius Priscus ex voto
EDCS-28400598.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Antonius Priscus, from a vow.
EDCS-28400598.summary: This inscription records a simple dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus made by Antonius Priscus as a fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-28400599.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Aurelius Valens Flavius Zora decuriones collegii fabrum ex voto posuerunt
EDCS-28400599.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Aurelius Valens and Flavius Zora, decurions of the college of craftsmen, placed this from a vow.
EDCS-28400599.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aurelius Valens and Flavius Zora, who were decurions (councilors) of a college of craftsmen, as a fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-28400600.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Aurelius Caius [---]
EDCS-28400600.summary: This inscription is a fragmentary dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aurelius Caius.

EDCS-28400601.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Aeterno pro salute Ianuari Augusti ex arcario et Vitiae Threptes eius Flavius Bellicus ex voto
EDCS-28400601.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Eternal, for the well-being of Ianuarius Augustus, formerly from the treasury, and of Vitia Threpte, his wife, Flavius Bellicus, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-28400601.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Jupiter by Flavius Bellicus for the health of Ianuarius Augustus and Vitia Threpte, likely dating to the early-to-mid Imperial period.

EDCS-28400602.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Aeterno Quintus Attius Anthimus Augustalis coloniae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28400602.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Eternal, Quintus Attius Anthimus, Augustalis of the colony, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28400602.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter by Quintus Attius Anthimus, an Augustalis of the colony, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400603.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo aeterno Titus Flavius Aper scriba coloniae ex voto
EDCS-28400603.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Eternal, Titus Flavius Aper, scribe of the colony, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-28400603.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Jupiter by Titus Flavius Aper, a scribe of the colony, probably from the Imperial era.

EDCS-28400604.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Gaius Gaianus et Proculus Apollofanes Suri negotiatores pro salute sua ex voto
EDCS-28400604.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest Dolichenus, Gaius Gaianus and Proculus Apollofanes, merchants from Syria, for their own well-being, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-28400604.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to Jupiter Dolichenus made by two Syrian merchants, Gaius Gaianus and Proculus Apollofanes, for their health, indicating a date within the Imperial period when this cult was popular.

EDCS-28400605.text_edition: Aurelius Annianus Deo Libero ex suo dedit
EDCS-28400605.translation: Aurelius Annianus gave this to the god Liber at his own expense.
EDCS-28400605.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Aurelius Annianus to the god Liber, funded by himself, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400606.translation: Titus Flavius Aper, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-28400606.summary: This inscription is a brief votive dedication by Titus Flavius Aper, likely to a deity whose name is omitted or was on a separate part of the monument, datable to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400607.text_edition: Minervae Augustae Caius Sempronius Urbanus procurator Augusti
EDCS-28400607.translation: To Minerva Augusta, Caius Sempronius Urbanus, procurator of the emperor.
EDCS-28400607.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Minerva Augusta by Caius Sempronius Urbanus, an imperial procurator, indicating a date within the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400609.text_edition: Invicto deo Serapidi et Isidi frugiferae pro salute atque incolumitate Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti Parthici maximi pontificis maximi Brittannici maximi VG FS
EDCS-28400609.translation: To the invincible god Serapis and to fruitful Isis, for the health and safety of Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, greatest Parthian, greatest pontiff, greatest Britannic... V.G.F.S.
EDCS-28400609.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Serapis and Isis for the health and safety of Emperor Caracalla (Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus), securely dating it to his reign (211-217 AD), likely expressing a general vow or offering.

EDCS-28400610.text_edition: Silvano Quartus milis sic legionis XIII geminae votum reddedit
EDCS-28400610.translation: To Silvanus, Quartus, a soldier of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28400610.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Silvanus by Quartus, a soldier of the Legio XIII Gemina, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400612.translation: Severus, freedman of Augustus, to Cautopates.
EDCS-28400612.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Severus, an imperial freedman, to Cautopates, a companion of Mithras, suggesting a date in the Imperial period when the Mithraic cult was active.

EDCS-28400613.summary: This inscription records Aelius Nepos fulfilling a vow, likely on a small altar or dedication.

EDCS-28400614.translation: Longus, a salt-seller, placed this out of a vow.
EDCS-28400614.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication made by Longus, a salt merchant, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-28400615.translation: Aurelius Valentinus placed this out of a vow.
EDCS-28400615.summary: This inscription records Aurelius Valentinus making a dedication in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-28400616.text_edition: Invicto Deo Mithrae Τ(ίτος) Ἑρένι(ος) Ἰανουάριος
EDCS-28400616.translation: To the Unconquered God Mithras, Titus Herenius Ianuarius.
EDCS-28400616.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the god Mithras by Titus Herenius Ianuarius, likely found in a Mithraeum.

EDCS-28400618.text_edition: Cassius Maximus Augustalis coloniae Sarmizegetusae Marcus Ulpius Gaius pro salute sua ex voto posuerunt
EDCS-28400618.translation: Cassius Maximus, Augustalis of the colony of Sarmizegetusa, and Marcus Ulpius Gaius placed this for their own well-being, out of a vow.
EDCS-28400618.summary: This inscription records a joint votive dedication by Cassius Maximus, an Augustalis of Sarmizegetusa, and Marcus Ulpius Gaius, made for their health.

EDCS-28400619.translation: [...]nus placed this out of a vow.
EDCS-28400619.summary: This inscription is a partial record of an individual, whose name ends in '-nus', fulfilling a vow by placing a dedication.

EDCS-28400620.text_edition: Deo Invicto Mithrae Iulius M Priscus
EDCS-28400620.translation: To the Unconquered God Mithras, Iulius M. Priscus.
EDCS-28400620.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Unconquered God Mithras by Iulius M. Priscus.

EDCS-28400621.text_edition: Soli Invicto Mithrae Caius Spedius Valerianus Augustalis coloniae donum dedit
EDCS-28400621.translation: To Sol Invictus Mithras, Gaius Spedius Valerianus, Augustalis of the colony, gave this gift.
EDCS-28400621.summary: This inscription records a gift given by Gaius Spedius Valerianus, an Augustalis of the colony, to Sol Invictus Mithras.

EDCS-28400623.text_edition: Soli Invicto Mithrae Valerius V votum libens posuit
EDCS-28400623.translation: To Sol Invictus Mithras, Valerius V. willingly placed his vow.
EDCS-28400623.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication made willingly by Valerius V. to Sol Invictus Mithras.

EDCS-28400624.text_edition: T Gaius us ex voto posuit
EDCS-28400624.translation: T. Gaius [...]us placed this out of a vow.
EDCS-28400624.summary: This inscription records a partial name, T. Gaius [...]us, making a dedication in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-28400625.text_edition: Nabarze deo pro salute Ampliati Augusti nostri dispensatoris et sua suorumque omnium Protas vikarius sic eius
EDCS-28400625.translation: To the god Nabarze, for the well-being of Ampliatus, our emperor's financial agent, and for himself and all his household, Protas, his deputy, willingly (set this up).
EDCS-28400625.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Nabarze by Protas, a deputy, for the health of Ampliatus, an imperial dispensator, and his own household, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-28400626.text_edition: Soli Invicto Mithrae Carpion Augusti libertus tabularius votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28400626.translation: To Sol Invictus Mithras, Carpion, a freedman of the emperor and a clerk, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28400626.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Carpion, an imperial freedman and clerk, to Sol Invictus Mithras, indicating a personal dedication within a Mithraic context.

EDCS-28400627.text_edition: Flavius Trofimus sic ex viso posuit
EDCS-28400627.translation: Flavius Trofimus set this up as a result of a vision.
EDCS-28400627.summary: This inscription is a votive offering by Flavius Trofimus, placed in response to a divine vision, typical of personal religious devotion.

EDCS-28400628.text_edition: MMarcii Ulpii Victorinus et Maius deccuriones coloniae Sarmizegetusae metropolis votum libenter posuerunt
EDCS-28400628.translation: Marcus Marcius Ulpius Victorinus and Maius, decurions of the colony of Sarmizegetusa Metropolis, willingly placed this vow.
EDCS-28400628.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by two decurions from the colony of Sarmizegetusa Metropolis, indicating a civic or personal religious dedication by local dignitaries.

EDCS-28400631.text_edition: Philotimus pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28400631.translation: Philotimus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for himself and his family.
EDCS-28400631.summary: This inscription records a personal votive offering by Philotimus, made for himself and his family, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-28400638.text_edition: Soli Invicto Lucius Domitius Primanus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28400638.translation: To Sol Invictus, Lucius Domitius Primanus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28400638.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Lucius Domitius Primanus to Sol Invictus, indicating a personal religious dedication.

EDCS-28400639.translation: Lucius Valerius Eutr--- placed this on account of a vow.
EDCS-28400639.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Lucius Valerius Eutr---, likely fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-28400640.text_edition: Diis Patriis Malagbel et Bebellahamon et Benefal et Manavat Publius Aelius Theimes IIviralis coloniae templum fecit solo et inpendio suo pro se suisque omnibus ob pietate ipsorum circa se iussus ab ipsis fecit et culinam subiunxit
EDCS-28400640.translation: To the Ancestral Gods Malagbel and Bebellahamon and Benefal and Manavat, Publius Aelius Theimes, a duumvir of the colony, made this temple from his own land and at his own expense for himself and all his household, on account of their piety towards him; he made it at their command and added a kitchen.
EDCS-28400640.summary: This inscription records the construction of a temple and an attached kitchen by Publius Aelius Theimes, a duumvir, dedicated to a group of Ancestral Gods (Malagbel, Bebellahamon, Benefal, Manavat) for his household, at his own expense and by divine command, highlighting a significant act of civic and personal piety.

EDCS-28400641.text_edition: Deo Sancto Malagbelo pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Severi Alexandri Pii Felicis Augusti et Iuliae Mamaeae Augustae matri Augusti nostri et castrorum Primitivos sic Augusti libertus tabularius provinciae Daciae Apulensis posuit
EDCS-28400641.translation: To the Holy God Malagbel, for the well-being of Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus and of Julia Mamaea Augusta, mother of our Augustus and of the camps, Primitivos, a freedman of Augustus and a provincial clerk of Dacia Apulensis, placed this.
EDCS-28400641.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Holy God Malagbel by Primitivos, an imperial freedman and provincial clerk, for the health of Emperor Severus Alexander and Julia Mamaea, dating it to the Severan dynasty (early 3rd century AD).

EDCS-28400645.text_edition: Tiberius Claudius Ianuarius Augustalis coloniae patronus decuriae I picturam porticus et accubitum item Claudius Verus filius eius ob honorem dupli proporticum et culinam et frontalem ex suo fecerunt
EDCS-28400645.translation: Tiberius Claudius Ianuarius, Augustalis of the colony and patron of the first decuria, (made) the painting of the portico and the dining couch; likewise, Claudius Verus, his son, for the honor of the double portico, made the portico, kitchen, and frontal at their own expense.
EDCS-28400645.summary: This inscription records the renovation and embellishment of a portico, including paintings, a dining couch, and a kitchen, by Tiberius Claudius Ianuarius, an Augustalis and decurial patron, and his son Claudius Verus, for a public building, showcasing their civic munificence.

EDCS-28400646.text_edition: Claudianus ex Khara sic eorum numini dicatus votum posuit
EDCS-28400646.translation: Claudianus from Khara, thus dedicated to their divine power, placed this vow.
EDCS-28400646.summary: This inscription records a vow made by Claudianus from Khara, dedicated to a divine power, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400648.text_edition: $ pro sal]ute tinae  et Prociliorum Flores sic Ingenui filiorum Marcus Procilius Primus libertus eius ex voto
EDCS-28400648.translation: For the health of Tina and the children of the Ingenui, Flores, Marcus Procilius Primus, his freedman, [placed this] as a vow.
EDCS-28400648.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication made by Marcus Procilius Primus, a freedman, for the health of Tina, and the children of Ingenui named Flores, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400656.text_edition: Publio Licinio Cornelio Valeriano nobilissimo Caesari filio Imperatoris Caesaris Publi Licini Egnati Gallieni Pii Felicis invicti Augusti colonia Ulpia Traiana Augusta Dacica Sarmizegetusa metropolis publice
EDCS-28400656.translation: To Publius Licinius Cornelius Valerianus, most noble Caesar, son of the Emperor Caesar Publius Licinius Egnatius Gallienus, Pious, Fortunate, Invincible Augustus, the colony Ulpia Traiana Augusta Dacica Sarmizegetusa, metropolis, publicly [dedicated this].
EDCS-28400656.summary: This inscription is a public dedication from the colony Ulpia Traiana Augusta Dacica Sarmizegetusa to Valerian II, son of Emperor Gallienus, dating to the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-28400658.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelio Cimioni qui vixit nnos  menses VI dies XI Synda scriba tabularii filio dulcissimo et pientissimo posuit
EDCS-28400658.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Aurelius Cimio, who lived for [---] years, 6 months, 11 days. Synda, scribe of the record office, placed this for her most sweet and dutiful son.
EDCS-28400658.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Synda, a public scribe, to her beloved son Aurelius Cimio, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400660.text_edition: I dies XXIIII VIEs libertus Auggustorum et Dubitata DDN
EDCS-28400660.translation: [---] 1 day, 24 [---] freedman of the Augusti, and Dubitata, [---]
EDCS-28400660.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely commemorates a freedman of the emperors and a woman named Dubitata, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400661.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Iulio Euchari familia consularis
EDCS-28400661.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Gaius Iulius Eucharus, of a consular family.
EDCS-28400661.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Eucharus, who belonged to a consular family, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400663.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aureliae Donatae vixit nnos LV Marcus Ulpius Marteialis sic veteranus et decurio coloniae Sarmizegetusae metropolis coniugi rarissimae
EDCS-28400663.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Aurelia Donata, who lived 55 years. Marcus Ulpius Martialis, veteran and decurion of the colony of Sarmizegetusa, metropolis, [placed this] for his most rare wife.
EDCS-28400663.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Marcus Ulpius Martialis, a veteran and decurion, for his wife Aurelia Donata, in Sarmizegetusa, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400665.text_edition: Marcus Iulius Papiria Iustus decurio coloniae ob honorem pontificatus campum cum suis aditibus clusit sic et statuam posuit
EDCS-28400665.translation: Marcus Iulius Iustus, of the Papirian tribe, decurion of the colony, in honor of his pontificate, enclosed the campus with its approaches and also erected a statue.
EDCS-28400665.summary: This inscription records that Marcus Iulius Iustus, a decurion, funded the enclosure of a public space and erected a statue to celebrate his tenure as pontifex, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400666.text_edition: Dis Manibus   Pomponiae Severae filiae annorumet Anclareniae Marcellinae privignae annorum XVIII Marcus Pomponius Severus coniugi carissimae cum filiis
EDCS-28400666.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Pomponia Severa, his daughter, [---] years old, and Anclarenia Marcellina, his stepdaughter, 18 years old. Marcus Pomponius Severus [placed this] for his dearest wife, with his sons.
EDCS-28400666.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Marcus Pomponius Severus, along with his sons, to his deceased daughter Pomponia Severa and his stepdaughter Anclarenia Marcellina, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400667.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Suronio Adrasto Augustali coloniae vixit annos L et Serviliae Primitivae coniugi vixit annos XL Adrastus Marcus Titianus LII
EDCS-28400667.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Marcus Suronius Adrastus, Augustalis of the colony, who lived 50 years, and for Servilia Primitiva, his wife, who lived 40 years. Adrastus Marcus Titianus, [lived] 52 [years].
EDCS-28400667.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Suronius Adrastus, an Augustalis, his wife Servilia Primitiva, and Adrastus Marcus Titianus, likely a relative, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400669.text_edition: Ave viator Dis Manibus Antoniae F SEC
EDCS-28400669.translation: Hail, traveler! To the Spirits of the Dead of Antonia, daughter of F. SEC.
EDCS-28400669.summary: This is a funerary inscription likely for a woman named Antonia, dedicated by an unknown person to the Spirits of the Dead.

EDCS-28400670.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelio Volusiano TVMIA Catu POM TZI O vixit annos XVI cum pietate sua  ECIA CATES Timocrates Augusti libertus et Aurelia Ireni mater filio posuerunt
EDCS-28400670.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead of Aurelius Volusianus, who lived 16 years with his devotion. Timocrates, freedman of Augustus, and Aurelia Ireni, his mother, placed this for their son.
EDCS-28400670.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Timocrates, a freedman of Augustus, and Aurelia Ireni, for their son Aurelius Volusianus, who died at the age of 16.

EDCS-28400671.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ulpiae Dubitatae vixit nnos LX et Ulpio Gemellino vixit nnos LX parentibus et Iustiae Ingenuae vixit annos XXX coniugi et Iustiae Sextiliae filiae Ulpii Dubitatus et Gemellinus
EDCS-28400671.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead of Ulpia Dubitata, who lived 60 years, and Ulpius Gemellinus, who lived 60 years, for their parents; and to Iustia Ingenua, who lived 30 years, for his wife; and to Iustia Sextilia, daughter of Ulpius Dubitatus and Gemellinus.
EDCS-28400671.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription dedicated to Ulpia Dubitata and Ulpius Gemellinus (likely parents), Iustia Ingenua (a wife), and Iustia Sextilia (their daughter), indicating a family burial plot.

EDCS-28400673.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning a flamen (priest) and part of a name, Valerius, making its full purpose unclear.

EDCS-28400674.text_edition: vixit nnos XVIII Ulpius C  coniugi
EDCS-28400674.translation: lived 18 years. Ulpius C. for his wife.
EDCS-28400674.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Ulpius C. for his wife, who lived 18 years.

EDCS-28400678.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Iulius Valentinus flamen municipii Tibiscensium pro salutem sic suam sic suorumque omnium contubernalium votum libens merito posuit
EDCS-28400678.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus. Julius Valentinus, flamen of the municipium of Tibiscum, willingly and deservedly placed this vow for his own welfare and that of all his comrades.
EDCS-28400678.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus by Julius Valentinus, a flamen of Tibiscum, for his and his comrades' well-being, indicating a religious dedication by a public official.

EDCS-28400679.text_edition: Dis MManibus Aelius Guras Iiddei optio ex numero Palmurenorum sic vixit nnos XXXXII militavit annos XXI Aelius Habibis pontifex et heres bene merenti posuit
EDCS-28400679.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Aelius Guras Iiddei, optio from the number of the Palmureni, lived 42 years and served 21 years as a soldier. Aelius Habibis, pontifex and heir, placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-28400679.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aelius Guras Iiddei, a military optio, dedicated by Aelius Habibis, a pontifex and his heir, commemorating his life and military service.

EDCS-28400682.text_edition: Dis Manibus MAR et heres mearitis suis MARTI I BIAIV vixit annos XXXII menses II rissimo bene merenti posuit
EDCS-28400682.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead of Mar and his heirs. His deserving Martius BIAIV lived 32 years, 2 months, the most deserving, placed this.
EDCS-28400682.summary: This is a funerary inscription to the Spirits of the Dead, likely for someone named Mar, placed by an heir for a very deserving individual named Martius BIAIV who lived 32 years and 2 months.

EDCS-28400683.text_edition: Aurelio Aciliano  et  HE FIL NIN vixit annos XVIIII
EDCS-28400683.translation: To Aurelius Acilianus and HE FIL NIN, who lived 19 years.
EDCS-28400683.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to Aurelius Acilianus and another individual whose name is partially preserved, noting that the latter lived 19 years.

EDCS-28400684.text_edition: Decimus Baebatius Chrysantus Augustalis coloniae Zermicegetusae sic vixit nnos LXVI hic situs est proscriptus per Baebatiam Gamicen coniugi et CO et comheredibus IIII
EDCS-28400684.translation: Decimus Baebatius Chrysantus, Augustalis of the colony of Zermicegetusa, lived 66 years. He lies here, inscribed by Baebatia Gamicen, his wife, and four co-heirs.
EDCS-28400684.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Decimus Baebatius Chrysantus, an Augustalis of the colony of Zermicegetusa, who lived 66 years, placed by his wife Baebatia Gamicen and four co-heirs.

EDCS-28400685.text_edition: Aeliae Lepidae vixit annis LXX Aelius Abascantus coniugi pientissimae posuit PVMIA
EDCS-28400685.translation: To Aelia Lepida, who lived 70 years. Aelius Abascantus placed this for his most devoted wife. PVMIA.
EDCS-28400685.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Aelius Abascantus dedicated to his wife Aelia Lepida, recording her age at death.

EDCS-28400686.text_edition: Dianae Augustae aram Lucius Samognatius Tertius Trever votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28400686.translation: To Diana Augusta, Lucius Samognatius Tertius, a Trever, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow with an altar.
EDCS-28400686.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an altar to Diana Augusta by Lucius Samognatius Tertius, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-28400687.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Sam(ognatius Tertius ex viso votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28400687.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Lucius Samognatius Tertius willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow as a result of a vision.
EDCS-28400687.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Lucius Samognatius Tertius, made in fulfillment of a vow inspired by a vision.

EDCS-28400688.text_edition: Matri Deum Magnae Abuccia Claudiana votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28400688.translation: To the Great Mother of the Gods, Abuccia Claudiana willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-28400688.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Abuccia Claudiana to the Magna Mater, the Great Mother of the Gods.

EDCS-28400689.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Hadrian, grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-grandson of the deified Nerva, Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus.
EDCS-28400689.summary: This is an imperial dedication, likely part of a larger monument, honoring Emperor Antoninus Pius, tracing his imperial lineage.

EDCS-28400690.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caius Quintinius Victor Iunior vixit annis VIII posuit Caius Quintinius Victor pater filio caro
EDCS-28400690.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Caius Quintinius Victor Junior lived 8 years. Caius Quintinius Victor, his father, placed this for his dear son.
EDCS-28400690.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Caius Quintinius Victor, dedicated to his young son, Caius Quintinius Victor Junior, who died at the age of eight.

EDCS-28400692.text_edition: Turmasgada Maximus Maximinus et Iulianus Maximinus ex voto posuerunt
EDCS-28400692.translation: Turmasgada Maximus Maximinus and Julianus Maximinus placed this in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-28400692.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Turmasgada Maximus Maximinus and Julianus Maximinus, likely to a deity or for a specific purpose.

EDCS-28400694.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Iulius Philippus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus tribunicia potestate V cons(ul) III pater patriae proconsul et Marcus Iulius Philippus iunior imperator cons(ul) II proconsul princeps iuventutis pater patriae Pius Felix Augustus et Marcia Otacilia Severa sanctissima Augusta nostra restitutores orbis totius ob tutelam civitatis coloniae suae Romulae circitum muri manu militari a solo fecerunt
EDCS-28400694.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, with tribunician power for the fifth time, consul for the third time, father of the fatherland, proconsul, and Marcus Julius Philippus Junior, emperor, consul for the second time, proconsul, prince of youth, father of the fatherland, Pius Felix Augustus, and Marcia Otacilia Severa, our most sacred Augusta, restorers of the entire world, for the protection of their colony Romula's city, built the circuit of the wall from the ground up with military force.
EDCS-28400694.summary: This monumental inscription records the construction of city walls for the colony of Romula by Emperors Philip the Arab and his son Philip II, and Empress Otacilia Severa, highlighting their roles as 'restorers of the world' and protectors of the city, likely dating to 247-248 CE.

EDCS-28400695.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelius Germanus veteranus vixit annis LXX Aelius Germanus decurio municipii fratri bene merenti posuit
EDCS-28400695.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aelius Germanus, a veteran, lived 70 years. Aelius Germanus, a decurion of the municipality, placed this for his well-deserving brother.
EDCS-28400695.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aelius Germanus, a decurion, to his brother, Aelius Germanus, a veteran who lived to 70 years.

EDCS-28400696.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelio Valeriano veterano ex duplicario vixit annos LX et Aelio Titiano veterano vixit annos LXX et Aelio Valeriano filio vixit annos L Aelius Candidinus tesserarius legionis XIII geminae et Aelius Veteranus filii posuerunt
EDCS-28400696.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Aelius Valerianus, a veteran formerly a duplicarius, who lived 60 years; and to Aelius Titianus, a veteran, who lived 70 years; and to Aelius Valerianus, his son, who lived 50 years. Aelius Candidinus, a tesserarius of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, and Aelius Veteranus, their sons, placed this.
EDCS-28400696.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aelius Candidinus and Aelius Veteranus to their deceased relatives: Aelius Valerianus (a veteran and former duplicarius), Aelius Titianus (a veteran), and Aelius Valerianus (son of the first-named Aelius Valerianus).

EDCS-28400698.text_edition: Dis Manibus Titus Patulcius Severus vixit annos
EDCS-28400698.translation: To the Divine Manes. Titus Patulcius Severus lived for years.
EDCS-28400698.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Patulcius Severus, with his age missing.

EDCS-28400699.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelius Valens qui et Esbenus vixit annos LXXX Sira coniux et Fortunatus libertus patrono bene merenti faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-28400699.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aelius Valens, also known as Esbenus, lived for 80 years. His wife Sira and his freedman Fortunatus took care of making this for their well-deserving patron.
EDCS-28400699.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by his wife Sira and freedman Fortunatus to Aelius Valens, also known as Esbenus, who lived for 80 years.

EDCS-28400704.text_edition: legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis Philippianae |(centuriae) VIIII hastati prioris signifer Aurelius Probus optio Publius Aelius Victorinus
EDCS-28400704.translation: Of the Seventh Legion Claudia, Pious and Faithful, Philippiana. From the ninth century of the 'hastatus prior', the standard-bearer Aurelius Probus, and the optio Publius Aelius Victorinus.
EDCS-28400704.summary: This inscription lists two soldiers, Aurelius Probus (standard-bearer) and Publius Aelius Victorinus (optio), from the 9th centuria of the 'hastatus prior' in the Legio VII Claudia Pia Fidelis Philippiana.

EDCS-28400705.text_edition: |(centurio) Ferox militavit annos XXV eques annos XVIII vixit annos VIIL Marcus Pompeius Proculus frater beneficiarius Tironis legati heres bene merenti posuit
EDCS-28400705.translation: The centurion Ferox served for 25 years, as a cavalryman for 18 years; he lived for 7 years. His brother Marcus Pompeius Proculus, a 'beneficiarius' of the legate Tiro, as heir, set this up for the well-deserving.
EDCS-28400705.summary: This is a funerary inscription for centurion Ferox, who served for 25 years and died at the age of 7, erected by his brother Marcus Pompeius Proculus, a 'beneficiarius' of the legate Tiro.

EDCS-28400718.text_edition: Imperatore Caesare Caio Vivio sic Traboniano sic Gallo Pio Felici Augusto patre patriae et Imperatore Caesare Caio Vivio sic Afinio Gallo Veldominiano sic Volusiano Augusto patre patriae ab Apulo milia passuum XLV
EDCS-28400718.translation: Under Imperator Caesar Gaius Vivius Trabonianus Gallus, Pious, Felix, Augustus, father of his country, and Imperator Caesar Gaius Vivius Afinius Gallus Veldominianus Volusianus, Augustus, father of his country, 45 miles from Apulum.
EDCS-28400718.summary: This is a milestone marking 45 miles from Apulum, dated to the reign of Emperors Trebonianus Gallus and Volusianus (251-253 CE).

EDCS-28400721.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  DoVlicheno#Dolicheno#DVLCENO Publius Caius Valerianus |(centurio) legionis X fEreAtensis#fretensis#ERATENSIS RESRNERIDONIS votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28400721.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, Publius Gaius Valerianus, centurion of the Tenth Legion Fretensis, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28400721.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, dedicated by Publius Gaius Valerianus, a centurion of the Legio X Fretensis, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-28400722.text_edition: Publius Aelius Paulinus templum instituit pro se suorumque salute Genio sancto scholae decurionum
EDCS-28400722.translation: Publius Aelius Paulinus established this temple for himself and for the safety of his family, to the sacred Genius of the college of decurions.
EDCS-28400722.summary: Publius Aelius Paulinus established a temple dedicated to the sacred Genius of the college of decurions, for his own and his family's well-being.

EDCS-28400723.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Iunoni RegCinae#Reginae#RCINAE Valerius Avitus veteranus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28400723.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Queen Juno, Valerius Avitus, a veteran, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28400723.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno Regina, dedicated by the veteran Valerius Avitus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-28400724.text_edition: Silvano Domestico Aul(us Cutius Secundanus pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28400724.translation: To Silvanus Domesticus, Aulus Cutius Secundanus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for himself and his family.
EDCS-28400724.summary: This is a votive inscription to Silvanus Domesticus, dedicated by Aulus Cutius Secundanus for himself and his family, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-28400725.text_edition: Gaius Iulius V stipendiorum XV stipendiorum XV et Iulius
EDCS-28400725.translation: Gaius Julius V, of 15 years of service, and Julius.
EDCS-28400725.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Gaius Julius who served for 15 years, and another individual named Julius.

EDCS-28400728.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Blasa eques stipendiorum  Aureli-- Tsinta vixit XXXI Aureli-- Aeli
EDCS-28400728.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Blasa eques, of Aurelius Tsinta's stipends, lived 31 years. Aurelius Aelius [set this up].
EDCS-28400728.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Blasa, possibly a female 'eques' or horse-rider, who lived 31 years, dedicated by Aurelius Aelius.

EDCS-28400730.text_edition: pro sal(ute) Cai Iuli Castini legati Augusti pro praetore Lucius Antonius Marinianus praefectus cohortis V Lingonum Antoninianae cum Lucio Antonio Marino filio suo Iulio Antonino fratre ex tribuno
EDCS-28400730.translation: For the well-being of Gaius Iulius Castinus, imperial legate with propraetorian power, Lucius Antonius Marinianus, prefect of the Fifth Cohort of Lingones, Antoniniana, with his son Lucius Antonius Marinus, and his brother Iulius Antoninus, formerly a tribune [set this up].
EDCS-28400730.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Lucius Antonius Marinianus, a prefect, along with his son and brother, for the well-being of Gaius Iulius Castinus, an imperial legate, likely dating to the Antonine period.

EDCS-28400731.text_edition: Dis Manibus Eustinae Afri vixit XXX Aurelius Iustinus |(centurio bene merenti posuit
EDCS-28400731.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Eustina Afri, who lived 30 years. Aurelius Iustinus, a centurion, set this up for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-28400731.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelius Iustinus, a centurion, to his well-deserving wife, Eustina Afri, who lived 30 years.

EDCS-28400734.text_edition: Dis Manibus Antonio Eroti patri vixit annos LIIIIVIII posuerunt Antonia Iulia et Antonius Eros filii bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-28400734.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Antonius Eros, my father, who lived 58 years. Antonia Iulia and Antonius Eros, his children, set this up for their well-deserving father.
EDCS-28400734.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Antonia Iulia and Antonius Eros to their father, Antonius Eros, who lived 58 years.

EDCS-28400735.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aureli Masinni duplicari alae |(miliariae) vixit annis XXXVI Iulia Severa coniux et Flavius Saturninus heres ob merita posuerunt faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-28400735.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Aurelius Masinnus, a duplicarius of a cavalry ala millitaria, who lived 36 years. Iulia Severa, his wife, and Flavius Saturninus, his heir, set this up because of his merits and took care of its making.
EDCS-28400735.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Masinnus, a cavalry officer (duplicarius), who lived 36 years, dedicated by his wife Iulia Severa and his heir Flavius Saturninus.

EDCS-28400736.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno pro salute ddominorum nnostrorum Marci Aureli Antonini Pii Augusti nostri Publius Iulius Firminus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28400736.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, for the well-being of our lords, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus, our emperor. Publius Iulius Firminus, a consular beneficiarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28400736.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus by Publius Iulius Firminus, a consular beneficiarius, for the well-being of the emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus.

EDCS-28400741.text_edition: Bono Puero Firminius Bellicus vetI(eranus)#vet(eranus)#VEI ex custode a(rmorum) votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28400741.translation: To the Good Boy. Firminius Bellicus, a veteran and former custos armorum, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28400741.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Bonus Puer by Firminius Bellicus, a veteran who served as a custos armorum.

EDCS-28400742.text_edition: Dis Manibus Quintus Cominius Pius vixit annum I et dies IIII PPVD ES  pater
EDCS-28400742.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Quintus Cominius Pius lived 1 year and 4 days. His father [set this up].
EDCS-28400742.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Cominius Pius, a child who lived just over a year, set up by his father.

EDCS-28400744.text_edition: Dianae Reginae sacrum pro salute et incolumitate Publi D Nepotiani praefecti cohortis IIII Hispanorum domo Roma et Gestiae Valentinae eius
EDCS-28400744.translation: Sacred to Diana Regina. For the health and safety of Publius D. Nepotianus, prefect of the Fourth Cohort of Spaniards, from Rome, and of Gestia Valentina, his wife.
EDCS-28400744.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Diana Regina for the health and safety of Publius D. Nepotianus, a cohort prefect from Rome, and his wife, Gestia Valentina.

EDCS-28400745.summary: This is a dedication to Dis Pater and Proserpina, likely part of a larger funerary or religious monument.

EDCS-28400746.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Cai Iuli Constantis pontificis Caius Iulius Marcus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28400746.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, for the well-being of Gaius Julius Constans, priest. Gaius Julius Marcus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28400746.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to Jupiter Best and Greatest, dedicated by Gaius Julius Marcus for the health of Gaius Julius Constans, a priest.

EDCS-28400747.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno pro salute Aeli Lycini procuratoris Auggustorum
EDCS-28400747.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest Dolichenus, for the well-being of Aelius Lycinus, procurator of the emperors.
EDCS-28400747.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Jupiter Dolichenus, made on behalf of Aelius Lycinus, a procurator of the emperors.

EDCS-28400748.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Ulpii Paternus et Iustinus
EDCS-28400748.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest Dolichenus, Ulpius Paternus and Iustinus (dedicate this).
EDCS-28400748.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter Dolichenus by Ulpius Paternus and Iustinus.

EDCS-28400749.text_edition: Deo Soli Invicto pro salute sua et suorum Marcus Cocceius Genialis vir egregius procurator Auggustorum nnostrorum provinciae Daciae Porolissensis votum libens merito posuit
EDCS-28400749.translation: To the Unconquered Sun God, for his own well-being and that of his family, Marcus Cocceius Genialis, an excellent man, procurator of our emperors in the province of Dacia Porolissensis, willingly and deservedly placed this vow.
EDCS-28400749.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the Unconquered Sun God, dedicated by Marcus Cocceius Genialis, an imperial procurator of Dacia Porolissensis, for himself and his family.

EDCS-28400750.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Victorio GCallicano#Gallicano#CALLICANO vixit annos XX Marcus Victorius Sep INI
EDCS-28400750.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Marcus Victorius Gallicanus, who lived for 20 years. Marcus Victorius Sep[---].
EDCS-28400750.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Victorius Gallicanus, who died at the age of 20, likely erected by a relative named Marcus Victorius.

EDCS-28400751.text_edition: Dis Manibus Victorina vixit annis XVI posuit Quieta mater bene merenti ponendum curavit
EDCS-28400751.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Victorina, who lived for 16 years. Quieta, her mother, placed this, taking care to have it set up for her well-deserving daughter.
EDCS-28400751.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Victorina, who died at 16, dedicated by her mother Quieta.

EDCS-28400752.summary: This fragment appears to be part of an inscription, possibly funerary or dedicatory, involving Marcia Longina and someone named Zoilus.

EDCS-28400754.text_edition: Dianae  Invictae fecit et posuit  Crescens
EDCS-28400754.translation: To Diana the Unconquered. Crescens made and placed this.
EDCS-28400754.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Diana the Unconquered, set up by Crescens.

EDCS-28400755.text_edition: Aelius Gorgias domino et dominae votum posuit
EDCS-28400755.translation: Aelius Gorgias placed a vow for his master and mistress.
