EDCS-28400755.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Aelius Gorgias on behalf of his master and mistress.

EDCS-28400756.text_edition: Iulius Maximianus |(centurio) Genio |(centuriae) votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28400756.translation: Julius Maximianus, a centurion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow to the Genius of his centuria.
EDCS-28400756.summary: This inscription is a votive offering made by Julius Maximianus, a centurion, to the protective spirit (Genius) of his military unit (centuria).

EDCS-28400757.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  Aelius Fronto VNM CV
EDCS-28400757.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Aelius Fronto, willingly, deserving.
EDCS-28400757.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus dedicated by Aelius Fronto, likely a private individual, expressing a fulfilled vow.

EDCS-28400758.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Aelius Mucianus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28400758.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Publius Aelius Mucianus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28400758.summary: This inscription records Publius Aelius Mucianus's fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-28400759.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Aurelius Quietus miles voto posuit
EDCS-28400759.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Aurelius Quietus, a soldier, placed this according to his vow.
EDCS-28400759.summary: This is a votive inscription by Aurelius Quietus, a soldier, dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, indicating a fulfilled vow.

EDCS-28400761.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum ConMcessus#Concessus#COMCESUS votum libens solvit
EDCS-28400761.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Best and Greatest. Concessus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28400761.summary: This inscription is a sacred dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Concessus, marking the willing fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-28400762.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Ulpius Magnus IIIIviralis posuit votum merito
EDCS-28400762.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Marcus Ulpius Magnus, a quattuorvir, deservedly placed this vow.
EDCS-28400762.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marcus Ulpius Magnus, a quattuorvir (a magistrate), indicating a fulfilled vow.

EDCS-28400764.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Balmarcodi et Iunoni arius veteranus legionis
EDCS-28400764.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, to Balmarcodes and to Juno, an army veteran of the legion.
EDCS-28400764.summary: This inscription is a dedication by a veteran of a legion to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Balmarcodes, and Juno, likely seeking divine favor or fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-28400765.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et HercQuli#Herculi#HRQULI Invicto et Libero Patri AurP(elius)#Aur(elius)#AUP Gaius et Aurelius Ingenuus sacerdotes ex voto posuerunt
EDCS-28400765.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, and to Hercules the Unconquered, and to Liber Pater. Aurelius Gaius and Aurelius Ingenuus, priests, placed this by vow.
EDCS-28400765.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication by priests Aurelius Gaius and Aurelius Ingenuus to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Hercules the Unconquered, and Liber Pater, made in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-28400766.translation: By vow. To Liber Pater. Annius Saturninus.
EDCS-28400766.summary: This short inscription is a votive dedication to Liber Pater by Annius Saturninus, made in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-28400767.text_edition: AuYrelius#Aurelius#AYR Timotheus signum Liberi  votum libens meritomerito
EDCS-28400767.translation: Aurelius Timotheus [dedicated] an image of Liber, a vow, willingly, deservedly.
EDCS-28400767.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a statue or image of Liber Pater by Aurelius Timotheus, fulfilling a vow willingly and deservedly.

EDCS-28400768.text_edition: Libero Patri et Liberae Marcus Ulpius Firminus immunis librarius posuit
EDCS-28400768.translation: To Liber Pater and Libera. Marcus Ulpius Firminus, an exempted scribe, placed this.
EDCS-28400768.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Marcus Ulpius Firminus, an exempted scribe, to Liber Pater and Libera.

EDCS-28400769.text_edition: Soli Invicto Mythrae sic Aurelius Victorinus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28400769.translation: To Sol Invictus Mithras, Aurelius Victorinus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28400769.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Aurelius Victorinus to the god Sol Invictus Mithras, expressing the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-28400770.text_edition: Deo Invicto votum posuit Flavius Marcellinus tesserarius
EDCS-28400770.translation: To the Unconquered God, Flavius Marcellinus, a tesserarius, set up this vow.
EDCS-28400770.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Flavius Marcellinus, a tesserarius (ticket-seller or orderly), to an unnamed Unconquered God.

EDCS-28400771.translation: To Holy Victory, Julius Callinicus, a simes (unclear meaning, possibly a variant of semissis, half-share holder, or a name), [set this up].
EDCS-28400771.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the goddess Victoria Sancta by Julius Callinicus, whose title or role 'simes' is uncertain.

EDCS-28701079.text_edition: Dis Manibus Victoriae AIIFR filiae vixit annos XX PATONIA patronus liber libertae bene merenti
EDCS-28701079.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Victoria AIIFR, his daughter, who lived for 20 years. PATONIA, patron, to his freedwoman, a well-deserving individual.
EDCS-28701079.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Victoria AIIFR, who died at age 20, set up by her patron, PATONIA, and identifying her as a freedwoman.

EDCS-28701080.text_edition: numini Asclepii pro se Marcus Aurelius Vitalianus libens merito
EDCS-28701080.translation: To the divinity of Asclepius, for himself, Marcus Aurelius Vitalianus willingly and deservedly [set this up].
EDCS-28701080.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Marcus Aurelius Vitalianus to the divinity of Asclepius for his own well-being.

EDCS-28701081.text_edition: XXXV Aelia Proba vixit annos XI Publius Aelius Ingenuus vixit  Aelius Probus flamenM#flamen#FLAMEM municipii  pientissimis et sibi vixit
EDCS-28701081.translation: Aelia Proba lived 11 years. Publius Aelius Ingenuus lived [---]. Aelius Probus, flamen of the municipality, to his most devoted [children] and for himself, lived [---].
EDCS-28701081.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Aelia Proba and Publius Aelius Ingenuus, set up by Aelius Probus, a municipal flamen, for his pious family members and himself.

EDCS-28701082.text_edition: Iulius Alexander veteranus ex beneficiario consularis et nnius Martinianus |centurio legC(ionis)#leg(ionis)#LEC ex voto posuerunt
EDCS-28701082.translation: Julius Alexander, a veteran, formerly a beneficiarius consularis, and [---]nianus Martinianus, a centurion of the legion, set this up in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-28701082.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Julius Alexander, a veteran and former beneficiarius consularis, and Martinianus, a legionary centurion.

EDCS-29000015.translation: [---]tilianus, soldier of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina Antoniniana, clerk to 'our' consular, and Aelia Paulina and Ulpia Dubitata, children, set this up for their most devoted parents.
EDCS-29000015.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by the children, Aelia Paulina and Ulpia Dubitata, for their parents, one of whom was a soldier of Legio XIII Gemina Antoniniana and a consular clerk.

EDCS-29000016.text_edition: SilvaVno#Silvano#SILVVNO sacrum pro salute Claudi Rufini Myro eiusdem
EDCS-29000016.translation: Sacred to Silvanus, for the health of Claudius Rufinus. Myro, his [slave/freedman].
EDCS-29000016.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the god Silvanus for the well-being of Claudius Rufinus, set up by Myro, likely his slave or freedman.

EDCS-29000021.text_edition: IA LAN vixit annos Aeli-- Alan
EDCS-29000021.translation: IA LAN lived years. Aeli[---] Alan[---].
EDCS-29000021.summary: This appears to be a fragmentary funerary inscription, likely commemorating individuals with names like 'Aelius' and 'Alan', and stating a lifespan.

EDCS-29000024.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulia Philumene vixit annis XXX hic sita est Philetus Iuli Rufini coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-29000024.translation: To the Divine Manes. Julia Philumene lived for 30 years; she lies here. Philetus, husband of Julia Rufina, placed this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-29000024.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Philetus for his wife, Julia Philumene, who lived for 30 years, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-29100046.text_edition: Sabino et Anullino consulibus dis deabusq(ue) immortalibus pro salute domini nostri in honorem nocturnorum Marcus Aurelius Frontonianus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-29100046.translation: In the consulship of Sabinus and Anullinus, to the immortal gods and goddesses for the health of our lord, in honor of the nocturnal ones, Marcus Aurelius Frontonianus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29100046.summary: This inscription records a vow made by Marcus Aurelius Frontonianus to the immortal gods and goddesses for the emperor's health, dated to the consulship of Sabinus and Anullinus (AD 243).

EDCS-29100047.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo M N Lucretianus praefectus alae II Pannoniorum votum solvit libens
EDCS-29100047.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. M. N. Lucretianus, prefect of the Second Pannonian cavalry wing, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29100047.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by M. N. Lucretianus, a prefect of a cavalry unit, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-29100048.text_edition: Invicto Mithrae Surus votum solvit libens
EDCS-29100048.translation: To Invictus Mithras. Surus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29100048.summary: This is a brief dedicatory inscription to the god Mithras, set up by an individual named Surus as a fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-29100051.text_edition: Deanae Augustae Caius Catullinius Adamantus et Caius Catullinius Achilleus
EDCS-29100051.translation: To Diana Augusta. Gaius Catullinius Adamantus and Gaius Catullinius Achilleus.
EDCS-29100051.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Diana Augusta by two individuals, Gaius Catullinius Adamantus and Gaius Catullinius Achilleus.

EDCS-29100052.translation: To Aesculapius and Hygia. Restituta [placed this].
EDCS-29100052.summary: This is a dedication to the healing gods Aesculapius and Hygia, made by a woman named Restituta.

EDCS-29100053.text_edition: IMD N Ulpius Manus An Marcus Ip Septimus Marcus Iulius M Iulius Vinc Iulius P
EDCS-29100053.translation: [---] N. Ulpius Manus An. Marcus Ip. Septimus Marcus Iulius M. Iulius Vinc. Iulius P.
EDCS-29100053.summary: This fragmentary inscription seems to list several Roman names, potentially indicating multiple dedicants or individuals associated with the monument, but its full purpose is unclear due to its incompleteness.

EDCS-29100055.translation: ---ignus placed this for the well-deserving Surilla.
EDCS-29100055.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, likely placed by an individual whose name ends in '-ignus' for Surilla, who is described as well-deserving.

EDCS-29100056.translation: To Silvanus. Rufinus Stennatis gave this gift.
EDCS-29100056.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the god Silvanus, presented as a gift by Rufinus Stennatis.

EDCS-29100057.text_edition: Herculi et Silvano vexillatio legionis XIII geminae Antoninianae Aurelio Arimo votum merito posuit  immuni V I
EDCS-29100057.translation: To Hercules and Silvanus. The vexillation of the Thirteenth Gemina Antoniniana Legion deservedly placed this vow for Aurelius Arimus, immune [from service?], V.I.
EDCS-29100057.summary: This inscription records a vow made to Hercules and Silvanus by a detachment (vexillatio) of the Legio XIII Gemina Antoniniana, placed for Aurelius Arimus, likely an individual with a special military status.

EDCS-29100059.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publius Aelius Ianuarius vixit annos LX et Publia Aelia Dionisia sic vixit annos XXXXV Publius Aelius Germanus vixit annos XXV Publius Aelius Dionisius vixit annos XVIII Publius Aelius Cassianus parentibus bene merentibus posuit
EDCS-29100059.translation: To the Divine Manes. Publius Aelius Ianuarius lived 60 years, and Publia Aelia Dionisia lived 45 years. Publius Aelius Germanus lived 25 years. Publius Aelius Dionisius lived 18 years. Publius Aelius Cassianus placed this for his well-deserving parents.
EDCS-29100059.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the deaths of multiple members of the Aelian family, erected by Publius Aelius Cassianus for his parents and other family members.

EDCS-29100060.text_edition: vixit nnos  annos XV Aulusanus heres bene merenti posuit arbitrante Ingenui
EDCS-29100060.translation: He lived for 15 years. Aulusanus, heir, placed this for the well-deserving, with Ingenuus acting as arbiter.
EDCS-29100060.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an unnamed individual who died at age 15, set up by their heir Aulusanus, with Ingenuus supervising the work.

EDCS-29100061.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Dionysius veteranus cohortis II Flaviae Commagenorum vixit annos Ulpia Demetria et --  faciendum curav---
EDCS-29100061.translation: To the Divine Manes. Dionysius, a veteran of the II Flavian Commagenian cohort, lived for [---] years. Ulpia Demetria and [---] took care of the making.
EDCS-29100061.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Dionysius, a veteran, commissioned by Ulpia Demetria and another unnamed individual.

EDCS-29100063.text_edition: Libero Patri et Liberae Matri Secundinus votum libens merito
EDCS-29100063.translation: To Father Liber and Mother Libera, Secundinus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29100063.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the deities Liber Pater and Libera Mater by Secundinus, indicating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-29100064.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Iulio Philippo Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate II consuli patri patriae proconsuli numerus singularium Britannicorum Philippiani devoti numini maiestatique eius
EDCS-29100064.translation: To the Imperator Caesar Marcus Iulius Philippus Pius Felix Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the second time, consul, father of the fatherland, proconsul: The unit of Britannic Bodyguards, the Philippiani, devoted to his divinity and majesty.
EDCS-29100064.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Emperor Philip the Arab, erected by the *numerus singularium Britannicorum Philippiani* (Britannic Bodyguards of Philip) in approximately 245 AD.

EDCS-29100065.text_edition: Dianae sacrum pro salute Cai Arri Antonini legati Augusti pro praetore Marcus Verius Superstes |(centurio) legionis V Macedonicae praepositus numeri Germanicianorum votum solvit
EDCS-29100065.translation: Sacred to Diana for the well-being of Gaius Arrius Antoninus, Augustan legate pro praetore. Marcus Verius Superstes, centurion of the V Macedonian Legion, commander of the unit of Germans, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29100065.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Diana for the welfare of the provincial governor, Gaius Arrius Antoninus, by Marcus Verius Superstes, a centurion and commander of a Germanic unit.

EDCS-29100066.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iovivipulsori Grattius Paternus decurio coloniae posuit
EDCS-29100066.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Jupiter Vipulsor, Grattius Paternus, decurion of the colony, placed this.
EDCS-29100066.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Jupiter Vipulsor by Grattius Paternus, a decurion of a colony.

EDCS-29100067.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Appennino Marcus Aurelius Valentinus decurio coloniae votum libens solvit merito
EDCS-29100067.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Appenninus, Marcus Aurelius Valentinus, decurion of the colony, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29100067.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Appenninus by Marcus Aurelius Valentinus, a decurion of a colony.

EDCS-29100068.text_edition: Marti Lucius Grattius decurio coloniae posuit
EDCS-29100068.translation: To Mars, Lucius Grattius, decurion of the colony, placed this.
EDCS-29100068.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Mars by Lucius Grattius, a decurion of a colony.

EDCS-29100071.text_edition: Deo Malagbeli Titus Flavius Aper scriba coloniae ex voto
EDCS-29100071.translation: To the god Malagbel, Titus Flavius Aper, scribe of the colony, in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-29100071.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the deity Malagbel by Titus Flavius Aper, a colonial scribe, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-29100072.text_edition: Soli Invicto Mithrae Cornelius Cornelianus ex voto posuit
EDCS-29100072.translation: Cornelius Cornelianus placed this, fulfilling a vow, to Sol Invictus Mithras.
EDCS-29100072.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Cornelius Cornelianus to the god Sol Invictus Mithras, placed in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-29100074.translation: Papiria Zoe placed this, fulfilling a vow.
EDCS-29100074.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Papiria Zoe, placed in fulfillment of a vow, likely to a deity or for a specific purpose which is not explicitly stated.

EDCS-29100078.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publius Aelius Theimes veteranus ex |(centurione) cohortis I Vindelicorum et IIviralis coloniae Dacicae Sarmizegetusae vixit nnos LXXXVIII et Publius Aelius Bericio vixit nnos XV et Publius Aelius Zabdibol vixit nnos XI et Aelia Phamenis vixit an[nos &
EDCS-29100078.translation: To the Divine Manes. Publius Aelius Theimes, a veteran who had been a centurion of the First Cohort of Vindelici and a duumvir of the Dacic colony of Sarmizegetusa, lived 88 years. And Publius Aelius Bericio lived 15 years. And Publius Aelius Zabdibol lived 11 years. And Aelia Phamenis lived [---] years.
EDCS-29100078.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Aelius Theimes, a veteran and former centurion and duumvir, along with Publius Aelius Bericio, Publius Aelius Zabdibol, and Aelia Phamenis, likely erected by surviving family members.

EDCS-29100079.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Lucio Cassio Marcioni Augustali coloniae vixit annos LXX et Cassiae Cassianae vixit annos XVIIII Cassia Rufina coniugi et filiae piissimis
EDCS-29100079.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Lucius Cassius Marcioni, Augustalis of the colony, who lived 70 years, and to Cassia Cassiana, who lived 19 years. Cassia Rufina to her most devoted husband and daughter.
EDCS-29100079.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Cassia Rufina to her deeply cherished husband, Lucius Cassius Marcioni, an Augustalis of the colony, and their daughter, Cassia Cassiana.

EDCS-29100081.text_edition: Caio Valerio Cai filio Papiria Valeriano IIviro coloniae Sarmizegetusae metropolis et decurioni coloniae Apulensis equiti Romano Valerii Niceforus sic et Protus liberti patrono dignissimo locus datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-29100081.translation: To Gaius Valerius Valerianus, son of Gaius, duumvir of the colony of Sarmizegetusa, metropolis and decurion of the colony of Apulum, a Roman knight. Valerii Niceforus, also known as Protus, his freedmen, to their most worthy patron. A place given by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-29100081.summary: This inscription is a dedication by freedmen, Valerii Niceforus (Protus), to their esteemed patron Gaius Valerius Valerianus, a prominent civic official and Roman knight, noting that the plot was granted by a decree of the decurions.

EDCS-29100082.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ulpio Chelidoni vixit annos XLVI Lucius Cassius
EDCS-29100082.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Ulpius Chelidon, who lived 46 years. Lucius Cassius [placed this].
EDCS-29100082.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Ulpius Chelidon, likely erected by Lucius Cassius, his presumed kinsman or associate.

EDCS-29100085.text_edition: Dis Inferis Manibus sacrum Septimius Bitus et Septimia Valentina vixerunt annis LIIII posuerunt Septimius Bassus et Aurelius Valens patribus bene merentibus
EDCS-29100085.translation: Sacred to the Infernal Divine Manes. Septimius Bitus and Septimia Valentina lived 54 years. Septimius Bassus and Aurelius Valens placed this for their well-deserving parents.
EDCS-29100085.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Septimius Bitus and Septimia Valentina, who lived for 54 years, erected by their sons, Septimius Bassus and Aurelius Valens, in honor of their meritorious parents.

EDCS-29100086.translation: To the Divine Manes of Aelius Philippus [---].
EDCS-29100086.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes of Aelius Philippus, with the rest of the text missing.

EDCS-29100116.summary: This is a brief possessive inscription likely marking an object or monument belonging to Theotimus Stephanus.

EDCS-29100117.summary: This is a brief possessive inscription likely marking an object or monument belonging to Attenia Cyttonis.

EDCS-29100118.text_edition: Legionis V Macedonicae piae fidelis pondus I  examinatum iussu dddominorum nostrorum Augustorum Valerius Sabinus  optio legionis supra scriptae dono dedit
EDCS-29100118.translation: Valerius Sabinus, an optio of the aforementioned Fifth Macedonian Legion, gave as a gift, by order of our most divine lords the Augusti, a weight of 1 unit, inspected, belonging to the Fifth Macedonian Legion, Pious and Faithful.
EDCS-29100118.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an inspected weight by Valerius Sabinus, an optio of the Legio V Macedonica, to his imperial patrons.

EDCS-29100435.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Publius Aelius Proculinus beneficiarius consularis legionis V Macedonicae Gordianae agens sub signo Samum cum regione Ansamensium pro salute sua et suorum omnium votum libens merito solvit Arriano et Papo consulibus
EDCS-29100435.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, Publius Aelius Proculinus, a consular beneficiarius of the Legio V Macedonica Gordiana, acting under the standard at Samum with the region of Ansamensium, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for his own safety and that of all his kin, during the consulship of Arrianus and Papus.
EDCS-29100435.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Publius Aelius Proculinus, a beneficiarius of the Legio V Macedonica Gordiana, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus for his and his family's safety, dated to 243 AD.

EDCS-29200184.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Marcus Aelius Antoninus decurio coloniae Napocae iuridicus municipii Porolissensis
EDCS-29200184.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, Marcus Aelius Antoninus, decurion of the colonia Napoca, juridical officer of the municipium Porolissensis.
EDCS-29200184.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus by Marcus Aelius Antoninus, a decurion from Napoca and a legal official from Porolissum.

EDCS-29200185.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Titintius beneficiarius legati votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-29200185.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, Titintius, a beneficiarius of the legate, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29200185.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus by Titintius, a beneficiarius of a legate.

EDCS-29200186.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Probus centurio legionis et Apollonia eius votum solverunt
EDCS-29200186.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, Probus, a centurion of the legion, and Apollonia, his wife, fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-29200186.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus by Probus, a legionary centurion, and his wife Apollonia.

EDCS-29601678.text_edition: Deae Nemesi Reginae Caecilius Antoninus ex voto posuit
EDCS-29601678.translation: To the goddess Nemesis Regina, Caecilius Antoninus placed this in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-29601678.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the goddess Nemesis Regina by Caecilius Antoninus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-29601679.text_edition: Hilarus pro Alexandro fratre deae Nemesi quem periculo liberavit
EDCS-29601679.translation: Hilarus for his brother Alexander, to the goddess Nemesis, whom she freed from danger.
EDCS-29601679.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the goddess Nemesis by Hilarus on behalf of his brother Alexander, who was saved from danger.

EDCS-29601680.text_edition: Nemesi Augustae Titus Varenius Gallicanus decurio collegii fabrorum coloniae Sarmizegetusae metropolis ter magister ex voto posuit
EDCS-29601680.translation: To Nemesis Augusta, Titus Varenius Gallicanus, decurion of the college of craftsmen of the colonia Sarmizegetusa metropolis, thrice master, placed this in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-29601680.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Nemesis Augusta by Titus Varenius Gallicanus, a decurion and thrice master of the college of craftsmen of Colonia Sarmizegetusa, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-29601681.translation: To the god Silvanus, Naevius D[---]
EDCS-29601681.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Silvanus by Naevius, whose full name or role is partially lost.

EDCS-29601684.text_edition: Marcus Procilius Theodorus IIvir coloniae quinquennalis decurio ex voto posuit
EDCS-29601684.translation: Marcus Procilius Theodorus, duumvir of the colonia for a five-year term, decurion, placed this in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-29601684.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Marcus Procilius Theodorus, a quinquennial duumvir and decurion of a colonia, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-29601688.translation: Amabilis willingly and deservedly placed this vow.
EDCS-29601688.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Amabilis, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-29601691.text_edition: Imperatore Caesare divi Traiani Parthici filio divi Nervae nepote Traiano Hadriano Augusto pontifice maximo tribunicia potestate XXIII consule III patre patriae et Imperatore Tito Aelio Caesare Antonino Traiani Hadriani Augusti filio divi Traiani Parthici nepote divi Nervae pronepote tribunicia potestate numerus burgariorum et veredariorum Daciae inferioris sub Flavio Constante procuratore Augusti
EDCS-29601691.translation: In the reign of Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, son of the deified Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the deified Nerva, Pontifex Maximus, in his 23rd tribunician power, consul for the 3rd time, father of the country, and Emperor Titus Aelius Caesar Antoninus, son of Trajan Hadrian Augustus, grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-grandson of the deified Nerva, in his tribunician power, the unit of burgarii and veredarii of Lower Dacia acted under Flavius Constans, procurator of Augustus.
EDCS-29601691.summary: This inscription records an action or construction undertaken by a unit of frontier guards and couriers in Lower Dacia, dated to the reign of Emperor Hadrian and the co-rule of Antoninus Pius, under the procurator Flavius Constans.

EDCS-29601692.text_edition: Imperatore Caesare Tito Aelio Hadriano Antonino Augusto Pio tribunicia potestate III consule III castra numerus burgariorum et veredariorum quod anguste tenderet duplicato valli pede et inpositis turribus ampliavit per Aquilam Fidum procuratorem Augusti
EDCS-29601692.translation: In the reign of Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrian Antoninus Augustus Pius, in his 3rd tribunician power, consul for the 3rd time, he enlarged the camp of the unit of burgarii and veredarii, which was too narrow, by doubling the width of the rampart and adding towers, through Aquila Fidus, procurator of Augustus.
EDCS-29601692.summary: This inscription records the enlargement and fortification of a camp for a unit of frontier guards and couriers, commissioned by Emperor Antoninus Pius through his procurator Aquila Fidus.

EDCS-29601693.text_edition: Nymphis Hylas vicesimarius ex voto posuit
EDCS-29601693.translation: Hylas, a vicesimarius (customs officer), placed this for the Nymphs in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-29601693.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Nymphs by Hylas, a customs officer, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-29900102.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ulpia Claudia vixit annos XXXV Ulpius Festus vixit annos XVIII Aelius Certus signifer cohortis I Batavorum coniugi pientissimae faciendum curavit
EDCS-29900102.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Ulpia Claudia lived 35 years. Ulpius Festus lived 18 years. Aelius Certus, standard-bearer of the First Cohort of Batavians, took care of having this made for his most devoted wife.
EDCS-29900102.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated by Aelius Certus, a standard-bearer, to his most devoted wife Ulpia Claudia and possibly another individual named Ulpius Festus.

EDCS-30100823.text_edition: Minervae Victrici pro salute nnorum nnostrorum Auggustorum Imperatoris Severi et Antonini Caesaris Aelius Sabinus librarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-30100823.translation: To Minerva Victrix, for the welfare of our Augusti, Emperor Severus and Caesar Antoninus, Aelius Sabinus, consular clerk, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30100823.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Minerva Victrix by Aelius Sabinus, a consular clerk, for the welfare of Emperors Septimius Severus and Caracalla.

EDCS-30100824.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelius Borafas Zabdiboli miles ex numero Palmyrenorum vixit annos et Valeria C et Zabdibolus  FNRVM FNVFR
EDCS-30100824.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aelius Borafas, son of Zabdibol, a soldier from the Palmyrene unit, lived [X] years, and Valeria C. and Zabdibol [illegible].
EDCS-30100824.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aelius Borafas, a Palmyrene soldier, possibly set up by Valeria and Zabdibol.

EDCS-30100826.text_edition: Dis Manibus Liccaius Vinentis miles cohortis III campestris beneficiarius tribuni militavit annis XIX vixit annis XL Linda Severus heredes bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-30100826.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Liccaius Vinentis, a soldier of the Third Campestrian Cohort, beneficiarius of the tribune, served 19 years and lived 40 years. Linda Severus, his heirs, placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-30100826.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated by his heirs, Linda Severus, to Liccaius Vinentis, a soldier and beneficiarius, who served for 19 years.

EDCS-30100827.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcus Valerius Marci filius Longinus medicus legionis VII Claudiae ornatus ornamentis decurionalibus a splendidissimo ordine municipii Hadriani Drobetensium vixit annos XXIII Marcus Victorius anio et Victoria Gemina filio pientissimo posuerunt
EDCS-30100827.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Valerius Longinus, son of Marcus, a physician of the Seventh Claudian Legion, honored with decurional ornaments by the most splendid order of the municipium of Hadriani Drobetensium, lived 23 years. Marcus Victorius Anio and Victoria Gemina placed this for their most devoted son.
EDCS-30100827.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated by Marcus Victorius Anio and Victoria Gemina to their most devoted son, Marcus Valerius Longinus, a legionary physician who received decurional honors.

EDCS-30100844.text_edition: Lucius Marius Valens patronus collegii fabrum
EDCS-30100844.translation: Lucius Marius Valens, patron of the college of craftsmen.
EDCS-30100844.summary: This is an inscription identifying Lucius Marius Valens as the patron of a college (guild) of craftsmen.

EDCS-30100845.summary: This inscription is a dedication to an individual named Marcus Procilius Niceta.

EDCS-30100846.summary: This is a simple name inscription, likely from a tombstone or a personal item, identifying Aelius Vitalis.

EDCS-30100852.text_edition: Nemesi sacrum Nepos centurio numeri Palmyrenorum
EDCS-30100852.translation: Sacred to Nemesis. Nepos, centurion of the unit of Palmyrenes.
EDCS-30100852.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the goddess Nemesis by Nepos, a centurion of the Numerus Palmyrenorum.

EDCS-30100854.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio Daciarum Marcus Caelius Iulianus tribunus laticlavius legionis XIII geminae donodono
EDCS-30100854.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of the Dacias, Marcus Caelius Iulianus, tribunus laticlavius of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, gave this gift.
EDCS-30100854.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the protecting spirit of the Dacias by Marcus Caelius Iulianus, a senior tribune of the Legio XIII Gemina.

EDCS-30100856.summary: This inscription records that Theotinus was the maker of the object it adorns.

EDCS-30100859.text_edition: Mercurio Plator Implei sic votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-30100859.translation: To Mercury. Plator Implei willingly, happily, and deservedly fulfilled his vow as requested.
EDCS-30100859.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to Mercury by Plator Implei, indicating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-30100860.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Platius.
EDCS-30100860.summary: This inscription is a sacred dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by an individual named Platius.

EDCS-30100861.text_edition: Platius Turi ITovi#Iovi#TOVI Depulsorio votum solvit
EDCS-30100861.translation: Platius Turi fulfilled his vow to Jupiter the Averter.
EDCS-30100861.summary: This inscription records that Platius Turi fulfilled a vow to Jupiter Depulsor, or Jupiter the Averter, likely for averting some danger.

EDCS-30100862.text_edition: Beucus Daeici aram Veneri votum solvit
EDCS-30100862.translation: Beucus, son of Daeicus, fulfilled his vow to Venus with an altar.
EDCS-30100862.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Beucus, son of Daeicus, who dedicated an altar to Venus in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-30100864.text_edition: Mercurio sacrum Verzo Platoris posuit donum dedit
EDCS-30100864.translation: Sacred to Mercury. Verzo, son of Plator, placed this gift.
EDCS-30100864.summary: This inscription records a dedication and gift to Mercury by Verzo, son of Plator.

EDCS-30100865.text_edition: Apollini sacrum Plator Implai donum dedit
EDCS-30100865.translation: Sacred to Apollo. Plator, son of Impla, gave this gift.
EDCS-30100865.summary: This is a votive inscription recording a gift given to Apollo by Plator, son of Impla.

EDCS-30100866.translation: Platius, son of Dasas, gave this gift to the Genius of the people of Sardia.
EDCS-30100866.summary: This is a votive inscription recording a gift given by Platius, son of Dasas, to the protective spirit (Genius) of the community of Sardia.

EDCS-30100867.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dasas Loni collegii Sardiatensium donum dedit
EDCS-30100867.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Dasas, son of Loni, from the collegium of Sardia, gave this gift.
EDCS-30100867.summary: This is a votive inscription recording a gift given to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Dasas, son of Loni, a member of the collegium of Sardia.

EDCS-30100870.text_edition: Aelius Quintus Dii Genio collegi(i) SardiatIarum#Sardiatarum#SARDAIARUM
EDCS-30100870.translation: Aelius Quintus [dedicated this] to the divine Genius of the collegium of Sardia.
EDCS-30100870.summary: This is a dedication by Aelius Quintus to the divine protective spirit (Genius) of the collegium of Sardia.

EDCS-30100871.text_edition: Fortunae Augustae sacrum Plator Sar votum solvit libens
EDCS-30100871.translation: Sacred to Fortuna Augusta. Plator, son of Sar, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30100871.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Plator, son of Sar, willingly fulfilling a vow to Fortuna Augusta.

EDCS-30100872.text_edition: Asclepio Marcus Ulpius Clemens et Aelius Mes ex voto posuerunt
EDCS-30100872.translation: To Asclepius. Marcus Ulpius Clemens and Aelius Mes placed this in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-30100872.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Asclepius by Marcus Ulpius Clemens and Aelius Mes, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-30100873.text_edition: Mercurio sacrum IRAT Beucus Sutti votum libens solvit
EDCS-30100873.translation: Sacred to Mercury. Iratus, son of Beucus Sutti, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30100873.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Iratus, son of Beucus Sutti, willingly fulfilling a vow to Mercury.

EDCS-30100874.text_edition: Apollini sacrum Verso Dasantis qui Veidavius votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-30100874.translation: Sacred to Apollo. Verso, son of Dasas, who is also Veidavius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30100874.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Verso, son of Dasas (also known as Veidavius), willingly and deservedly fulfilling a vow to Apollo.

EDCS-30100875.text_edition: Silvano ex voto Dexter et Martialis fratreIs#fratres#FRATRIS
EDCS-30100875.translation: To Silvanus, in accordance with a vow. Dexter and Martialis, brothers [placed this].
EDCS-30100875.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Silvanus by the brothers Dexter and Martialis, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-30100876.text_edition: Libero et Liberae ex voto Beucus Dasantis
EDCS-30100876.translation: To Liber and Libera, in accordance with a vow. Beucus, son of Dasas [placed this].
EDCS-30100876.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Liber and Libera by Beucus, son of Dasas, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-30100877.text_edition: Terrae Matri ex voto Dasius Sta qui et Durius
EDCS-30100877.translation: To Mother Earth, in fulfillment of a vow, Dasius Sta, also known as Durius.
EDCS-30100877.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Terra Mater made by Dasius Sta (also known as Durius) to fulfill a vow, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-30100878.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo ex voto Panes Stagilis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-30100878.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, in fulfillment of a vow, Panes Stagilis willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-30100878.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Panes Stagilis, who willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-30100880.text_edition: Apto Delmatarum sacrum Purtus et Germanus votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-30100880.translation: Sacred to Aptus of the Dalmatians. Purtus and Germanus willingly and deservedly paid their vow.
EDCS-30100880.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the local deity Aptus of the Dalmatians, made by Purtus and Germanus in fulfillment of a vow, probably from the imperial period.

EDCS-30100881.text_edition: Apollini Piruneno sacrum Macrianus Surionis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-30100881.translation: Sacred to Apollo Pirunenus. Macrianus son of Surio willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-30100881.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the deity Apollo Pirunenus by Macrianus, son of Surio, in fulfillment of a vow, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-30100882.text_edition: Dibus Artavis Bato Secundi votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-30100882.translation: To the divine Artavis. Bato, son of Secundus, willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-30100882.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the deities Artavis by Bato, son of Secundus, who willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-30100883.text_edition: Ianus Dasas Verzonis et Nevato Implai
EDCS-30100883.translation: Ianus Dasas son of Verzo and Nevato Implai.
EDCS-30100883.summary: This inscription, likely a funerary or dedicatory monument, records the names Ianus Dasas, son of Verzo, and Nevato Implai, without further explicit details of its purpose or date.

EDCS-30100884.text_edition: Iano Gemino Lonius Tizius Celsi votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-30100884.translation: To Janus Geminus. Lonius Tizius, son of Celsus, willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-30100884.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Janus Geminus by Lonius Tizius, son of Celsus, who willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-30100885.text_edition: Libero Patri Suttis Panentis filius votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-30100885.translation: To Father Liber. Suttis, son of Panens, willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-30100885.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Father Liber by Suttis, son of Panens, who willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-30100887.text_edition: Nimphis sic Aelius Mes collegii Sardiatensium votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-30100887.translation: To the Nymphs, thus Aelius Mes of the Sardiatensian college willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-30100887.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Nymphs by Aelius Mes, a member of the Sardiatensian college, who willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-30100888.text_edition: Terrae Matri sacrum Siurioio Sumeletis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-30100888.translation: Sacred to Mother Earth. Siurioio, son of Sumeletis, willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-30100888.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Terra Mater by Siurioio, son of Sumeletis, who willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-30100889.text_edition: Dianae Augustae sacrum pro salute et incolumitate Marci Aureli Antonini Pii Augusti et Iuliae Augustae matris Augusti castrorum senatus ac patriae Caius Valerius Valentinus tribunus legionis XIIII geminae Antoninianae  item tribunus numeri singularium Brittannicianorum
EDCS-30100889.translation: Sacred to Diana Augusta, for the safety and well-being of Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus and Julia Augusta, mother of the Augustus, of the camps, of the Senate, and of the fatherland, Gaius Valerius Valentinus, tribune of the Fourteenth Legion Gemina Antoniniana, likewise tribune of the detachment of Britannician household troops [numerus singularium Britannicianorum].
EDCS-30100889.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Diana Augusta by a military tribune, Gaius Valerius Valentinus, for the welfare of Emperor Caracalla and Julia Domna, likely dating to Caracalla's reign (c. 212-217 AD).

EDCS-30200690.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelia Plotina annis vixit XXXXXX Aurelius Saturninus mileIs#miles#MILIS legionis
EDCS-30200690.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelia Plotina lived 60 years. Aurelius Saturninus, a soldier of the legion.
EDCS-30200690.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Aurelia Plotina, dedicated by Aurelius Saturninus, a legionary soldier, likely her husband or a family member.

EDCS-30200692.text_edition: Deo Apollini Augusto Caius Iulius Lysias |(centurio) legionis XIII gCeminae#geminae#CEM Severianae iussu numinis eius porticum pecunia sua fecit
EDCS-30200692.translation: To the August god Apollo. Gaius Julius Lysias, a centurion of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina Severiana, by command of his divinity, built this portico at his own expense.
EDCS-30200692.summary: This inscription records the construction of a portico dedicated to Apollo Augustus by Gaius Julius Lysias, a centurion, undertaken by divine command, likely during the reign of Septimius Severus or his successors (early 3rd century AD).

EDCS-30200694.text_edition: Badonibus Reginis Sextia Augustina ex voto
EDCS-30200694.translation: To the Queens of the Baths, Sextia Augustina, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-30200694.summary: This is a votive dedication by Sextia Augustina to a local deity or personification, 'The Queens of the Baths,' possibly associated with healing springs or bathhouses.

EDCS-30200695.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iulius Chrysampelus voto libens posuit
EDCS-30200695.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Julius Chrysampelus willingly placed this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-30200695.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Julius Chrysampelus in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-30200696.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Octavia Digna pro salute suis et suorum votum libens solvit merito
EDCS-30200696.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Octavia Digna willingly and deservedly paid her vow for her own well-being and that of her family.
EDCS-30200696.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Octavia Digna for the health and well-being of herself and her family.

EDCS-30200697.text_edition: Nemesi deae Terentius Marcianus beneficiarius templum a novo fecit ex viso votum solvit
EDCS-30200697.translation: To the goddess Nemesis. Terentius Marcianus, a beneficiarius, built this temple anew from a vision; he paid his vow.
EDCS-30200697.summary: This inscription records the renovation or reconstruction of a temple to the goddess Nemesis by Terentius Marcianus, a beneficiarius, in fulfillment of a vow made after a vision.

EDCS-30200699.text_edition: Lucius Aurelius Marinus veteranus legionis XIII geminae Antoninianae pro salute sua suorumque ex visu posuit
EDCS-30200699.translation: Lucius Aurelius Marinus, a veteran of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina Antoniniana, placed this for his own well-being and that of his family, based on a vision.
EDCS-30200699.summary: This is a votive dedication by Lucius Aurelius Marinus, a veteran, for his own and his family's welfare, made in response to a vision, likely from the early 3rd century AD during Caracalla's reign.

EDCS-30200700.text_edition: LIVI hastatus legionis XIII geminae et Marcus Ulpius Restitutus |(centurio) exercitator equitum votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-30200700.translation: LIVIUS, a hastatus of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, and Marcus Ulpius Restitutus, a centurion and cavalry trainer, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vows.
EDCS-30200700.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of vows by two soldiers, Livius, a hastatus, and Marcus Ulpius Restitutus, a centurion and cavalry instructor.

EDCS-30200704.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus maximus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VIIII imperator XI et Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus tribunicia potestate III muros caespiticios castrorum cohortis I Aureliae Brittonum |(miliariae) Antoninianae vetustate dilapsos lapide eos restituerunt per Octavium Iulianum legatum ipsorum pro praetore
EDCS-30200704.translation: Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the ninth time, hailed imperator for the eleventh time, and Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, holding tribunician power for the third time, restored with stone the turf walls of the camp of the First Aurelian Cohort of Britons (millia), Antoniniana, which had collapsed from age, through Octavius Julianus, their propraetorian legate.
EDCS-30200704.summary: This monumental inscription records the restoration of the turf walls of a military camp with stone by Emperors Septimius Severus and Caracalla (Marcus Aurelius Antoninus), dating to 201 AD, carried out through their legate Octavius Julianus.

EDCS-30200705.text_edition: Dis Manibus Gaius Crispinus Gai filius Claudia Firmus Aspendo veteranus legionis I Italicae Aureliae Severae coniugi vixit annos XXVI et nepotibus Aureliae Primillae suae bene maerentibus sic posuit
EDCS-30200705.publication: CIL 03, 14491 = Conrad 00456 = ILBulg 00059 = IDR-02, 00204 = CERom-04, 00261 = PLINovae p 206
EDCS-30200705.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Gaius Crispinus Firmus, son of Gaius, of the Claudia tribe, from Aspendos, a veteran of the First Italic Legion, built this for his wife Aurelia Severa, who lived for 26 years, and for his deeply mourning granddaughters Aurelia Primilla and her sister.
EDCS-30200705.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Gaius Crispinus Firmus, a veteran, for his deceased wife Aurelia Severa and his mourning granddaughters, likely dating from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-30300645.translation: The Thirteenth Twin Legion. Aelius Julius.
EDCS-30300645.summary: This inscription records the name of Aelius Julius and his affiliation with Legio XIII Gemina, likely a military dedication or identification from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-30300652.summary: This is a short possessive inscription, likely part of a personal dedication or an ownership mark, identifying Marcus Opellius Adiutor, potentially from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-30500562.summary: This fragmentary inscription provides the name Gaius Iulius Valerius, likely from a personal dedication or funerary monument, possibly from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-30500568.summary: This is a genitive inscription indicating ownership or dedication by or for Claudius Domitius Euarestus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-30500610.translation: The Thirteenth Twin Legion. Aurelius Apollonius.
EDCS-30500610.summary: This inscription identifies Aurelius Apollonius and his connection to Legio XIII Gemina, likely a military record or dedication from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-30500616.summary: This short inscription gives the name Gaius Iulius Victor, likely from a personal or funerary context, possibly from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-30500620.translation: Of Gaius Ulpius C---, procurator of the emperor.
EDCS-30500620.summary: This inscription names Gaius Ulpius C---, an imperial procurator, suggesting an official or honorary monument, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-30500621.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription containing the name Gaius Spedius Victor, likely from a personal dedication or funerary monument, possibly from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-30500625.summary: This inscription records the name Publius Aelius Valens, likely from a personal or funerary context, possibly from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-30500626.summary: This is a dedication to the republic by a person named Amabilis, likely on a public monument or building.

EDCS-30500627.summary: This is a dedication to the republic by an individual whose name is abbreviated as A. Sec., likely part of a public work.

EDCS-30500629.summary: This inscription records the name of Manius Servilius Donatus, possibly as an owner, dedicant, or person commemorated.

EDCS-30500632.summary: This inscription records the name of Iulius Maro, possibly indicating ownership, dedication, or commemoration.

EDCS-30500637.text_edition: Tertullo et Sacerdote consulibus Iulius Alexander fecit
EDCS-30500637.translation: When Tertullus and Sacerdos were consuls, Iulius Alexander made this.
EDCS-30500637.summary: This inscription records that Iulius Alexander made something during the consulship of Tertullus and Sacerdos, providing a precise date for the work.

EDCS-30500639.summary: This inscription records that Marcus Arretio made or constructed something.

EDCS-30500642.translation: Fronto, son of Paulinus, made this.
EDCS-30500642.summary: This inscription records that Fronto, the son of Paulinus, made or constructed something.

EDCS-30500645.summary: This inscription records the name of Gaius Iulius V., likely a fragmentary dedication or commemoration.

EDCS-30500648.translation: A vow to Sol by Iulius.
EDCS-30500648.summary: This inscription records a fulfilled vow made to the god Sol by a person named Iulius, likely on an altar or votive tablet.

EDCS-31300300.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  Conservatori municipii Aeli Hadriani Napocae cura IIvirorum quinquennalium Cai Numeri Deciani et Iuli Ingenui votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-31300300.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, preserver of the municipium Aelium Hadrianum Napocae, Gaius Numerius Decianus and Iulius Ingenuus, quinquennial duumvirs, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-31300300.summary: This inscription records a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus as conservator of the city of Napoca, made by the quinquennial duumvirs Gaius Numerius Decianus and Iulius Ingenuus, indicating a public act of gratitude or petition.

EDCS-31400109.text_edition: Pro salute Luciliae Musae Felicis nostri Florentinus et Suriacus
EDCS-31400109.translation: For the well-being of Lucilia Musa, our Felix, Florentinus and Suriacus [made this].
EDCS-31400109.summary: This is a dedication made by Florentinus and Suriacus for the health of Lucilia Musa and their Felix.

EDCS-31400112.translation: Aurelius Aeternalis, in accordance with a vow, set this up.
EDCS-31400112.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Aurelius Aeternalis to fulfill a vow.

EDCS-31400114.text_edition: Invicto Mithrae I Q II votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-31400114.translation: To the Unconquered Mithras, I. Q. II. willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-31400114.summary: This is a religious dedication to Mithras, fulfilling a vow, likely by an individual identified by initials.

EDCS-31400116.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, likely recording the name and age of the deceased.

EDCS-31400117.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari divi Traiani Parthici filio divi Nervae nepoti Traiano Hadriano Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XVII consuli III patri patriae Suri sagiattarii sub Tito Flavio Constante procuratore Augusti
EDCS-31400117.translation: To the Imperator Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, son of the deified Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the deified Nerva, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 17th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of his country; the Syrian archers, under Titus Flavius Constans, procurator of the Augustus.
EDCS-31400117.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Hadrian by Syrian archers under the procurator Titus Flavius Constans, dated to approximately 133 AD based on Hadrian's tribunician power and consulships.

EDCS-31400118.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari divi Traiani Parthici filio divi Nervae nepoti Traiano Hadriano Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XXII consuli III patri patriae Suri sagittarii sub Tito Flavio Constante procuratore Augusti
EDCS-31400118.translation: To the Imperator Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, son of the deified Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the deified Nerva, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 22nd time, consul for the 3rd time, father of his country; the Syrian archers, under Titus Flavius Constans, procurator of the Augustus.
EDCS-31400118.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Hadrian by Syrian archers under the procurator Titus Flavius Constans, dated to approximately 138 AD based on Hadrian's tribunician power and consulships.

EDCS-31400119.text_edition: Valerius Valerianus eques librarius cohortis I Hispanorum ex voto posuit
EDCS-31400119.translation: Valerius Valerianus, cavalryman and clerk of the First Cohort of Spaniards, set this up in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-31400119.summary: This is a votive dedication made by Valerius Valerianus, a cavalryman and clerk from the First Cohort of Spaniards.

EDCS-31700005.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus basilicam legionis V Macedonicae
EDCS-31700005.translation: Imperator Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus [built] the basilica of the Fifth Macedonian Legion.
EDCS-31700005.summary: This inscription records the building or restoration of the basilica of the Fifth Macedonian Legion by Emperor Gordian III, dating to his reign (238-244 AD).

EDCS-31800206.translation: Claudius Iulianus, soldier of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, from the centuria of Claudius Marius, bought and took possession, by formal transfer, of a woman named Theudotes (or by whatever other name she is known), of Cretan nationality, free from encumbrances, for two unciae, four hundred and twenty denarii, from Claudius Philetus, with Alexander Antipater acting as guarantor. This woman was delivered sound to the aforementioned buyer, and if anyone should evict the buyer from this woman (or any part of her) in such a way that the buyer, or to whom the matter pertains, is not rightly permitted to use, enjoy, have, and possess her, then Claudius Iulianus, the aforementioned soldier, asked for a guarantee that such money, equal to the value of what was evicted or removed, or what was not permitted, would be paid correctly and reliably. Claudius Philetus promised to pay it reliably. Alexander Antipater ordered it to be by his guarantee. Claudius Philetus declared that he had received and held the price of this woman, the aforementioned four hundred and twenty denarii, from Claudius Iulianus, the aforementioned soldier. Transacted at Kanabis of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, on the 4th Nones of October, when Bradua and Varus were consuls. Claudius Iulianus, soldier of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, from the centuria of Claudius Marius, bought and took possession, by formal transfer, of a woman named Theudotes (or by whatever other name she is known), of Cretan nationality, free from encumbrances, for two unciae, four hundred and twenty denarii, from Claudius Philetus, with Alexander Antipater acting as guarantor. This woman was delivered sound to the aforementioned buyer, and if anyone should evict the buyer from this woman (or any part of her) in such a way that the buyer, or to whom the matter pertains, is not rightly permitted to use, enjoy, have, and possess her, then Claudius Iulianus, the aforementioned soldier, asked for a guarantee that such money, equal to the value of what was evicted or removed, or what was not permitted, would be paid correctly and reliably. Claudius Philetus promised to pay it reliably. Alexander Antipater ordered it to be by his guarantee. Claudius Philetus declared that he had received and held the price of this woman, the aforementioned four hundred and twenty denarii, from Claudius Iulianus, the aforementioned soldier. Transacted at Kanabis of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, on the 4th Nones of October, when Bradua and Varus were consuls. Valerius Valens of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, Gnaeus Varius A. Ae. Aelius Dionysius, veteran of the Legion, Paulinus, [---] Iulius Victorinus. Alexander Antipater, as second guarantor, signed on behalf of Claudius Philetus, the seller himself.
EDCS-31800206.summary: This inscription records a detailed legal document from 160 AD for the purchase of a Cretan woman named Theudotes as a slave by Claudius Iulianus, a soldier of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, from Claudius Philetus, with specific guarantees and witnesses.

EDCS-31900186.text_edition: Dis Manibus us Iusti VIII P Philippianae MI  vivBi#vivi#VIBI vixit  LXXXV IVO AN  fecit Valeria Iustina XXXV
EDCS-31900186.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iustus (or similar name ending in -us Iusti), a soldier of the Praetorian Guard, lived 85 years, [died] on the 4th Nones of [month]. Valeria Iustina, [aged] 35, made this.
EDCS-31900186.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a soldier named Iustus of the Praetorian Guard, who lived 85 years, erected by Valeria Iustina, likely a relative, who was 35 years old.

EDCS-31900187.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulio Claudio vixit annos II Aelia Va pie Cleo filio bene merenti fecrunt
EDCS-31900187.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aelia Va. Cleo made this for her son Iulius Claudius, who lived for two years, to their well-deserving son. He rests here.
EDCS-31900187.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Aelia Va. Cleo for her son, Iulius Claudius, who died at the age of two.

EDCS-31900188.text_edition: AREATINO cohorte I BatI(avorum)#Bat(avorum)#BAI vixit annos  Claudia Actav soror
EDCS-31900188.translation: To Areatinus of the First Cohort of Batavians, who lived for [---] years. Claudia Actav, his sister, [made this].
EDCS-31900188.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Claudia Actav to her brother, Areatinus, a soldier of the First Cohort of Batavians.

EDCS-31900189.text_edition: birsi militIi#militi#MILIII cohortis  annorum XXX Marcus Aurelius as frater Aur(elia)soror ponendum curaverunt fratri
EDCS-31900189.translation: To [---] of the [---] Cohort, a soldier who lived for 30 years. Marcus Aurelius [---] and Aurelia [---], his sister, took care of placing this for their brother.
EDCS-31900189.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Marcus Aurelius [---] and Aurelia [---] for their brother, a soldier who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-31900190.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Aurelius Drebias vBotum#votum#BOT rettulit
EDCS-31900190.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Aurelius Drebias fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-31900190.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aurelius Drebias, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-31900191.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Aurelius Cup[itus &
EDCS-31900191.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Marcus Aurelius Cupitus [---]
EDCS-31900191.summary: This is a fragmentary votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marcus Aurelius Cupitus.

EDCS-31900196.text_edition: Dis Manibus Dasas Capito vixit annos LV-- ipse posuit hic est situs
EDCS-31900196.translation: To the Divine Manes. Dasas Capito lived for 55 years. He himself placed this. He is buried here.
EDCS-31900196.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Dasas Capito, who lived to be 55, and placed the monument for himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-32200976.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Iunoni Reginae Iulius Valerius Emeritus praefectus cohortis
EDCS-32200976.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and to Queen Juno. Iulius Valerius Emeritus, prefect of the cohort [---]
EDCS-32200976.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno Regina by Iulius Valerius Emeritus, a cohort prefect.

EDCS-32200978.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publio Aelio Diophanto veterano cohortis V Gallorum vixit annos LXXXVI hic situs est Aelia Ammis filia et Aelia Eutychia libertae heredes fecerunt
EDCS-32200978.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Publius Aelius Diophantus, a veteran of the Fifth Cohort of Gauls, who lived for 86 years. He is buried here. Aelia Ammis, his daughter, and Aelia Eutychia, his freedwoman, his heirs, made this.
EDCS-32200978.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Aelius Diophantus, a veteran of the Fifth Cohort of Gauls who lived 86 years, erected by his daughter Aelia Ammis and his freedwoman Aelia Eutychia, his heirs.

EDCS-32200979.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelius Bassus veteranus legionis V Macedonicae vixit nnis LXVI mensibus X diebus XVI Aelius Helpizon libertus et heres eius posuit
EDCS-32200979.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aelius Bassus, a veteran of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, lived for 66 years, 10 months, and 16 days. Aelius Helpizon, his freedman and heir, placed this.
EDCS-32200979.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aelius Bassus, a veteran of the Fifth Macedonian Legion who died at the age of 66, erected by his freedman and heir, Aelius Helpizon.

EDCS-32200980.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Iulius Melchidianus veteranus ex beneficiario consularis legionis V Macedonicae vixit annos LXI Ulpia Marcellina coniugi pientissimo bene merenti posuit
EDCS-32200980.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulius Melchidianus, a veteran from being a beneficiary of a consular and of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, lived for 61 years. Ulpia Marcellina placed this for her most devoted and well-deserving husband.
EDCS-32200980.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Ulpia Marcellina for her husband, Iulius Melchidianus, a veteran of the Fifth Macedonian Legion who had served as a consular beneficiary and died at 61.

EDCS-32200981.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Iulio Verecundo veterano ex decurione alae Claudiae Claudius Longinus amicus et heres pos(uit
EDCS-32200981.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Claudius Longinus, friend and heir, set this up for Gaius Iulius Verecundus, a veteran, formerly a decurion of the Ala Claudia.
EDCS-32200981.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Claudius Longinus, a friend and heir, to Gaius Iulius Verecundus, a veteran and former decurion of the Ala Claudia.

EDCS-32200982.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valeria Gemina vixit nnos XVII dies X hic sita est Publius Aelius Papirianus |(centurio) cohortis III Campestris coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-32200982.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Valeria Gemina lived 17 years, 10 days. She lies here. Publius Aelius Papirianus, centurion of the Cohors III Campestris, set this up for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-32200982.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Publius Aelius Papirianus, a centurion, for his wife Valeria Gemina, who died at the age of 17.

EDCS-32200983.text_edition: Dis Manibus Primius Aelius Ionicus negotiator vixit annos L Iulia Priscilla uxor et Aelius Iulianus filius coheredes per Aelio! Primitivo sic liberto!
EDCS-32200983.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Primius Aelius Ionicus, a merchant, lived 50 years. Iulia Priscilla, his wife, and Aelius Iulianus, his son, co-heirs, [set this up] through Aelius Primitivus, his freedman.
EDCS-32200983.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Primius Aelius Ionicus, a merchant, set up by his wife Iulia Priscilla and son Aelius Iulianus through his freedman, Aelius Primitivus.

EDCS-32200985.text_edition: Dis Manibus Asclepiae Chronae vixit annos XL hic sita est Asclepius Asclepiades patronus coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-32200985.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Asclepias Chrona lived 40 years. She lies here. Asclepius Asclepiades, her patron, set this up for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-32200985.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Asclepius Asclepiades, a patron, for his wife Asclepias Chrona, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-32200986.text_edition: Sabina Labrionis vixit annos L Senecio Suri vixit annos XX
EDCS-32200986.translation: Sabina Labrionis lived 50 years. Senecio Suri lived 20 years.
EDCS-32200986.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the ages of Sabina Labrionis and Senecio Suri, likely a joint monument or part of a larger family burial.

EDCS-32200989.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Caius Iulius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix  Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate II consul proconsul pater patriae et Caius Iulius Verus Maximinus nobilissimus Caesar III milia passuum
EDCS-32200989.translation: Imperator Caesar Gaius Iulius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, in his second year of tribunician power, consul, proconsul, father of the country, and Gaius Iulius Verus Maximinus, most noble Caesar, 3,000 paces.
EDCS-32200989.summary: This inscription is a milestone or public dedication, dating to around 236 CE, honoring Emperor Maximinus Thrax and his son Maximus Caesar, marking a distance of 3,000 paces.

EDCS-32200990.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Antoniniana made this for Virius, six eminae.
EDCS-32200990.summary: This inscription records that the Legio XIII Gemina Antoniniana made something for Virius, possibly an object or a structure, involving a measure of six eminae.

EDCS-32500064.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iuliae Gratae filiae Iulius Strato |(centurio) legionis IIII Flaviae et Aelia Adaucta parentes fecerunt
EDCS-32500064.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Iulius Strato, centurion of Legio IIII Flavia, and Aelia Adaucta, parents, made this for their daughter Iulia Grata.
EDCS-32500064.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Iulius Strato, a centurion, and Aelia Adaucta for their daughter, Iulia Grata.

EDCS-32500065.text_edition: Dianae Reginae Marcus Ulpius Herculanus ex praefecto et Iulia Teupiboea eius votum solverunt
EDCS-32500065.translation: To Queen Diana. Marcus Ulpius Herculanus, former prefect, and Iulia Teupiboea, his wife, fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-32500065.summary: This is a votive inscription to Diana Regina, set up by Marcus Ulpius Herculanus, a former prefect, and his wife Iulia Teupiboea, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-32500067.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription containing parts of names, possibly 'Aurelius' or 'Aurelia,' and other illegible words, likely a funerary or dedicatory text with much information missing.

EDCS-32500070.text_edition: Titus Iulius Euanthus matri piissimae fecit
EDCS-32500070.translation: Titus Julius Euanthus made this for his most devoted mother.
EDCS-32500070.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Titus Julius Euanthus erected a monument for his mother.

EDCS-32500279.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Scenobarbus Dassi filius vixit
EDCS-32500279.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Scenobarbus, son of Dasso, lived.
EDCS-32500279.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes, recording the name of the deceased, Scenobarbus, son of Dasso.

EDCS-34100094.text_edition: Genio |(centuriae) Valeri Iustini Aurelius Muca pro salute  commanuculorum posuit
EDCS-34100094.translation: To the Genius of the century of Valerius Iustinus, Aurelius Muca placed this for the well-being of his comrades.
EDCS-34100094.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Aurelius Muca to the Genius of a military century, on behalf of his fellow soldiers.

EDCS-34100095.text_edition: Genio armamentarii posuit Aelius Vitalis custos armorum
EDCS-34100095.translation: Aelius Vitalis, keeper of arms, placed this to the Genius of the armory.
EDCS-34100095.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Aelius Vitalis, an arms keeper, to the Genius of an armory.

EDCS-34100096.text_edition: Terrae Matri Septimius Asclepius Hermes libertus numinis Aesculapi habens ornamenta decurionalia coloniae Apulensis et Augustalis coloniae eiusdem votum posuit
EDCS-34100096.translation: To Mother Earth, Septimius Asclepius Hermes, freedman of the divinity Aesculapius, holding decurional ornaments of the colony of Apulum and Augustalis of the same colony, placed this vow.
EDCS-34100096.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Septimius Asclepius Hermes, a freedman with significant civic honors, to Mother Earth, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-34100099.text_edition: Marti Toutatico Caius Valerius Hermes pro salute sua votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-34100099.translation: To Mars Toutaticus, Gaius Valerius Hermes willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for his own well-being.
EDCS-34100099.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Gaius Valerius Hermes to the deity Mars Toutaticus, for his own health and well-being.

EDCS-34100104.summary: This inscription is a fragmentary record, likely part of a funerary or dedicatory text, simply preserving the name Julius Sempronius.

EDCS-34100105.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno pro salute Impperatorum et cohortis primae sagittariorum Silvanus Flavius et Athennais sic sacerdotes cohortis supra scriptae ex voto posuerunt
EDCS-34100105.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, for the well-being of the Emperors and the First Cohort of Archers, Silvanus Flavius and Athennais, thus priests of the above-written cohort, placed this ex voto.
EDCS-34100105.summary: This inscription records a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus by two priests of the First Cohort of Archers, for the well-being of the emperors and their cohort.

EDCS-34500866.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a possessive genitive indicating ownership or dedication, preserving parts of a Roman's name, Marcus Ulpius.

EDCS-34500875.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a possessive genitive, preserving the name of Quintus Aponius Bassus.

EDCS-34500878.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely part of a dedication or record, mentioning Marcus Iulius D.

EDCS-34500885.translation: Of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, of Pomponius Fortis.
EDCS-34500885.summary: This inscription fragment identifies an individual, Pomponius Fortis, as belonging to the Thirteenth Legion Gemina.

EDCS-34500905.translation: Augusta, of the Papirian tribe, daughter of Domitius N.
EDCS-34500905.summary: This inscription likely records the name and filiation of a woman named Augusta, belonging to the Papirian tribe.

EDCS-34500912.text_edition: Caio Iulio Rufino filio Cai Iuli I  coll(egii) decuriae IX
EDCS-34500912.translation: To Gaius Iulius Rufinus, son of Gaius Iulius I, of the college of the ninth decuria.
EDCS-34500912.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Gaius Iulius Rufinus, noting his filiation and his membership in a college's ninth decuria.

EDCS-34500965.translation: The troop of Marcus Cartei, under Severus.
EDCS-34500965.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, the troop of Marcus Cartei, operating under the command of Severus.

EDCS-37200091.text_edition: Terentius Fegel Tit<i>us Degibitus Loscaiius sic Gemellus Titus A Marcianus ABCDEFGHIKLM NOPQRSTVXYZ
EDCS-37200091.translation: Terentius Fegel, Titus Degibitus Loscaiius, Gemellus, Titus A. Marcianus, ABCDEFGHIKLM NOPQRSTVXYZ.
EDCS-37200091.summary: This inscription lists multiple personal names, possibly indicating a group or community, and concludes with a lengthy, seemingly experimental or practice alphabet.

EDCS-38100329.text_edition: Silvano Domestico Publius Aelius Maximus sesquiplicarius
EDCS-38100329.translation: To Silvanus Domesticus, Publius Aelius Maximus, a sesquiplicarius.
EDCS-38100329.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Publius Aelius Maximus, a sesquiplicarius (a soldier receiving one-and-a-half times regular pay), to the deity Silvanus Domesticus.

EDCS-38100330.text_edition: Herculi Apro eques promissum fecit votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-38100330.translation: To Hercules Aper, a cavalryman made a promise, fulfilled a vow willingly, deservedly.
EDCS-38100330.summary: This inscription records a fulfilled vow made by a cavalryman to Hercules Aper.

EDCS-38100333.text_edition: propro tili Rufini conductoris salinarum Ursio servus actor verna
EDCS-38100333.translation: For Propro Tilius Rufinus, manager of the saltworks, Ursio, a slave, agent, home-born slave.
EDCS-38100333.summary: This inscription records the roles of Ursio, a slave, agent, and home-born slave, in connection with Propro Tilius Rufinus, the manager of saltworks.

EDCS-38100334.text_edition: Imperatoris Traiani metalla Ulpiana  Imperatoris Traiani metalla Ulp(iana)  Coloniae Ulp(iae)? D(acicae)? S(armizegetusae)  Coloniae Ulpiae Dacicae Sarmizegetusae
EDCS-38100334.translation: The Ulpian mines of Emperor Trajan. The Ulpian mines of Emperor Trajan. Of Colonia Ulpia Dacica Sarmizegetusa. Of Colonia Ulpia Dacica Sarmizegetusa.
EDCS-38100334.summary: This inscription reiterates the ownership of the Ulpian mines by Emperor Trajan and associates them with the Roman colony of Ulpia Dacica Sarmizegetusa.

EDCS-38100335.summary: This inscription, likely from the early 2nd century AD, marks imperial metal production, possibly referring to a mine or workshop named after Trajan's family name.

EDCS-38100338.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caius Domitius Alexander veteranus legionis V Macedonicae ex signifero vixit annos LXX Caius Domitius Nicostratus Augustalis coloniae Septimiae Drobetensium patrono secundum voluntatem testamenti
EDCS-38100338.translation: To the Divine Manes. Gaius Domitius Alexander, a veteran of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, formerly a standard-bearer, lived 70 years. Gaius Domitius Nicostratus, Augustalis of the colony Septimia Drobetensium, to his patron, according to the will's instruction.
EDCS-38100338.summary: This funerary inscription, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD, records the dedication of a tomb by Gaius Domitius Nicostratus to his patron, Gaius Domitius Alexander, a veteran standard-bearer, as specified in the patron's will.

EDCS-38800044.text_edition: Διὶ Ὑψίστῳ ἐπηκόῳ Αἴλ(ιος)∙ Ἀπολινάριος∙ ἐπίτροπος καὶ Μαξίμα εὐχαριστήριον
EDCS-38800044.translation: To Zeus Hypsistos, who listens. Aelius Apolinarius, procurator, and Maxima, in gratitude.
EDCS-38800044.summary: This dedication, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD, is a thanksgiving offering to Zeus Hypsistos (the Highest God) by Aelius Apolinarius, a procurator, and Maxima.

EDCS-41300196.text_edition: Apollini Granno et Sironae dis praesentibus Quintus Axius Aelianus vir egregius procurator Auggustorum
EDCS-41300196.translation: To Apollo Grannus and Sirona, present deities. Quintus Axius Aelianus, a most distinguished man, procurator of the Emperors.
EDCS-41300196.summary: This dedicatory inscription records an offering to the Gallo-Roman deities Apollo Grannus and Sirona by Quintus Axius Aelianus, an equestrian procurator, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-41300197.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Antonino Pio Augusto colonia Sarmizaegethusa sic
EDCS-41300197.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Antoninus Pius Augustus, the colony Sarmizegethusa thus [dedicates this].
EDCS-41300197.summary: This inscription, dating to the reign of Antoninus Pius (138-161 AD), is a dedication by the Roman colony of Sarmizegethusa to the emperor.

EDCS-44200277.text_edition: Titus Aelius Celer praefectus equitum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-44200277.translation: Titus Aelius Celer, prefect of cavalry, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-44200277.summary: This inscription, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD, records the fulfillment of a vow by Titus Aelius Celer, a cavalry prefect.

EDCS-44200281.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely from a dedicatory or funerary monument, preserving only parts of a name, possibly Publius Maximus or similar, and is undatable.

EDCS-44200282.translation: Carago B set up an altar to Silvanus.
EDCS-44200282.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an altar to the god Silvanus by an individual named Carago, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-44200284.text_edition: Nemesi Iunia Abascanti coniuncx sic ex visu votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-44200284.translation: To Nemesis. Iunia Abascanti, his wife, willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow according to a vision.
EDCS-44200284.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to the goddess Nemesis by Iunia Abascanti, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century AD, who acted upon a divine vision.

EDCS-44200285.text_edition: Minervae Augustae et Genio numeri Palmyrenorum Tibiscensium Valerius Rufinus actarius
EDCS-44200285.translation: To Minerva Augusta and the Genius of the Numerus Palmyrenorum Tibiscensium. Valerius Rufinus, actarius.
EDCS-44200285.summary: This inscription, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD, records a dedication to Minerva Augusta and the Genius of the Numerus Palmyrenorum Tibiscensium (a Palmyrene military unit at Tibiscum) by Valerius Rufinus, an actarius (clerk or record-keeper).

EDCS-44800149.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Tito Aelio Hadriano Antonino Pio pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XII imperatori II consuli III patri patriae et Aurelio Vero Caesari consuli II colonia Ulpia Oescus ob
EDCS-44800149.translation: To Imperator Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Pius, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 12th time, acclaimed imperator for the 2nd time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the country, and to Aurelius Verus Caesar, consul for the 2nd time, the colony Ulpia Oescus on account of...
