EDCS-44800149.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the colony Ulpia Oescus to Emperor Antoninus Pius and his adopted son Lucius Verus (then Aurelius Verus Caesar), likely commemorating a public work or benefaction, dating to around 149 AD.

EDCS-44800179.translation: The Cavalry Wing of Frontonianus, [commanded by] Valerius Firmus.
EDCS-44800179.summary: This fragment likely records the name of a cavalry unit, the Ala Frontoniana, and its commander, Valerius Firmus, possibly from a military dedication or monument.

EDCS-45100232.text_edition: Aurel annis ius Do annis I
EDCS-45100232.translation: Aurelius for years, Ius for years, D(ominus) for years, I(ulius) for years.
EDCS-45100232.summary: This fragment appears to be a funerary inscription, possibly listing individuals and their ages or years of service, though its meaning is obscured by its fragmentary state.

EDCS-45100233.summary: This is a funerary inscription or dedication mentioning Aelius Nisa Proculina, likely a female individual, with additional unclear abbreviations.

EDCS-45100234.text_edition: Sever annos XVII ius vixit annos
EDCS-45100234.translation: Severus [lived] for XVII years, [and/or another] lived for years.
EDCS-45100234.summary: This is a funerary inscription, recording the age of an individual named Severus as 17 years, and possibly mentioning another person's age, indicating a premature death.

EDCS-45100235.text_edition: nnos L a vixit nnos  Martinianus  bene merentibus posuit
EDCS-45100235.translation: [lived] for L years, [and/or another] lived for years. Martinianus placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-45100235.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Martinianus for one or more individuals who lived for 50 years, honoring them as 'well-deserving'.

EDCS-45100244.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Conservatori Aelius Super Augustalis municipii Septimi M Potaissensis votum libens posuit
EDCS-45100244.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Conservator, Aelius Super, Augustalis of the municipium Septimum M. Potaissensis, willingly placed this vow.
EDCS-45100244.summary: This is a religious dedication by Aelius Super, an Augustalis (priest of the imperial cult) from Potaissa, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Conservator, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-45100245.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Capitolino votum libens a<n=R>(imo#an(imo#AR solvit Aurelius Castor mensor legionis V Macedonicae piae
EDCS-45100245.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Capitolinus, Aurelius Castor, surveyor of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, Pious, willingly and gladly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-45100245.summary: This is a religious dedication by Aurelius Castor, a mensor (surveyor) of Legio V Macedonica, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Capitolinus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-45100246.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Aelius  NDN N IX
EDCS-45100246.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Aelius NDN N IX.
EDCS-45100246.summary: This is a fragmentary religious dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by an individual named Aelius, with the remaining text being unclear.

EDCS-45100249.text_edition: Silvano Domestico Aurelius Maximus miles legionis V Macedonicae piae Gordianae votum laetus libens
EDCS-45100249.translation: To Silvanus Domesticus, Aurelius Maximus, soldier of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, Pious Gordian, happily and willingly [placed this] vow.
EDCS-45100249.summary: This is a religious dedication by Aurelius Maximus, a soldier of Legio V Macedonica (likely during the reign of Gordian III), to Silvanus Domesticus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-45100250.text_edition: Silvano Domestico Aurelius Ianuarius votum libens posuit
EDCS-45100250.translation: To Silvanus Domesticus, Aurelius Ianuarius willingly placed this vow.
EDCS-45100250.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Aurelius Ianuarius to the god Silvanus Domesticus.

EDCS-45100251.summary: This is a fragmentary dedication to the deities Liber Pater and Libera.

EDCS-45100252.summary: This is a dedication to the deities Liber Pater and Libera.

EDCS-45100255.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valeria Maximilla vixit annos XXIX menses VII Valerius Maximus veteranus ex |(centurione) pater et Valeria Marcellina mater filiae pientissimae et Publius Aelius Tertius veteranus ex decurione coniugi optimae et pientissimae et Terentius et Valeria et Maximianus filii matri carissimae fecerunt
EDCS-45100255.translation: To the Divine Manes. Valeria Maximilla lived 29 years, 7 months. Valerius Maximus, a veteran who was a centurion, her father, and Valeria Marcellina, her mother, made this for their most devoted daughter, and Publius Aelius Tertius, a veteran who was a decurion, made this for his most excellent and devoted wife, and Terentius, Valeria, and Maximianus, her children, made this for their dearest mother.
EDCS-45100255.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Valeria Maximilla, recording her age and the multiple family members (parents, husband, children) who erected the monument.

EDCS-45100256.text_edition: Valeria Dulae vixit annos XXI ITTVIVN posuit Volusius Titianus coniugi pie  bene merenti
EDCS-45100256.translation: Valeria Dulae lived 21 years... Volusius Titianus placed this for his pious and well-deserving wife.
EDCS-45100256.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Valeria Dulae, erected by her husband Volusius Titianus, recording her age at death.

EDCS-45100259.text_edition: Dis Manibus ianus vixit nnos PANIANVS EI
EDCS-45100259.translation: To the Divine Manes. Ianus lived... (fragmentary and illegible details follow).
EDCS-45100259.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for an individual named Ianus, whose age is partially preserved.

EDCS-45100260.text_edition: itius Re supra script- filius eoannos XI Domiius filius  posuit
EDCS-45100260.translation: Titius, son mentioned above... lived 11 years. Domitius, son, placed this.
EDCS-45100260.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for a son, Titius, who lived 11 years, erected by another son, Domitius, and possibly referring to a previously mentioned father.

EDCS-45100261.text_edition: vixit --- annis IX Marcus Ulpius Valentinus vixit annis X Aureli
EDCS-45100261.translation: lived [---] 9 years. Marcus Ulpius Valentinus lived 10 years. Aureli[us/a]...
EDCS-45100261.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription recording the ages of at least two individuals, one who lived 9 years and Marcus Ulpius Valentinus who lived 10 years, with a third name partially preserved.

EDCS-45100262.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription preserving parts of two names: Bassinus Vitalis and Quintus.

EDCS-45100263.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription preserving the name Aurelius Crescens.

EDCS-45100264.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Surus, identifying his age and possibly a social or professional affiliation.

EDCS-45100273.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Gaius Iulius Aemilianus.
EDCS-45100273.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes, likely for Gaius Iulius Aemilianus himself or by him for another.

EDCS-45100274.text_edition: Volkano sic Augusto pro salute Imperatoris Luci Septimi Severi Pertinacis Augusti et Marci Aureli Antonini Caesaris destinati collegium fabrum municipii Septimii Potaissensis
EDCS-45100274.translation: To Vulcan Augustus, for the health of the Emperor Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Caesar Designate, the college of craftsmen of the municipality of Septimius Potaissensis [dedicates this].
EDCS-45100274.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by a college of craftsmen to Vulcan Augustus for the well-being of Emperor Septimius Severus and his son Caracalla (Marcus Aurelius Antoninus), dating to the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-45100278.text_edition: IunoniIunoniginae votum solvit Aelia Calli=Pope#Calliope#CALPOPE pro se et suis libens merito
EDCS-45100278.translation: To Juno Regina, Aelia Calliope willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow for herself and her family.
EDCS-45100278.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Juno Regina by Aelia Calliope, for herself and her kin.

EDCS-45100288.text_edition: Iunoni Raeginae sic Aelius Iulius veteranus ex |(centurione) votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-45100288.translation: To Juno Regina, Aelius Iulius, a veteran formerly a centurion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-45100288.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Juno Regina by Aelius Iulius, a veteran and former centurion.

EDCS-45100290.text_edition: Apollini Aelius Iulius veteranus ex |(centurione) votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-45100290.translation: To Apollo, Aelius Iulius, a veteran formerly a centurion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-45100290.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Apollo by Aelius Iulius, a veteran and former centurion.

EDCS-45100291.text_edition: Mercurio Aelius Iulius veteranus ex |(centurione) votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-45100291.translation: To Mercury, Aelius Iulius, a veteran formerly a centurion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-45100291.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Mercury by Aelius Iulius, a veteran and former centurion.

EDCS-45100292.text_edition: Silvano Domestico Aelius Iulius veteranus ex |(centurione) votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-45100292.translation: To Silvanus Domesticus, Aelius Iulius, a veteran formerly a centurion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-45100292.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Silvanus Domesticus by Aelius Iulius, a veteran and former centurion.

EDCS-45100293.text_edition: Dibus deabus et Fortunae Conservatrici Aelius Iulius veteranus ex |(centurione) votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-45100293.translation: To the gods and goddesses and to Fortuna Conservatrix, Aelius Iulius, a veteran formerly a centurion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-45100293.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the gods and goddesses and to Fortuna Conservatrix by Aelius Iulius, a veteran and former centurion.

EDCS-45100294.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Silvanus Domesticus, likely by an individual or group abbreviated as AVLV.

EDCS-45100325.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Aelius Malachus flamen quinquennalis municipii Septimii Porolissensis et sacerdos Dei numeri Palmyrenorum Porolissensium votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-45100325.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Publius Aelius Malachus, quinquennial flamen of the municipium Septimium Porolissense and priest of the Divine Spirit of the Palmyrenes of Porolissum, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-45100325.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Publius Aelius Malachus, a local official and priest in Porolissum, likely a votive offering.

EDCS-45100326.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Aelius Iacubus decurio municipii Porolissensis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-45100326.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Publius Aelius Iacubus, decurion of the municipium Porolissense, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-45100326.summary: This inscription records a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Publius Aelius Iacubus, a decurion of the municipium Porolissense.

EDCS-45100329.text_edition: Deo Libero Patri Titus Flavius Valentianus beneficiarius consularis
EDCS-45100329.translation: To the god Liber Pater, Titus Flavius Valentianus, consular beneficiarius.
EDCS-45100329.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Liber Pater by Titus Flavius Valentianus, a beneficiarius serving a consular legate, likely a votive offering.

EDCS-45100331.text_edition: annos X Aelius Firmus |(centurio) cohortis I Brittonum filiae piissimae
EDCS-45100331.translation: Aelius Firmus, centurion of the First Cohort of Britons, for his most dutiful daughter, aged 10 years.
EDCS-45100331.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument dedicated by Aelius Firmus, a centurion, to his deceased daughter, who lived for ten years.

EDCS-45100332.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulia Iustina vixit nnos XLII Cassius Martialis beneficiarius consularis coniugi carissimae Iustina coniux sit tibi terra levis
EDCS-45100332.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulia Iustina lived 42 years. Cassius Martialis, consular beneficiarius, [erected this] for his dearest wife. May the earth be light for you, Iustina, my wife.
EDCS-45100332.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument erected by Cassius Martialis, a consular beneficiarius, for his beloved wife Iulia Iustina, who died at the age of 42.

EDCS-45100335.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly a funerary monument, indicating that someone named Aelius or Aelia made this for themselves while still alive.

EDCS-45100336.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, mentioning a person named Aelia and possibly an age or number.

EDCS-45100337.translation: [...] Aelius (?) [...] and I/one [...] of the municipium (?).
EDCS-45100337.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly mentioning someone named Aelius and a connection to a municipium.

EDCS-45100361.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Aelius Ruf[us...] and [...].
EDCS-45100361.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aelius Rufus and another unnamed individual.

EDCS-45100362.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  Aelius Victor - votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-45100362.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Aelius Victor willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-45100362.summary: This inscription records a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aelius Victor, who fulfilled his vow.

EDCS-45100363.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo F Aurelius C us
EDCS-45100363.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. F. Aurelius C. us.
EDCS-45100363.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by F. Aurelius C. us, likely from a stone altar or monument.

EDCS-45100365.text_edition: Deae Nemesi sacrum Aurelius Rufinus beneficiarius consularis legionis XIII geminae Severianae votum libens posuit Maximo et Albino consulibus
EDCS-45100365.translation: Sacred to the goddess Nemesis. Aurelius Rufinus, consular beneficiary of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina Severiana, willingly and deservedly placed this vow when Maximus and Albinus were consuls.
EDCS-45100365.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the goddess Nemesis by Aurelius Rufinus, a soldier of Legio XIII Gemina, placed during the consulship of Maximus and Albinus (227 AD).

EDCS-45100369.text_edition: dis deabusque omnibus sacratum Publius Aelius Sextilianus beneficiarius consularis legionis V M[acedonicae &
EDCS-45100369.translation: Sacred to all gods and goddesses. Publius Aelius Sextilianus, consular beneficiary of the Fifth Legion M[acedonica].
EDCS-45100369.summary: This is a dedication to all gods and goddesses by Publius Aelius Sextilianus, a soldier of Legio V Macedonica, likely a votive offering.

EDCS-45100371.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelius Sura vixit annos L Aureli-
EDCS-45100371.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Aelius Sura lived 50 years. Aureli-
EDCS-45100371.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Aelius Sura, who lived for 50 years, likely set up by someone whose name began with Aureli-.

EDCS-45100372.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelius Viator vixit annos XXV Aelius Iunianus vixit annos XVI CN SAI AVR R RAN veteranus cohortis I Britannicae vivus posuit filiis piisimis
EDCS-45100372.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Aelius Viator lived 25 years. Aelius Iunianus lived 16 years. Gnaeus Saius Aurelius R. Ran, a veteran of the First Cohort Britannica, placed this while living for his most dutiful sons.
EDCS-45100372.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Gnaeus Saius Aurelius R. Ran, a veteran of the Cohors I Britannica, during his lifetime for his two deceased sons, Aelius Viator and Aelius Iunianus.

EDCS-45100377.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari divi Traiani Parthici filio divi Nervae nepoti Traiano Hadriano Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XV consuli III patri patriae ala I Tungrorum Frontoniana Antoniniana
EDCS-45100377.translation: To Imperator Caesar, son of the deified Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the deified Nerva, Trajan Hadrian Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the 15th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the country. The First Tungrian Ala Frontoniana Antoniniana.
EDCS-45100377.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Hadrian, acknowledging his full imperial titles, by the Ala I Tungrorum Frontoniana Antoniniana, likely dating to 131 AD when he held tribunician power for the 15th time.

EDCS-45100378.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelius Maximinus vixit nnos VL et Aeli Maximus vixit nnos VII Ursinus vixit nnos IIII Serena Licconis coniugi et filiis bene merentibus posuit
EDCS-45100378.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Aelius Maximinus lived 55 years, and Aelius Maximus lived 7 years, Ursinus lived 4 years. Serena, daughter of Licco, placed this for her well-deserving husband and sons.
EDCS-45100378.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Serena, daughter of Licco, for her husband Aelius Maximinus and their two young sons, Aelius Maximus and Ursinus.

EDCS-45100379.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Brisanus vixit annis L Aurelius Mucatius et Aurelius Maximianus filii ed sic heredes pro pietate posuerunt
EDCS-45100379.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Aurelius Brisanus lived 50 years. Aurelius Mucatius and Aurelius Maximianus, his sons and thus his heirs, placed this out of piety.
EDCS-45100379.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Aurelius Mucatius and Aurelius Maximianus for their father, Aurelius Brisanus, who lived for 50 years.

EDCS-45900188.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning a Titus Valerius, with the remainder of the text unclear.

EDCS-46400280.text_edition: Publio Aelio Publi filio Papiria Marcello viro egregio primo pilo ex praefecto legionis VII Claudiae et I adiutricis sub principe peregrinorum |(centurioni) frumentario sacerdoti Laurentium Lavi<natium patrono et decurioni colo>niae patrono rerum publicarum Fulginiatium Foroflaminensium itemque Iguvinorum
EDCS-46400280.translation: To Publius Aelius Marcellus, son of Publius, of the Papiria tribe, an excellent man, primus pilus (chief centurion), formerly prefect of the Seventh Claudia and First Adiutrix Legions, under the Princeps Peregrinorum, centurion in charge of provisions, priest of Laurentum Lavinatium, patron and decurion of the colony, patron of the communities of Fulginiae, Foroflaminii, and also Iguvium.
EDCS-46400280.summary: This is an honorary inscription for Publius Aelius Marcellus, detailing his distinguished military and civilian career, including service as chief centurion and various municipal patronages.

EDCS-46400873.text_edition: Ulpius     Claudius  beneficiarius triTbuni#tribuni#TRTB Pullius?  Aurelius   READSB Claudius Florus strator legati Tertius Iust Aurelius Secc speculator Statius Alexander tubR(icen)#tub(icen)#TUR Aurelius Licinius oiptio fabricae consularis Aelius Proc Aurelius Val strator legati Aelius Ma Aurelius Pri custos armorum Ulpius Bu immunis Pere M Aelius Cl tesserarius Laberius S beneficiarius consularis Helvius Iu  consularis Aurelius V n T m     Ulpius Bassinus dupliarius  Valerianus duplicarius  Florus duplicarius    Inus Modestus Valerianus duplicarius COI ato duplicarius Genialis duplicarius  Valentinus duplicarius er     Val<en=IV>s#Val<en>[s#VALIV   Ingenuus  Maximus   ILucund[us#Iucundus#LUCUND   Iulius Aelianus  Te<r=I>(tius#Te<r>(tius#TEI Marti    Ian     duplarius      Pu  Licini
EDCS-46400873.translation: Ulpius Claudius, beneficiarius of the tribune; Pullius Aurelius, READSB; Claudius Florus, groom of the legate; Tertius Iust Aurelius Secc, speculator; Statius Alexander, trumpeter; Aurelius Licinius, optio of the consular fabrica; Aelius Proc Aurelius Val, groom of the legate; Aelius Ma Aurelius Pri, custos armorum; Ulpius Bu, immunis; Pere M Aelius Cl, tesserarius; Laberius S, beneficiarius consularis; Helvius Iu, consularis; Aurelius V n T m; Ulpius Bassinus, dupliarius; Valerianus, duplicarius; Florus, duplicarius; Inus Modestus Valerianus, duplicarius; COI ato, duplicarius; Genialis, duplicarius; Valentinus, duplicarius; Valens Ingenuus Maximus; Iucundus; Iulius Aelianus; Tertius Marti; Ian, dupliarius; Pu Licini.
EDCS-46400873.summary: This inscription is a list of military personnel with their ranks and names, likely recording members of a military unit or their contributions.

EDCS-46400919.text_edition: Dis Manibus Dasas Liccai Delmata kastello Starvae vixit annos XXXV posuerunt Beucus Sarius et Da i heredes bene merenti
EDCS-46400919.translation: To the Divine Manes. Dasas, son of Liccai, a Dalmatian from the fort of Starva, lived for 35 years. Beucus Sarius and Da[s]i, his heirs, placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-46400919.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by the heirs of Dasas, a Dalmatian from a fort, recording his name, origin, age, and that he was well-deserving.

EDCS-46400920.text_edition: Dis Manibus Panenti Bizonis filio Delmatae kastello Starvae vixit annos XXV Plator Scenobarbi nepoti bene merenti posuit
EDCS-46400920.translation: To the Divine Manes. Panentis, son of Bizonis, a Dalmatian from the fort of Starva, lived for 25 years. Plator, grandson of Scenobarbus, placed this for his well-deserving nephew.
EDCS-46400920.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Panentis, a young Dalmatian from a fort, dedicated by his grandfather for his well-deserving nephew.

EDCS-46700087.text_edition: Deo Forti pro salute Impersacerdotalis Daciae  Aurelius Quintianus  Aurelius Augustianus Iulius Rufinus  Aurelius Surus  Aurelius Marc            ex custode armorum   ex speculatore ex duplicario ex optione ex optione ex duplicario        Aurelius Victorinus --- Aurelius Farnax  Aurelius Lucilius ex optione Aurelius Gaianus Aurelius Mucianus ex custode armorum Aurelius Maximus ex custode armorum Aelius Cassius IL Aurelius Lucius ex custode armorum Aurelius Agrippa ex custode armorum  ius ex duplicario nes nus  ex duplicario us s us ex duplicario  ex duplicario Aurelius Vitalis ex duplicario Aurelius Celsus ex duplicario Aurelius ValerianusEM G N Aurelius Sabinus Septimius Crisi CAD Aelius Lu OVP ex optione  Aurelius Iulianus Aurelius Statilius ex duplicario Aurelius Valens Aurelius Valens Septimus Alexander ex duplicario Valerius Valens Aurelius Flavianus ex immuni Aurelius Viatorinus Aurelius Crispus Aurelius Verus Aurelius Maximus ex armorum custode Aurelius  Aelius Valerius ex custode armorum Aurelius Domnio ex immuni Aurelius Valerius  Aurelius Anici Aurelius Firmidius Aelius Aufidius Aurelius Victorinus ex equite Aurelius Verus ex equite Aurelius Firmus ex duplicario Aurelius Maximinus Aurelius Sedatus ex immuni librario Aurelius Crescens Aurelius Iulius ex BETRI Aurelius Quintillianus Petronius Marcianus ex librario Aurelius Valentinus ex tubicine Aelius Maximianus ex tesserario SFI Volusius us veteranus ex beneficiario legionis MRA ex suo posuit
EDCS-46700087.translation: To the Strong God, for the welfare of the Imperial priesthood of Dacia. Aurelius Quintianus, Aurelius Augustianus, Iulius Rufinus, Aurelius Surus, Aurelius Marc, former custos armorum, former speculator, former duplicarius, former optio, former optio, former duplicarius; Aurelius Victorinus; Aurelius Farnax; Aurelius Lucilius, former optio; Aurelius Gaianus; Aurelius Mucianus, former custos armorum; Aurelius Maximus, former custos armorum; Aelius Cassius IL; Aurelius Lucius, former custos armorum; Aurelius Agrippa, former custos armorum; [---]ius, former duplicarius; [---]nes, [---]nus, former duplicarius; [---]us s us, former duplicarius; former duplicarius; Aurelius Vitalis, former duplicarius; Aurelius Celsus, former duplicarius; Aurelius ValerianusEM G N; Aurelius Sabinus; Septimius Crisi CAD; Aelius Lu OVP, former optio; Aurelius Iulianus; Aurelius Statilius, former duplicarius; Aurelius Valens; Aurelius Valens; Septimus Alexander, former duplicarius; Valerius Valens; Aurelius Flavianus, former immunis; Aurelius Viatorinus; Aurelius Crispus; Aurelius Verus; Aurelius Maximus, former armorum custos; Aurelius [---]; Aelius Valerius, former custos armorum; Aurelius Domnio, former immunis; Aurelius Valerius; Aurelius Anici; Aurelius Firmidius; Aelius Aufidius; Aurelius Victorinus, former eques; Aurelius Verus, former eques; Aurelius Firmus, former duplicarius; Aurelius Maximinus; Aurelius Sedatus, former immunis librarius; Aurelius Crescens; Aurelius Iulius, former BETRI; Aurelius Quintillianus; Petronius Marcianus, former librarius; Aurelius Valentinus, former tubicen; Aelius Maximianus, former tesserarius; SFI Volusius, veteran, former beneficiarius of the legion MRA, placed this at his own expense.
EDCS-46700087.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Strong God by a large group of individuals, many of whom are former military personnel of various ranks, for the well-being of the Imperial priesthood of Dacia, with one veteran placing it at his own expense.

EDCS-46700089.text_edition: Lucius Aninius Luci filius Papiria Firminus Tridente equo publico ex CCCtrecenario primus pilus legionis XIII geminae tetrastylum fecit et aquilam argenteam posuit
EDCS-46700089.translation: Lucius Aninius, son of Lucius, of the Papiria tribe, Firminus, with a public horse, formerly a trecenarius, first centurion of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, built a tetrastyle and placed a silver eagle.
EDCS-46700089.summary: This inscription records that Lucius Aninius Firminus, a distinguished veteran and former centurion of Legio XIII Gemina, constructed a tetrastyle and dedicated a silver eagle.

EDCS-49500005.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Aurelius Iulianus ex voto posuit
EDCS-49500005.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Aurelius Iulianus placed this by vow.
EDCS-49500005.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Aurelius Iulianus to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-55702024.text_edition: Dianae sacrum et omnibus Dis posuit kastellum Maniatium Nevato Ladi
EDCS-55702024.translation: Sacred to Diana and all gods. Nevato Ladi placed this for the fort of Maniatium.
EDCS-55702024.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Diana and all gods, placed by Nevato Ladi for the benefit of the fort of Maniatium.

EDCS-55702025.text_edition: Asclepio Augusto sacrum Fronto Plarentis dono dedit collegio
EDCS-55702025.translation: Sacred to Asclepius Augustus. Fronto, son of Plarens, gave this as a gift to the college.
EDCS-55702025.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Asclepius Augustus, given as a gift by Fronto to a college.

EDCS-56900095.summary: This is a fragment of an imperial inscription, likely part of a dedication or honorary monument involving an emperor.

EDCS-57100063.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Firmus vixit nnos XXX Aurelius Iulianus frater bene merenti posuit
EDCS-57100063.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelius Firmus lived for 30 years. Aurelius Iulianus, his brother, placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-57100063.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelius Iulianus to his brother, Aurelius Firmus, who lived for 30 years.

EDCS-57200151.text_edition: Herculi Aurelius Dubitatus votum solvit libens
EDCS-57200151.translation: To Hercules, Aurelius Dubitatus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-57200151.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Aurelius Dubitatus to Hercules, fulfilling a vow, likely on an altar or votive tablet.

EDCS-59500069.summary: This inscription is a brief record of a payment transaction, likely a third of some unit, made at the Temple of Castor.

EDCS-59500070.text_edition: Deo Soli Invicto Mithraethraeacrum MAA NA
EDCS-59500070.translation: To the Unconquered Sun God Mithras, Thraeacrum MAA NA.
EDCS-59500070.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the god Mithras Sol Invictus, likely made by an individual or group named Thraeacrum.

EDCS-59500075.text_edition: Dis Maioribus Domno et Domnae Caius Postumius Pansa |(centurio) legionis III Italicae votum solvit
EDCS-59500075.translation: To the Greater Gods, Lord and Lady, Gaius Postumius Pansa, centurion of the Third Italian Legion, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-59500075.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication made by Gaius Postumius Pansa, a centurion of Legio III Italica, to unspecified 'Greater Gods,' fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-62800026.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Queen Juno, Publius Aelius Stadylo, decurion and aedile, for his vow.
EDCS-62800026.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno Regina, made by Publius Aelius Stadylo, a decurion and aedile, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-64100113.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the syncretic deity Jupiter Optimus Maximus Silvanus, likely indicating a votive offering.

EDCS-64100114.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Lari Viali ex imperio muro M
EDCS-64100114.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and to Lar Vialis, by command, from a wall M.
EDCS-64100114.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Lar Vialis, made 'by command' (ex imperio), possibly indicating a divine instruction, and found on a wall.

EDCS-64100116.text_edition: Dis Manibus Crescens Maximi vixit nnos XXXV Iulius filius eius vixit annos IIII Iulia Saturnina fecit coniugi carissimo VI
EDCS-64100116.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Crescens, son of Maximus, lived 35 years. Iulius, his son, lived 4 years. Iulia Saturnina made this for her dearest husband.
EDCS-64100116.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument erected by Iulia Saturnina for her dearest husband Crescens Maximi, who lived 35 years, and their son Iulius, who lived 4 years.

EDCS-64100119.text_edition: Cultores dei Solis Malagbeli --- Proculus a exstruxerunt quorum nomina subscripta sunt  Spedius V coloniae Marcus Marcus Aurelius Iu Spedius anus Spedius?  Augustalis coloniae Pomponi(us) Avitus Valerius nus Spedius Primanus quiqui Rex Aurelius Aquilinus Aurelius Zopyrus Cominius Maximinus Aurelius Viator Aurelius Marcianus Antonius Barbas Aelius Geminus   Marcus Valentinus  Antipater  Crescentio  Eutyches Valerius Rufus Aurelius Vitalianus Esurio Domitius I
EDCS-64100119.translation: The worshippers of the god Sol Malagbel [...] Proculus and [others] built [this], whose names are subscribed: Spedius V of the colony, Marcus Marcus Aurelius Iu[---], Spedius [---]anus, Spedius Primanus, Augustalis of the colony, Pomponius Avitus, Valerius [---]nus, Spedius Primanus, Quiqui Rex, Aurelius Aquilinus, Aurelius Zopyrus, Cominius Maximinus, Aurelius Viator, Aurelius Marcianus, Antonius Barbas, Aelius Geminus, Marcus Valentinus, Antipater, Crescentio, Eutyches, Valerius Rufus, Aurelius Vitalianus, Esurio Domitius I.
EDCS-64100119.summary: This inscription records the construction of a monument by a group of worshippers of the god Sol Malagbel, with a lengthy list of their names, indicating a communal religious dedication.

EDCS-64100120.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aemilio Proculo Lucilia Marcia coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-64100120.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucilia Marcia placed this for Aemilius Proculus, her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-64100120.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication made by Lucilia Marcia for her well-deserving husband, Aemilius Proculus.

EDCS-65000086.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina. Aurelius Gaius.
EDCS-65000086.summary: This is a simple inscription, likely a soldier's mark, naming the Thirteenth Legion Gemina and the individual Aurelius Gaius, probably from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-65000087.summary: This inscription records the Thirteenth Legion and an individual named Aurelius Cam, likely a soldier's mark from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-66600394.translation: To Julia Augusta, mother of the most holy and pious Emperor Antoninus, and of the camps, the Senate, and the homeland, the Thirteenth Legion Gemina Antoniniana [dedicated this].
EDCS-66600394.summary: This dedication honors Julia Augusta (likely Julia Domna), mother of Emperor Antoninus (Caracalla), erected by the Thirteenth Legion Gemina Antoniniana in the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-68500278.translation: Zanax, soldier, from the century of Genialis.
EDCS-68500278.summary: This is a military inscription identifying Zanax as a soldier belonging to the century commanded by Genialis, dating probably to the Imperial period.

EDCS-69100013.text_edition: Legionis V Macedonicae piae fidelis pondus I  examinatum iussu dddominorum nostrorum Augustorum Valerius Sabinus  optio legionis supra scriptae dono dedit
EDCS-69100013.translation: Weight of the Fifth Legion Macedonica, pious, faithful. One [unit]. Examined by order of our most august lords, Valerius Sabinus, optio of the above-written legion, gave this as a gift.
EDCS-69100013.summary: This inscription records a weight belonging to the Fifth Legion Macedonica, given as a gift by Valerius Sabinus, an optio, under the command of the emperors, dating likely to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-69100017.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina. Aelius Iust.
EDCS-69100017.summary: This inscription names the Thirteenth Legion Gemina and Aelius Iust, probably a soldier's mark, from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-69100018.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina. Antonius Nigr.
EDCS-69100018.summary: This inscription identifies the Thirteenth Legion Gemina and Antonius Nigr., likely a soldier's mark, from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-69100023.text_edition: Legio XIII gemina Lucius Aurelius Secundinus
EDCS-69100023.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina. Lucius Aurelius Secundinus.
EDCS-69100023.summary: This is a soldier's mark, naming the Thirteenth Legion Gemina and the individual Lucius Aurelius Secundinus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-69100024.summary: This inscription records the Thirteenth Legion and an individual, Caesius Potitus, probably a soldier, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-69100025.summary: This inscription identifies Cornelius Valerianus as a member of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, likely a soldier's mark from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-69100026.summary: This inscription records the name of a soldier, Cupitus, associated with the Legio XIII Gemina, likely on a military object or memorial.

EDCS-69100027.summary: This inscription records the name of Flavius Heliodorus, associated with the Thirteenth Legion, likely from a military context.

EDCS-69100028.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina. Lucius Marcianus.
EDCS-69100028.summary: This inscription records Lucius Marcianus as a member of the Legio XIII Gemina, probably on a military artifact or dedication.

EDCS-69100029.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina. Lucretius Aquila.
EDCS-69100029.summary: This inscription records Lucretius Aquila as belonging to the Legio XIII Gemina, likely from a military context.

EDCS-69100030.translation: Memmius Fronto, of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina.
EDCS-69100030.summary: This inscription identifies Memmius Fronto as a soldier of the Legio XIII Gemina, suggesting a military record or dedication.

EDCS-69100031.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina. Pomponius Fortis.
EDCS-69100031.summary: This inscription records Pomponius Fortis as a member of the Legio XIII Gemina, likely from a military monument or object.

EDCS-69100032.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina. Statius Sentianus.
EDCS-69100032.summary: This inscription records Statius Sentianus as part of the Legio XIII Gemina, probably on a military dedication or marker.

EDCS-69100033.summary: This inscription records the name Tadius, associated with the Legio XIII Gemina, likely from a military context.

EDCS-69100034.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina. Of Terseni Maximus.
EDCS-69100034.summary: This inscription records Terseni Maximus as belonging to the Legio XIII Gemina, likely denoting ownership or dedication on a military item.

EDCS-69100036.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina. Valerius Anamus.
EDCS-69100036.summary: This inscription records Valerius Anamus as a member of the Legio XIII Gemina, likely from a military context or monument.

EDCS-69200144.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Roemetalces, trooper of the Second Ala of Pannonians, lived 38 years. Brisenus, standard-bearer, his heir, had this set up.
EDCS-69200144.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Brisenus, the heir and standard-bearer, for Roemetalces, a trooper of the Second Ala of Pannonians, who died at the age of 38.

EDCS-69200146.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelius Maximus, a veteran decurion, lived 5[...] years. His sons, Aurelii Manes, a centurion, Musaevus, a vexillarius, and Maximus Faustinus, set this up for their most devoted father.
EDCS-69200146.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by the three sons, Aurelii Manes (a centurion), Musaevus (a vexillarius), and Maximus Faustinus, to their most devoted father, Aurelius Maximus, a veteran decurion.

EDCS-69200637.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Valerius Masculus, son of Lucius, a veteran of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, aged 70 years, lies here. Set up for his dearest father out of piety.
EDCS-69200637.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Valerius Masculus, a 70-year-old veteran of Legio XIII Gemina, set up by his son out of piety.

EDCS-69200641.translation: Bitus gladly fulfilled his vow with a willing spirit.
EDCS-69200641.summary: This is a votive inscription by Bitus, recording the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-70100044.translation: ...BASAV... of the Numerus Palmyrenorum Porolissensium... for his most devoted and well-deserving wife, set this up.
EDCS-70100044.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by an unnamed individual, possibly associated with the Numerus Palmyrenorum Porolissensium, for his most devoted and well-deserving wife.

EDCS-70100060.translation: Of the First Cohort of Britons, the century of Artemidorus. Gaius R. In. curator armorum.
EDCS-70100060.summary: This is a military inscription indicating a century within the First Cohort of Britons, specifically the century of Artemidorus, and mentioning Gaius R. In., a curator armorum.

EDCS-70100067.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Aurelius Oceanus.
EDCS-70100067.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aurelius Oceanus.

EDCS-70100068.translation: ...Iunianus, decurion of the municipium of Porolissum, quadrumvir...
EDCS-70100068.summary: This is a fragmented inscription referencing Iunianus, a decurion of the municipium of Porolissum and a quadrumvir, likely part of a public or honorary monument.

EDCS-71900181.translation: The First Quingenary Cohort of Spaniards, of Titus P. Liberalis, centurion.
EDCS-71900181.summary: This military inscription records the First Quingenary Cohort of Spaniards and the centurion Titus P. Liberalis, possibly marking a building or boundary.

EDCS-71900185.translation: The First Quingenary Cohort of Spaniards, of Titus P. Liberalis, centurion.
EDCS-71900185.summary: This military inscription records the First Quingenary Cohort of Spaniards and the centurion Titus P. Liberalis, possibly marking a building or boundary.

EDCS-72200039.translation: Weight of the Fifth Legion Macedonica, 1. Examined by order of our three lords, the Augusti. Valerius Sabinus, optio of the aforesaid legion, gave this as a gift.
EDCS-72200039.summary: This inscription records a gift of a standard weight, examined by order of the Augusti, given by Valerius Sabinus, an optio of the Legio V Macedonica, likely dating to a period with multiple co-emperors.

EDCS-72200040.translation: Weight of the Fifth Legion Macedonica, 1. Examined by order of our three lords, the Augusti. Valerius Sabinus, optio of the aforesaid legion, gave this as a gift.
EDCS-72200040.summary: This inscription records a gift of a standard weight, examined by order of the Augusti, given by Valerius Sabinus, an optio of the Legio V Macedonica, likely dating to a period with multiple co-emperors.

EDCS-72200041.translation: Weight of the Fifth Legion Macedonica, 1. Examined by order of our three lords, the Augusti. Valerius Sabinus, optio of the aforesaid legion, gave this as a gift.
EDCS-72200041.summary: This inscription records a gift of a standard weight, examined by order of the Augusti, given by Valerius Sabinus, an optio of the Legio V Macedonica, likely dating to a period with multiple co-emperors.

EDCS-72700066.translation: To Sol Invictus (the Unconquered Sun). Mitae, the money-changer, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for the well-being of Publius Aelius Marius, flamen of the colony.
EDCS-72700066.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Sol Invictus made by Mitae, a money-changer, on behalf of the well-being of Publius Aelius Marius, a flamen of a colony.

EDCS-72700067.translation: Sacred to the Transit Gods. Vitalis G. S. fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-72700067.summary: This inscription is a votive offering dedicated to the 'Transit Gods' by Vitalis G. S.

EDCS-72700068.translation: Sacred to Cautopates. Vitalis, the money-changer G. S., fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-72700068.summary: This inscription is a votive offering dedicated to Cautopates by Vitalis, a money-changer.

EDCS-72700159.translation: To Silvanus Domesticus. Aurelius Annianus, procurator.
EDCS-72700159.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Silvanus Domesticus made by Aurelius Annianus, a procurator.

EDCS-74100308.translation: The First Cohort of Ituraeans, century of Mocimus. Seranus. Noeni.
EDCS-74100308.summary: This inscription records the names Seranus and Noeni associated with the centuria of Mocimus within the First Cohort of Ituraeans, likely a military record or dedication.

EDCS-74800007.translation: To the god Cautopates. Loc(ius?) Cratus, Augustalis of the colony.
EDCS-74800007.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the god Cautopates made by Loc(ius?) Cratus, an Augustalis of a colony.

EDCS-75000319.translation: Century of the primus pilus. Decimus.
EDCS-75000319.summary: This inscription records the name Decimus associated with the century of the primus pilus, likely a military personnel record.

EDCS-76400176.translation: To Liber and Libera. [---] Sec(undus?) Valenti(n)us, beneficiarius consularis of the Legio XIII Gemina Severiana, serving under the standards, willingly and deservedly placed this offering in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-76400176.summary: This is a votive altar dedicated to Liber and Libera by a consular beneficiarius of Legio XIII Gemina Severiana, likely from the Severan period.

EDCS-78300657.translation: Sacred to Liber Pater Augustus. [---] Iulius [---]us [---].
EDCS-78300657.summary: This is a dedication to Liber Pater Augustus, likely an altar or monument, by an individual whose name, Iulius, is partially preserved.
